headline: Cardinal Health Issues Voluntary Nationwide Recall of Webcol™ Large Alcohol Prep Pad

image.caption: Cardinal Health Issues Voluntary Nationwide Recall of Webcol™ Large Alcohol Prep Pad

description: Webcol announced a recall involving Large Alcohol Prep Pads (70% isopropyl alcohol) after issues linked to microbial contamination. FDA recall notice...
keywords: Large Alcohol Prep Pads (70% isopropyl alcohol)

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Cardinal Health has voluntarily recalled select lots of Webcol™ Large Alcohol Prep Pads (70% isopropyl alcohol) due to microbial contamination identified as Paenibacillus phoenicis. The recall affects products distributed in the United States, Puerto Rico, and Japan between September 2025 and February 2026. The contaminated prep pads pose a risk of local and systemic infections, particularly in at-risk populations such as patients with weakened immune systems, including those undergoing chemotherapy or with poorly controlled diabetes. These infections could be life-threatening, including bacteremia or central nervous system infections.

The Webcol™ Alcohol Prep Pads are intended for external use only and are commonly used for cleansing the skin. Cardinal Health informed customers of the recall by overnight mail on March 2, 2026, and has provided guidance to consumers on how to respond.

Consumers who have questions about the recall can contact Cardinal Health at GMB-FieldCorrectiveAction@cardinalhealth.com or call 800-292-9332, Monday through Friday from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. Eastern Standard Time. If any adverse reactions or problems have occurred after using the product, consumers should consult their healthcare provider. Such events may also be reported to the FDA's MedWatch program.

This recall is being conducted in coordination with the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA).

Product Lot Number Distribution Period
Webcol™ Large Alcohol Prep Pads (70% isopropyl alcohol) Various (select lots) September 2025 – February 2026

Consumers and healthcare professionals who wish to report adverse events or quality problems related to this recall can do so to the FDA MedWatch program by:

  • Submitting an online report at www.fda.gov/medwatch/report.htm
  • Downloading a reporting form from www.fda.gov/MedWatch/getforms.htm and submitting it by mail or fax
  • Calling 1-800-332-1088 to request a form
  • Faxing the completed form to 1-800-FDA-0178

headline: Mama Grande Tortilla Factory Issues Allergy Alert on Undeclared Wheat and Soy in Gorditas de Azucar and Doraditas de Azucar

image.caption: Mama Grande Tortilla Factory Issues Allergy Alert on Undeclared Wheat and Soy in Gorditas de Azucar and Doraditas de Azucar

description: Mama Grande announced a recall involving Gorditas de Azucar and Doraditas de Azucar after issues linked to undeclared wheat and soy. FDA recall notice...
keywords: Gorditas de Azucar and Doraditas de Azucar

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Mama Grande Tortilla Factory of Mission, Texas, has issued a recall for two of its products, Gorditas de Azucar and Doraditas de Azucar, due to undeclared wheat and soy allergens. These allergens were not properly indicated on the product labels, posing a risk of serious or life-threatening allergic reactions for individuals with wheat or soy allergies or severe sensitivities.

The affected products were distributed between February 2, 2026, and March 5, 2026, to retail stores and wholesale customers within Texas. The products are packaged in plastic bags with the product name and corresponding UPC code clearly displayed on the front of the package.

Mama Grande Tortilla Factory has not received any reports of illnesses related to this recall. Consumers who have purchased these products and have an allergy or sensitivity to wheat or soy are advised not to consume them. They should either return the products to the place of purchase for a refund or discard them. Retailers have been instructed to immediately remove the items from sale and hold them for pickup.

For questions or to arrange for product pickup, consumers and retailers can contact Agustin Armendaiz at 956-905-8234 during Monday to Friday, 8 a.m. to 4 p.m. CST. This recall is conducted in coordination with the U.S. Food and Drug Administration.

Product Name UPC Expiration Dates
Gorditas de Azucar 860010238134 All Expiration Dates
Doraditas de Azucar 5901234123457 All Expiration Dates
  • Do not consume the recalled products if allergic or sensitive to wheat or soy.
  • Return products to the place of purchase for a refund or discard them.
  • Retailers should remove recalled products from sale immediately and hold them for pickup.
  • Contact Agustin Armendaiz at 956-905-8234 for questions or to arrange product pickup (Monday-Friday, 8am-4pm CST).

headline: Pure Vitamins and Natural Supplements, LLC Issues Voluntary Nationwide Recall of Honey Products Due to Undeclared Sildenafil and Tadalafil

image.caption: Pure Vitamins and Natural Supplements, LLC Issues Voluntary Nationwide Recall of Honey Products Due to Undeclared Sildenafil and Tadalafil

description: Boner Bear, Red Bull and Blue Bull announced a recall involving Honey dietary supplement after issues linked to product contains sildenafil and tadalafil....
keywords: Pure Vitamins and Natural Supplements, LLC
Boner Bear, Red Bull and Blue Bull

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Pure Vitamins and Natural Supplements, LLC of Tampa, FL has issued a voluntary nationwide recall of certain honey dietary supplement products due to the presence of undeclared pharmaceutical ingredients. The affected products — marketed under the Boner Bear, Red Bull, and Blue Bull brand names — were found to contain sildenafil and tadalafil, active ingredients in the prescription medications Viagra and Cialis. These substances are not listed on the product labels and can pose serious health risks.

Sildenafil and tadalafil are used to treat erectile dysfunction under medical supervision. Their presence in these supplements without proper labeling may cause harmful interactions, especially for people taking nitrates for conditions such as diabetes, high blood pressure, high cholesterol, or heart disease. Such interactions can lead to dangerously low blood pressure.

The recalled products are packaged in single-serving plastic tubes and identified by brand names and specific lot numbers and expiration dates. Pure Vitamins and Natural Supplements, LLC is notifying customers via email to stop using and discard these products. Consumers who have purchased these items should return them to the place of purchase for a full refund, including shipping costs.

If you have experienced any health problems related to the use of these products, contact your healthcare provider promptly. Adverse reactions can be reported to the FDA’s MedWatch program. For additional information or questions, consumers may contact Pure Vitamins and Natural Supplements, LLC directly.

Product Name Lot Number Expiration Date UPC Code
Boner Bears Honey BBH0128 01/28 788362191603
Red Bull Extreme ORB524 05/2029 9554100205595
Blue Bull Extreme N/A 02/2029 707443349917

To report adverse reactions or quality issues, consumers can use the following options:

  • Online form: www.fda.gov/medwatch/report.htm
  • Download reporting forms: www.fda.gov/MedWatch/getforms.htm
  • Request form by phone: 1-800-332-1088
  • Mail completed form to the address on the pre-addressed form
  • Fax to: 1-800-FDA-0178

For consumer inquiries about the recall, contact Pure Vitamins and Natural Supplements, LLC at 1-800-362-4772 or via email at sales@purevitaminsusa.com. Customer service is available Monday through Friday from 9:00 AM to 4:30 PM EST.

headline: Insulet Initiates Voluntary Medical Device Correction for Certain Omnipod® 5 Pods in the U.S.
description: Omnipod announced a recall involving Alternate controller enabled insulin infusion pump after issues linked to pods may have a small tear in the internal...
keywords: Alternate controller enabled insulin infusion pump
Pods may have a small tear in the internal tubing that delivers insulin. This may leak inside the Pod instead of being fully infused into the body as intended.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Insulet Corporation has initiated a voluntary Medical Device Correction for certain lots of its Omnipod® 5 Pods distributed in the United States. This action follows the identification of a manufacturing issue involving a small tear in the internal tubing that delivers insulin inside the affected Pods. This defect may cause insulin to leak inside the Pod instead of being fully infused into the user's body as intended.

As a result, users of these affected Pods may experience high blood glucose levels due to under-delivery of insulin. In severe cases, this can lead to diabetic ketoacidosis (DKA), a serious condition requiring prompt medical attention. Insulet has received 18 reports of serious adverse events related to high blood glucose levels, including hospitalizations and cases of DKA, but no deaths have been reported. This issue does not affect continuous glucose monitoring (CGM) systems or their readings.

Customers are encouraged to verify if their Omnipod® 5 Pods are part of the affected lots by visiting omnipod.com/check-pods. Replacement Pods will be provided free of charge. If a Pod from an affected lot is currently in use, customers should discontinue its use immediately and switch to a Pod from an unaffected lot. Insulet has updated its manufacturing processes and quality controls to prevent recurrence of this issue and notified the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA).

For assistance or questions, customers may contact Insulet Product Support at 1-800-641-2049 (available 24/7) or use the live chat at omnipod.com/current-podders.

Affected Omnipod® 5 Pod Lot Numbers
Specific lot numbers can be checked online at omnipod.com/check-pods as they were not explicitly listed in the announcement.
  • Visit omnipod.com/check-pods to check your Pod lot number and request replacements.
  • If you have a Pod from an affected lot, discontinue use immediately and replace it with a Pod from a safe lot.
  • Contact Insulet Product Support at 1-800-641-2049 for assistance (available 24/7).
  • Use live agent chat at omnipod.com/current-podders for additional support.

headline: Lidl US Issues Allergy Alert on Undeclared Hazelnuts in Favorina Chocolate Ladybugs – German-Style Nougat

image.caption: Lidl US Issues Allergy Alert on Undeclared Hazelnuts in Favorina Chocolate Ladybugs – German-Style Nougat

description: Favorina announced a recall involving Chocolate Ladybugs - German-Style Nougat Candy after issues linked to undeclared hazelnut allergen. FDA recall notice...

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Lidl US has announced a recall of all lots of Favorina Chocolate Ladybugs - German-Style Nougat candy due to the presence of undeclared hazelnuts. This allergen was not indicated on the packaging, posing a potential risk to individuals with hazelnut allergies. Consuming the product could result in serious or life-threatening allergic reactions.

The recalled product was distributed from January 28, 2026, through March 11, 2026, at Lidl store locations in Delaware, District of Columbia, Georgia, Maryland, New Jersey, New York, North Carolina, Pennsylvania, South Carolina, and Virginia. To date, there have been no reported illnesses linked to this product.

Consumers who have purchased this candy are advised not to consume it and to return the product to their nearest Lidl store for a full refund. A receipt is not required for the return. For additional questions or concerns, customers can contact the Lidl US Customer Care Hotline at (844) 747-5435, available Monday through Saturday from 8 am to 8 pm ET.

Favorina Chocolate Ladybugs - German-Style Nougat 3.52 oz box 20304492 All lots 01/28/2026 – 03/11/2026 DE, DC, GA, MD, NJ, NY, NC, PA, SC, VA

  • Return the product to any Lidl US store for a full refund; no receipt required.

headline: Primal Herbs Issues Voluntary Nationwide Recall of Primal Herbs Volume Due to Undeclared Sildenafil

image.caption: Primal Herbs Issues Voluntary Nationwide Recall of Primal Herbs Volume Due to Undeclared Sildenafil

description: Primal Herbs announced a recall involving Dietary supplement after issues linked to product contains sildenafil. FDA recall notice published in 2026.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Primal Supplements Group LLC has issued a voluntary nationwide recall of Primal Herbs Volume dietary supplement due to the presence of undeclared sildenafil. This action follows FDA laboratory analysis that confirmed sildenafil, the active ingredient in the prescription drug Viagra, is present in the product but not listed on the label.

Sildenafil is used to treat erectile dysfunction under medical supervision. Its undisclosed inclusion in Primal Herbs Volume may pose serious health risks, especially for individuals taking nitrates for conditions such as diabetes, high blood pressure, high cholesterol, or heart disease. The interaction can cause dangerously low blood pressure.

The recall applies to all Primal Herbs Volume orders placed between July 2, 2025, and September 19, 2025, purchased via the website primalherbs.com. The product is supplied in 8.5 oz round containers featuring green labels. Consumers who purchased the product during this timeframe are advised to discontinue use immediately and contact the company for a replacement or full store credit.

For assistance, customers can reach out to Primal Supplements Group LLC by email at hello@primalherbs.com or by phone at +1 (856) 420-6117. Individuals experiencing adverse effects related to this product should consult a healthcare provider.

headline: Frito-Lay Issues Voluntary Allergy Alert on Undeclared Milk in Miss Vickie’s Spicy Dill Pickle Potato Chips

image.caption: Frito-Lay Issues Voluntary Allergy Alert on Undeclared Milk in Miss Vickie’s Spicy Dill Pickle Potato Chips

description: Miss Vickie’s announced a recall involving Spicy Dill Pickle Potato Chips after issues linked to undeclared milk allergen. FDA recall notice published in 2026.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Frito-Lay has issued a voluntary recall for select 8 oz. bags of Miss Vickie’s Spicy Dill Pickle Potato Chips due to the potential presence of undeclared milk allergens. The issue arises from the possible inclusion of jalapeño-flavored potato chips in the bags, which contain milk. Individuals with a milk allergy or severe sensitivity are at risk of a serious or life-threatening allergic reaction if they consume the recalled products.

The recalled products were distributed beginning January 15, 2026, through grocery, convenience, and drug stores, as well as local digital retailers across Arkansas, Louisiana, Mississippi, New Mexico, Oklahoma, and Texas. No other Miss Vickie’s products, including other flavors, sizes, or Variety Packs, are affected by this recall.

The issue was identified following a consumer report, and to date, no allergic reactions have been reported in connection with this recall. Consumers who have the recalled product and who are allergic or sensitive to milk should discard it immediately. Those without milk allergies may continue to consume the product safely.

Consumers seeking more information can visit the Miss Vickie’s Contact Us page or call Frito-Lay at 1-877-984-2543 (available Monday through Friday, 9 a.m. to 4:30 p.m. CST). Frito-Lay has notified the FDA of this recall, and additional updates will be available online.

Miss Vickie’s Spicy Dill Pickle Potato Chips 8 oz. 0 28400 76177 2 21 APR 2026 38U301414, 48U101514

  • Contact Frito-Lay at 1-877-984-2543 (Monday-Friday, 9 a.m. – 4:30 p.m. CST).

headline: USA LESS Co. is Recalling Rhino Choco VIP 10X Due to Undeclared Tadalafil

description: RHINO CHOCO VIP 10X announced a recall involving Chocolate after issues linked to undeclared tadalafil. FDA recall notice published in 2026.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
USA LESS Co. has issued a recall for its product RHINO CHOCO VIP 10X due to the presence of undeclared tadalafil, a prescription drug ingredient used to treat erectile dysfunction. The chocolate product is packaged in 10 gram, 12-piece boxes with a black cardboard outer box and is marked with UPC Code 724087947668.

Tadalafil, the active ingredient in Cialis, is not listed on the product label. Its undisclosed presence poses health risks, especially for individuals taking prescription nitrates such as nitroglycerin. These interactions can cause a dangerous drop in blood pressure. This risk is particularly concerning for people with diabetes, high blood pressure, high cholesterol, or heart disease who commonly use nitrate medications.

The recalled product was available for purchase both in retail stores and on various online platforms, including the company's website. The item has been removed from the online store pending resolution of the issue. Consumers who have purchased RHINO CHOCO VIP 10X are advised to return it to the place of purchase for a full refund.

For further inquiries, consumers can contact USA LESS Co. at 1-800-872-5377 or via email at 409749@email4pr.com.

headline: Savannah Bee Company Recalls Honey BBQ Sauce-Mustard Due to Undeclared Wheat and Soy

image.caption: Savannah Bee Company Recalls Honey BBQ Sauce-Mustard Due to Undeclared Wheat and Soy

description: Savannah Bee Company announced a recall involving BBQ sauce after issues linked to undeclared wheat and soy. FDA recall notice published in 2026.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Savannah Bee Company has issued a recall for their Honey BBQ Sauce-Mustard, 16 FL OZ, lot number B1L1360525, due to the presence of undeclared wheat and soy allergens. The product carries a best before date of 05/16/27 and UPC 8 50033 93758 9. Consumers with allergies or severe sensitivities to wheat or soy are at risk of potentially serious or life-threatening allergic reactions if they consume this product.

The affected honey BBQ sauce was distributed nationwide to distribution centers, retail stores, and consumers between July 30, 2025, and February 26, 2026. The sauce is packaged in clear glass bottles with an orange Honey BBQ Sauce-Mustard label. The lot number and best before date are etched on the neck of the bottle.

The recall was initiated after a customer reported that Honey BBQ Sauce-Sweet was mislabeled with a Honey BBQ Sauce-Mustard label. Investigation revealed that the mislabeled product omitted wheat and soy allergens from the ingredient statement. No illnesses have been reported so far.

Consumers who have purchased the affected product are advised to dispose of it immediately and seek a full refund. For further questions, consumers can contact Savannah Bee Company customer service at 800-955-5080, Monday through Friday, 8 AM to 5 PM EST. headline: GO Raw LLC. Expands Voluntary Recall of Quest Diet Cat Food Products Due to Low Thiamine Levels and Enacts Stop Sale of All Quest Products

image.caption: GO Raw LLC. Expands Voluntary Recall of Quest Diet Cat Food Products Due to Low Thiamine Levels and Enacts Stop Sale of All Quest Products

description: Quest announced a recall involving Cat Food Chicken Recipe Frozen after issues linked to may contain low levels of thiamine (vitamin b1). FDA recall notice...
keywords: Animal & Veterinary Food & Beverages Pet Food
May contain low levels of thiamine (Vitamin B1)

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Go Raw LLC. has expanded its voluntary recall of Quest Diet Cat Food products due to potential low levels of thiamine (Vitamin B1). The recall now includes two lots of Quest Cat Food Chicken Recipe Frozen products, in addition to a previously recalled freeze-dried product. The company has also enacted a stop sale of all Quest products at all retailers until the thiamine content issue is resolved.

Thiamine is an essential vitamin for cats, and deficiency can lead to serious health issues. Symptoms of thiamine deficiency include decreased appetite, salivation, vomiting, failure to grow, weight loss, and, in advanced cases, neurological signs such as neck ventroflexion, mental dullness, vision changes, unsteady walking, circling, falling, and seizures. Prompt veterinary treatment can typically reverse the condition.

The recalled frozen products were distributed nationwide through retail stores in multiple states including Colorado, Utah, Washington, Oregon, Pennsylvania, Rhode Island, Michigan, California, Texas, Illinois, Georgia, North Carolina, South Carolina, Florida, Minnesota, New York, Ohio, Wisconsin, Idaho, and Montana. The company has instructed retailers to remove the affected products from shelves immediately and is replacing all existing inventory with newly formulated products.

Consumers who have purchased the recalled products should stop feeding them to their cats and return the products to the place of purchase for a full refund or replacement. Replacement products are expected to be available starting mid-March. For questions, consumers can contact Go Raw LLC at cs@gorawllc.com or call 801-432-7478 (Monday through Friday, 9 am to 4 pm MST).

headline: Saputo USA Recalls Great Value Cottage Cheese Because of Possible Health Risk

image.caption: Saputo USA Recalls Great Value Cottage Cheese Because of Possible Health Risk

description: Great Value announced a recall involving Cottage Cheese after issues linked to not fully pasteurized.. FDA recall notice published in 2026.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Saputo Cheese USA Inc. is voluntarily recalling select Great Value cottage cheese products due to the possible use of liquid dairy ingredients that were not fully pasteurized according to state regulatory standards. Consuming these products may pose a significant health risk, particularly for young children, elderly individuals, and those with weakened immune systems.

The issue was identified during pasteurizer troubleshooting in collaboration with the California Department of Food and Agriculture. The affected pasteurizer has since been restored to normal function, verified, and sealed by the Department. No other products from the facility are impacted by this recall.

Affected products were distributed to Walmart stores and distribution centers across multiple states between February 17 and February 20, 2026. Consumers who purchased any of the recalled cottage cheese products should not consume them and may return the items to the place of purchase for a full refund.

For further inquiries, customers can contact Saputo at 1-888-587-2423, Monday through Friday, 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. CT. This recall is conducted with the knowledge of the U.S. Food and Drug Administration.

headline: Lockout Supplements Issues Voluntary Nationwide Recall of Boner Bears Chocolate Syrup Due to Undeclared Sildenafil

image.caption: Lockout Supplements Issues Voluntary Nationwide Recall of Boner Bears Chocolate Syrup Due to Undeclared Sildenafil

description: Boner Bears announced a recall involving Chocolate syrup after issues linked to undeclared sildenafil. FDA recall notice published in 2026.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Lockout Supplements has announced a voluntary nationwide recall of all lots of Boner Bears Chocolate Syrup due to the presence of undeclared sildenafil. Sildenafil is the active ingredient in the prescription drug Viagra, which is intended to be used only under the supervision of a licensed healthcare professional. Its inclusion in this product was not disclosed on the label.

The undeclared sildenafil in Boner Bears Chocolate Syrup poses a significant health risk, especially to individuals taking nitrates for conditions such as diabetes, high blood pressure, high cholesterol, or heart disease. Combining sildenafil with nitrates can dangerously lower blood pressure. Although no adverse events have been reported to Lockout Supplements so far, consumers are advised to stop using the product immediately.

The recall includes all lots of Boner Bears Chocolate Syrup distributed from January 1, 2025, through February 13, 2026. The product was sold nationwide online via lockoutsupplements.com. Lockout Supplements is notifying customers through its website and is offering refunds for returned products.

Consumers who have purchased Boner Bears Chocolate Syrup should discontinue use and return the product to the place of purchase or discard it. Those experiencing any health issues related to the product should contact their healthcare provider. Adverse reactions can also be reported to the FDA’s MedWatch program.

Product Name UPC Lot Dates
Boner Bears Chocolate Syrup 000856683570 01/01/25 - 02/13/26
  • Stop using Boner Bears Chocolate Syrup immediately.
  • Return the product to the place of purchase or discard it.
  • Contact a healthcare provider if any adverse effects occur.
  • Report adverse reactions or quality issues to the FDA MedWatch program:
    • Online: FDA MedWatch
    • Mail or fax information available on the FDA site
  • For questions, contact Lockout Supplements at 972-548-1988 or email glenn@lockoutforums.com (Mon–Fri, 9:00 AM–4:00 PM CST).

headline: Elite Treats LLC Recalls Single Lot of Chicken Chips Due to Salmonella Contamination

image.caption: Elite Treats LLC Recalls Single Lot of Chicken Chips Due to Salmonella Contamination

description: Elite Treats announced a recall involving Chicken dog treats after issues linked to may be contaminated with salmonella. FDA recall notice published in 2026.
keywords: Animal & Veterinary Food & Beverages Pet Food Foodborne Illness

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Elite Treats, LLC., based in Boca Raton, FL, has issued a recall for a single lot of its "Elite Treats Chicken Chips for Dogs" 6-ounce bags due to potential Salmonella contamination. The affected lot is marked with lot number 24045 and an expiration date of 04/2027. Salmonella contamination in pet food poses health risks to both pets and humans.

Pets consuming contaminated products may experience symptoms such as lethargy, diarrhea (possibly bloody), fever, vomiting, decreased appetite, and abdominal pain. Some pets may be asymptomatic carriers, spreading the bacteria through feces and saliva, placing other pets and household members at risk.

Humans can be exposed to Salmonella through handling the product, contact with infected pets, or contact with contaminated surfaces. Symptoms in humans can include nausea, vomiting, diarrhea, abdominal pain, and fever, and in rare cases can lead to severe infections affecting various organs. Individuals who develop symptoms after exposure should seek medical advice promptly.

The recalled product was distributed to feed stores in Alabama, Florida, Georgia, North Carolina, and South Carolina via Florida Hardware, LLC. There have been no reported illnesses linked to this product as of this announcement. Consumers who have purchased this specific lot are advised to stop use immediately and dispose of the product safely, while thoroughly cleaning all utensils and surfaces that came into contact with it.

Product Package Size Lot Number Expiration Date
Elite Treats Chicken Chips for Dogs 6-ounce bag 24045 04/2027
  • Consumers should not sell or donate the recalled product.
  • Dispose of the product in a manner inaccessible to children, pets, and wildlife.
  • Wash and sanitize pet food bowls, cups, storage containers, utensils, and all surfaces that came in contact with the recalled product.
  • Wash hands thoroughly after handling the product or contact with pets that have consumed it.
  • Contact your healthcare provider if you develop symptoms after exposure.
  • If your pet shows symptoms or you suspect infection, contact your veterinarian.
  • For questions about the recall or for a refund or replacement, contact Elite Treats at elite.treats@yahoo.com or call 561-901-5310 Monday through Friday, 8:00 AM to 4:00 PM EST.

headline: Made Fresh Salads, Inc. Recalls Assorted Flavors of Cream Cheese Because of Possible Health Risk

image.caption: Made Fresh Salads, Inc. Recalls Assorted Flavors of Cream Cheese Because of Possible Health Risk

description: Made Fresh Salad announced a recall involving Various Cream Cheeses and Tofu Spread after issues linked to listeria monocytogenes. FDA recall notice...
keywords: Various Cream Cheeses and Tofu Spread

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Made Fresh Salads, Inc., located in Bay Shore, NY, is recalling multiple flavors of its cream cheese products due to potential contamination with Listeria monocytogenes. This bacterium can cause serious and sometimes fatal infections particularly in young children, elderly individuals, and those with weakened immune systems. Pregnant women infected with Listeria risk miscarriage or stillbirth, while healthy individuals may experience symptoms such as high fever, severe headache, stiffness, nausea, abdominal pain, and diarrhea.

The recalled cream cheese products were distributed in Brooklyn, Queens, the Bronx, and broader New York City areas via direct delivery to retail stores and distributors. The affected products come in 5-pound white plastic tubs labeled with Made Fresh Salads branding. All affected items have expiration dates through February 27, 2026, located on the bottom left corner of the label.

This recall was initiated after a routine company sampling program detected Listeria monocytogenes contamination within a mixer used for production. Made Fresh Salads has ceased using and removed the contaminated mixer from service. To date, no illnesses related to these products have been reported.

Consumers who have purchased any of the affected Made Fresh Salads cream cheese products are advised to return them to the place of purchase for a full refund. For further inquiries, customers can contact Made Fresh Salads at 1-718-765-0082, Monday through Friday from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET.

headline: Go Raw, LLC. Issues Voluntary Recall of a Single Lot of Quest Cat Food Chicken Recipe Freeze Dried Nuggets Due to Low Levels of Thiamine (Vitamin B1)

image.caption: Go Raw, LLC. Issues Voluntary Recall of a Single Lot of Quest Cat Food Chicken Recipe Freeze Dried Nuggets Due to Low Levels of Thiamine (Vitamin B1)

description: Quest announced a recall involving Quest Cat Food Chicken Recipe Freeze Dried Nuggets, 10oz bag after issues linked to may contain low levels of thiamine...
keywords: Quest Cat Food Chicken Recipe Freeze Dried Nuggets, 10oz bag
Animal & Veterinary Food & Beverages Pet Food
May contain low levels of thiamine (Vitamin B1)

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Go Raw LLC has announced a voluntary recall of a single lot of Quest Cat Food Chicken Recipe Freeze Dried Nuggets, 10 oz bag, due to potentially low levels of thiamine (Vitamin B1). This recall was initiated after one confirmed illness report linked to the product, with testing confirming thiamine levels below requirements for a feline diet.

Thiamine is an essential nutrient for cats, and inadequate intake over time can lead to thiamine deficiency. Symptoms in cats may start with gastrointestinal issues such as decreased appetite, salivation, vomiting, failure to grow, and weight loss. If deficiency advances, neurological symptoms may develop, including neck bending towards the floor (ventroflexion), mental dullness, vision changes, unsteady walking, circling, falling, and seizures. Prompt veterinary attention is recommended if any such symptoms are observed, as thiamine deficiency is usually reversible with early treatment.

The recalled product was sold nationwide in retail stores located in Colorado, Utah, Washington, Oregon, Pennsylvania, Rhode Island, Michigan, California, Texas, and Illinois. Consumers who have purchased this specific lot should stop feeding the product and return it to the place of purchase for a full refund. For questions, consumers can contact Go Raw LLC customer service by email or phone during business hours.

headline: Shaman Botanicals, LLC Issues Voluntary Nationwide Recall of Alkaloids Chewable Tablets—White Vein Due to Undeclared Concentration of 7-Hydroxymitragynine (7-OH)

image.caption: Shaman Botanicals, LLC Issues Voluntary Nationwide Recall of Alkaloids Chewable Tablets—White Vein Due to Undeclared Concentration of 7-Hydroxymitragynine (7-OH)

description: Advanced Alkaloids announced a recall involving Alkaloids Chewable Tablets—White Vein after issues linked to product contains 7-hydroxymitragynine (7-oh) in...
keywords: Product contains 7-Hydroxymitragynine (7-OH) in an amount more than the declared value of 7.5 mg/tablet.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Shaman Botanicals, LLC has voluntarily recalled one lot of its Alkaloids Chewable Tablets—White Vein dietary supplement due to an undeclared concentration of 7-Hydroxymitragynine (7-OH) exceeding the labeled amount of 7.5 mg per tablet. This recall affects products distributed nationwide to wholesalers, retailers, and consumers through online sales.

Consumers using the affected product may ingest a higher dose of 7-Hydroxymitragynine than intended, which could potentially lead to adverse health effects. To date, Shaman Botanicals, LLC has not received any reports of adverse events related to the recalled lot.

The recall applies to Lot B# AAW.501.3 of Alkaloids Chewable Tablets—White Vein, regardless of expiration date. The product is sold in various packaging formats identified by their UPC codes and lot number printed on the package or bottle bottom.

Customers and distributors who possess the recalled product should immediately stop use, quarantine the item, and return it to Shaman Botanicals, LLC for a full refund or replacement. Additional information and return registration can be found at the company’s dedicated recall website.

Product Name Lot Number UPC Code
Alkaloids Chewable Tablets—White Vein (2-count bag) Lot B# AAW.501.3 810057763724
Alkaloids Chewable Tablets—White Vein (20-count bag) Lot B# AAW.501.3 810057763830
Alkaloids Chewable Tablets—White Vein (30-count bottle) Lot B# AAW.501.3 810057763779

For questions or to arrange returns, contact Shaman Botanicals, LLC during business hours (Monday through Friday, 9am to 5pm CST) at:

Vince Sanders, Quality Department
1501 Iron Street
North Kansas City, MO 64116
Phone: 855-427-7386
Email: Quality@CBDAmericanShaman.com
Recall information and registration: https://recall.cbdamericanshaman.com/

Consumers who experience any adverse health issues related to this product should consult their healthcare provider. Adverse reactions or quality problems may also be reported to the FDA’s MedWatch program using one of the following methods:

  • Submit online at: www.fda.gov/medwatch/report.htm
  • Regular mail or fax: Download the reporting form at www.fda.gov/MedWatch/getforms.htm or call 1-800-332-1088 to request a form, then mail to the address on the form or fax to 1-800-FDA-0178

This recall is being conducted with the knowledge of the U.S. Food and Drug Administration.

headline: Ambrosia Brands, LLC Recalls Rosabella Moringa Capsules Because of Possible Health Risk

image.caption: Ambrosia Brands, LLC Recalls Rosabella Moringa Capsules Because of Possible Health Risk

description: Rosabella announced a recall involving Moringa capsules after issues linked to possible contamination with salmonella. FDA recall notice published in 2026.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Ambrosia Brands, LLC of New York, New York, is voluntarily recalling certain lots of its Rosabella Moringa Capsules due to possible contamination with Salmonella. This bacterium can cause serious and sometimes fatal infections, particularly in young children, elderly people, and individuals with weakened immune systems. Healthy persons infected with Salmonella may experience symptoms such as fever, diarrhea, nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain. In rare cases, infection can lead to severe complications including bloodstream infections and arterial infections.

The recalled product, Rosabella Moringa Capsules in 60-count bottles, was sold nationwide in the United States via the company's direct-to-consumer website and TikTok Shop since February 2025. There is concern about unauthorized third-party sales through platforms such as eBay and Shein. Although the recalled lots have not been sold on Amazon.com, consumers who have purchased this product from Amazon are advised to verify their lot numbers.

To date, seven illnesses and three hospitalizations across the U.S. have been linked to Salmonella contamination possibly associated with these products. Ambrosia Brands has stopped purchasing the implicated raw moringa leaf powder and is working with the FDA and CDC to investigate the outbreak. Customers who have bottles from the affected lots should not consume, sell, or distribute the capsules and are instructed to dispose of them immediately.

Consumers with questions can contact Ambrosia Brands' customer service Monday through Friday from 9 a.m. to 4 p.m. CT at 914-768-1357.

headline: Updated Company Announcement to August 28, 2025, Recall Announcement
description: Green Lumber announced a recall involving Dietary Supplement after issues linked to undeclared prescription drug-tadalafil. FDA recall notice published in 2026.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Green Lumber Holding, LLC has issued an updated consumer alert related to a recall of dietary supplements falsely marketed as Green Lumber products. The recall was originally announced on August 28, 2025, after the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA) detected the presence of tadalafil—an undeclared prescription drug—in some products labeled as Green Lumber. Tadalafil is not an ingredient in any authentic Green Lumber product, and its presence poses significant health risks.

The FDA’s February 9, 2026 Enforcement Report referenced this recall but did not announce a new recall. Green Lumber’s internal investigation found that a former employee had misappropriated genuine packaging and customer information to distribute counterfeit or adulterated products. That individual has since been terminated. Consumers should be aware that only products bearing the lot number LOT308EXP03/28 are counterfeit and subject to the recall, and these products should be immediately discontinued and discarded.

Authentic Green Lumber products have specific lot numbers that are not affected by the recall. Consumers are urged to verify the lot numbers on their packaging to ensure product authenticity. Using the counterfeit products may expose consumers to dangerous side effects, especially if they are taking medications for heart disease, blood pressure, or other health conditions due to the undeclared tadalafil. Anyone experiencing unexpected side effects after using these supplements should seek medical attention promptly.

Consumers are encouraged to report suspected counterfeit products both to Green Lumber and the FDA’s MedWatch Adverse Event Reporting Program. Green Lumber has strengthened its safeguards and cooperated with the FDA and law enforcement to protect customers from such risks.

Lot Number Expiration Date
LOTGL0130151EXP0926 09/26
GL01EXP09/27 09/27
GL110725EXP110727 11/07/27

The following lot number is the only one subject to recall due to being identified as counterfeit and containing undeclared tadalafil:

Lot Number Expiration Date
LOT308EXP03/28 03/28

If you have products with the recalled lot number, discontinue use immediately and discard the product. To report suspected counterfeit products or adverse events, consumers can:

  • Contact Green Lumber Holding, LLC at 949.426.8622 or email consumer-safety@greenlumber.com
  • Report to the FDA’s MedWatch Adverse Event Reporting Program via MedWatch website

For more detailed information, visit Green Lumber’s recall alert page at www.greenlumber.com/alert.

headline: IF Copack LLC dba Initiative Foods Recalls “Tippy Toes” Brand Apple Pear Banana Because of Possible Health Risk

image.caption: IF Copack LLC dba Initiative Foods Recalls “Tippy Toes” Brand Apple Pear Banana Because of Possible Health Risk

description: Tippy Toes announced a recall involving Baby Food after issues linked to elevated levels of patulin. FDA recall notice published in 2026.
keywords: IF Copack, LLC. d.b.a. Initiative Foods

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
IF Copack LLC d.b.a. Initiative Foods is recalling one lot of the “Tippy Toes” Apple Pear Banana Fruit puree due to elevated levels of patulin, a mycotoxin produced by mold that can grow on various fruits. Long-term exposure to patulin may cause immune suppression, nerve damage, headache, fever, and nausea. No illnesses or injuries have been reported so far.

The recalled product was distributed nationwide across the United States, excluding Alaska, and may have also reached Guam and Puerto Rico. The recall follows findings by the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA) during its Total Diet Study, which detected patulin levels higher than normally expected. Initiative Foods is working with the FDA to ensure consumer safety by recalling the affected lot.

Consumers who have purchased the product with the specified lot number and “Best By” date should stop use immediately. They may dispose of the item or return it to the place of purchase for a refund. Any health concerns after consuming the product should be discussed with a healthcare provider.

Retailers are advised to check their stock and remove the recalled lot from sale or distribution promptly. A dedicated toll-free number is available for further information.

Product Name Packaging Format UPC Lot Number / Expiration Date Package Code Identifier
Tippy Toes Apple Pear Banana 2-pack plastic tubs, with product information sleeve 036800 265783 Lot # 07174 / Best By Date: BB 07/17/2026 INIA0120

  • Consumers experiencing health issues after consumption should contact their healthcare provider.
  • Retailers should immediately remove the recalled lot from sale or distribution and document the affected inventory.
  • For additional information, contact Initiative Foods at 1-855-215-5730, Monday through Friday, 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. EST.

headline: Junebar Issues Allergy Alert on Undeclared Milk and Soy in Chocolate Cherry and Peanut Butter Chocolate Chip Flavor Bars

image.caption: Junebar Issues Allergy Alert on Undeclared Milk and Soy in Chocolate Cherry and Peanut Butter Chocolate Chip Flavor Bars

description: Junebar announced a recall involving Snack Bars after issues linked to undeclared milk and soy. FDA recall notice published in 2026.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Juniper Granola, LLC., based in Rochester, NY, has issued a recall for select Junebar snack bars due to the presence of undeclared milk and soy allergens. The affected products are the Chocolate Cherry and Peanut Butter Chocolate Chip Junebars with batch labels L1300 or L1301. These bars may pose a serious or life-threatening risk to individuals allergic or severely sensitive to milk or soy.

The impacted bars were distributed throughout New York State in January 2026 via grocery retailers and direct website orders. The undeclared allergens were identified after a customer experienced an allergic reaction to milk, prompting an investigation. The issue was traced to a sourcing error, where non-vegan chocolate chips containing milk and soy were mistakenly used during production instead of the usual dairy-free, soy-free chips.

Consumers who have purchased these specific Junebars are advised to check the batch labels and return any affected bars to the point of purchase for a full refund. For additional information or questions, customers can contact Juniper Granola directly by phone or email.

Product Batch Labels Distribution Area Distribution Date
Chocolate Cherry Junebar L1300, L1300A, L1300B, L1301A New York State January 2026
Peanut Butter Chocolate Chip Junebar L1300, L1300A, L1300B, L1301A New York State January 2026
  • Check the back of the bar pouch near the bottom for the batch label.
  • Do not consume Junebars with batch labels L1300, L1300A, L1300B, or L1301A if you have milk or soy allergies.
  • Return affected products to the place of purchase for a full refund.
  • Contact Juniper Granola at 315-226-3339 or email contact@junebars.com for questions or more information.

headline: Slade Gorton & Co., Inc, Recalls One Lot of Wellsley Farms Farm-Raised Atlantic Salmon Sold at BJ’s Wholesale Club due to Potential Listeria Monocytogenes Contamination

image.caption: Slade Gorton & Co., Inc, Recalls One Lot of Wellsley Farms Farm-Raised Atlantic Salmon Sold at BJ’s Wholesale Club due to Potential Listeria Monocytogenes Contamination

description: Wellsley Farms announced a recall involving Salmon after issues linked to listeria monocytogenes. FDA recall notice published in 2026.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Slade Gorton & Co., Inc. is recalling one lot of Wellsley Farms Farm-Raised Atlantic Salmon due to potential contamination with Listeria monocytogenes. The affected product was sold exclusively at BJ’s Wholesale Club stores in Delaware, Maryland, New Jersey, New York, North Carolina, Pennsylvania, and Virginia from January 31 through February 7, 2026.

The recalled salmon is packaged in 2-pound bags featuring the Wellsley Farms logo and the label "Farm-Raised Atlantic Salmon." The product’s UPC code is 888670025963, and the specific lot number subject to recall is 3896. The suspected contamination was identified through a random sample collected by the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA).

While healthy individuals may experience symptoms such as high fever, severe headache, stiffness, nausea, abdominal pain, and diarrhea, Listeria infection poses a higher risk to pregnant women as it can lead to miscarriages and stillbirths. No illnesses related to this recall have been reported to date. Consumers who experience symptoms should seek medical advice promptly.

BJ’s Wholesale Club is notifying customers who may have purchased the recalled product. Consumers who have this product are advised to stop using it immediately and contact Slade Gorton & Co., Inc. at 1-888-628-0730 for information on how to obtain a full refund and proper disposal instructions.

headline: Trividia Health, Inc. Initiates Labeling Correction for all TRUE METRIX® Blood Glucose Monitoring Systems

image.caption: Trividia Health, Inc. Initiates Labeling Correction for all TRUE METRIX® Blood Glucose Monitoring Systems

description: TRUE METRIX announced a recall involving Blood Glucose Monitoring Systems after issues linked to as currently written, the owner’s booklets/system...
keywords: As currently written, the Owner’s Booklets/System Instructions for Use fails to emphasize that users must seek medical attention immediately if they receive an E-5 error code and are experiencing symptoms of high glucose.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Trividia Health, Inc. has announced a labeling correction for all TRUE METRIX® Blood Glucose Monitoring Systems, including the TRUE METRIX, TRUE METRIX AIR, TRUE METRIX GO, and TRUE METRIX PRO models. This update affects devices distributed in the United States, United Kingdom, Mexico, Australia, and the Caribbean. The correction addresses the current Owner’s Booklets/System Instructions for Use, which do not sufficiently instruct users to seek immediate medical attention if they receive an E-5 error code and are experiencing symptoms of high blood glucose.

The E-5 error code signifies either a very high blood glucose reading (greater than 600 mg/dL) or a test strip error. Without appropriate warnings, users might delay necessary medical treatment, which can result in serious health consequences or death, particularly for those with extremely high glucose levels. Since the product launch in August 2014, there have been 114 reported serious injuries and one fatality linked to this error code.

Trividia Health has updated the instructions for users to retest with a new test strip and seek medical help immediately if symptoms such as fatigue, excessive urination, thirst, or blurry vision persist alongside an E-5 error. The company is distributing product notices to pharmacies, distributors, and mail order companies to inform end users and request the posting of these notices where products are stored or sold. The devices themselves do not require return or replacement, and users may continue their normal use.

For questions regarding the labeling update, users and customers can contact Trividia Health Customer Care or visit the company’s dedicated web page. Adverse events related to these products may be reported through the FDA’s MedWatch program.

Product Description Regions Affected Customer Contact Support Phone
TRUE METRIX, TRUE METRIX AIR, TRUE METRIX GO, TRUE METRIX PRO Blood Glucose Monitoring Systems requiring updated Owner’s Booklets/System Instructions for Use United States, United Kingdom, Mexico, Australia, Caribbean www.trividiahealth.com/E-5productnotice 1-888-835-2723 (Mon-Fri, 8AM-8PM EST)
1-800-803-6025 (Mon-Fri, 8AM-8PM EST for E-5 assistance)

Updated Instructions for E-5 Error Code for TRUE METRIX, TRUE METRIX AIR, TRUE METRIX GO, and TRUE METRIX PRO:

Display Reason Recommended Action
E-5 Error Code Very high blood glucose (>600 mg/dL) or test strip error
  • Retest with a new test strip.
  • If error persists and symptoms such as fatigue, excess urination, thirst, or blurry vision are present, seek medical attention immediately.
  • If no symptoms, retest with a new strip again.
  • If error continues without symptoms, call 1-800-803-6025 for assistance.

How to Report Adverse Events to FDA MedWatch:

  • Online: www.fda.gov/medwatch/report.htm
  • Regular Mail or Fax: Download and complete forms from www.fda.gov/MedWatch/getforms.htm or call 1-800-332-1088 to request a form.
  • Return completed form to the address on the form or fax to 1-800-FDA-0178.

This labeling correction involves updates to the physical Owner’s Booklets provided with the devices as well as online instructions and help guides. Trividia Health is actively communicating with customers and regulatory bodies, including the U.S. FDA, to ensure safety and awareness.

headline: Prime Food Processing LLC Issues Safety Warning Regarding Uneviscerated Dried Croaker Fish

image.caption: Prime Food Processing LLC Issues Safety Warning Regarding Uneviscerated Dried Croaker Fish

description: No Brand announced a recall involving Dried Croaker Fish after issues linked to product was not adequately eviscerated and may harbor harmful bacteria or...
keywords: Product was not adequately eviscerated and may harbor harmful bacteria or toxins

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Prime Food Processing LLC has issued a voluntary recall of 80 cases of Dried Croaker Fish due to inadequate evisceration. The fish may contain internal organs that harbor harmful bacteria or toxins, posing a potential health risk to consumers. This action was taken in compliance with the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA) regulations after the issue was identified through routine testing by the New York Department of Agriculture.

The affected product was imported from Vietnam and packaged in clear packaging with green trim. The recall specifically concerns products distributed to Asian grocery stores across multiple states including AL, AZ, CA, FL, GA, HI, ID, MA, MD, MO, NJ, NV, NY, OH, OK, PA, TN, TX, UT, VA, WA, and WI.

So far, no illnesses or adverse reactions linked to this product have been reported. Consumers who have purchased the recalled Dried Croaker Fish are advised to return the product and its packaging to the place of purchase for a full refund.

For further information or questions, consumers can contact Prime Food Processing LLC at 718-963-2323, Monday through Friday from 9:00 AM to 5:00 PM EST.

image.caption: Mondelēz Global LLC Expands Voluntary Recall of 2 SKUs of CHIPS AHOY! Baked Bites Brookie in the USA Previous Release Was Issued on December 24, 2025

description: CHIPS AHOY announced a recall involving Baked brownie bites after issues linked to product safety – choking threats. FDA recall notice published in 2026.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Mondelēz Global LLC has expanded its voluntary recall of CHIPS AHOY! Baked Bites Brookie products in the United States. This expansion includes an additional code date of 10MAY2026 and two more UPCs for product pouches within the recalled carton units. The recall addresses a product safety concern resulting from an incorrect mixing process that caused small corn starch clumps to form in the baked brownie bites.

These small starch clumps pose a choking hazard, particularly for vulnerable groups such as young children and the elderly. The recall is limited to CHIPS AHOY! Baked Bites Brookie products with specific "Best When Used By" dates listed below. Mondelēz Global LLC confirms that no other CHIPS AHOY! or company products are affected by this recall.

To date, no injuries or illnesses have been reported in connection with this product. Consumers who have purchased the affected items are advised not to consume them. For further information, customers can contact Mondelēz Global LLC by phone at 1-855-535-5948, Monday through Friday, from 9 a.m. to 6 p.m. ET. The recall is being carried out in cooperation with the U.S. Food and Drug Administration.

headline: Akkarco LLC Recalls Product Due to Possible Health Risk

image.caption: Akkarco LLC Recalls Product Due to Possible Health Risk

description: Ashfiat Alharamain announced a recall involving Honey Product for Energy Support after issues linked to product contains tadalafil. FDA recall notice...

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Akkarco LLC of Lorton, Virginia, has initiated a voluntary recall of its product Ashfiat Alharamain Energy Support due to the presence of undeclared tadalafil. Tadalafil is a prescription drug approved by the FDA for treating male erectile dysfunction and belongs to the family of phosphodiesterase (PDE5) inhibitors. This substance is not permitted in dietary supplements and its safety and efficacy in this product have not been established.

The product, marketed as a honey-based energy support supplement, may pose health risks, especially to individuals with underlying medical conditions or those taking medications that could interact with tadalafil. Potential adverse effects include cardiovascular complications, changes in blood pressure, dizziness, and headache.

This recall affects products distributed nationwide in the United States, including through third-party e-commerce platforms such as Amazon. Consumers are advised to stop using the product immediately and follow any return or disposal instructions provided by the seller or distributor.

To date, no confirmed adverse health events have been reported in connection with this recall. Consumers with the affected product or with questions may contact Akkarco LLC or report adverse reactions to the FDA’s MedWatch program.

Product Name UPC Batch Number Expiration Date
ASHFIAT ALHARAMAIN 1234561870003 ENGOT24 October 2028

Consumers affected by this recall should:

  • Stop using the product immediately.
  • Follow return or disposal instructions provided by the seller or distributor.
  • Contact Akkarco LLC with any questions at office@unitedlegalexperts.com, Monday to Friday, 08:00–16:00 EST.
  • Report any adverse reactions or quality problems related to the product to the FDA’s MedWatch program online at www.fda.gov/medwatch/report.htm.
  • Alternatively, download the reporting form at www.fda.gov/MedWatch/getforms.htm, or call 1-800-332-1088 to request a form. Completed forms can be returned by mail or fax to 1-800-FDA-0178.

headline: Neogen Issues Voluntary Nationwide Recall of Neogen®Vet HYCOAT® Hyaluronate Sodium Sterile Solution
description: Neogen announced a recall involving Vet HYCOAT® Hyaluronate Sodium Sterile Solution after issues linked to microbial contamination. FDA recall notice...
keywords: Vet HYCOAT® Hyaluronate Sodium Sterile Solution

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Neogen Corporation has issued a voluntary nationwide recall of all lots within expiry of Neogen®Vet HYCOAT® Hyaluronate Sodium Sterile Solution. This product is intended for topical wound management in dogs, cats, and horses and is distributed to veterinarians and animal health distributors across the United States. The recall was initiated due to microbial contamination found in certain product lots, which compromises the sterility of the solution.

The contamination poses a risk of infection in animals, especially if the product is used in ways inconsistent with its labeled instructions, such as intraarticular injections. While adverse events have been reported in horses following such injections, no adverse events have been documented when the product is used as directed for topical wound care on surgical wounds, burns, ulcers, and autograft procedures. As a precautionary measure, all 2 mL/20 mg vials are also included in the recall while the investigation continues.

Consumers and veterinarians who currently possess the recalled Neogen®Vet HYCOAT® product should immediately discontinue use and quarantine the product. Neogen is coordinating returns of the recalled items and has notified distributors and direct customers to facilitate this process. Questions regarding the recall can be directed to Neogen Animal Safety Customer Support.

Veterinarians who observe any adverse effects potentially related to this product are encouraged to report these instances to the FDA using Form FDA 1932a. This recall is being conducted in cooperation with the U.S. Food and Drug Administration, and no other Neogen products are affected.

headline: Why Not Natural, Houston, Texas, is Recalling its Why Not Natural Organic Moringa - Green Superfood because of Possible Health Risk

image.caption: Why Not Natural, Houston, Texas, is Recalling its Why Not Natural Organic Moringa - Green Superfood because of Possible Health Risk

description: Why Not Natural announced a recall involving Organic Moringa – Green Superfood after issues linked to potential foodborne illness - salmonella. FDA recall...

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Why Not Natural, based in Houston, Texas, has initiated a voluntary recall of its Organic Moringa – Green Superfood due to potential contamination with Salmonella. Salmonella is a harmful bacterium that can cause serious and sometimes fatal infections, especially in young children, elderly individuals, and those with weakened immune systems. In healthy individuals, infection typically results in symptoms such as fever, diarrhea (which may include blood), nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain.

The affected product consists of Moringa Capsules, which were distributed nationwide via mail orders through Why Not Natural’s own e-commerce platform as well as third-party online retailers, including Amazon.com. Sales occurred between July 2025 and January 2026. This recall follows observations by the FDA related to an outbreak investigation involving Salmonella contamination in other products containing moringa powder.

Production and distribution of the Moringa Capsules have been suspended while the FDA and Why Not Natural continue their investigation. The company has also notified its customers about the recall and urges consumers who have purchased the affected product to discontinue its use immediately. Consumers may return the product to Why Not Natural for a full refund or direct any questions to the company’s customer care at care@whynotnatural.com.

headline: Koikoi Trading Inc., Issues Allergy Alert on Undeclared Wheat and Sesame in FU ZHOU FISH BALL (200g / 400g / 5lb)

image.caption: Koikoi Trading Inc., Issues Allergy Alert on Undeclared Wheat and Sesame in FU ZHOU FISH BALL (200g / 400g / 5lb)

description: Fu Zhou announced a recall involving Fish balls after issues linked to undeclared allergen – sesame, wheat. FDA recall notice published in 2026.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Koikoi Trading Inc. has issued a recall for certain lots of FU ZHOU FISH BALL products due to undeclared allergens, specifically wheat and sesame. These allergens are not listed on the product packaging, which poses a serious risk for individuals with allergies or severe sensitivities to wheat and sesame. Consumption of these products could lead to serious or life-threatening allergic reactions.

The affected products were distributed through foodservice and e-commerce channels, potentially reaching consumers via restaurant deliveries, distributors, and online orders. Distribution areas include California, Washington, Oregon, New York, Florida, Texas, New Jersey, Illinois, Massachusetts, Georgia, Arizona, Nevada, and Colorado.

To date, no illnesses have been reported in connection with these products. The recall was initiated after it was discovered that packaging and labeling failed to declare wheat and sesame in the ingredient and allergen statements. This action is being conducted in cooperation with the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA).

Consumers with allergies to wheat or sesame should not eat these fish balls. Foodservice customers are advised to stop using the product immediately, segregate remaining inventory, and mark it as "Hold – Recall." For return, credit, or replacement instructions, contact Koikoi Trading Inc. at 323-588-9888 (Monday to Friday, 9:00 AM to 5:00 PM Pacific Time) or via email at info@koikoitrading.com.

image.caption: Gerber Products Company Announces Voluntary Recall of Limited Batches of Arrowroot Biscuits Out of an Abundance of Caution Due to Potential Presence of Foreign Material Following Supplier Recall

description: Gerber announced a recall involving Arrowroot biscuits after issues linked to potential presence of soft plastic and/or paper pieces. FDA recall notice...
keywords: potential presence of soft plastic and/or paper pieces

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Gerber Products Company has voluntarily recalled limited batches of Gerber® Arrowroot Biscuits due to the potential presence of soft plastic and/or paper pieces. This precautionary measure follows a recall initiated by the arrowroot flour supplier. The company has ceased working with this supplier to ensure product safety.

The recall affects only Gerber® Arrowroot Biscuits in 5.5oz packaging produced between July 2025 and September 2025. This action applies nationwide within the United States, and no other Gerber® products are impacted. Consumers are advised to check the batch codes located on the back of the packaging before the best before date to identify affected products.

Consumers who have purchased the recalled biscuits should refrain from feeding the product to their children and may return it to the retailer for a refund. Although no illnesses or injuries have been reported, the recall is undertaken out of caution. Gerber is cooperating fully with the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA) and prioritizes the safety and quality of its products.

For any questions or consumer support, Gerber offers assistance 24/7 via their customer service line at 1-800-4-GERBER (1-800-443-7237).

headline: Navitas Organics Voluntarily Recalls Select Lots of 8oz Organic Chia Seeds Because of Possible Health Risk

image.caption: Navitas Organics Voluntarily Recalls Select Lots of 8oz Organic Chia Seeds Because of Possible Health Risk

description: Navitas Organice announced a recall involving Organic Chia Seeds after issues linked to potential foodborne illness - salmonella. FDA recall notice...

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Navitas Organics is voluntarily recalling select lots of its 8oz Organic Chia Seeds due to potential Salmonella contamination. This precautionary recall follows a similar action by the company's chia seed supplier. The affected product was distributed nationwide through various retail stores including Whole Foods Market and Target, as well as online platforms such as Amazon, iHerb, and Vitacost.com.

The recall applies exclusively to the sealed stand-up pouches of the 8oz Organic Chia Seeds with specific lot codes and expiration dates as listed below. There have been no reports of Salmonella infections, illnesses, or adverse medical events linked to these products at this time. No other Navitas Organics products are impacted by this recall.

Salmonella infection can pose serious health risks, especially to young children, elderly individuals, and people with weakened immune systems. Symptoms often include fever, diarrhea, nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain. In rare cases, the infection can lead to more severe complications if it spreads to the bloodstream.

Consumers who possess any of the affected chia seeds should not consume them. Instead, they are advised to discard the product and its packaging securely and return the product to the place of purchase for a refund. For further questions or to request a replacement, customers can contact Navitas Organics at 855-215-5702, Monday through Friday, from 8am to 8pm ET.

headline: IKM Recalls Product Because of Possible Health Risk

image.caption: IKM Recalls Product Because of Possible Health Risk

description: IKM announced a recall involving Metal Cookware Items after issues linked to potential foodborne illness – lead contamination. FDA recall notice published...

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
IKM of San Jose, California, has announced a recall of several metal cookware items due to potential contamination with elevated levels of lead (Pb). Lead is a toxic metal that can leach into food from these products, posing a health risk to consumers of all ages. There is no known safe level of lead exposure, and even small amounts can cause serious health issues, particularly in children and fetuses.

The affected products include specific aluminum and brass cookware items. Lead exposure can lead to various health problems such as learning difficulties, reduced IQ, behavioral changes in children, and symptoms like fatigue, headaches, stomach pain, vomiting, and neurological changes in severe cases. The recall follows a routine FDA sampling program that detected unsafe lead levels in these finished products.

The contaminated items were distributed primarily to grocery stores in various cities across California, including Sunnyvale, Santa Clara, Fremont, Hayward, and others. IKM has stopped distribution of the affected cookware while investigating the source of contamination. No illnesses related to this issue have been reported to date.

Consumers who have purchased any of the recalled products should return them to the place of purchase for a full refund. For further information, customers may contact IKM at 1-650-695-9009 during business hours from 10:00 to 17:00 PST, Monday through Friday, excluding national holidays.

headline: Superfoods Inc. dba Live it Up. Recalls Live it Up Super Greens Because of Possible Health Risk

image.caption: Superfoods Inc. dba Live it Up. Recalls Live it Up Super Greens Because of Possible Health Risk

description: Live it Up announced a recall involving Super Greens Original and Wild Berry Flavored pouches and packs after issues linked to potential foodborne illness -...
keywords: Superfoods, Inc. DBA as Live it Up
Super Greens Original and Wild Berry Flavored pouches and packs

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Superfoods, Inc., doing business as Live it Up, has issued a recall for certain batches of its Live it Up Super Greens dietary supplements due to potential contamination with Salmonella. The recall affects both Original and Wild Berry flavored products in pouch and stick pack forms. The company identified a possible health risk stemming from Salmonella, which can lead to serious infections, particularly in young children, elderly individuals, and those with weakened immune systems.

Symptoms of Salmonella infection typically include fever, diarrhea (which may be bloody), nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain. In rare cases, the infection can spread to the bloodstream and cause more severe conditions such as arterial infections, endocarditis, and arthritis. As of the announcement, there have been 45 reported illnesses and 12 hospitalizations in the United States linked to this outbreak.

The recalled products were distributed nationwide in the U.S., including Puerto Rico, Guam, and the Virgin Islands, and have also been sold in the United Kingdom. Sales were made through Live it Up’s direct-to-consumer website starting September 2024 and Amazon.com starting July 30, 2025. There is also possible unauthorized resale through third-party platforms such as eBay and Walmart.

Consumers who possess the affected products, identifiable by lot codes starting with the letter “A” or “3” for stick packs and expiration dates ranging from August 2026 to January 2028, are urged to stop use immediately. Customers should dispose of the products and contact Live it Up to request a refund.

Product Net Weight UPC
Live it Up Super Greens Original (pouch) 8.5 oz (240g) 860013190804
Live it Up Super Greens Original, 30 - 0.28 oz (8g) stick packs 8.47 oz (240g) 850077468063
Live it Up Super Greens Wild Berry (pouch) 9.49 oz (269.16g) 860013190811
Live it Up Super Greens Wild Berry, 30 - 0.32 oz (9g) stick packs 9.52 oz (270g) 850077468070

The recalled products feature green packaging, with lot codes printed in black on the bottom back of the package. Original pouch products have lot codes beginning with “A,” and stick pack products have lot codes beginning with “3.” Expiration dates on the recalled items range from 08/2026 to 01/2028.

Consumers who have purchased the affected products should:

  • Stop using the product immediately.
  • Do not eat, sell, or serve the product to others.
  • Dispose of the product safely.
  • Contact Live it Up by emailing recall@artofenso.com to request a refund.
  • Provide your name, order number, and a photo showing the lot code with your refund request.

Production of the affected product has been suspended while the FDA and the company continue their investigation. No other Live it Up products are involved in this recall at this time.

headline: Tri-Union Seafoods Identifies Additional Quantities of Recalled Genova® Tuna at Limited Retailers Due to Inadvertent Distribution of Previously Recalled Product

image.caption: Tri-Union Seafoods Identifies Additional Quantities of Recalled Genova® Tuna at Limited Retailers Due to Inadvertent Distribution of Previously Recalled Product

description: Genova announced a recall involving Yellowfin Tuna after issues linked to potential contamination with clostridium botulinum. FDA recall notice published in...

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Tri-Union Seafoods has announced a recall of additional quantities of Genova® Yellowfin Tuna products due to the inadvertent distribution of previously recalled items. The initial recall, issued in February 2025, was prompted by a manufacturing defect in the “easy open” pull tab can lids that may compromise the product seal, increasing the risk of contamination with Clostridium botulinum, a potentially fatal cause of food poisoning.

Recently, Tri-Union Seafoods discovered that quarantined cases related to the initial recall were mistakenly shipped by a third-party distributor to limited retail stores in several states. Affected products were sold at Meijer locations in Illinois, Indiana, Kentucky, Michigan, Ohio, and Wisconsin; Giant Foods in Maryland and Virginia; and Safeway, Albertsons, Vons, and Pavilions stores in California.

Consumers are advised not to consume the recalled tuna products even if they appear normal, as the contamination risk remains regardless of appearance or smell. Anyone experiencing illness after consumption should seek immediate medical attention. Customers with recalled products should either return them to the store for a full refund, dispose of them, or contact Tri-Union Seafoods for a retrieval kit and replacement coupon.

For questions or to request a replacement product, consumers can contact Tri-Union Seafoods at support@thaiunionhelp.zendesk.com or call 833-374-0171. The company’s toll-free line is available Monday through Friday, from 9:00 a.m. to 5:00 p.m. EST.

headline: Olympus Expands Voluntary Recall for ViziShot 2 FLEX (19G) EBUS -TBNA Needles
description: Olympus announced a recall involving ViziShot 2 FLEX (19G) EBUS -TBNA needles after issues linked to device & drug safety - defect. FDA recall notice...

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Olympus Corporation has expanded its voluntary recall of the ViziShot 2 FLEX (19G) EBUS -TBNA needles due to safety concerns. This recall now includes all lots of the device, following complaints about components ejecting or detaching during medical procedures. The initial recall in August 2025 addressed only certain lots, but subsequent investigations revealed issues affecting the entire product line.

The detachment of device components poses significant risks, including the possibility of unintended foreign objects in the tracheobronchial tree, which may require bronchoscopic extraction or surgical removal. Additionally, degradation of the device’s heat-shrink material can lead to complications such as difficulty in sample extraction, fluid leakage, impaired needle operation, or breakage.

Healthcare professionals are advised to immediately stop using all ViziShot 2 FLEX needles and quarantine any inventory. Affected devices should be returned to Olympus following the instructions in the recall communication. The ViziShot 2 FLEX is used with ultrasound endoscopes for fine needle aspiration and biopsy of submucosal and extramural lesions of the tracheobronchial tree. Other EBUS-TBNA needles are not impacted by this recall.

Adverse events or quality issues related to this device can be reported to the FDA’s MedWatch program. U.S. customers seeking more information or assistance can contact the Olympus Technical Assistance Center.

Product
ViziShot 2 FLEX (19G) EBUS -TBNA needles (all lots)
  • Stop use immediately and quarantine all ViziShot 2 FLEX devices.
  • Return affected devices to Olympus following recall instructions.
  • Report adverse events or quality problems to FDA MedWatch: https://www.fda.gov/safety/medwatch-fda-safety-information-and-adverse-event-reporting-program/reporting-serious-problems-fda.
  • For assistance, call Olympus Technical Assistance Center at 1-800-848-9024, Option 1 (Monday-Friday, 7:00 AM to 8:00 PM ET).
  • Email reports or inquiries to complaints@olympus.com.

headline: Spring & Mulberry Expands Voluntary Recall of Select Chocolate Bars Due to Possible Salmonella Contamination

image.caption: Spring & Mulberry Expands Voluntary Recall of Select Chocolate Bars Due to Possible Salmonella Contamination

description: Spring & Mulberry announced a recall involving Multiple Flavors of Chocolate Bars after issues linked to potential foodborne illness - salmonella. FDA...

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Spring & Mulberry has announced an expansion of their voluntary recall of select chocolate bar flavors due to potential contamination with Salmonella. The recall, initially affecting only the Mint Leaf flavor, now includes additional flavors produced on the same equipment and during the same time period. The company first identified the risk after routine third-party testing revealed the presence of Salmonella in finished products.

Salmonella infection can cause serious health issues, especially for young children, elderly individuals, and those with weakened immune systems. Symptoms typically include fever, diarrhea (which may be bloody), nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain. In rare cases, the infection may spread to the bloodstream, leading to more severe conditions such as arterial infections, endocarditis, and arthritis. Individuals experiencing symptoms should seek medical attention promptly.

The affected products were available for purchase online and through select retail partners nationwide starting September 15, 2025. Consumers can identify recalled items by the brand name Spring & Mulberry, flavor name, specific lot codes, and box color. Lot codes are found on the back of the packaging and on the inner flow wrap.

To date, no confirmed illnesses or adverse health effects have been reported related to these products. Customers who have purchased the affected items are advised not to consume them and to dispose of them immediately. Refunds or replacements are available upon request by contacting Spring & Mulberry with a photo of the lot code.

Mint Leaf ML1025 Green

Orange Zest OZ1025 Orange

Consumers seeking a refund or replacement should contact Spring & Mulberry at recalls@springandmulberry.com and include a photo of the product’s lot code. For additional assistance or information, customers may also reach out to Spring & Mulberry Customer Service at the same email address.
