headline: Abbott Initiates Medical Device Correction for Certain FreeStyle Libre® 3 and FreeStyle Libre 3 Plus Sensors in the U.S.
description: FreeStyle Libre announced a recall involving FreeStyle Libre 3 and 3 Plus Glucose monitoring sensors after issues linked to internal testing determined that...
keywords: FreeStyle Libre 3 and 3 Plus Glucose monitoring sensors
Internal testing determined that some sensors may provide incorrect low glucose readings.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Abbott has initiated a medical device correction in the United States for certain FreeStyle Libre 3 and FreeStyle Libre 3 Plus glucose monitoring sensors. This action follows internal testing that identified an issue causing some sensors to provide incorrect low glucose readings. Incorrect readings may lead to improper treatment decisions, potentially resulting in serious health risks for people with diabetes.

The issue originates from one specific production line among several manufacturing Libre 3 and Libre 3 Plus sensors. Abbott has resolved the manufacturing problem and continues to produce these sensors to meet replacement and new order demands without expecting significant supply disruptions. This correction affects approximately 3 million sensors in the U.S., with about half already expired or used.

Consumers are advised to visit www.FreeStyleCheck.com to determine if their sensor is affected and to obtain a free replacement if necessary. Users of potentially impacted sensors should stop using them immediately and dispose of them safely. Abbott recommends relying on a blood glucose meter or the meter in the FreeStyle Libre 3 reader for treatment decisions when sensor readings do not align with symptoms or expectations.

The correction does not affect other FreeStyle Libre sensors, readers, or apps, nor other Abbott biowearable products. For assistance, consumers can contact Abbott customer service at 1-833-815-4273, available seven days a week from 8 a.m. to 8 p.m. Eastern Time, or use live chat support available 24/7 at Abbott Support.

Impacted Product Details
FreeStyle Libre 3 Sensors Subset from one production line; approximately 3 million devices in the U.S.
FreeStyle Libre 3 Plus Sensors Subset from one production line; included in the approximately 3 million devices in the U.S.

Actions for Consumers:

  • Visit www.FreeStyleCheck.com to check if your sensor is affected.
  • If affected, discontinue use immediately and dispose of the sensor safely.
  • Request a free replacement via the website.
  • Use a blood glucose meter or the built-in meter in the FreeStyle Libre 3 reader for treatment decisions if sensor results are inconsistent with symptoms.
  • Contact Abbott customer service at 1-833-815-4273 (8 a.m. to 8 p.m. ET, daily) or use 24/7 live chat at Abbott Support.

headline: The Ambriola Company Issues Recall of Cheese Products Because of Listeria Health Risk

image.caption: The Ambriola Company Issues Recall of Cheese Products Because of Listeria Health Risk

description: Ambriola, Locatelli, Member’s Mark, Pinna, and Boar’s Head announced a recall involving Cheese after issues linked to potential to be contaminated with...
keywords: Ambriola, Locatelli, Member’s Mark, Pinna, and Boar’s Head
Potential to be contaminated with Listeria monocytogenes

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
The Ambriola Company has issued a recall of select cheese products due to the detection of Listeria monocytogenes during routine testing. This bacterium can cause serious and sometimes fatal infections, particularly in young children, elderly individuals, pregnant women, and those with weakened immune systems. While healthy individuals may experience only short-term symptoms such as high fever, severe headache, stiffness, nausea, abdominal pain, and diarrhea, pregnant women face risks of miscarriage and stillbirth from Listeria infection.

The recalled cheese products were distributed nationwide to retail stores and distributors between November 3, 2025, and November 20, 2025. Additional cheese products processed at the same West Caldwell, New Jersey facility are also included in the recall out of an abundance of caution. No illnesses have been reported to date.

Consumers who have purchased any affected products are advised not to consume them. Instead, they should either discard the products or return them to the point of purchase for a full refund. Customers experiencing symptoms of listeriosis should contact their healthcare provider promptly.

The Ambriola Company is cooperating with the FDA and has suspended production and distribution of the affected products while undertaking a thorough review of sanitation and food safety protocols. For more information, consumers can contact Ambriola at 1-800-962-8224, Monday through Friday, from 9:00 am to 4:00 pm ET.

headline: Diva Fam Inc. Announces Voluntary Recall Of Sea Moss Gel Superfood Products Due to Possible Health Risk

image.caption: Diva Fam Inc. Announces Voluntary Recall Of Sea Moss Gel Superfood Products Due to Possible Health Risk

description: True Sea Moss announced a recall involving Sea Moss Gel Superfood in various flavors (16 FL OZ glass jars) after issues linked to potential foodborne...
keywords: Sea Moss Gel Superfood in various flavors (16 FL OZ glass jars)

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Diva Fam Inc. has announced a voluntary recall of all lots and flavors of its True Sea Moss Sea Moss Gel Superfood products packaged in 16 FL OZ (473 mL) glass jars. This action is due to the company's lack of required regulatory authorization and temperature monitoring records for pH-controlled food products, which may increase the risk of microbial growth. Such growth could include organisms producing toxins associated with botulism, a rare but serious illness affecting the nervous system.

Symptoms of botulism can include general weakness, dizziness, double vision, difficulty speaking or swallowing, and in severe cases, muscle weakness or difficulty breathing. To date, no illnesses or adverse effects have been reported related to the recalled products. The affected products were distributed nationwide through select retailers, online at truеsеamоss.cоm, and other channels.

The recall includes all flavors and batch numbers of True Sea Moss brand Sea Moss Gel Superfood manufactured before January 9, 2026. The manufacture date is indicated on the can lid in MM/YYYY format. Consumers who have purchased these products are advised to stop using them immediately and follow return or disposal instructions provided by the place of purchase.

The recall follows a California Department of Public Health inspection questioning the regulatory status and production records of the products. Diva Fam is cooperating with regulatory authorities and conducting this recall with knowledge of the U.S. Food and Drug Administration. For additional information, consumers can contact Diva Fam customer support.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Karison Foods & Snacks Inc, based in Port Washington, NY, has announced a recall of several of its products due to the presence of an undeclared milk allergen. The recall affects 12 oz containers of the following items: PANJIRI, ALSI PINNI, PUNJABI PINNI, BESAN LADDOO, and NO SUGAR ADDED BESAN LADDOO. These products were distributed through retail stores in New York, New Jersey, and Virginia.

The products come in clear plastic containers and include specific UPC codes and an expiration date of August 29, 2026. The issue was identified during a routine inspection, which revealed that the milk allergen was not declared on the product labels. No illnesses related to these products have been reported so far.

Consumers who have a milk allergy or severe sensitivity to milk are at risk of serious or life-threatening allergic reactions if they consume these items. Residents who purchased any of the affected products are advised to return them to the place of purchase for a full refund. For further inquiries, customers may contact Karison Foods & Snacks Inc at 516-944-9000.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Spring & Mulberry has announced a voluntary recall of lot #025255 of its Mint Leaf Date Sweetened Chocolate Bar (2.1 oz) due to potential contamination with Salmonella. This recall follows routine testing by a third-party laboratory that identified the possible presence of this harmful bacterium. Salmonella infections can be serious, particularly for young children, elderly individuals, and those with weakened immune systems. Symptoms may include fever, diarrhea (which may be bloody), nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain. In rare cases, the infection can spread to the bloodstream and cause severe complications.

The affected Mint Leaf Date Sweetened Chocolate Bar was sold online and through select retail partners nationwide starting September 15, 2025. The recalled product can be identified by the Spring & Mulberry brand, teal-colored box, Mint Leaf flavor, and the lot code #025255, which appears on both the back of the packaging and inner flow wrap. To date, no illnesses or adverse health effects have been reported in connection with this product.

Consumers who have purchased this lot are advised to dispose of the product immediately. Refunds are available by contacting Spring & Mulberry with a photo of the lot code. For additional information or assistance, contact Spring & Mulberry Customer Service at recalls@springandmulberry.com.

headline: VH Foods Inc. DBA Outside The Breadbox Issues Allergy Alert on Undeclared Egg and Milk in "Bread Crumbs"

image.caption: VH Foods Inc. DBA Outside The Breadbox Issues Allergy Alert on Undeclared Egg and Milk in "Bread Crumbs"

description: Outside the Breadbox announced a recall involving Bread Crumbs after issues linked to potential or undeclared allergen – egg and milk. FDA recall notice...
keywords: VH Foods Inc. DBA Outside The Breadbox
Potential or Undeclared Allergen – Egg and Milk

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
VH Foods Inc., operating as Outside The Breadbox in Colorado Springs, CO, has issued a recall of its 8 ounce packages of "Bread Crumbs" due to the potential presence of undeclared egg and milk allergens. This recall affects products distributed to retail stores in Colorado as well as those sold through mail orders via the company website between November 24, 2025, and December 1, 2025.

The affected bread crumbs are packaged in clear stand-up pouches, each weighing 8 ounces. The packages bear the UPC code 873899001554 and an expiration date of May 20, 2026, stamped on the bottom. The recall was initiated after it was discovered that the packaging failed to disclose the inclusion of egg and milk ingredients due to a temporary lapse in the company's production and packaging procedures. This issue appears to be limited to a single production run.

No illnesses related to the undeclared allergens have been reported to date. Customers who have purchased the recalled product are advised to return it to the original place of purchase for a full refund. For further inquiries, consumers may contact VH Foods Inc. directly at 1-719-633-3434.

keywords: Gastro Care capsule and Hingwastika powder and capsule dietary supplements

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
HerbsForever LLC of Los Angeles, California, has issued a recall on certain dietary supplements due to the potential presence of undeclared wheat. The affected products include HerbsForever brand Hingwastik Churna Powder, Hingwastika Extract capsules, and Gastro Care capsules. These items were distributed nationwide through mail order.

Individuals with wheat allergies are at risk of serious or life-threatening allergic reactions if they consume these products. The recall was initiated after a routine FDA inspection at the manufacturing facility in India revealed that an ingredient called Hing (Ferula Asafoedita) is dried with flour that may contain wheat.

To date, no illnesses related to this issue have been reported. Consumers who have purchased the recalled products are advised to contact HerbsForever LLC via email for return instructions and to receive a full refund. This recall is being conducted in cooperation with the U.S. Food and Drug Administration.

headline: Anthony Trinh, 123Herbals LLC (123HERBALS.COM) Issues Nationwide Recall of Silintan Capsules Due to the Presence of Undeclared Meloxicam

image.caption: Anthony Trinh, 123Herbals LLC (123HERBALS.COM) Issues Nationwide Recall of Silintan Capsules Due to the Presence of Undeclared Meloxicam

description: Silintan announced a recall involving Dietary supplement capsules marketed for relief of joint and body aches after issues linked to unapproved new drug...
keywords: Dietary supplement capsules marketed for relief of joint and body aches
Unapproved new drug found to contain undeclared meloxicam

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Anthony Trinh, 123Herbals LLC is issuing a voluntary nationwide recall of all lots of Silintan capsules, a dietary supplement marketed for relief of joint and body aches. The recall follows FDA analysis that found the product contains undeclared meloxicam, an approved nonsteroidal anti-inflammatory drug (NSAID) not permitted in dietary supplements.

Meloxicam is approved for osteoarthritis pain management but products containing it cannot be marketed as dietary supplements. The presence of meloxicam in Silintan capsules makes the product an unapproved new drug with unestablished safety and efficacy, leading to the recall.

Exposure to meloxicam above the recommended maximum daily dose of 15 mg may cause serious health risks including blood clots, heart attack, stroke, hypersensitivity reactions, gastrointestinal bleeding, ulceration, perforation, and acute kidney injury, especially in elderly patients. Dangerous drug interactions can occur in patients taking blood thinners or other NSAIDs, increasing bleeding risk.

Consumers and retailers who possess Silintan capsules should stop using the product immediately. They are advised to return the product to the place of purchase for a refund, discard it, or consult a healthcare provider. To date, no adverse events have been reported in relation to this recall.

Product Description Packaging Distribution
Silintan capsules Dietary supplement capsules marketed for relief of joint and body aches 25-count plastic bottles with blue labels Nationwide online distribution at 123herbals.com

For questions about this recall, consumers can contact Anthony Trinh, 123Herbals LLC by email at hello@123herbals.com or by phone at 1-626-656-3202 (Monday through Friday, 8am to 5pm Pacific Time). Customers who purchased directly from 123Herbals.com may return product to:

Return Address
300 West Valley Blvd., P.O. Box 1925, Alhambra, CA 91803

Consumers experiencing adverse reactions or quality issues with Silintan capsules are encouraged to report to the FDA's MedWatch Adverse Event Reporting program using one of the following methods:

  • Online: www.fda.gov/medwatch/report.htm
  • Regular Mail or Fax: Download form at www.fda.gov/MedWatch/getforms.htm
  • Request a reporting form by calling 1-800-332-1088, then complete and return by mail or fax to 1-800-FDA-0178

headline: Modern Warrior Recalls “Modern Warrior Ready” Dietary Supplement Due to Undeclared 1,4-DMAA and Aniracetam, as Well as Tianeptine, Which has Not Been Approved for Supplement Use by the FDA.

image.caption: Modern Warrior Recalls “Modern Warrior Ready” Dietary Supplement Due to Undeclared 1,4-DMAA and Aniracetam, as Well as Tianeptine, Which has Not Been Approved for Supplement Use by the FDA.

description: Modern Warrior announced a recall involving Dietary supplement capsules marketed for metabolism moosting, improving brain function, and reducing cravings...
keywords: Dietary supplement capsules marketed for metabolism moosting, improving brain function, and reducing cravings
Unapproved new drug found to contain undeclared 1,4-DMAA and aniracetam, and tianeptine

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Modern Warrior has issued a voluntary recall for all lots of their dietary supplement "Modern Warrior Ready" following the detection of undeclared ingredients during regulatory testing. The supplement, marketed to boost metabolism, improve brain function, and reduce cravings, was found to contain unapproved substances including tianeptine, 1,4-DMAA, and aniracetam.

Tianeptine is associated with serious health risks, such as life-threatening events including suicidal ideation or behavior in individuals aged 25 years and younger. Overdose symptoms can include confusion, seizures, drowsiness, dry mouth, and shortness of breath, which may worsen with alcohol consumption. Additionally, tianeptine combined with monoamine oxidase inhibitor (MAOI) antidepressants may lead to severe complications including stroke and death.

Use of 1,4-DMAA may cause elevated blood pressure and cardiovascular problems such as heart attack, shortness of breath, and chest tightness. The product also contains synephrine, which can increase heart rate and blood pressure, especially when combined with caffeine, potentially causing adverse cardiovascular effects.

The recalled product was sold nationwide online from April 2022 through December 8, 2025. Modern Warrior has ceased distribution, removed the product from sale, and quarantined remaining stock to prevent further exposure. Consumers who have purchased Modern Warrior Ready are advised to stop using the product immediately.

Product Name Description Packaging Distribution Dates
Modern Warrior Ready Dietary supplement capsules for metabolism boosting, brain function improvement, and cravings reduction Black plastic bottle with black screw-top lid, black and gold tamper-evident shrink wrap, 60 capsules per bottle April 2022 – December 8, 2025

Consumers with questions or concerns about the recall should consult their healthcare providers.

headline: Gusto Group Inc. Recalls Shrimp Paste (Klong Kone) ‘'454 G. (1 LBS)’ and ‘910 G. (2 LBS)’ because of Possible Health Risk

image.caption: Gusto Group Inc. Recalls Shrimp Paste (Klong Kone) ‘'454 G. (1 LBS)’ and ‘910 G. (2 LBS)’ because of Possible Health Risk

description: Klong Kone announced a recall involving Klong Kone Shrimp Paste after issues linked to potential metal contaminant - lead. FDA recall notice published in 2026.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Gusto Group Inc. of Paterson, NJ, has issued a recall for two sizes of Klong Kone Shrimp Paste due to the potential presence of elevated levels of lead. The affected products include the ‘454 G. (1 LBS)’ and ‘910 G. (2 LBS)’ variants. Lead contamination poses health risks, particularly for children, who may suffer long-term neurological damage from prolonged exposure. Adults are also at risk of kidney problems, hypertension, and cognitive impairments from chronic lead exposure.

The recalled products were distributed between April 2023 and December 2023 to two retailers located in Iowa and New Jersey. No online sales were reported. The shrimp paste is packaged in a white plastic jar with a red cap and originates from Thailand, packed and distributed by P. Prateepthong 2000.

There has been one report involving four children who became ill after consuming the product; they experienced diarrhea and elevated blood lead levels. This incident prompted the recall and subsequent FDA lab testing, which confirmed the presence of unsafe lead levels. Gusto Group Inc. has ceased distribution of these items and is investigating the source of contamination.

Consumers who purchased the recalled shrimp paste should return it to the point of purchase for a full refund. For further questions, consumers can contact Gusto Group Inc. by phone or email during business hours.

headline: Primavera Nueva Inc. Issues Voluntary Recall of Select 4-Count Tamales Because of Possible Health Risk- Revised to Include Roasted Pumpkin & White Cheddar, a Seasonal Item

image.caption: Primavera Nueva Inc. Issues Voluntary Recall of Select 4-Count Tamales Because of Possible Health Risk- Revised to Include Roasted Pumpkin & White Cheddar, a Seasonal Item

description: Primavera announced a recall involving Tamales after issues linked to potential presence of listeria monocytogenes. FDA recall notice published in 2026.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Primavera Nueva Inc., based in Sonoma, CA, has issued a voluntary recall of select 4-count tamales due to the potential presence of Listeria monocytogenes, a harmful bacterial organism. This bacteria can cause serious and sometimes fatal infections, especially in young children, elderly individuals, pregnant women, and those with weakened immune systems. Healthy individuals may experience symptoms such as high fever, severe headache, stiffness, nausea, abdominal pain, and diarrhea. Pregnant women infected with Listeria risk miscarriages and stillbirths.

The recalled tamales were distributed to retail stores in California and Nevada. This action follows an FDA inspection on October 10, 2025, which found that internal cooking temperatures were not consistently verified during earlier production, raising concerns about controlling potential biological hazards like Listeria monocytogenes. Primavera Nueva Inc. has since implemented enhanced cooking temperature monitoring and verification starting October 11, 2025.

Consumers are advised not to consume these products if the tamales have date codes between October 22, 2024, and October 22, 2025. Instead, they should discard the items. To date, no illnesses have been reported in connection with this recall.

For questions or additional information, consumers can contact Araceli Santoyo at 707-939-9350 or via email at primaveranueva@aol.com, Monday through Friday from 9 a.m. to 3 p.m. Pacific Time.

description: Newway Import announced a recall involving Preserved mustard in soybean oil after issues linked to undeclared wheat. FDA recall notice published in 2026.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Newway Import Inc., located in La Puente, California, has issued a recall for the product "Preserved Mustard in Soybean Oil" under the Cai Hua Xiang brand due to the presence of undeclared wheat. This allergen is not listed on the product packaging, which poses a risk of serious or potentially life-threatening allergic reactions for individuals with wheat allergies or severe sensitivities.

The recalled product was available for purchase in retail grocery stores throughout California from early July 2025 until early November 2025. Consumers are advised to stop using the product immediately and return it to the store where it was purchased for a full refund.

To date, no illnesses related to this recall have been reported. The recall was initiated after it was discovered that the product contained wheat but failed to declare it on the packaging, potentially endangering consumers with wheat allergies.

Consumers with questions about this recall can contact Newway Import Inc. at (626) 766-3037. Customer service is available Monday through Friday from 10:00 a.m. to 5:00 p.m. Pacific Standard Time. This recall is being conducted in cooperation with the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA).

headline: Gold Star Distribution Inc., Issues Recall of Certain FDA-Regulated Products in Three States Including Drugs, Devices, Cosmetics, Human Foods, and Pet Foods
description: Multiple brand names announced a recall involving All FDA-regulated products held at facility including drugs, medical devices, cosmetics, dietary...
keywords: All FDA-regulated products held at facility including drugs, medical devices, cosmetics, dietary supplements, human food, and pet food
Animal & Veterinary Cosmetics Dietary Supplements Drugs Food & Beverages Medical Devices Foodborne Illness
Potential Salmonella contamination, presence of rodent and avian contamination and insanitary conditions during the storage process.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Gold Star Distribution, Inc. has issued a recall of all FDA-regulated products stored at its facility due to potential contamination risks. The products include drugs, medical devices, cosmetics, dietary supplements, human food, and pet food. The recall was initiated after the discovery of rodent and avian contamination, as well as insanitary storage conditions that may expose the products to bacterial contamination, including Salmonella, posing significant health risks.

Exposure to the recalled products could lead to serious illness or infection. Salmonella infection symptoms typically include fever, diarrhea, nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain. Certain populations such as infants, elderly individuals, pregnant persons, and people with weakened immune systems are at greater risk of severe complications. In addition to human health risks, the contamination can also affect animals consuming the pet food products.

The recall covers all relevant FDA-regulated products held at the Gold Star facility, but does not apply to frozen or refrigerated items shipped directly to stores by distributors or manufacturers. No illnesses linked to these products have been reported to date.

Consumers and retailers who have purchased affected products are advised to destroy them promptly and provide verification of destruction to Gold Star Distribution at their Minneapolis address. Products should not be returned to the company. Refunds are available upon request. Anyone experiencing health problems after using the products should contact a healthcare provider, and pet owners should consult their veterinarian if concerned.

For questions or assistance, customers can contact Gold Star Distribution at 612-617-9800, Monday through Sunday from 8:00 am to 5:00 pm CST. Adverse reactions or quality issues can be reported to the FDA through MedWatch or the FDA’s SmartHub.

  • Report online via the FDA MedWatch website
  • Request a reporting form by calling 1-800-332-1088
  • Submit completed forms via regular mail or fax to 1-800-FDA-0178
  • Access FDA SmartHub for guidance on reporting product or facility issues
authorityLinks: FDA recall page here.
Company consumer information here.
Company website here.

headline: Atwater’s Issues Allergy Alert on Undeclared Tree Nut Allergen on Cookie Tin Labels

image.caption: Atwater’s Issues Allergy Alert on Undeclared Tree Nut Allergen on Cookie Tin Labels

description: Atwater's announced a recall involving Cookie tins after issues linked to undeclared tree nut allergens, almonds, pecans, and walnuts.. FDA recall notice...
keywords: Undeclared tree nut allergens, almonds, pecans, and walnuts.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Atwater’s, based in Baltimore, MD, has issued a recall for 197 cookie tins due to undeclared tree nut allergens. The affected products contain almonds, pecans, and walnuts that were not disclosed on the product labels, posing a serious risk to individuals with allergies or severe sensitivities to these nuts.

The cookie tins were distributed between December 13, 2025, and December 22, 2025, in Baltimore, Towson, and Catonsville, MD. They were sold through retail stores and also gifted to three wholesalers. The tins are circular metal containers tied with a bow, marked with an “Atwater’s cookie tin” label on the bottom.

The recall was voluntarily initiated after the labeling issue was identified, which appeared to be caused by a temporary breakdown in Atwater’s labeling process. To date, no allergic reactions or illnesses have been reported. Consumers are advised to return the affected cookie tins to the place of purchase for a full refund.

For additional information or to report concerns, consumers can contact Atwater’s directly at 410-644-3435.
authorityLinks: FDA recall page here.

Consumers should check the product packaging for Atwater's brand cookie tins and avoid consumption if they have a tree nut allergy or sensitivity. For further assistance or questions, consumers can contact Atwater's customer support at 410-644-3435.

headline: Gregory’s Foods Issues Allergy Alert on Undeclared Peanuts in "Bag Full of Cookies" White Chocolate Macadamia Nut Frozen Cookie Dough

image.caption: Gregory’s Foods Issues Allergy Alert on Undeclared Peanuts in "Bag Full of Cookies" White Chocolate Macadamia Nut Frozen Cookie Dough

description: Gregory's Foods announced a recall involving Bag Full of Cookies White Chocolate Macadamia Nut Frozen Cookie Dough after issues linked to undeclared...
keywords: Bag Full of Cookies White Chocolate Macadamia Nut Frozen Cookie Dough

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Gregory’s Foods, Inc., located in Eagan, MN, has issued a recall for certain 2-pound 8.5-ounce packages of its "Bag Full of Cookies" White Chocolate Macadamia Nut frozen cookie dough. The recall stems from the possibility that some packages labeled as White Chocolate Macadamia Nut may contain Frozen Monster Cookie Dough, which includes undeclared peanuts.

This mislabeling poses a serious risk for individuals with peanut allergies, as consuming these products could lead to life-threatening allergic reactions. The affected cookie dough was distributed in retail grocery stores and distribution centers across Minnesota, Wisconsin, and Nebraska.

The recalled product is packaged in a 2-pound 8.5-ounce plastic bag with a clear window and is marked with a "BEST BY 12/6/2026" date near the top front of the bag. Notably, the Frozen Monster Cookie Dough visible through the window contains chocolate chips and colored candy-coated pieces, while the White Chocolate Macadamia Nut dough does not.

To date, no illnesses have been reported related to this recall. The issue was isolated to certain units produced on one day, and the company has since corrected the problem. Consumers who have purchased the affected product are advised to return it to the place of purchase for a full refund. For additional information or questions, customers can contact Gregory’s Foods at 1-800-231-4734, Monday through Friday from 8 a.m. to 4 p.m. CT.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Willy Pete’s Chocolate Company LLC of Harwinton has issued a recall for its Almond Despair chocolate bars due to the presence of almonds not declared on the ingredient label. The ingredient statement mistakenly lists macadamia nuts instead of almonds. This poses a risk for consumers with almond allergies or sensitivities, as ingestion could lead to serious or potentially life-threatening allergic reactions.

The recalled Almond Despair chocolate bars come in 2 oz purple wrappers, with UPC code 0 987261 5. These products were distributed to stores located in Connecticut, Massachusetts, and South Carolina.

To date, no illnesses have been reported in connection with this recall. Consumers who have purchased the affected chocolate bars are advised to return them to the point of purchase for a full refund. For further questions, customers may contact Willy Pete’s Chocolate Company LLC at 860-878-0302.

Consumers wishing to report complaints to the Connecticut Department of Consumer Protection Food and Standards Division can email DCP.FoodandStandards@ct.gov.

headline: Handelnine Global Limited d/b/a as Navafresh Issues Nationwide Recall of Rheumacare Capsules by Virgo UAP Pharma Pvt. Ltd. (Virgo), Due to Elevated Lead Levels

image.caption: Handelnine Global Limited d/b/a as Navafresh Issues Nationwide Recall of Rheumacare Capsules by Virgo UAP Pharma Pvt. Ltd. (Virgo), Due to Elevated Lead Levels

description: Virgo announced a recall involving Rheumacare Capsules after issues linked to elevated levels of lead. FDA recall notice published in 2025.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Handelnine Global Limited, operating as Navafresh, has announced a voluntary nationwide recall of Rheumacare Capsules manufactured by Virgo UAP Pharma Pvt. Ltd. (Virgo) due to elevated lead levels. Testing by the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA) revealed lead concentrations up to 11,100 ppm, which significantly exceed permissible limits, posing potential health risks.

The recalled product is intended for reducing joint pain and comes in white plastic containers labeled as Rheumacare Capsules. The affected lots are CAM040 and CALO79-N, distributed nationwide through the Navafresh website. Consumers who possess these lots are advised to stop using the medication immediately and discard it. Refunds will be provided for affected purchases.

Exposure to high levels of lead can cause serious health problems including cognitive impairment, gastrointestinal symptoms, motor coordination issues, peripheral neuropathy, and anemia. Reproductive health risks include increased chance of miscarriage and reduced fertility. Acute lead poisoning may cause severe symptoms such as seizures, coma, or even death.

Consumers with concerns or who have experienced adverse effects should consult a healthcare professional promptly. For questions about the recall or to request a refund, customers can contact Handelnine Global Limited at +1 442-223-0999 or via email at legal@handelnine.com during business hours. Adverse events can be reported to the FDA’s MedWatch program using the methods provided below.

Product Lot Number
Rheumacare Capsules CAM040
Rheumacare Capsules CALO79-N
  • Report adverse events online at www.fda.gov/medwatch/report.htm
  • Request MedWatch form by phone: 1-800-332-1088
  • Download MedWatch form at www.fda.gov/MedWatch/getforms.htm
  • Submit forms by mail to the address on the pre-addressed form
  • Submit forms by fax to 1-800-FDA-0178

headline: Direct Source Seafood LLC is Recalling Frozen Raw Shrimp Because Product May Have Become Contaminated with Cesium-137 (Cs-137)

image.caption: Direct Source Seafood LLC is Recalling Frozen Raw Shrimp Because Product May Have Become Contaminated with Cesium-137 (Cs-137)

description: Market 32 and Waterfront Bistro announced a recall involving Frozen Raw Shrimp after issues linked to product may have been prepared, packed, or held under...
keywords: Product may have been prepared, packed, or held under conditions whereby they may have become exposed to very low levels of cesium-137 (Cs-137)

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Direct Source Seafood LLC, based in Bellevue, WA, announced a recall on December 19, 2025, involving approximately 83,800 bags of frozen raw shrimp imported from Indonesia. These products, sold under the Market 32 and Waterfront Bistro brands, may have been prepared, packed, or held under conditions that exposed them to contamination by cesium-137 (Cs-137), a man-made radioisotope.

Cesium-137 can be present in the environment at varying levels, particularly in areas with environmental contamination. Prolonged, repeated low-dose exposure to Cs-137—such as through contaminated food or water—may increase the risk of cancer by causing DNA damage in living cells. The recalled products were distributed in multiple states and sold between mid-2025 and 2027.

The U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA) is investigating reports of Cs-137 contamination linked to frozen shrimp products and shipping containers manufactured in Indonesia by PT. Bahari Makmur Sejati (BMS Foods). According to the FDA, no contaminated products have been confirmed in the U.S. marketplace, and no illnesses have been reported so far.

Consumers who purchased the affected shrimp should not consume the products. They are advised to discard the shrimp or return it to the place of purchase for a full refund. For further questions, Direct Source Seafood LLC can be reached at 425-455-2291, Monday through Friday, 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. PST.

headline: Olympus Announces Voluntary Corrective Action for the Single-Use Ligating Device
description: Olympus announced a recall involving Single use ligating device after issues linked to polyloop device may fail to detach as expected and become anchored to...
keywords: PolyLoop device may fail to detach as expected and become anchored to patient anatomy

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Olympus Corporation has announced a voluntary corrective action for its Single-Use Ligating Device ("Polyloop") due to a potential safety concern. The Polyloop device is intended for use with Olympus endoscopes to deliver a nylon loop snare that helps prevent or control bleeding after polypectomy of pedunculated polyps.

This initiative follows reports of the device failing to release or detach as designed, causing the loop to become unintentionally anchored around patient anatomy during use. Although no deaths have been reported, the failure of the ligation loop to release can lead to procedural complications requiring emergency intervention. Potential risks include bleeding, mucosal injury, perforation, and in severe cases, surgical intervention or hospitalization.

In November 2025, Olympus notified customers of this corrective action and advised that all personnel review the supplemental illustrative guidance along with existing warnings, cautions, and emergency treatment procedures outlined in the Instructions for Use. U.S. customers are asked to acknowledge receipt of this notification through the Olympus recall portal.

Adverse reactions or quality issues related to the Polyloop device can be reported to the FDA’s MedWatch program. For additional information or to report device concerns, Olympus provides a dedicated Technical Assistance Center.

Recall Details
Device: Single-Use Ligating Device ("Polyloop")
Issue: Device may fail to detach, anchoring loop to patient anatomy
Risks: Bleeding, mucosal injury, perforation, need for emergency intervention
Notification Date: November 2025
Recall Number: 0476
  • U.S. customers should acknowledge receipt at Olympus recall web portal by entering recall number "0476".
  • Report adverse events or quality issues to FDA MedWatch online: FDA MedWatch Reporting.
  • Contact Olympus Technical Assistance Center for information or to report device problems: 1-800-848-9024, Option 1 or email complaints@olympus.com.

headline: Primavera Nueva Inc. Issues Voluntary Recall of Select 4-Count Tamales Because of Possible Health Risk
description: No Brand announced a recall involving Tamales after issues linked to potential presence of listeria monocytogenes.. FDA recall notice published in 2025.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Primavera Nueva Inc., based in Sonoma, CA, has issued a voluntary recall of select 4-count tamale products due to potential contamination with Listeria monocytogenes. This bacterium can cause serious and sometimes fatal infections, particularly in young children, elderly individuals, and those with weakened immune systems. Pregnant women are also at risk as infection can lead to miscarriages or stillbirths.

The recall affects tamales produced between October 10, 2024, and October 10, 2025, which were distributed by Primavera Nueva Inc. to retail stores in California and Nevada. No other products from Primavera Nueva are involved, and no illnesses have been reported so far. The concern arose after an FDA inspection on October 10, 2025, found gaps in verifying internal cooking temperatures needed to control biological hazards, including Listeria monocytogenes.

Primavera Nueva has since taken corrective actions, implementing enhanced cooking-temperature monitoring and verification starting October 11, 2025. Consumers who have purchased the affected tamales with date codes between 10/22/2024 and 10/22/2025 are advised not to consume the products and to discard them.

For further information or questions, consumers can contact Araceli Santoyo at primaveranueva@aol.com or by phone at 707-939-9350, Monday through Friday from 9 a.m. to 3 p.m. Pacific Time. This recall is being conducted in cooperation with the U.S. Food and Drug Administration.

Product Variety

Black Bean Bonanza & Jack Cheese
Butternut Squash with Roasted Green Chiles & Cheese
BBQ Chipotle Bean & White Cheddar
Mushroom Spinach & Salsa with Two Cheeses

Butternut Squash with Roasted Green Chiles
Mushroom Spinach & Salsa

Consumer Guidance:

  • Do not consume affected tamales with date codes from 10/22/2024 through 10/22/2025.
  • Discard these products immediately.
  • Contact Primavera Nueva Inc. for questions at 707-939-9350 or primaveranueva@aol.com.

headline: MediNatura New Mexico, Inc. Expands Voluntary Nationwide Recall of ReBoost Nasal Spray and to include ClearLife Allergy Nasal Spray Due to Microbial Contamination

image.caption: MediNatura New Mexico, Inc. Expands Voluntary Nationwide Recall of ReBoost Nasal Spray and to include ClearLife Allergy Nasal Spray Due to Microbial Contamination

description: ReBoost, ClearLife announced a recall involving Nasal spray, 0.68 fl oz after issues linked to product has been found to contain yeast/mold and microbial...
keywords: Product has been found to contain yeast/mold and microbial contamination with one species identified as Achromobacter, at levels above specifications.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
MediNatura New Mexico, Inc. has expanded a voluntary nationwide recall involving all lots of its ReBoost Nasal Spray and ClearLife Allergy Nasal Spray. The recall is due to microbial contamination detected in both products, including yeast, mold, and the bacterium Achromobacter at levels above established specifications.

The ReBoost Nasal Spray is intended to temporarily relieve nasal congestion and related symptoms, while the ClearLife Allergy Nasal Spray is used for minor allergy symptom relief. Both products are sold in 20 mL bottles packaged in distinct cartons and were distributed nationwide through retail outlets and online at medinatura.com.

There is a reasonable risk that using these contaminated products could result in serious infections, especially in immunocompromised individuals. Although no adverse events have been reported to date, consumers are urged to stop using these nasal sprays immediately. Those who purchased the products directly from MediNatura should contact the company for a refund, and those who bought from retailers should return the product to the place of purchase.

Consumers with questions or concerns can contact MediNatura New Mexico, Inc. via phone or email. Additionally, any adverse reactions or quality issues should be reported to the FDA's MedWatch program. This recall is conducted in cooperation with the U.S. Food and Drug Administration.

Product NDC Number UPC Expiration Dates Package Description
ReBoost Nasal Spray 62795-4005-9 787647 10186 3 12/2022 to 12/2025 20 mL bottle in white and yellow carton
ClearLife Allergy Nasal Spray 62795-4006-9 787647 10188 7 12/2022 to 12/2025 20 mL bottle in white and green carton

Consumers impacted by this recall should take the following steps:

  • Immediately discontinue use of the recalled ReBoost and ClearLife nasal sprays.
  • Contact MediNatura New Mexico, Inc. at recall@medinatura.com for refund arrangements if purchased directly from the company.
  • Return the product to the retailer if purchased in stores.
  • Contact MediNatura customer service by phone at 800-621-7644 (Monday-Friday, 8:00 AM to 5:00 PM MST) for additional information.
  • Consult a healthcare provider if any health issues arise from use of the recalled products.
  • Report any adverse reactions or quality concerns to the FDA MedWatch program:
    • Online at www.fda.gov/medwatch/report.htm
    • By mail or fax using forms downloadable at www.fda.gov/MedWatch/getforms.htm
    • Request forms by calling 1-800-332-1088
    • Return completed forms by mail or fax to 1-800-FDA-0178

headline: StuffbyNainax LLC Issues Voluntary Nationwide Recall of MR.7 SUPER 700000 Dietary Supplement Due to the Presence of Undeclared Sildenafil and Tadalafil

image.caption: StuffbyNainax LLC Issues Voluntary Nationwide Recall of MR.7 SUPER 700000 Dietary Supplement Due to the Presence of Undeclared Sildenafil and Tadalafil

description: MR7 Super 700000 announced a recall involving Dietary supplement for male enhancement after issues linked to undeclared sildenafil and tadalafil. FDA recall...

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
StuffbyNainax LLC is voluntarily recalling all lots of MR.7 SUPER 700000 dietary supplement capsules distributed nationwide in the United States. Testing by the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA) revealed that these products contain undeclared sildenafil and tadalafil, active pharmaceutical ingredients approved for prescription use to treat erectile dysfunction. Because of the presence of these substances, MR.7 SUPER 700000 capsules are considered unapproved new drugs and are subject to recall due to safety concerns.

Consumption of products containing sildenafil or tadalafil without appropriate medical supervision can pose serious health risks. These ingredients may interact dangerously with nitrates found in some prescription medications, potentially causing a significant drop in blood pressure that could be life-threatening. Individuals with diabetes, high blood pressure, high cholesterol, or heart disease—conditions often treated with nitrates—are especially vulnerable.

MR.7 SUPER 700000 capsules are marketed as a dietary supplement for male enhancement but do not bear lot numbers or expiration dates. The product was sold online to customers across the United States between August 2025 and November 2025. Consumers who have purchased this product are advised to stop using it immediately and dispose of it properly.

This recall is being conducted in coordination with the FDA to protect public health.

Product Name Brand Form Lot Number Distribution Period Distribution
MR.7 SUPER 700000 MR.7 Capsules Not available / unlisted August 2025 – November 2025 Sold online in the United States

content.text: Undeclared Sildenafil and Tadalafil

If your product contains these substances or you have concerns, you can contact StuffbyNainax LLC for support at (346) 441-9470 or via email at stuffbynainaxllc.info@gmail.com. Consumers are advised to discontinue use of the product until further guidance from the company.

headline: Ukrop’s Homestyle Foods Announces Recall Due to Undeclared Allergen

image.caption: Ukrop’s Homestyle Foods Announces Recall Due to Undeclared Allergen

description: Ukrop’s announced a recall involving Decorated pound cakes after issues linked to undeclared soy. FDA recall notice published in 2025.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Ukrop’s Homestyle Foods, LLC, based in Richmond, VA, has announced a recall of four varieties of its Decorated Pound Cakes due to the presence of an undeclared soy allergen. The recall specifically affects products with a Best By Date of 12/15/2025. The issue arose because a cake releasing agent containing soy was used during the production of these cakes, and this ingredient was not disclosed on the product labels.

Consumers with soy allergies should be aware that exposure to soy can cause reactions ranging from mild symptoms such as hives and itching to severe anaphylaxis. No other Ukrop’s Homestyle Foods products are impacted by this recall.

The affected products were distributed primarily through Kroger Mid-Atlantic stores located in Virginia and West Virginia as well as Ukrop's Market Hall in Richmond, VA. All retail locations have been notified and are actively removing the recalled cakes from their shelves.

Customers who have purchased the recalled Decorated Pound Cakes are advised to return them to the place of purchase for a full refund. For further information or questions, consumers can contact Ukrop’s Homestyle Foods directly at 804-340-3050 during business hours, Monday through Friday, 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. EST.

Product Best By Date Retailers
Decorated Pound Cakes (4 varieties) 12/15/2025 Kroger Mid-Atlantic (28 locations in VA and WV), Ukrop's Market Hall (Richmond, VA)
  • Return the recalled product to the retailer where it was purchased for a full refund.
  • Contact Ukrop’s Homestyle Foods at 804-340-3050 for additional questions or concerns.
  • Expect business hours for customer inquiries: Monday-Friday, 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. EST.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
South Asian Food Inc., located in Maspeth, Queens, NY, has issued a recall for its Bengal King Jhal Chanachur food treats due to the potential presence of undeclared peanuts. This poses a significant risk for individuals with peanut allergies, as consuming the product could cause serious or life-threatening allergic reactions.

The recalled product is sold in 12.34 oz (350 gm) clear plastic jars and was distributed nationwide through retail stores and mail orders. The recall was prompted after routine testing by the New York State Department of Agriculture and Markets confirmed that packages without peanut labeling contained peanuts.

No reports of illnesses or allergic reactions related to this product have been received so far. Consumers who have purchased this specific product are advised to return it to the place of purchase for a full refund. headline: Town Food Service Equipment Co., Inc. Recalls Aluminum Saucepans Because of Possible Health Risk

image.caption: Town Food Service Equipment Co., Inc. Recalls Aluminum Saucepans Because of Possible Health Risk

description: Town Food Service Equipment Co. announced a recall involving 4 sizes of aluminum saucepans with capacities ranging from 1 quart to 3 quarts after issues...
keywords: Town Food Service Equipment Co., Inc.
4 sizes of aluminum saucepans with capacities ranging from 1 quart to 3 quarts
Aluminum saucepans recalled because they have the potential to be contaminated with lead (Pb) which may leach into food.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Town Food Service Equipment Co., Inc. of Brooklyn, NY, is recalling certain aluminum saucepans due to potential lead (Pb) contamination, which may leach into food. Lead exposure poses serious health risks, particularly to children and fetuses, with no known safe exposure level. Even low levels can impair learning, IQ, and behavior, while higher levels may cause symptoms such as fatigue, headache, stomach pain, vomiting, or neurological changes.

The recall affects four sizes of aluminum saucepans with capacities ranging from 1 quart to 3 quarts. These saucepans feature indented interior graduations and a plated steel handle riveted to the body. They were distributed between January 1, 2023, and November 30, 2025, through authorized food service equipment dealers including retailers across various states in the U.S., Puerto Rico, and Quebec City, Canada.

The recall stems from FDA testing that detected leachable lead in the finished products. Town Food Service Equipment Co. has halted production and distribution of the affected saucepans as the investigation continues. No illnesses have been reported to date.

Consumers who have purchased any of the recalled saucepans should return them to the place of purchase for a full refund. For questions, customers may contact Town Food at 718-388-5650 (Monday to Friday, 9 AM to 5 PM EST) or via email at recall@townfood.com. Additional updates will be posted on the company's recall webpage at https://townfood.com/recall-alum/.

headline: BAKKAVOR USA Issues Allergy Alert for 16-Ounce Meal Simple Red Lentil Dal Soup Due to Undeclared Milk

image.caption: BAKKAVOR USA Issues Allergy Alert for 16-Ounce Meal Simple Red Lentil Dal Soup Due to Undeclared Milk

description: Meal Simple announced a recall involving Red Lentil Dal Soup after issues linked to may contain undeclared milk allergens. FDA recall notice published in 2025.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Bakkavor USA of Charlotte, North Carolina, has announced a recall of the 16-ounce Meal Simple Red Lentil Dal Soup due to the presence of undeclared milk. This recall applies to products with a BEST BY date of January 11, 2026. The undeclared milk poses a risk to individuals with milk allergies or severe sensitivities, potentially causing serious or life-threatening allergic reactions.

The affected product was distributed between November 3, 2025, and December 8, 2025, and was sold exclusively at H-E-B retail stores in Texas. The soup is packaged in clear plastic cups with the BEST BY date printed on the bottom. To date, no allergic reactions or illnesses have been reported in connection with this recall.

The recall resulted from a consumer report that the product labeled as Meal Simple Red Lentil Dal Soup contained poultry and rice soup instead. An internal investigation found that the Meal Simple Turkey & Wild Rice Soup contains milk ingredients, while the Red Lentil Dal Soup does not list milk on its label. The cause of this labeling error is still under investigation.

Consumers who have purchased the affected 16-ounce Meal Simple Red Lentil Dal Soup are advised to return the product to the place of purchase for a full refund. For further questions, customers may contact H-E-B Customer Service at 1-855-432-4438, available Monday through Friday from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. Central Standard Time.

Product Size Best By Date Retailer Distribution Dates
Meal Simple Red Lentil Dal Soup 16 ounces 1/11/2026 H-E-B stores in Texas 11/3/2025 – 12/8/2025

  • Customer service hours: Monday to Friday, 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. CST.

headline: Cargill Conducts Voluntary Recall of a Single Lot of Nutrena® Country Feeds® Cracked Corn for Livestock Due to Elevated Levels of Aflatoxin

image.caption: Cargill Conducts Voluntary Recall of a Single Lot of Nutrena® Country Feeds® Cracked Corn for Livestock Due to Elevated Levels of Aflatoxin

description: Nutrena® Country Feeds® announced a recall involving Nutrena® Country Feeds® Cracked Corn for livestock, 50 lb. bag after issues linked to product may...
keywords: Nutrena® Country Feeds® Cracked Corn for livestock, 50 lb. bag
Product may contain aflatoxin levels that exceed the Food and Drug Administration (FDA) action level for immature animals, wildlife, equines, small ruminants, and dairy animals.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Cargill Animal Nutrition & Health has announced a voluntary recall of a single lot of Nutrena® Country Feeds® Cracked Corn for livestock due to elevated levels of aflatoxin. The aflatoxin levels in this product exceed the Food and Drug Administration (FDA) action level for immature animals, wildlife, equines, small ruminants, and dairy animals. Exposure to high aflatoxin levels can cause serious health issues in livestock, including weight loss, reduced feed intake, liver damage, immune suppression, and in severe cases, cancerous tumors. Dairy animals may also pass aflatoxin into their milk.

The recalled product was distributed through retail stores in Texas. The recall was initiated after testing by the Office of the Texas State Chemist revealed aflatoxin concentration of 35 parts per billion (ppb) in a sample. Cargill’s retained sample from the same lot tested below 1 ppb, and the company has not yet received any reports of injury or illness linked to this product. Distribution of the affected lot has stopped as investigations continue.

Consumers who have purchased the impacted lot are advised to stop using the product immediately. Remaining product can be returned to the original point of purchase for a refund or replacement. If any animals have consumed the recalled feed and display symptoms such as vomiting, diarrhea, decreased milk production, or other health issues, owners should contact a veterinarian promptly.

For further information, consumers can contact Cargill at 1 800-292-7694, Monday through Friday, 8:30 a.m. to 4:30 p.m. Central Time. This recall is being carried out in cooperation with the FDA to ensure animal safety.

Product Lot Code Manufacture Date Package Size Distribution Area
Nutrena® Country Feeds® Cracked Corn for livestock Specific lot code (found on bottom right-hand side of label) Not specified in announcement 50 lb. bag Texas retail stores
  • Stop using the affected Nutrena® Country Feeds® Cracked Corn immediately.
  • Return remaining product to your local dealer or retailer for a full refund or replacement.
  • If your livestock shows symptoms such as vomiting, diarrhea, weight loss, decreased milk or egg production, or other health concerns, contact your veterinarian right away.
  • For additional questions, call Cargill’s consumer information line at 1 800-292-7694 (Monday to Friday, 8:30 a.m.–4:30 p.m. Central Time).

headline: MediNatura New Mexico, Inc. Issues Voluntary Nationwide Recall of ReBoost Nasal Spray Due to Microbial Contamination

image.caption: MediNatura New Mexico, Inc. Issues Voluntary Nationwide Recall of ReBoost Nasal Spray Due to Microbial Contamination

description: ReBoost announced a recall involving Nasal spray, 0.68 fl oz after issues linked to product has been found to contain yeast/mold and microbial contamination...
keywords: Product has been found to contain yeast/mold and microbial contamination with one species identified as Achromobacter, at levels above specifications.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
On December 10, 2025, MediNatura New Mexico, Inc. announced a voluntary nationwide recall of one lot of ReBoost Nasal Spray, a homeopathic nasal spray used to temporarily relieve nasal congestion, sinus headache and pressure, postnasal drip, sneezing, runny nose, and nasal itching. The recall was initiated after microbial contamination was detected, including yeast, mold, and Achromobacter species at levels above established safety specifications.

There is a reasonable probability that using the affected product could cause adverse health consequences, including life-threatening infections, particularly in immunocompromised individuals. As of the announcement date, no adverse events related to this recalled product have been reported to the company.

The recalled nasal spray is packaged in a 20 mL bottle with a white and yellow carton. It was distributed nationwide through retail outlets and online sales via medinatura.com. Consumers who purchased this lot are advised to stop using the product immediately.

Customers who bought the product directly from MediNatura New Mexico, Inc. should contact the company to arrange a refund, while those who purchased it from retail stores should return the product to the place of purchase. Healthcare providers should be consulted if users experience any health problems potentially related to using this nasal spray.

Product Name Lot Number Expiration Date NDC Number UPC Package Size
ReBoost Nasal Spray 224268 12/2027 62795-4005-9 787647 10186 3 0.68 fl oz (20 mL)

For questions regarding this recall, consumers can contact MediNatura New Mexico, Inc. by phone or email during business hours:

  • Phone: 800-621-7644 (Monday-Friday, 8:00 AM to 5:00 PM MST)
  • Email: recall@medinatura.com

Any adverse reactions or quality issues related to this product should be reported to the FDA’s MedWatch Adverse Event Reporting program by one of the following methods:

  • Online: www.fda.gov/medwatch/report.htm
  • Regular Mail or Fax: Download the form at www.fda.gov/MedWatch/getforms.htm or call 1-800-332-1088 to request a reporting form, then complete and return it by mail or fax to 1-800-FDA-0178

This recall is being conducted in cooperation with the U.S. Food and Drug Administration.

headline: Lil’ Turtles Issues a Voluntary Recall Due to Undeclared Milk Allergen

image.caption: Lil’ Turtles Issues a Voluntary Recall Due to Undeclared Milk Allergen

description: Grandma Belle’s announced a recall involving Tomato Basil Soup after issues linked to potential or undeclared allergen – undeclared milk. FDA recall notice...
keywords: Potential or Undeclared Allergen – Undeclared Milk

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Lil’ Turtles has issued a voluntary recall of all lots of Grandma Belle’s Tomato Basil Soup due to undeclared milk on the product label. The recall is being conducted because the milk allergen was not listed, posing a risk to individuals with milk allergies or severe sensitivities.

The affected product is packaged in 17 oz glass jars and was sold between September 23, 2022, and December 3, 2025, at multiple retail locations across several states. The issue was identified during a routine inspection by the Ohio Department of Agriculture.

Although there have been no reported illnesses related to the consumption of this product, people allergic to milk could experience serious allergic reactions if they eat this soup. Consumers who have purchased the product and have a milk allergy or sensitivity are advised to contact Lil’ Turtles for a replacement.

Questions related to this recall can be directed to Josh Coblentz at 330-897-6400.

headline: TBC Distribution Corporate Recalls Lucky Foods Brand Cinnamon Powder Because of Possible Health Risk

image.caption: TBC Distribution Corporate Recalls Lucky Foods Brand Cinnamon Powder Because of Possible Health Risk

description: Lucky Foods announced a recall involving Lucky Foods brand Cinnamon Powder 40g, best before date 15.09.2027, packaged in a vacuum or modified atmosphere 40g...
keywords: Lucky Foods brand Cinnamon Powder 40g, best before date 15.09.2027, packaged in a vacuum or modified atmosphere 40g pouch with a brown label
Potential to be contaminated with elevated levels of lead

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
TBC Distribution Corporate of Brooklyn, NY, has issued a recall for Lucky Foods brand Cinnamon Powder 40g due to potential contamination with elevated levels of lead. The affected product has a best before date of 15.09.2027 and is packaged in a vacuum or modified atmosphere 40g pouch with a brown label.

Exposure to lead, even at low levels, can be harmful. While short-term exposure to very low levels may not cause symptoms, increased blood lead levels can be the only sign of exposure. Prolonged exposure, especially in children, may cause serious health issues including developmental defects and permanent damage to the central nervous system. Adults exposed chronically may experience kidney dysfunction, hypertension, and neurocognitive effects.

The recalled cinnamon powder was distributed to retail locations including grocery stores, convenience stores, supermarkets, delis, bakeries, and restaurants in multiple states between April 11, 2025, and September 1, 2025. The recall follows testing by the New York State Department of Agriculture and Markets, which detected elevated lead levels. No illnesses have been reported at this time.

Consumers who have purchased the product are advised not to consume it. For further information, contact the company at 718-444-5556, Monday through Friday, from 8:00 AM to 5:00 PM Eastern Time.

  • Do not consume the affected Lucky Foods Cinnamon Powder 40g with best before date 15.09.2027.

headline: Mellace Family Brands California, Inc. Recalls Wegmans Deluxe Mixed Nuts Unsalted, 34 oz and 11.5 oz Packages Because Of Possible Health Risk

image.caption: Mellace Family Brands California, Inc. Recalls Wegmans Deluxe Mixed Nuts Unsalted, 34 oz and 11.5 oz Packages Because Of Possible Health Risk

description: Wegmans announced a recall involving Deluxe Mixed Nuts Unsalted (34 oz tubs and 11.5 oz bags) after issues linked to product may be contaminated with...
keywords: Deluxe Mixed Nuts Unsalted (34 oz tubs and 11.5 oz bags)
Product may be contaminated with Salmonella

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Mellace Family Brands California, Inc. has recalled Wegmans Deluxe Mixed Nuts Unsalted in 34 oz tubs and 11.5 oz bags due to possible Salmonella contamination. Salmonella is a harmful bacteria that can cause serious infections, especially in young children, elderly adults, and individuals with weakened immune systems. Symptoms often include fever, diarrhea, nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain, and severe cases may lead to bloodstream infections and other complications.

The recalled products were sold at Wegmans stores in Connecticut, Delaware, Maryland, Massachusetts, North Carolina, New Jersey, New York, Pennsylvania, Virginia, and Washington, D.C. between November 3 and December 1, 2025. No illnesses have been reported in connection with these products to date.

The recall follows routine testing of a supplier’s raw pistachios that revealed a positive Salmonella result. These pistachios were used in the affected Deluxe Mixed Nuts. Consumers who have purchased the specified lots should return the product to the store for a full refund.

For further information, customers can contact Wegmans Food Markets at 1-855-934-3663, available daily from 8:00 a.m. to 7:00 p.m. EST.

headline: Mondelēz Global LLC Conducts Limited Voluntary Recall of 1 SKU of RITZ Peanut Butter Cracker Sandwiches in New York, New Jersey, Pennsylvania, Georgia, Arkansas, Missouri, Oklahoma, and Alabama

image.caption: Mondelēz Global LLC Conducts Limited Voluntary Recall of 1 SKU of RITZ Peanut Butter Cracker Sandwiches in New York, New Jersey, Pennsylvania, Georgia, Arkansas, Missouri, Oklahoma, and Alabama

description: Ritz announced a recall involving Peanut Butter Cracker Sandwiches after issues linked to potential or undeclared allergen – undeclared peanut. FDA recall...
keywords: Potential or Undeclared Allergen – Undeclared Peanut

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Mondelēz Global LLC has issued a limited voluntary recall of RITZ Peanut Butter Cracker Sandwiches sold in select U.S. states due to a potential undeclared peanut allergen. The recall affects 70 cases of one specific SKU with particular “Best When Used By” dates and a plant code, originally distributed in New York, New Jersey, Pennsylvania, Georgia, Arkansas, Missouri, Oklahoma, and Alabama.

The issue involves individually wrapped packs inside the cartons which may be mislabeled as the Cheese variety, although the product inside is the Peanut Butter variety. This could pose a serious or life-threatening risk for individuals allergic or severely sensitive to peanuts. The outer cartons are correctly labeled and include an allergen advisory stating “contains peanuts.”

This recall is not an expansion of a previous recall from July but is a voluntary action taken out of an abundance of caution. No other Ritz products or other Mondelēz Global LLC products are impacted by this recall. To date, there have been no reports of injury or illness related to this product.

Consumers who have a peanut allergy are advised not to consume this product and to discard any affected cartons. Customers with questions can contact Mondelēz Global LLC customer service at 1-844-366-1171, Monday through Friday from 9 a.m. to 6 p.m. ET.

headline: Vetoquinol USA, Inc. Recalls Six Lots of FOLLTROPIN Injectable Kits for Cattle Due to Presence of Particulate Matter in Sterile Diluent Vial

image.caption: Vetoquinol USA, Inc. Recalls Six Lots of FOLLTROPIN Injectable Kits for Cattle Due to Presence of Particulate Matter in Sterile Diluent Vial

description: Vetoquinol announced a recall involving FOLLTROPIN Injectable Kits for Cattle after issues linked to presence of particulate matter. FDA recall notice...

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Vetoquinol USA, Inc., based in Fort Worth, TX, has issued a recall for six lots of its FOLLTROPIN® Injectable Kits for cattle. The recall is due to the presence of particulate matter identified in the sterile diluent vial during routine examination of retained samples. The affected product is used for inducing superovulation in beef and dairy heifers and cows and is a prescription veterinary medicine in the U.S.

The presence of particulate matter in an injectable product may cause injection site reactions or hypersensitivity responses in treated animals. Possible adverse signs include pain, swelling, or wounds at the injection site, decreased activity, fever, and labored breathing. Veterinary attention should be sought immediately if any such signs occur after administration of the product. As of the recall announcement, no adverse events have been reported related to these lots.

The recall only concerns the sterile diluent vial included in the FOLLTROPIN Kit, which contains both the follicle stimulating hormone powder and a sterile bacteriostatic saline diluent. The FOLLTROPIN powder vials and the Dual Pack format (which does not contain diluent vials) are not affected by this recall. The affected products were distributed nationwide through veterinary distributors.

Customers who have received the recalled product lots should stop using them immediately and quarantine all affected product. Vetoquinol USA encourages customers to contact them for further instructions on product return or disposal. Any questions or reports of adverse reactions related to this recall can also be directed to the FDA and Vetoquinol USA, Inc.

Recall Information
Product: FOLLTROPIN® Injectable Kits (porcine pituitary-derived FSH for injection)
Reason for Recall: Presence of particulate matter in sterile diluent vial
Impacted Product Format: FOLLTROPIN Kit (one vial of FSH powder + one vial of sterile diluent)
Unaffected Product Format: FOLLTROPIN Dual Pack (two vials of FSH powder, no diluent)
Distribution: Veterinary distributors nationwide, United States
Impacted Lots
Six lots of FOLLTROPIN sterile diluent vials (specific lot numbers were referenced in the announcement but not detailed here)

For further assistance or questions regarding this recall, consumers and distributors may contact Vetoquinol USA, Inc. at:

  • Phone: 1-800-267-5707 (Monday-Friday, 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. CST)

To report adverse events or quality issues, contact the FDA:

  • Phone: 1-888-FDA-VETS
  • Online: http://www.fda.gov/reportanimalae

This recall is being conducted with the knowledge of the U.S. Food and Drug Administration.

headline: Wegmans Recalls Locatelli Grated Pecorino Romano Cheese Due to Possible Health Risk

image.caption: Wegmans Recalls Locatelli Grated Pecorino Romano Cheese Due to Possible Health Risk

description: Locatelli announced a recall involving Grated Pecorino Romano Cheese after issues linked to potential presence of listeria monocytogenes.. FDA recall notice...

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Wegmans Food Markets, Inc. has issued a recall for Locatelli Grated Pecorino Romano Cheese sold with Wegmans’ scale labels due to the potential presence of Listeria monocytogenes. This bacterium can cause serious and potentially fatal infections, particularly in young children, elderly individuals, pregnant women, and those with weakened immune systems.

The recalled product was available at Wegmans stores in Connecticut, Delaware, Maryland, Massachusetts, North Carolina, New Jersey, New York, Pennsylvania, Virginia, and Washington, D.C. between November 14 and November 24, 2025. Consumers are advised not to consume this product and to return it to the store for a full refund.

Symptoms of Listeria infection include high fever, severe headache, stiffness, nausea, abdominal pain, and diarrhea. Pregnant women infected with Listeria risk miscarriage or stillbirth. No illnesses related to this product have been reported to date.

Consumers with questions about the recall can contact Wegmans Food Markets at 1-855-934-3663 during the following hours:

  • Wednesday, November 26: 8 a.m. – 8 p.m.
  • Thursday, November 27: 8 a.m. – 1 p.m.
  • Friday, November 28: 8 a.m. – 4:30 p.m.
  • Starting Saturday, November 29: daily from 8 a.m. – 7 p.m.

headline: Prairie Farms Announces Recall of Prairie Farms Gallon Fat Free Milk

image.caption: Prairie Farms Announces Recall of Prairie Farms Gallon Fat Free Milk

description: Prairie Farms announced a recall involving Fat Free Milk after issues linked to may contain food-grade cleaning agents. FDA recall notice published in 2025.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Prairie Farms has announced a recall of select Prairie Farms Gallon Fat Free Milk due to potential contamination with food-grade cleaning agents. The affected milk was produced at the Dubuque, Iowa facility and distributed exclusively to Woodman’s stores in Illinois and Wisconsin. Consumption of this product may cause illness.

The recall specifically applies to Prairie Farms Gallon Fat Free Milk with a code date of DEC08 and plant code PLT19-145, produced during the timeframe of 17:51 to 21:23 on that date. Approximately 320 gallons of the affected milk were sold before the issue was identified on November 24, 2025. Prairie Farms promptly removed all remaining impacted product from store shelves and initiated this recall to ensure consumer safety.

Consumers who purchased the recalled milk are advised not to consume it. The product can be safely disposed of or returned to the store for a refund. Other Prairie Farms products are not affected by this recall.

Prairie Farms is cooperating with regulatory authorities and Woodman’s Markets to investigate the incident and manage the recall process. The affected product was distributed to multiple locations across Illinois and Wisconsin.

headline: MAMTAKIM Inc Recalls Dried Whole Smelts Salted (KORUSHKA) Because of Possible Health Risk

image.caption: MAMTAKIM Inc Recalls Dried Whole Smelts Salted (KORUSHKA) Because of Possible Health Risk

description: Belevini announced a recall involving Dried Whole Salted Smelt, Mullet, Goby after issues linked to potential to be contaminated with clostridium...
keywords: Dried Whole Salted Smelt, Mullet, Goby
Potential to be contaminated with Clostridium botulinum.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
MAMTAKIM Inc. has issued a recall for the product "Dried Whole Smelts Salted (Korushka)" under the Belevini brand due to a potential health risk of contamination with Clostridium botulinum. This bacterium can cause botulism, a serious and potentially fatal foodborne illness. The recalled product consists of dried, uneviscerated fish longer than 5 inches, which increases the risk of bacterial growth.

The product is packaged in a transparent vacuum-sealed plastic pouch with a net weight of 5.29 oz (150 g) and was stored refrigerated between 18°F and 68°F. It was sold exclusively to walk-in customers at the Cherry Hill Emmons retail store located at 1901 Emmons Ave, Brooklyn, NY 11235. Consumers are advised not to consume this product, even if it appears normal, and to return it to the place of purchase for a full refund.

Symptoms of botulism can include general weakness, dizziness, double vision, difficulty speaking or swallowing, trouble breathing, muscle weakness, abdominal distension, and constipation. Anyone experiencing these symptoms should seek immediate medical attention. No illnesses have been reported in connection with this recall to date.

The recall was initiated following an FDA inspection which found that the fish had not been properly eviscerated, allowing the potential growth of Clostridium botulinum. Consumers with questions can contact MAMTAKIM Inc. directly for further information.

Product Name Package Net Weight Brand Barcode (UPC) Storage Expiration Date Item Number

  • Do not consume the recalled product, even if it appears normal.
  • Return the product to the place of purchase for a full refund.
  • Contact MAMTAKIM Inc. with any questions or concerns.
  • Seek immediate medical attention if experiencing botulism symptoms such as weakness, dizziness, double vision, difficulty speaking or swallowing, or respiratory trouble.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Supreme Service Solutions LLC (doing business as Supreme Deli) is participating in a recall linked to pecorino romano cheese supplied by Ambriola Company, which impacts certain Boar’s Head Brand products. The recall has been issued due to the potential presence of Listeria monocytogenes, a harmful bacteria that can pose serious health risks.

Ambriola Company initiated a Class I recall for specific pecorino romano cheese items following notification from the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA) about possible contamination. As a precaution, Boar’s Head has voluntarily withdrawn all products produced by Ambriola Company for Boar’s Head, including items that are not directly affected by the contamination.

The recalled products were distributed in Kroger retail stores located in Kentucky and Indiana. These products are packaged in clear plastic grab-and-go containers and include deli salads and wraps. To date, no illnesses or consumer complaints related to this recall have been reported by Supreme Service Solutions.

Consumers who may have purchased these products are advised not to consume them and to discard any remaining quantities. Although the products are likely past their shelf life and removed from distribution, anyone with health concerns should consult a physician. For further questions, consumers can contact Ambriola Company via their information email.

description: Prime Food announced a recall involving Lava Buns after issues linked to undeclared milk allergen. FDA recall notice published in 2025.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Prime Food Processing LLC of Brooklyn, New York, has announced a voluntary recall of 2,243 cases of two varieties of dessert buns under the Prime Food brand due to undeclared milk allergens. The affected products contain milk derived from unsalted butter, which is not properly listed in the “Contains” allergen statement on the packaging as required by the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA).

This recall poses a significant risk to individuals with milk allergies or severe milk sensitivities, as consuming these products could trigger a serious or life-threatening allergic reaction. The affected lava buns were distributed to Asian grocery stores in 38 states between April 2, 2025, and November 14, 2025.

No illnesses or allergic reactions have been reported to date. The recall was initiated after an internal product review identified the labeling issue, attributed to a temporary failure in the label review process at Prime Food Processing LLC.

Consumers who have purchased the recalled lava buns are advised to return the products along with the packaging to the place of purchase for a full refund. For additional information, consumers can contact Prime Food Processing LLC at 718-963-2323, Monday through Friday, from 9:00 AM to 5:00 PM EST.
