headline: SHATA TRADERS INC RECALLS CHEF BRAND MILK PAN 24cm BECAUSE OF POSSIBLE HEALTH RISK

image.caption: SHATA TRADERS INC RECALLS CHEF BRAND MILK PAN 24cm BECAUSE OF POSSIBLE HEALTH RISK

description: Majestic Chef Pan announced a recall involving Milk Pan 24cm after issues linked to product has the potential to be contaminated with significant levels of...
keywords: Product has the potential to be contaminated with significant levels of lead (Pb) which may leach into food

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Shata Traders Inc, based in Brooklyn, NY, is recalling the Chef Brand Milk Pan 24 cm due to potential contamination with significant levels of lead (Pb), which may leach into food during use. Lead is a toxic metal with no safe level of exposure and poses serious health risks, especially to children and fetuses.

Exposure to lead can cause severe health issues including learning difficulties, low IQ, behavioral problems in children, and symptoms such as fatigue, headache, stomach pain, vomiting, or neurological changes at higher levels. The recalled Milk Pan 24 cm was distributed in retailers across New York, New Jersey, Connecticut, Maryland, Virginia, and Massachusetts. The product is sold individually as part of a 6-piece box set.

No illnesses have been reported to date. The FDA identified the contamination after testing product samples collected at a retail store. Consumers who have purchased the Chef Brand Milk Pan 24 cm should immediately stop using it and return it to the place of purchase for a full refund.

For additional information or questions, contact Shata Traders Inc at (718) 369-7000 and ask for Mahboob Rahman.

Product Size Distribution Areas Packaging
Chef Brand Milk Pan 24 cm NY, NJ, CT, MD, VA, MA Sold individually, 6 pieces per box
  • Stop using the recalled Milk Pan 24 cm immediately.
  • Return the product to the retail store for a full refund.
  • Contact Shata Traders Inc at (718) 369-7000 and ask for Mahboob Rahman for questions or concerns.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Mangalm LLC of Newark, California, has announced a recall of all batches of Tops Mixed Fruit Jam and Tops Strawberry Jam due to the presence of carmoisine, an unapproved color additive. The inclusion of this additive poses a potential health risk to consumers. Both products affected are the 475g jars with batch number 4F1101 and a best before date of October 6, 2026.

The recalled products were distributed across several states including Utah, Oregon (Portland), Washington, and California. To date, no illnesses related to the consumption of these products have been confirmed. The recall action follows an FDA inspection at the manufacturing site, which detected the undeclared use of carmoisine in these jams.

Mangalm LLC has halted further distribution of Tops Mixed Fruit Jam and Tops Strawberry Jam and is actively removing the affected products from retail shelves. Consumers who have purchased these items are advised not to consume them and to take appropriate actions.

If you have these products, you should discontinue use immediately. For additional information or assistance, consumers can contact Mangalm LLC directly.

headline: Africa Imports Issues Voluntary Recall of Organic Moringa Leaf Powder Due to Potential Salmonella Contamination

image.caption: Africa Imports Issues Voluntary Recall of Organic Moringa Leaf Powder Due to Potential Salmonella Contamination

description: Africa Imports announced a recall involving Organic Moringa Leaf Powder after issues linked to potential to be contaminated with salmonella. FDA recall...
keywords: Potential to be contaminated with Salmonella

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Africa Imports has issued a voluntary recall of its Organic Moringa Leaf Powder (1 kilogram box) due to potential contamination with Salmonella. Salmonella is a bacterium that can cause serious infections, particularly in young children, the elderly, and individuals with weakened immune systems. Healthy individuals infected with Salmonella may experience symptoms such as fever, diarrhea (which may be bloody), nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain. In rare instances, the infection can lead to more severe health issues including arterial infections, endocarditis, or arthritis.

The affected product was sold nationwide through Africa Imports' official website, africaimports.com, and is packaged in 1-kilogram boxes labeled "Organic Moringa Leaf Powder." All product purchased after June 5, 2025, is included in this recall following the detection of Salmonella in some samples from this batch.

Consumers who have purchased the product are advised to stop using it immediately and dispose of it safely. Africa Imports requests that customers confirm disposal by responding to the recall notification email or by contacting the company directly. If the product has been further distributed, distributors are urged to notify their customers and provide a copy of the recall notice.

This recall is being conducted in cooperation with the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA). For additional information or assistance, customers can contact Wayne Kiltz, President of Africa Imports, at 201-457-1995 ext. 236 or via email at africa@africaimports.com. More details are also available through the FDA Outbreak Advisory.

Product Packaging Recall Details
Organic Moringa Leaf Powder 1 kilogram box All product purchased after June 5, 2025

  • Dispose of the product safely.
  • Confirm disposal by replying to the recall notification email or contacting Africa Imports directly.
  • If you have distributed the product, notify your customers about the recall and share this notice.
  • Contact information: Wayne Kiltz, President, Africa Imports, Phone: 201-457-1995 ext. 236, Email: africa@africaimports.com.

headline: ByHeart Updates Information Regarding Voluntary Recall of all Batches of ByHeart Whole Nutrition Infant Formula Cans and Packs Because of Possible Health Risk

image.caption: ByHeart Updates Information Regarding Voluntary Recall of all Batches of ByHeart Whole Nutrition Infant Formula Cans and Packs Because of Possible Health Risk

description: ByHeart announced a recall involving Whole Nutrition Infant formula 24 oz cans and 0.6oz packets after issues linked to potential foodborne illness –...
keywords: Whole Nutrition Infant formula 24 oz cans and 0.6oz packets

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
ByHeart Inc. has updated the information regarding its voluntary recall of all batches of ByHeart Whole Nutrition Infant Formula in 24 oz cans and 0.6 oz Anywhere Pack™ packets due to the potential presence of Clostridium botulinum, a bacterium that can cause life-threatening illness or death. This recall follows an ongoing investigation related to a multistate outbreak of infant botulism potentially linked to the consumption of this formula.

Infant botulism is a rare but serious condition that occurs when Clostridium botulinum spores are ingested and produce neurotoxins in the immature intestinal tract of infants. Symptoms may include constipation, poor feeding, drooping eyelid, sluggish pupils, low muscle tone, difficulty sucking and swallowing, weak cry, generalized weakness, respiratory problems, and possibly respiratory arrest. Immediate medical attention should be sought if any of these symptoms appear.

The recall affects all batch codes and expiration dates of the affected ByHeart Whole Nutrition Infant Formula products distributed nationwide through retail and online channels. Consumers are advised to stop using the formula immediately, even if the product appears normal, and keep the containers labeled as “DO NOT USE.” Testing is ongoing in collaboration with independent laboratories, the FDA, and other public health authorities.

Parents and caregivers with questions or concerns can contact ByHeart directly at hello@byheart.com or call 1 (833) 429-4327, available 24/7. It is important to report any product problems or adverse events through appropriate FDA channels.

Product Size UPC Additional Info
ByHeart Whole Nutrition Infant Formula 24 oz can 5004496800 All batch codes and expiration dates included in recall
ByHeart Whole Nutrition Infant Formula Anywhere Pack™ 0.60 oz packets (14 packets per package) 5004496802 All batch codes and expiration dates included in recall

Consumers should follow these recommended steps if they have purchased the recalled products:

  • Take a photo or record the information on the bottom of the package for reference.
  • Keep the container in a safe place and label it as “DO NOT USE.”
  • If your child develops symptoms consistent with infant botulism, seek medical attention immediately. Health departments may request the formula container for testing.
  • If no symptoms develop within 30 days, discard the formula containers safely.
  • Report any product problems or adverse effects through the FDA’s MedWatch system:
    • Report a Product Problem via SmartHub
    • Complete an electronic Voluntary MedWatch form online
    • Complete a paper Voluntary MedWatch form to mail to FDA

The recall and investigation are being conducted in close coordination with the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA), the California Department of Public Health (CDPH), and other partners. As of November 19, 2025, 31 infants from 15 states with suspected or confirmed infant botulism and exposure to ByHeart formula have been reported, with all hospitalized and no deaths reported to date.

For further details and updates, visit the ByHeart website or the FDA website.

headline: Moonlight Companies Voluntarily Recalls California-Grown Conventional Yellow and White Peaches Because of Possible Health Risk- Revised to Clarify Upc Codes; This Recall Has Not Been Expanded

description: Moonlight/Kroger announced a recall involving Yellow and white peaches after issues linked to potential to be contaminated with listeria monocytogenes.. FDA...
keywords: Potential to be contaminated with Listeria monocytogenes.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Moonlight Companies has announced a voluntary recall of California-grown conventional yellow and white peaches due to a potential contamination with Listeria monocytogenes. This bacterium can cause serious infections, particularly in young children, the elderly, pregnant women, and individuals with weakened immune systems. Healthy individuals may experience symptoms such as high fever, severe headache, stiffness, nausea, abdominal pain, and diarrhea. Pregnant women infected with Listeria risk miscarriages and stillbirths.

The recalled peaches were sold at retail stores nationwide from September 16, 2025, through October 29, 2025. These peaches were available either as individual fruit with PLU stickers or in multi-packs. It is important to note that this recall does not include packages or PLU stickers labeled “Washington” or “Organic.” The recall was initiated after Listeria monocytogenes was detected in the packing facility environment. No related illnesses have been reported at this time.

Consumers who have purchased the affected peaches are advised not to consume them and should discard or return the products to the place of purchase. For further information or questions, customers can contact Moonlight Companies at 855-215-5017, Monday through Friday from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. Eastern Time. This recall is being conducted in consultation with the U.S. Food and Drug Administration.

Conventional Yellow and White Peaches Various (multi-packs) September 16, 2025 – October 29, 2025 Multi-pack, excluding “Washington” and “Organic” packages

To report any adverse reactions or illnesses related to consuming this product, consumers and healthcare professionals can use the FDA’s MedWatch system by:

headline: In Response to a Broader FDA Investigation, ByHeart Initiates a Voluntary Recall of Two Batches of Infant Formula

image.caption: In Response to a Broader FDA Investigation, ByHeart Initiates a Voluntary Recall of Two Batches of Infant Formula

description: ByHeart announced a recall involving Whole Nutrition Infant formula after issues linked to potential foodborne illness – clostridium botulinum. FDA recall...

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
ByHeart Inc. has initiated a voluntary recall of two batches of its Whole Nutrition Infant Formula following a broader investigation by the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA) into an outbreak of infant botulism. Infant botulism is a rare but serious illness caused by ingestion and colonization of Clostridium botulinum spores in an infant's intestinal tract, leading to potentially severe symptoms including constipation, poor feeding, muscle weakness, and respiratory difficulties.

The FDA reported approximately 83 cases of infant botulism nationwide since August 2025, with 13 of the affected infants having received ByHeart formula at some point. However, the FDA has not found a direct link between any infant formula and these cases, and there is no historical precedent for infant formula causing infant botulism. Botulism is typically associated with environmental sources such as soil and dust rather than dairy products or infant formulas.

ByHeart adheres to strict global and U.S. safety standards and has found no contamination in its products. Despite this, the company is taking the precautionary measure of recalling the specified batches to eliminate any potential risk and uphold the highest safety standards for infants.

Consumers who have purchased the recalled batches are advised to stop using the product immediately and dispose of it. ByHeart will replace affected cans free of charge. Any infant showing symptoms of botulism should be taken to a healthcare provider without delay.

Batch Code Use By Date UPC
251261P2 01 Dec 2026 5004496800
251131P2 01 Dec 2026

To report an illness or adverse event related to this recall, consumers can:

  • Contact an FDA Consumer Complaint Coordinator to speak directly with a representative.
  • Complete an electronic Voluntary MedWatch form online.
  • Complete a paper Voluntary MedWatch form and mail it to the FDA.

For further questions or support, parents and caregivers can contact ByHeart experts via email at hello@byheart.com or call 1 (833) 429-4327, available 24/7. Additional information is also available on the company website at byheart.com.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Fresenius Kabi USA, LLC is voluntarily recalling three lots of Famotidine Injection, USP 20 mg per 2 mL (10 mg per mL), 2 mL fill in a 2 mL vial. The recall affects products distributed within the United States and is due to out-of-specification endotoxin results found in certain reserve samples from one lot. Two additional lots were included as a precaution following the investigation.

The company has informed distributors and customers and is coordinating the return of the affected products. Healthcare facilities should immediately stop distributing, dispensing, or using the recalled lots and return all units to Fresenius Kabi.

Consumers or healthcare providers with questions about the recall can contact Fresenius Kabi USA Quality Assurance at 1-866-716-2459, Monday through Friday, from 8:00 a.m. to 5:00 p.m. Central Standard Time. Patients experiencing any adverse effects potentially related to this drug should consult their healthcare provider.

Adverse reactions or quality issues may be reported to Fresenius Kabi Medical Affairs or Vigilance departments at 1-800-551-7176 during the same hours, or via email at productcomplaint.USA@fresenius-kabi.com or adverse.events.USA@fresenius-kabi.com. Reports can also be submitted to the FDA MedWatch program online or by mail or fax.

headline: African Food on Wheels Inc. Recalls Oven Dried Fish (Scomberomorus Cavalla) Because of Possible Health Risk
description: African Food on Wheels Inc. announced a recall involving Oven Dried Fish (Scomberomorus cavalla) after issues linked to potential to be contaminated with...
keywords: Potential to be contaminated with Clostridium botulinum

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
African Food on Wheels Inc., based in Bronx, New York, has announced a recall of its Oven Dried Fish (Scomberomorus cavalla) due to a potential health risk. The recalled product consists of dried, un-eviscerated fish greater than 5 inches in length, which may be contaminated with Clostridium botulinum. This bacterium can cause botulism, a serious and potentially fatal illness.

Consumers are advised not to consume the product, even if it appears or smells normal. Botulism symptoms include general weakness, dizziness, double vision, difficulty speaking or swallowing, breathing difficulties, muscle weakness, abdominal swelling, and constipation. Anyone experiencing these symptoms should seek immediate medical care.

The affected products were sold only to walk-in customers at African Food on Wheels Inc., located at 1376 Randall Avenue, Bronx, NY 10474. The products are packaged in corrugated brown boxes. To date, no illnesses have been reported in connection with this product.

The recall followed an FDA inspection that found the fish had not been properly eviscerated. Consumers who have purchased this product are urged to return it to the place of purchase for a full refund. For further information, customers may contact African Food on Wheels Inc. via email at africanfoodonwheelsinc@gmail.com or by phone at 646-632-2911, Monday through Friday, from 11 a.m. to 5 p.m. EST.

headline: Blue Oven Bakery, Inc. Issues a Voluntary Recall Due to Undeclared Milk Allergens in Their English Muffin
description: Blue Oven Bakery, Inc. announced a recall involving English muffin after issues linked to undeclared milk. FDA recall notice published in 2025.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Blue Oven Bakery, Inc. has announced a voluntary recall of their English muffins due to an undeclared milk allergen on the product labels. The recall affects English muffins packaged in 13.04 oz sealed plastic bags, which were sold fresh as baked products. This mislabeling poses a risk to individuals with milk allergies.

The affected English muffins were produced from April 24, 2025, through October 24, 2025. These products were distributed and sold at select retail locations in Ohio, Kentucky, and Indiana.

To date, no illnesses or allergic reactions related to this product have been reported. Customers who have purchased the recalled English muffins are advised to stop consumption and may return the product for a full refund. Returns will be accepted at Blue Oven Bakery’s Cincinnati location on November 4 and 5, 2025.

Consumers with allergic reactions or symptoms of foodborne illness should seek medical attention promptly. For further information or questions, Blue Oven Bakery can be contacted by phone at 513.706.0901 or via email at blueovencustomerservice@gmail.com.

headline: SiluetaYa Recalls Tejocote Product Because of Possible Health Risk

image.caption: SiluetaYa Recalls Tejocote Product Because of Possible Health Risk

description: SiluetaYa announced a recall involving Tejocote Root dietary supplement after issues linked to product contains yellow oleander. FDA recall notice published...

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
SiluetaYa is recalling its Tejocote Root dietary supplement after FDA analysis revealed the product contains Thevetia peruviana, commonly known as yellow oleander. This plant contains cardiac glycosides, which are highly toxic and can cause serious neurological, gastrointestinal, and cardiovascular health issues in humans and animals.

Symptoms of yellow oleander poisoning may include nausea, vomiting, dizziness, diarrhea, abdominal pain, changes in heart rhythm, arrhythmia, and other severe effects. The recalled Tejocote Root product was distributed online in 8-ounce containers. To date, no illnesses related to this product have been reported.

Consumers who have purchased the Tejocote Root supplement from SiluetaYa are urged not to consume it. Instead, they should return the product to the place of purchase for a full refund. For questions, consumers may contact SiluetaYa at contacto@siluetaya.com, Monday through Friday, 10:00 am to 4:00 pm Eastern Time.

For more information, please refer to the FDA Advisory.

Product Container Size Distribution
Tejocote Root dietary supplement (SiluetaYa brand) 8-ounce container Online distribution

  • Return the product to the place of purchase for a full refund.
  • Contact SiluetaYa at contacto@siluetaya.com for further assistance (Monday–Friday, 10:00 am to 4:00 pm ET).

headline: Dreyer's Grand Ice Cream, Inc. Issues Allergy Alert on Undeclared Wheat in Haagen-Dazs Chocolate Dark Chocolate Mini Bars in 6 Count Pack

image.caption: Dreyer's Grand Ice Cream, Inc. Issues Allergy Alert on Undeclared Wheat in Haagen-Dazs Chocolate Dark Chocolate Mini Bars in 6 Count Pack

description: Haagen-Dazs announced a recall involving Ice Cream Bars after issues linked to may contain undeclared wheat. FDA recall notice published in 2025.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
On November 3, 2025, Dreyer’s Grand Ice Cream, Inc. issued a voluntary recall of a limited batch of Häagen-Dazs Chocolate Dark Chocolate Mini Bars due to the potential presence of undeclared wheat. This undeclared allergen poses a serious health risk to individuals with wheat allergies or severe sensitivities, who may experience life-threatening allergic reactions if they consume the product.

The recall specifically concerns the 6-count package of Häagen-Dazs Chocolate Dark Chocolate Mini Bars with batch code LLA519501 and a Best By date of January 31, 2027. This batch was distributed exclusively to Kroger and Giant Eagle stores across selected states.

Consumers who have purchased this product are advised not to consume it. Instead, they should dispose of the affected bars or return them to the place of purchase for a full refund. To date, no illnesses or injuries related to this issue have been reported. Dreyer’s Grand Ice Cream is coordinating with the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA) on this recall and continues to investigate its root cause, which is believed to involve repackaging errors early in production.

For further information or inquiries, consumers can contact Dreyer’s Grand Ice Cream, Inc. customer service.

headline: First and Last Bakery, LLC Recalls First and Last Brand Tomato Sauce Products Because Of Possible Health Risk

image.caption: First and Last Bakery, LLC Recalls First and Last Brand Tomato Sauce Products Because Of Possible Health Risk

description: First & Last announced a recall involving Marinara Sauce, Puttanesca Sauce, and Meat Flavored Pasta Sauce after issues linked to potential for clostridium...
keywords: Marinara Sauce, Puttanesca Sauce, and Meat Flavored Pasta Sauce
Potential for Clostridium botulinum hazard as the product is manufactured without an approved schedule

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
First and Last Bakery, LLC, based in Hartford, CT, has announced a recall of certain First & Last brand tomato sauce products due to a potential health risk. The recall follows an identification by the Connecticut Department of Consumer Protection related to manufacturing without an approved scheduled process, which may allow the growth of Clostridium botulinum bacteria. This bacteria can produce botulinum toxin, a serious and potentially fatal foodborne toxin.

The affected products include Marinara Sauce, Puttanesca Sauce, and Meat Flavored Pasta Sauce. These items were distributed in Connecticut and Massachusetts, primarily through Big Y stores and possibly other retailers. Sales and production of these sauces have been suspended while the company ensures compliance with regulatory requirements. No illnesses associated with these products have been reported at this time.

Botulism poisoning from contaminated foods can cause symptoms such as general weakness, dizziness, double vision, difficulty speaking or swallowing, breathing problems, muscle weakness, abdominal swelling, and constipation. Anyone experiencing these symptoms after consuming these products should seek immediate medical care.

Consumers who have purchased any of the recalled products are advised to return them to their place of purchase for a full refund. For further information or questions, customers can contact First and Last Bakery, LLC at 860-956-5000, Monday through Friday, from 9:00 am to 3:00 pm EST.

headline: Olympus Issues Voluntary Labeling Update for Bronchoscopes Used with Laser Therapy Equipment
description: Olympus announced a recall involving Bronchofiberscopes and bronchovideoscopes after issues linked to potential for endobronchial combustion. FDA recall...

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Olympus Corporation has announced a voluntary global update to the labeling of its bronchofiberscopes and bronchovideoscopes used with laser therapy, argon plasma coagulation, or high-frequency cauterization equipment. This action aims to provide clearer instructions to ensure the safe and effective use of these bronchoscopes during therapeutic procedures in the tracheobronchial tree.

The update follows a previous corrective action in 2023 after reports of endobronchial combustion incidents involving Olympus bronchoscopes, including one fatality. Since then, Olympus has investigated further and identified the need for additional instructions to reduce the risk of combustion. Since the 2023 communication, four more serious injury incidents have been reported worldwide, including one from the United States.

Key safety recommendations include maintaining a separation of more than 4 centimeters between the endoscope and the endotracheal tube, keeping oxygen levels below 40%, limiting energy output to less than 40 watts, avoiding excessive energy concentration in a single spot, and using suction to evacuate smoke. Endobronchial combustion can cause severe internal burns requiring additional medical interventions, prolonged hospitalization, or even result in death. Device damage during combustion may also lead to injury or necessitate surgical removal of fragments.

Users are advised to carefully review the updated instructions provided in the September 2025 customer letter. For concerns or to report problems, Olympus Technical Assistance is available, and adverse events can also be reported to the FDA's MedWatch program.

  • Olympus Technical Assistance Center: 1-800-848-9024, Option 1
  • Email: complaints@olympus.com
  • FDA MedWatch reporting: https://www.fda.gov/safety/medwatch-fda-safety-information-and-adverse-event-reporting-program/reporting-serious-problems-fda

headline: Otsuka ICU Medical LLC Issues Voluntary Nationwide Recall of 20 mEq Potassium Chloride Injection Due To Overwrap Mislabeled As 10 mEq Potassium Chloride Injection

image.caption: Otsuka ICU Medical LLC Issues Voluntary Nationwide Recall of 20 mEq Potassium Chloride Injection Due To Overwrap Mislabeled As 10 mEq Potassium Chloride Injection

description: ICU Medical announced a recall involving 20 mEq Potassium Chloride Injection after issues linked to potential for potassium chloride overdose: 20 meq...
keywords: Potential for Potassium chloride overdose: 20 mEq Potassium Chloride Injection is Mislabeled As 10 mEq Potassium Chloride Injection

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Otsuka ICU Medical LLC has issued a voluntary nationwide recall of a specific lot of their 20 mEq Potassium Chloride Injection due to a labeling error. The overwrap of the recalled lot incorrectly identifies the product as 10 mEq Potassium Chloride Injection, which could lead to a potassium overdose if the mislabeled dosage is used. The correct dosage of 20 mEq is printed on the product bag label but is obscured by the incorrect overwrap.

Potassium chloride overdose may cause serious health risks including hyperkalemia, which can result in neuromuscular dysfunction, cardiac dysrhythmias, or cardiac arrest. Certain patient populations such as premature infants, those with kidney issues, or patients on potassium-sparing medications are at higher risk for severe complications. To date, no adverse events related to this issue have been reported.

The affected product lot, manufactured on April 15, 2025, was distributed in the United States between May 23, 2025, and August 26, 2025. Customers and distributors who have this lot should stop using or distributing the product and arrange for its return.

For product returns or further assistance, customers can contact Sedgwick or Otsuka ICU Medical LLC directly. Adverse reactions or quality issues can be reported to the FDA's MedWatch program.

description: Monarch Premium announced a recall involving Bali Gold, Red Bali, Green Maeng Da, and White Elephant Kratom powder after issues linked to potential to be...
keywords: Vanguard Enterprises, LLC. DBA Bedrock MFG
Bali Gold, Red Bali, Green Maeng Da, and White Elephant Kratom powder
Potential to be contaminated with Salmonella

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Vanguard Enterprises, LLC. DBA Bedrock MFG of Boise, Idaho, has announced a recall of its Monarch Premium Kratom powders, including Bali Gold, Red Bali, Green Maeng Da, and White Elephant varieties. The recall is due to the potential contamination of these products with Salmonella, a bacteria that can cause serious and sometimes fatal infections, especially in young children, elderly individuals, and those with weakened immune systems.

Symptoms of Salmonella infection can include fever, diarrhea (which may be bloody), nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain. In rare cases, the infection can spread to the bloodstream, leading to severe illnesses such as arterial infections, endocarditis, and arthritis.

The affected Monarch Premium Kratom powders were distributed nationwide in retail stores and through mail orders via the company’s website from April 2023 through September 2023. These products were not sold in Alabama, Arkansas, Indiana, Rhode Island, or Wisconsin. No illnesses have been reported in connection with this recall to date.

This recall follows laboratory testing by the Florida Department of Agriculture & Consumer Services, which identified Salmonella in a retail sample. Consumers who have purchased the affected products should not consume them and are encouraged to contact Vanguard Enterprises for a full refund.

headline: New Hoque & Sons Inc Issues Alert on Uneviscerated “Dry Ghoinnya Fish”

image.caption: New Hoque & Sons Inc Issues Alert on Uneviscerated “Dry Ghoinnya Fish”

description: Hoque announced a recall involving Dry Ghoinnya Fish after issues linked to product was found to be uneviscerated. uneviscerated fish have been linked to...
keywords: Product was found to be uneviscerated. Uneviscerated fish have been linked to outbreaks of botulism poisoning.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
New Hoque & Sons Inc. of Maspeth, NY, has issued a recall for its “Dry Ghoinnya Fish” due to the product being uneviscerated. The recall affects packages distributed nationwide through retail stores. Uneviscerated fish pose a health risk as they may contain Clostridium botulinum spores concentrated in the viscera, which have been linked to outbreaks of botulism poisoning.

The affected product is packaged in clear plastic bags weighing 10 to 12 pounds and features an expiration date of 5/19/25 stamped on the bottom. The product UPC code is 908172635412. The recall follows routine sampling and testing conducted by the New York State Department of Agriculture and Markets Food Inspectors and Food Laboratory staff, which identified improper evisceration prior to processing.

Symptoms of botulism poisoning can include dizziness, blurred or double vision, difficulty speaking or swallowing, breathing difficulties, muscle weakness, abdominal bloating, and constipation. Individuals experiencing any of these symptoms should seek immediate medical attention. To date, no illnesses have been reported in relation to this recall.

Consumers who have purchased the recalled “Dry Ghoinnya Fish” are advised to return the product to the place of purchase for a full refund. For further information or questions, customers can contact New Hoque & Sons Inc. at (718) 391-0992.

Product Package Weight Expiration Date UPC Code
Dry Ghoinnya Fish (uneviscerated) 10-12 pounds 5/19/25 908172635412

  • If experiencing symptoms of botulism, seek immediate medical attention.

headline: Teasdale Latin Foods Issues Allergy Alert on Potential Undeclared Milk in Certain Taco Dinner Kits

image.caption: Teasdale Latin Foods Issues Allergy Alert on Potential Undeclared Milk in Certain Taco Dinner Kits

description: Martin’s and Giant/Casa Mamita announced a recall involving Taco Dinner Kits after issues linked to may contain undeclared milk.. FDA recall notice...

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Teasdale Foods, Inc. has issued a voluntary recall of certain Taco Dinner Kits due to the potential presence of undeclared milk. This allergy alert is important for individuals with milk allergies or severe sensitivities, as consuming the affected products could trigger serious or life-threatening allergic reactions.

The recall involves specific lots of Martin’s and Giant Crunchy Taco Dinner Kits as well as Casa Mamita Soft Taco Dinner Kits. The issue arose after consumer complaints indicated that cocoa mix packets containing milk were mistakenly included instead of taco seasoning packets. This mislabeling by a third-party seasoning supplier led to milk being present in products without proper ingredient disclosure on the packaging.

The affected products were distributed through Giant, Martin’s, and Aldi stores across multiple states including Alabama, Connecticut, Florida, Illinois, New York, Ohio, Pennsylvania, and others. No illnesses have been reported to date in connection with these items. Consumers who have purchased any of the recalled products are advised to return them to the retailer for a full refund and to avoid consumption.

For questions, consumers may contact Teasdale Foods at teasdalecomplaints@teasdalefoods.com.

headline: Hampton Farms Recalls Mixed Nuts Roasted Unsalted 8 oz and Mixed Nuts Roasted and Salted 8 oz Packages Due to Undeclared Hazelnuts

image.caption: Hampton Farms Recalls Mixed Nuts Roasted Unsalted 8 oz and Mixed Nuts Roasted and Salted 8 oz Packages Due to Undeclared Hazelnuts

description: Hampton Farms announced a recall involving Mixed Nuts after issues linked to undeclared hazelnut allergen. FDA recall notice published in 2025.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Hampton Farms has issued a recall for its Mixed Nuts Roasted Unsalted 8 oz and Mixed Nuts Roasted and Salted 8 oz packages due to an undeclared hazelnut allergen. Although hazelnuts are listed in the ingredient statement, they were not included in the allergen warning label, posing a risk to individuals with hazelnut allergies or severe sensitivities. Consumption of these products may result in serious or life-threatening allergic reactions.

The issue was identified on September 15, 2025, during a routine paperwork review, when the labeling omission was discovered. To date, no illnesses related to this recall have been reported. The affected products were distributed between September 2 and September 9, 2025, and sold in Stop&Shop stores across Massachusetts, Connecticut, New Jersey, New York, and Rhode Island.

Consumers who have purchased these items are advised to return them to the place of purchase for a full refund or dispose of the products. For further inquiries, Hampton Farms Customer Service can be reached at 1-800-313-2748, Monday through Friday, 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. EST.

This recall is conducted in cooperation with the U.S. Food and Drug Administration.

headline: Pacific International Marketing Recalls Fresh Italian Parsley Because of Possible Health Risk

image.caption: Pacific International Marketing Recalls Fresh Italian Parsley Because of Possible Health Risk

description: Pacific announced a recall involving Italian Parsley after issues linked to potential foodborne illness – salmonella contamination. FDA recall notice...

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Pacific International Marketing has issued a recall of 474 cases of bulk Italian Parsley due to potential contamination with Salmonella. This bacteria can cause serious infections, particularly in young children, elderly individuals, and those with weakened immune systems. Symptoms of Salmonella infection include fever, diarrhea (which may be bloody), nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain. In rare cases, the infection can become severe and lead to complications such as arterial infections, endocarditis, or arthritis.

The affected Italian Parsley was distributed to wholesalers in Arizona, California, Florida, Minnesota, Michigan, Georgia, Ohio, and Nevada between September 22 and September 25, 2025. The product was sold in bulk cases containing 30 or 60 bunches with a twist tie, and in 24 count bunches in bags, all intended for wholesale distribution. The shelf life of the parsley is 18 days from harvest, with the expiry date listed as October 10, 2025.

Pacific International Marketing was alerted to the issue after a sample taken on October 6 tested positive for Salmonella. Although the product should no longer be available directly to consumers unless frozen, anyone who has this product is advised to discard it or return it to the place of purchase for a full refund. No illnesses related to this recall have been reported so far.

Consumers or businesses with questions about the recall can contact Pacific International Marketing by phone, mail, or email. The company is cooperating with the FDA during the ongoing investigation and is conducting its own internal review.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Purity Products has announced a recall of its dietary supplement, MyBladder, due to possible contamination with Escherichia coli strains O7:K1 and 1303. This contamination may pose health risks, particularly for vulnerable groups such as neonates, young children, older adults, and people with weakened immune systems. The recall is a precautionary measure to ensure consumer safety.

The affected product was distributed in the United States via direct consumer delivery, Walmart, and Amazon websites. The supplement is packaged in a white HDPE 150 CC bottle with a white cap, containing 60 clear capsules of brown powder. The specific lot under recall is number 030425176.

Consumers who have purchased this lot are advised to immediately stop using the product. They should return it to the place of purchase for a full refund or discard it. Anyone experiencing unexpected side effects after using the supplement should contact their healthcare provider.

Purity Products has attributed the contamination to a temporary change in suppliers. For further information or assistance, consumers may contact Richard Conant at Purity Products.

headline: Twin Sisters Creamery Recalls Whatcom Blue, Farmhouse, Peppercorn, and Mustard Seed Cheese Products Because of Possible Health Risk

image.caption: Twin Sisters Creamery Recalls Whatcom Blue, Farmhouse, Peppercorn, and Mustard Seed Cheese Products Because of Possible Health Risk

description: Twin Sisters Creamery announced a recall involving Whatcom Blue, Farmhouse, Peppercorn and Mustard Seed cheese products after issues linked to potential...
keywords: Whatcom Blue, Farmhouse, Peppercorn and Mustard Seed cheese products
Potential Foodborne Illness - Shiga toxin-producing Escherichia coli (STEC) and Escherichia coli O103

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Twin Sisters Creamery, Inc., based in Ferndale, Washington, is voluntarily recalling several cheese products due to potential contamination with Shiga toxin-producing Escherichia coli (STEC) and Escherichia coli O103. The recalled cheeses include Whatcom Blue, Farmhouse, Peppercorn, and Mustard Seed varieties made from raw and unpasteurized milk and aged for at least 60 days.

These bacterial contaminants can cause severe health risks, especially in young children, elderly individuals, and those with weakened immune systems. Infection symptoms include severe abdominal cramps, bloody diarrhea, and vomiting, typically appearing 1 to 10 days after exposure. In some cases, the infection may lead to Hemolytic Uremic Syndrome (HUS), a life-threatening condition causing kidney failure.

The recalled products were shipped between July 27, 2025, and October 22, 2025, to distributors in Oregon and Washington. They may have been further distributed to retail outlets and repackaged or sold as pre-cut half-moon slices with different lot numbers or expiration dates. Three cases of STEC infections linked to these products have been reported so far, including one in Oregon involving the Farmhouse cheese.

Consumers who have purchased these cheeses should not eat them. Instead, discard the products safely and thoroughly clean and sanitize any surfaces they have contacted. Anyone experiencing illness after consuming these products should seek medical advice. For refunds or questions, consumers can contact Twin Sisters Creamery at 360-656-5240, Tuesday through Saturday, 11:00 AM to 5:00 PM Pacific Time.

headline: Peterson Company Recalls Twin Sisters Creamery Brand Whatcom Blue and Farmhouse Cheese Products Because of Possible Health Risk

image.caption: Peterson Company Recalls Twin Sisters Creamery Brand Whatcom Blue and Farmhouse Cheese Products Because of Possible Health Risk

description: Twin Sisters Creamery announced a recall involving Whatcom Blue and Farmhouse cheese products after issues linked to potential foodborne illness - shiga...
keywords: Whatcom Blue and Farmhouse cheese products
Potential Foodborne Illness - Shiga toxin-producing Escherichia coli (STEC) and Escherichia coli O103

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
On October 26, 2025, Peterson Company of Auburn, Washington, announced a voluntary recall of Twin Sisters Creamery brand Whatcom Blue and Farmhouse cheese products due to potential contamination with Shiga toxin-producing Escherichia coli (STEC) and Escherichia coli O103. These cheeses, made from raw milk, pose a risk of serious foodborne illness.

STEC infections can cause severe and sometimes fatal complications, particularly in young children, the elderly, and individuals with weakened immune systems. Infection symptoms include severe abdominal cramps, bloody diarrhea, and vomiting, typically appearing 1 to 10 days after exposure. E. coli O103 infection may lead to Hemolytic Uremic Syndrome (HUS), a life-threatening condition that can result in kidney failure.

The affected Whatcom Blue (item #28855) and Farmhouse (item #29608) cheeses were distributed to retailers, distributors, caterers, and restaurants in Colorado, Idaho, Oregon, and Washington between August 14, 2025, and October 24, 2025. These products are approximately 5 to 6 ounces in weight, half-moon shaped, and packaged in clear plastic wrap. They are identified by specific manufacturer codes ink jet printed or attached as small tan-to-orange stickers on each piece.

Three confirmed STEC infections linked to E. coli O103 have been reported in Oregon and Washington, with one Oregon case associated with consumption of the Farmhouse cheese. The recall follows supplier Twin Sisters Creamery’s notification after third-party lab testing and government agency analyses confirmed the presence of these bacteria in the affected cheeses. Consumers are advised not to consume these products, to discard any remaining cheese, and to thoroughly clean any surfaces that the product contacted. Those concerned about illness should seek medical advice.

Consumers who purchased the recalled cheeses should return them to their place of purchase for a full refund. Questions can be directed to Twin Sisters Creamery at 360-656-5240 (Tuesday-Saturday, 11am-5pm PT) or Peterson Company at (800) 735-0313 ext. 2101.

headline: E.A. Sween Company Announces Product Recall Due to Choking Hazard

image.caption: E.A. Sween Company Announces Product Recall Due to Choking Hazard

description: Deli Express announced a recall involving BBQ Pulled Pork Sandwich after issues linked to due to the potential presence of foreign particles (plastic).. FDA...
keywords: Due to the potential presence of foreign particles (plastic).

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
E.A. Sween Company has announced a voluntary recall of its Deli Express® BBQ Pulled Pork sandwich due to the potential presence of plastic foreign particles in the product. These particles could pose a choking hazard if ingested. The affected products were distributed to convenience stores, grocery stores, and mass retail outlets across the Continental U.S., Hawaii, and Guam.

The recall applies to specific lot codes of the Deli Express® BBQ Pulled Pork sandwich, with a UPC of 0-41433-13087-2. The products were available for purchase between January 16 and October 23, 2025. Although there have been no reported injuries or illnesses related to this issue, consumers are strongly advised not to eat the product if they have purchased any from the affected lots.

Retailers have been directed to remove the recalled products from shelves and inventory immediately. Consumers who have the affected product may return it to the place of purchase for a full refund. headline: Haitai, Inc. Recalls Haetae (HT) Brand Cinnamon Powder 8 oz of Possible Risk

image.caption: Haitai, Inc. Recalls Haetae (HT) Brand Cinnamon Powder 8 oz of Possible Risk

description: Haetae announced a recall involving Ground cinnamon after issues linked to potential metal contaminant - lead. FDA recall notice published in 2025.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Haitai, Inc. of Cerritos, CA, announced a recall on October 15, 2025, for the Haetae (HT) brand Cinnamon powder 8 oz due to potential contamination with lead. Although short-term exposure to low levels of lead may not produce immediate symptoms, increased blood lead levels can be the sole indicator of exposure. High lead intake can cause severe symptoms, prompting medical attention.

Lead exposure poses significant health risks, especially to children, where prolonged exposure may lead to permanent central nervous system damage, including learning disorders and developmental defects. Adults experiencing acute lead poisoning may suffer from abdominal pain, muscle weakness, nausea, and other symptoms. Chronic exposure is linked to kidney problems, hypertension, and neurocognitive effects.

The affected product was distributed nationwide through various supermarkets. It is packaged in a square plastic bottle with a medium brown color and orange-red undertones. The recall covers the product with the UPC 0 20914 81415 9 and a Best By date of 02/09/25. No injuries or illnesses related to the contamination have been reported so far.

The recall was initiated after the FDA detected elevated lead levels in product samples. The source of contamination may be adulterated raw materials or the natural presence of lead in cinnamon. Consumers who have purchased this cinnamon powder are advised to return it for a full refund and consult a healthcare provider if concerned about lead exposure.

Brand Product Package Size UPC Best By Date
Haetae (HT) Cinnamon Powder 8 oz 0 20914 81415 9 02/09/25

Consumers with questions can contact Haitai, Inc. at 1-323-890-0101 from 8:00 AM to 5:00 PM Pacific time, Monday through Friday.

For questions or support, contact Haitai, Inc directly at (323) 890-0101. No specific instructions for returns or replacements have been provided.

headline: Nat’s Nuts Issues Allergy Alert on Potential Undeclared Cashews in Nat’s Nuts Brand Cinnamon Whiskey Pecans

image.caption: Nat’s Nuts Issues Allergy Alert on Potential Undeclared Cashews in Nat’s Nuts Brand Cinnamon Whiskey Pecans

description: Nat’s Nuts announced a recall involving Cinnamon Whiskey Pecans after issues linked to undeclared cashews. FDA recall notice published in 2025.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Nat’s Nuts, a Rochester, NY-based company, has issued a recall for their Cinnamon Whiskey Pecans due to the potential presence of undeclared cashews. This recall is important for individuals allergic or highly sensitive to cashews, as consuming the affected product may lead to serious or life-threatening allergic reactions.

The recall involves approximately 600 bags of Nat’s Nuts brand Cinnamon Whiskey Pecans, 4 oz., which were distributed to 25 retail stores across Connecticut, New York, New Jersey, New Hampshire, Pennsylvania, Ohio, Nevada, Florida, and Wisconsin between October 8 and October 16, 2025. Additionally, the product was sold online through Amazon and NatsNuts.com from October 9 to October 16, 2025.

The issue was identified after a retailer discovered cashews in a package of Cinnamon Whiskey Pecans during sampling. Investigations revealed this contamination resulted from a temporary failure in the company’s production and packaging processes. Although no illnesses have been reported so far, consumers who purchased the affected product are advised to return it to the place of purchase for a full refund.

For further information or questions, consumers can contact Nat’s Nuts at 585-310-0157, Monday through Friday, from 8 AM to 5 PM Eastern Time. Retailers have been instructed to remove the recalled product from their shelves. Additional details about the recall are available on the company’s website.

Product Size UPC Lot Code Best By Date
Nat’s Nuts Cinnamon Whiskey Pecans 4 oz. 860002146720 1.22 9/29/2026
  • Do not consume the recalled product if you have a cashew allergy or sensitivity.
  • Return the product to the place of purchase for a full refund.
  • Contact Nat’s Nuts at 585-310-0157 for questions (Mon-Fri, 8 AM–5 PM ET).
  • Visit www.natsnuts.com/pages/food-safety for more information.

headline: Kenz Henz Recalls "Pastured Raised Eggs" Because of Possible Health Risk
description: Kenz Henz announced a recall involving Grade AA Large Pasture Raised Eggs after issues linked to potential to be contaminated with salmonella. FDA recall...
keywords: Grade AA Large Pasture Raised Eggs
Potential to be contaminated with Salmonella

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Kenz Henz, based in Santa Fe, TX, has issued a recall of its 12 count packages of "Grade AA Large Pasture Raised Eggs" due to the potential presence of Salmonella contamination. Salmonella is a harmful bacterium that can cause severe infections, particularly in young children, older adults, and individuals with weakened immune systems. Symptoms of Salmonella infection in healthy individuals typically include fever, diarrhea (sometimes bloody), nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain.

The recalled eggs were sold in retail stores in Houston, TX. Although no illnesses have been reported linked to this recall, Salmonella infections can occasionally lead to more serious conditions such as bloodstream infections, arterial infections, endocarditis, or arthritis.

Recent FDA inspections of the Black Sheep Egg Company’s processing facility found multiple environmental samples positive for various strains of Salmonella, some of which are known to cause human illness. However, the FDA has not identified this facility as the source of any ongoing outbreak at this time.

Consumers who have purchased Kenz Henz Grade AA Large Pasture Raised Eggs in 12 count cartons matching the affected products should return them to the place of purchase for a full refund. For questions, the company can be reached at 1-(409)457-5934 Monday through Friday, from 9:00 am to 4:00 pm.

headline: Aquastar (USA) Corp Recalls Shrimp Because of Possible Health Risk

image.caption: Aquastar (USA) Corp Recalls Shrimp Because of Possible Health Risk

description: AquaStar, Best Yet, Waterfront Bistro, Publix announced a recall involving Frozen Raw Shrimp after issues linked to product may have been prepared, packed,...
keywords: AquaStar, Best Yet, Waterfront Bistro, Publix
Product may have been prepared, packed, or held under conditions whereby they may have become exposed to very low levels of cesium-137 (Cs-137)

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
On October 17, 2025, AquaStar (USA) Corp, based in Seattle, WA, announced a voluntary recall of a limited quantity of frozen raw shrimp imported from Indonesia. The recall was initiated because the products may have been prepared, packed, or held under conditions where they could have been exposed to very low levels of cesium-137 (Cs-137), a man-made radioisotope of cesium.

It is important to note that none of the recalled products have tested positive for Cs-137, and no illnesses have been reported to date. The recall is a precautionary measure to ensure consumer safety and is being conducted in cooperation with the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA).

Consumers who have purchased the affected frozen shrimp are advised not to consume the products. They should either dispose of them safely or return them to the place of purchase for a full refund. Cs-137 can be found in the environment at background levels and may be higher in certain areas with environmental contamination, but the risk is considered very low in this case.

For any questions or concerns, consumers can contact AquaStar directly at 1-800-331-3440.

description: Ben’s Original announced a recall involving Long Grain White, Whole Grain Brown, and Long Grain & Wild Ready Rice after issues linked to potential foreign...

Potential Foreign Body Contaminant – Small Stones

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Ben’s Original has announced a voluntary recall of select Ready Rice products in the United States due to the possible presence of small stones naturally occurring from the rice farm. The affected products include certain lots of Long Grain White, Whole Grain Brown, and Long Grain & Wild Ready Rice. These small stones could pose a risk of oral or digestive tract injury if consumed.

The recall applies only to specific batch codes and best by dates, which are printed on the bottom seal of the back of the package. While the distribution involved multiple retailers through distributors Associated Grocers, C&S, and Dot Foods during August through September, this issue is isolated to the batches listed below. No other Ben’s Original products are affected by this recall.

To date, there have been no reported injuries or illnesses related to the recalled products. Consumers who have purchased the affected items are advised not to consume them and to contact Ben’s Original Consumer Care for return instructions or any questions. Consumer Care can be reached at 1-800-548-6253, Monday through Friday from 8 a.m. to 8 p.m. CST, and weekends from 8 a.m. to 4 p.m. CST.

headline: CORRECTION: Foodynamics Recalls Raw Dog Barkery, BellePepper Cats, and Kanu Pets Brand Freeze-Dried Pet Treats Because Of Possible Salmonella Contamination

image.caption: CORRECTION: Foodynamics Recalls Raw Dog Barkery, BellePepper Cats, and Kanu Pets Brand Freeze-Dried Pet Treats Because Of Possible Salmonella Contamination

description: Foodynamics announced a recall involving Freeze Dried Pet Treats after issues linked to potential foodborne illness – salmonella. FDA recall notice...
keywords: Animal & Veterinary Food & Beverages Foodborne Illness

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Foodynamics of Wales, Wisconsin, has issued a voluntary recall of specific lots of freeze-dried pet treats under the Raw Dog Barkery, BellePepper Cats, Kanu Pets, and What’s In the Bowl brands due to potential contamination with Salmonella. This contamination poses a health risk to both pets consuming the products and humans handling them, especially if proper hand hygiene is not maintained after contact.

Salmonella infection in healthy individuals can cause symptoms such as nausea, vomiting, diarrhea (which may be bloody), abdominal cramps, and fever. More severe complications are rare but can include arterial infections, endocarditis, arthritis, muscle pain, eye irritation, and urinary tract issues. Pets infected with Salmonella may exhibit lethargy, diarrhea or bloody diarrhea, fever, vomiting, decreased appetite, and abdominal pain. Infected pets can also be asymptomatic carriers, posing a risk of transmitting the bacteria to other animals and humans. Anyone experiencing symptoms following contact with these products should consult a healthcare professional, and pet owners should contact their veterinarian if their pets show symptoms after consuming the recalled treats.

The recalled products were shipped via UPS from Wisconsin to retailers in Wisconsin, New York, and Florida. A total of eight packages distributed to consumers have been identified. Foodynamics has traced all product distribution, informed all direct customers, and is cooperating with the FDA in the ongoing investigation. No illnesses have been reported to date. Production and distribution of the products have been suspended as a precaution.

Consumers who have purchased these products should stop feeding them to pets immediately and return the items to the place of purchase for a full refund. It is important to dispose of any recalled products securely to prevent access by children, pets, or wildlife. Consumers and retailers should also thoroughly wash and sanitize any pet food bowls, utensils, and surfaces that have come into contact with the recalled products. For more information, questions can be directed to Teresa Perry at info@foodynamics.com or by calling (262) 421-5339 Monday through Friday, 9 AM to 5 PM CST.

Brand Product Description Net Weight Use By Date / Lot #
Raw Dog Barkery Whole Chicken Hearts 16 oz. (1 lb.) 030527
What’s In the Bowl Whole Chicken Hearts 3 oz., 16 oz. (1 lb.) 030527
BellePepper Cats Freeze Dried Chicken Heart Slices 3 oz. 031627
BellePepper Cats Freeze Dried Sample Treats (Unpackaged & Unlabeled) 0.1 oz. 121426, 011526
Kanu Pets Freeze Dried Chicken Heart Raw Treats 3 oz. 031627
  • Stop feeding the recalled treats to pets immediately.
  • Return any affected products to the retailer for a full refund.
  • Dispose of recalled products safely to prevent access by children, pets, or wildlife.
  • Wash and sanitize pet food bowls, cups, storage containers, and all surfaces and utensils that contacted the recalled products.
  • Wash hands thoroughly after handling any recalled pet treats or packaging.
  • Monitor pets and humans for symptoms of Salmonella infection and contact healthcare providers or veterinarians if symptoms arise.
  • Contact Foodynamics with questions at info@foodynamics.com or (262) 421-5339, Monday-Friday, 9 AM–5 PM CST.

headline: Tai Foong USA Issues Allergy Alert on Undeclared Shrimp in Fusia Asian Inspirations Veggie Spring Rolls

image.caption: Tai Foong USA Issues Allergy Alert on Undeclared Shrimp in Fusia Asian Inspirations Veggie Spring Rolls

description: Fusia Asian Inspirations announced a recall involving Veggie Spring Rolls after issues linked to undeclared shrimp allergen. FDA recall notice published in...

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Tai Foong USA of Seattle, Washington, has announced a recall of a limited quantity of Fusia Asian Inspirations Veggie Spring Rolls, net weight 10 oz. The product may contain undeclared shrimp, a known allergen, which poses a risk of a serious or life-threatening allergic reaction to individuals with shrimp or shellfish allergies.

The affected Veggie Spring Rolls were distributed exclusively to ALDI stores nationwide under the Fusia Asian Inspirations brand. The recall involves only the 10-ounce retail package with UPC 4099100222258 and the Best Before date of 05/17/2027 marked on the back panel. No other products from the Fusia Asian Inspirations line are included in this recall.

This action was initiated after it was discovered that certain cases of Shrimp Spring Rolls may have been mistakenly packaged in boxes labeled as Vegetable Spring Rolls. Tai Foong USA is investigating the source of this labeling error. The recall is being conducted in cooperation with ALDI and the U.S. Food and Drug Administration. To date, there have been no confirmed reports of allergic reactions or illnesses related to this issue.

Consumers who have purchased the affected product are advised not to consume it. Instead, they should discard the item or return it to the place of purchase for a full refund. For further questions, Tai Foong USA can be contacted at (206) 883-2317, Monday through Friday, 8:00 a.m. to 5:00 p.m. Pacific Time, or by email at bcox@northernchef.com.

headline: Durra Ground Cinnamon 100 G Because of Possible Health Risk

image.caption: Durra Ground Cinnamon 100 G Because of Possible Health Risk

description: Durra announced a recall involving Ground cinnamon after issues linked to potential metal contaminant - lead. FDA recall notice published in 2025.
keywords: Food & Beverages Spices, Flavors & Salts Contaminants

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Eureka Inc of Pomona, CA, announced a recall on October 6, 2025, for Durra Ground Cinnamon 100 gram plastic containers due to potential contamination with lead. Lead exposure can have serious health implications, particularly for children and individuals with prolonged or high-level exposure.

Short-term exposure to low levels of lead may not show symptoms, but increased blood lead levels can be an indicator. High levels of lead exposure can cause severe symptoms such as abdominal pain, muscle weakness, nausea, vomiting, and neurological effects. Chronic exposure may lead to kidney dysfunction, hypertension, and permanent damage to the central nervous system, especially in children. No illnesses have been reported to date in connection with this product.

The affected product was distributed to grocery stores in California and Michigan between August 24, 2024, and October 6, 2025. The recall follows the detection of elevated lead levels during FDA testing of the product. Consumers are advised to return the product to the place of purchase for a full refund and consult their healthcare providers if they have consumed the recalled cinnamon.

For further information, customers can contact Eureka Inc at 1-951-444-7779, available Monday through Friday from 10 am to 5 pm PST. This recall is being conducted with the knowledge of the U.S. Food and Drug Administration.

Brand Product Package Size Lot Code Best By Date UPC Distribution Area Distribution Dates
Durra Ground Cinnamon 100 g Clear Plastic Container Batch No.: 06 B:02 May 2026 6251136 034139 California, Michigan 08/24/2024 – 10/6/2025
  • Consumers who have purchased the product should return it to the place of purchase for a full refund.
  • If you have consumed the product, consider consulting a healthcare provider about lead exposure testing.
  • Contact Eureka Inc at 1-951-444-7779 from 10 am to 5 pm PST, Monday to Friday, for questions or additional information.
  • Report any adverse reactions or problems experienced with the product to the FDA MedWatch program via the MedWatch website.

headline: Trividia Health, Inc. Initiates Voluntary Recall of Limited Number of TRUE METRIX® Blood Glucose Meters
description: TRUE METRIX announced a recall involving Blood Glucose Meters after issues linked to potential for defective lcd displays that can affect product...
keywords: Potential for defective LCD displays that can affect product performance

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Trividia Health, Inc. has announced a voluntary recall of a limited number of TRUE METRIX® self-monitoring blood glucose meters co-branded under the Good Neighbor Pharmacy® brand and distributed in the United States. The recall applies to 601 meters from lot number KD0746, manufactured on September 4, 2025, and distributed between September 8 and September 16, 2025. These units may have defective LCD displays that could affect product performance.

The issue with the LCD display may cause partial or missing numerical segments or characters, or ghosting (fading) of these characters. This defect could potentially lead users to misinterpret test results or experience delays in obtaining results. For individuals with low blood glucose (hypoglycemia), such delays could impact timely treatment or therapy decisions. Trividia Health reports no injuries related to the recall at this time.

Consumers who believe they have an affected TRUE METRIX® meter can confirm by checking the serial number on the back of the device or the lot number (KD0746) printed on the side of the product box. Assistance is available through the Trividia Health Customer Care Department, which can help verify affected products and provide return and replacement support.

Trividia Health is notifying customers, pharmacies, distributors, and mail order companies that sell these meters in the U.S. Consumers may continue using TRUE METRIX® blood glucose meters from other lots not included in this recall. This action is being taken in coordination with the U.S. Food and Drug Administration to ensure user safety.

Product Lot Number Manufacture Date Distribution Dates Quantity
TRUE METRIX® Self-Monitoring Blood Glucose Meter (Co-branded Good Neighbor Pharmacy®) KD0746 September 4, 2025 September 8, 2025 – September 16, 2025 601 units

If you suspect you have an affected blood glucose meter, Trividia Health recommends the following steps for confirmation and assistance:

  • Call the Trividia Health Customer Care Department toll-free at 1-888-835-2723, Monday through Friday, 8 AM to 8 PM EST (excluding holidays), for help checking your meter’s serial number.
  • Email trividia0925CC@trividiahealth.com for guidance on locating and verifying your meter’s serial number.
  • Check the lot number (KD0746) printed on the side of the product box.
  • Use the online tool at www.trividiahealth.com/productnotice for a quick serial number search.

headline: Giant Eagle Recalls Smoked Mozzarella Pasta Salad Due to Potential Listeria Monocytogenes Contamination Associated with Nationwide Recall from Nate’s Fine Foods

image.caption: Giant Eagle Recalls Smoked Mozzarella Pasta Salad Due to Potential Listeria Monocytogenes Contamination Associated with Nationwide Recall from Nate’s Fine Foods

description: Giant Eagle announced a recall involving Smoked Mozzarella Pasta Salad after issues linked to potential listeria monocytogenes contamination. FDA recall...

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Giant Eagle, Inc. has announced a voluntary recall of its Smoked Mozzarella Pasta Salad sold in the prepared foods departments at Giant Eagle and Market District stores located in western Pennsylvania, Ohio, Maryland, and Indiana. The recall is due to potential contamination with Listeria monocytogenes and follows a nationwide recall of ready-to-eat pasta products from Nate’s Fine Foods.

Listeria monocytogenes can cause severe and sometimes fatal infections, particularly in young children, elderly individuals, pregnant women, and those with weakened immune systems. While healthy individuals may experience only mild and short-term symptoms such as fever, headache, nausea, abdominal pain, and diarrhea, pregnant women face additional risks including miscarriage and stillbirth. To date, there have been no reported illnesses linked to the Giant Eagle recall.

The recalled Smoked Mozzarella Pasta Salad was sold beginning September 25, 2025. Customers are advised not to consume the product if they have purchased it. Instead, they should dispose of the salad and can return it with a qualifying receipt to any Giant Eagle or Market District store for a full refund.

For questions or more information, consumers may contact Giant Eagle Customer Care at 1-800-553-2324, available Monday through Friday from 8 a.m. to 8 p.m. EST. Giant Eagle has also initiated a consumer notification process using purchase data from its myPerks loyalty program to directly alert affected customers with updated contact information.

headline: Best Buy Bones, Inc. Recalls Nature’s Own Pet Chews Bully Bites Because of Possible Salmonella Health Risk

image.caption: Best Buy Bones, Inc. Recalls Nature’s Own Pet Chews Bully Bites Because of Possible Salmonella Health Risk

description: Nature’s Own announced a recall involving Pet Chews Bully Bites Treats after issues linked to potential foodborne illness - salmonella. FDA recall notice...

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Best Buy Bones, Inc., based in Mount Morris, Michigan, has initiated a recall of four lots of Nature’s Own Pet Chews Bully Bites 1 Pound pet treats due to potential contamination with Salmonella. The affected product comes in 16 oz. resealable bags with an expiration date of September 2027. Salmonella poses a health risk to both animals consuming the treats and humans handling them, particularly if proper handwashing is not observed after contact.

Salmonella infection in healthy individuals can cause symptoms such as nausea, vomiting, diarrhea or bloody diarrhea, abdominal cramps, and fever. In rare cases, more severe illness may occur, including arterial infections, endocarditis, arthritis, muscle pain, eye irritation, and urinary tract symptoms. Those experiencing symptoms after handling the product should seek medical advice promptly.

Pets affected by Salmonella may show lethargy, diarrhea or bloody diarrhea, fever, vomiting, decreased appetite, and abdominal pain. Even asymptomatic pets can carry and transmit the bacteria to other animals or humans. Veterinarians should be contacted if pets develop these symptoms after consuming the product.

The recall involves 34 bags distributed primarily in Minnesota and sold through retail locations in Wisconsin after September 23, 2025. The investigation began after FDA testing of product samples on September 11, 2025, confirmed the presence of Salmonella. The company has halted production and distribution of the affected lots while the FDA and Best Buy Bones continue their investigation.

Product Lot Number Expiration Date UPC Package Size
Nature’s Own Pet Chews Bully Bites 19379, 19380, 19381, 19382 September 2027 739598900750 16 oz. resealable pouch

Consumers who purchased these products after September 23, 2025, should take the following actions:

  • Return the product to the place of purchase or dispose of it safely to prevent access by children, pets, and wildlife.
  • Do not sell, donate, or feed the recalled treats to pets or other animals.
  • Thoroughly wash and sanitize pet food bowls, cups, and storage containers.
  • Always wash and sanitize hands after handling the recalled product or any items that may have come into contact with it.

For additional information, customers may contact Best Buy Bones, Inc. at (810) 687-2106, Monday through Thursday from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. EST.

headline: Demers Food Group Voluntarily Recalls Select Scott & Jon's Shrimp Scampi with Linguini Bowls Due to an Ingredient Recall Initiated by Nate’s Fine Foods for Possible Listeria Monocytogenes Contamination

image.caption: Demers Food Group Voluntarily Recalls Select Scott & Jon's Shrimp Scampi with Linguini Bowls Due to an Ingredient Recall Initiated by Nate’s Fine Foods for Possible Listeria Monocytogenes Contamination

description: Scott & Jon's announced a recall involving Shrimp Scampi with Linguini after issues linked to potential listeria monocytogenes contamination.. FDA recall...

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Demers Food Group has voluntarily recalled select Scott & Jon's Shrimp Scampi with Linguini bowls due to a potential contamination with Listeria monocytogenes. This action follows a recall initiated by Nate’s Fine Foods, the supplier of the linguini pasta used in the product, which was found to be contaminated. The recall applies only to specific Scott & Jon's Shrimp Scampi with Linguini products and does not affect other products under the Scott & Jon's brand.

No illnesses have been reported in connection with this recall. However, consumers who have purchased the affected items are advised not to consume them. Instead, they should either dispose of the products or return them to the store where purchased for a full refund. The product was distributed nationwide to wholesale distributors and operators between September 15, 2025, and September 25, 2025.

Listeria monocytogenes can survive refrigerated temperatures and may spread to other foods and surfaces. The FDA recommends thorough cleaning and sanitizing of any surfaces and containers that might have come into contact with the recalled products to prevent cross-contamination. Listeria infection can cause serious health issues, especially for young children, elderly individuals, pregnant women, and those with weakened immune systems. Symptoms may include fever, headache, stiffness, nausea, abdominal pain, and diarrhea. Pregnant women are at risk for miscarriage or stillbirth.

Consumers seeking additional information or assistance can contact Demers Food Group’s Customer Service at 1-800-239-9731, available Monday through Friday from 8 a.m. to 4 p.m. EST.

headline: UPDATE - Albertsons Companies Voluntarily Recalls Select Store-Made Deli Items Containing Bowtie Pasta Supplied by Fresh Creative Foods Due to an Ingredient Recall for Possible Listeria monocytogenes Contamination

image.caption: UPDATE - Albertsons Companies Voluntarily Recalls Select Store-Made Deli Items Containing Bowtie Pasta Supplied by Fresh Creative Foods Due to an Ingredient Recall for Possible Listeria monocytogenes Contamination

description: Multiple brand names announced a recall involving Bowtie Pasta Salads and Meals after issues linked to possible listeria monocytogenes contamination. FDA...

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Albertsons Companies has voluntarily recalled certain store-made deli items containing bowtie pasta supplied by Fresh Creative Foods, following a recall issued by Fresh Creative Foods, a division of Reser’s Fine Foods, Inc. The recall affects products made with a bowtie pasta ingredient produced by Nate’s Fine Foods in Roseville, California, due to possible contamination with Listeria monocytogenes.

Listeria monocytogenes is a harmful bacterium that can cause serious and sometimes fatal infections, particularly in young children, elderly individuals, pregnant women, and those with weakened immune systems. Symptoms of listeriosis include high fever, severe headache, stiffness, nausea, abdominal pain, and diarrhea. Pregnant women infected with Listeria risk miscarriages and stillbirths. No illnesses or injuries have been reported so far related to the recalled products.

Consumers who have purchased any of the affected deli items are urged not to consume them. Instead, they should either dispose of the products or return them to the store where purchased for a full refund. The FDA advises extra care in thoroughly cleaning and sanitizing any surfaces or containers that may have come into contact with the recalled products to prevent cross-contamination, as Listeria can survive in refrigerated conditions and spread easily.

The affected deli items were available at a range of stores including Albertsons, Safeway, Vons, Pavilions, Andronico's Community Markets, and others across many states including Alaska, Arizona, California, Texas, and more. For questions or concerns, customers can contact Albertsons Companies’ Customer Service Center at 1-877-723-3929, Monday through Friday, from 5 a.m. to 9 p.m. PST.

headline: Sea Port Products Corp is Recalling Raw Frozen Easy Peel White Shrimp Because Product May Have Become Contaminated with Cesium-137 (Cs-137)

image.caption: Sea Port Products Corp is Recalling Raw Frozen Easy Peel White Shrimp Because Product May Have Become Contaminated with Cesium-137 (Cs-137)

description: Sea Port announced a recall involving Raw Frozen Easy Peel White Shrimp Jumbo size 16/20 after issues linked to product may have become contaminated with...
keywords: Raw Frozen Easy Peel White Shrimp Jumbo size 16/20
Product May Have Become Contaminated with Cesium-137 (Cs-137)

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Sea Port Products Corp of Kirkland, WA is voluntarily recalling certain Sea Port brand Frozen Raw Easy Peel White Shrimp Jumbo size 16/20 count per pound. The recall involves limited quantities of bagged shrimp that may have been exposed to insanitary conditions, potentially leading to contamination with Cesium-137 (Cs-137), a man-made radioactive isotope.

Cesium-137 can be found in the environment at low background levels but may be present at elevated levels in contaminated food or water. Prolonged exposure to Cs-137 through intake of contaminated food poses a health risk, primarily by increasing the risk of cancer due to DNA damage within living cells.

The affected shrimp products were distributed in small amounts to retailers located in Alaska, California, Hawaii, Montana, Oregon, Washington State, and American Samoa between July 10, 2025, and September 22, 2025. Consumers are advised to check their freezers for the recalled Sea Port brand shrimp using the specified lot codes printed on the UPC barcode, located on the back of the bag.

No illnesses related to this contamination have been reported to date. The FDA is investigating Cs-137 contamination reports linked to frozen shrimp products from PT. Bahari Makmur Sejati (BMS Foods) in Indonesia. The FDA notes that no products testing positive for Cs-137 have entered U.S. commerce. Consumers who have purchased the affected shrimp should not consume the product and are encouraged to dispose of it or return it to the place of purchase for a full refund. For questions, customers can contact Sea Port Products Corp at 425-896-0186, Monday through Friday, 8:00 a.m. to 5:00 p.m. PST.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
New Age International Inc., located in Brooklyn, NY, has issued a recall for its 200g (7.05 OZ) packaged Signature Enoki Mushrooms due to potential contamination with Listeria monocytogenes. This bacterium can cause serious and potentially fatal infections, especially in vulnerable groups such as young children, the elderly, pregnant women, and individuals with weakened immune systems.

The affected product, distributed to retail stores and distributors in New York City, is packaged in a clear 200g plastic container and is labeled with the code GGN: 4063061794758 on the back. While no illnesses have been reported so far, the contamination was identified following routine testing by the New York State Department of Agriculture and Markets Food Inspectors and confirmed by laboratory analysis.

Consumers who purchased Signature Enoki Mushrooms from August to September 2025 are advised to either destroy the product immediately or return it to the place of purchase for a full refund. The company confirms that no products from this batch remain in their warehouse.

For further information or questions, consumers can contact New Age International Inc. at 718-808-1018.
