headline: Albertsons Companies Voluntarily Recalls Select Store-Made Deli Items Containing Bowtie Pasta Supplied by Fresh Creative Foods Due to an Ingredient Recall for Possible Listeria monocytogenes Contamination

image.caption: Albertsons Companies Voluntarily Recalls Select Store-Made Deli Items Containing Bowtie Pasta Supplied by Fresh Creative Foods Due to an Ingredient Recall for Possible Listeria monocytogenes Contamination

description: Albertsons Companies announced a recall involving Bowtie Pasta Salads and Meals after issues linked to possible listeria monocytogenes contamination. FDA...

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Albertsons Companies has issued a voluntary recall of select store-made deli items containing bowtie pasta supplied by Fresh Creative Foods, a division of Reser’s Fine Foods, Inc. This recall follows Fresh Creative Foods’ recall of an ingredient due to possible contamination with Listeria monocytogenes. The affected bowtie pasta ingredient was manufactured by Nate’s Fine Foods of Roseville, California.

Listeria monocytogenes can cause severe infections, particularly in young children, elderly individuals, pregnant women, and those with weakened immune systems. Symptoms in healthy people may include fever, headache, stiffness, nausea, abdominal pain, and diarrhea. For pregnant women, infection can lead to miscarriage or stillbirth. There have been no reported illnesses linked to these recalled products to date.

Consumers who have purchased these deli items are advised not to consume them. They should either dispose of the products or return them to the place of purchase for a full refund. The U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA) also recommends thorough cleaning and sanitizing of any surfaces or containers that may have come into contact with the recalled products to prevent cross-contamination, as Listeria can survive under refrigerated conditions.

The recalled products were available at Albertsons Companies stores, including Albertsons, Safeway, Vons, Randalls, Tom Thumb, Pavilions, Carrs-Safeway, and other banners, across multiple states including Alaska, Arizona, Arkansas, California, Colorado, Hawaii, Louisiana, Nebraska, Nevada, New Mexico, Oklahoma, South Dakota, Texas, Utah, and Wyoming. For questions, consumers can contact Albertsons Companies’ Customer Service Center at 1-877-723-3929 from 5 a.m. to 9 p.m. PST, Monday through Friday.

headline: Wholesale Produce Supply of Minneapolis, Minnesota is Recalling Fresh Cut/Processed Cantaloupe, Because it has the Potential to be Contaminated With Listeria Monocytogenes

image.caption: Wholesale Produce Supply of Minneapolis, Minnesota is Recalling Fresh Cut/Processed Cantaloupe, Because it has the Potential to be Contaminated With Listeria Monocytogenes

description: Wholesale Produce Supply, LLC. announced a recall involving Fresh Cut Cantaloupe after issues linked to potential to be contaminated with listeria...
keywords: Food & Beverages Fruit/Fruit Product Foodborne Illness
Potential to be contaminated with Listeria monocytogenes

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Wholesale Produce Supply, LLC. of Minneapolis, Minnesota, is recalling fresh cut and processed cantaloupe due to potential contamination with Listeria monocytogenes. This bacterium can cause serious and sometimes fatal infections, especially in young children, elderly individuals, and those with weakened immune systems. Healthy individuals may experience symptoms such as high fever, severe headache, stiffness, nausea, abdominal pain, and diarrhea. Pregnant women infected with Listeria may face miscarriage or stillbirth risks.

The affected fresh cut cantaloupe products were sold under the “Harvest Cuts” and “Fresh and Finest” brands. These items were distributed to locations in Nebraska, North Dakota, and Wisconsin, and may have reached other states through grocery distributors. Products were packaged in traditional plastic clamshell containers and included both cantaloupe-only and mixed fruit containers.

This recall follows a routine sampling program by Wholesale Produce Supply that detected the presence of Listeria monocytogenes in the finished products. To address the issue, the company has suspended production and distribution of the identified lots while investigating the source of contamination. No illnesses have been reported related to these products at this time.

Consumers who purchased the recalled “Harvest Cuts” or “Fresh and Finest” products are advised to return them to the place of purchase for a full refund. For further questions or concerns, Wholesale Produce Supply can be contacted at (612) 378-2025, asking for Steve Foster, Vice President of Food Safety & QA.

Fresh and Finest Fresh Cut Cantaloupe (cantaloupe-only and mixed fruit containers) Nebraska, North Dakota, Wisconsin (and possibly other states) Plastic clamshell containers
  • Check if you have purchased “Harvest Cuts” or “Fresh and Finest” fresh cut cantaloupe products from Nebraska, North Dakota, or Wisconsin distributors.

  • Contact Wholesale Produce Supply at (612) 378-2025 for additional information.

headline: Georgia Nut Company (GNC), Third-Party Manufacturer for Tru Fru, LLC Issues Voluntary Recall of Specific Varieties of Tru Fru Freeze Dried Products Due to Potential Presence of Metal in Product

image.caption: Georgia Nut Company (GNC), Third-Party Manufacturer for Tru Fru, LLC Issues Voluntary Recall of Specific Varieties of Tru Fru Freeze Dried Products Due to Potential Presence of Metal in Product

description: Tru Fru announced a recall involving Freeze Dried Fruit snacks after issues linked to due to potential presence of metal in the product.. FDA recall notice...
keywords: Due to potential presence of metal in the product.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Tru Fru, LLC has announced that its third-party manufacturer, Georgia Nut Company (GNC), is voluntarily recalling specific varieties of Tru Fru Freeze Dried products due to the potential presence of metal fragments. The presence of hard or sharp foreign objects in food poses a risk of traumatic injuries, such as lacerations or perforations of the mouth, tongue, throat, stomach, or intestines, as well as possible damage to teeth and gums.

The affected products were distributed across the United States and may have been sold at retail stores and online platforms, including Albertson’s, CVS, Food Lion, H-E-B, Hungryroot, Ingles Markets, Kroger, Stew Leonard’s, and Target. The recall specifically targets certain lots of Tru Fru Freeze Dried Strawberries in Dark & White Chocolate and Tru Fru Freeze Dried Strawberries & Crème.

There have been no reported injuries or illnesses linked to these products to date. The recall was initiated following a consumer report identifying the potential issue. Details to identify the recall include a 10-digit manufacturing code on the back of the package, where the first four digits and/or letters correspond to the impacted products listed below.

Consumers who have purchased any of the recalled items should not consume the products and are advised to dispose of them. For questions or support, consumers can contact Tru Fru at (888) 293-7748 or via email at trufru@rqa-inc.com. Reimbursement requests can be made through https://www.rqahttps://rqa-inc.com/client/trufru/. Additional information is available at www.trufru.com.

headline: Gansu Zhaofeng Agricultural Development Co., Ltd. Is Voluntarily Recalling Its Dried Bean Curd Due to Undeclared Wheat

image.caption: Gansu Zhaofeng Agricultural Development Co., Ltd. Is Voluntarily Recalling Its Dried Bean Curd Due to Undeclared Wheat

description: G&L Food announced a recall involving Dried Bean Curd after issues linked to undeclared wheat allergen. FDA recall notice published in 2025.
keywords: Gansu Zhaofeng Agricultural Development Co., Ltd.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Gansu Zhaofeng Agricultural Development Co., Ltd., located in Hui County Industrial Concentration Zone, Longnan City, Gansu Province, has issued a voluntary recall for its 500g bags of G&L FOOD Dried Bean Curd. The recall is due to the failure to declare wheat as an allergen on the product labeling. Wheat is present as a part of the soy sauce used, which poses a serious risk to individuals with wheat allergies or severe sensitivities, potentially causing life-threatening allergic reactions.

The affected dried bean curd products are primarily sold in restaurants and retail stores in the Bay Area of Northern California. Product packaging consists of 500g food plastic bags with expiration dates of 25/10/2025, 11/06/2026, and 20/06/2026. The allergen information was missing from the label, as identified during an FDA inspection. There have been no reported illnesses or adverse reactions linked to this product to date.

Consumers who have purchased G&L FOOD Dried Bean Curd with the listed expiration dates are advised not to consume the product. Instead, they should return the product to the place of purchase for a full refund. For further inquiries, customers can contact Mr. Gu at 415-218-2332 during weekdays from 8:00 AM to 5:00 PM.

headline: SLR Food Distribution, Inc. Recalls Wise Wife Brand Ground Cinnamon Because of Possible Health Risk

image.caption: SLR Food Distribution, Inc. Recalls Wise Wife Brand Ground Cinnamon Because of Possible Health Risk

description: Wise Wife announced a recall involving Cinnamon powder after issues linked to potential to be contaminated with elevated levels of lead. FDA recall notice...
keywords: Food & Beverages Spices, Flavors & Salts Contaminants
Potential to be contaminated with elevated levels of lead

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
SLR Food Distribution, Inc., based in Hicksville, NY, has issued a recall for Wise Wife brand Ground Cinnamon due to the potential contamination with elevated levels of lead. This recall involves 1.76 oz (50g) clear plastic jars with black lids, distributed to retailers in New Jersey, New York, Florida, Maryland, Minnesota, Oklahoma, and Ohio between February 15, 2024, and June 28, 2025.

Exposure to lead, even at low levels, can pose health risks. While short-term low-level exposure may not cause immediate symptoms, increased blood lead levels could be the only sign. Prolonged or high levels of lead exposure can lead to serious health issues including learning disorders and developmental defects in children, as well as kidney dysfunction, hypertension, and neurocognitive effects in adults.

No illnesses related to this recall have been reported to date. The recall was initiated after testing by the U.S. Food and Drug Administration detected lead levels above acceptable limits. SLR Food Distribution has ceased sales of the product as of June 28, 2025.

Consumers who have purchased Wise Wife brand Ground Cinnamon should return the product to the place of purchase for a full refund. For further inquiries, customers may contact SLR Food Distribution at 516-437-3782 EST.

Product Package Size UPC Distribution Dates Retail States
Wise Wife Ground Cinnamon 1.76 oz (50g) jar 0 688474 302853 02/15/2024 – 06/28/2025 NJ, NY, FL, MD, MN, OK, OH

headline: AquaStar (USA) Corp Recalls AquaStar Raw Shrimp Skewers Because of Possible Health Risk

image.caption: AquaStar (USA) Corp Recalls AquaStar Raw Shrimp Skewers Because of Possible Health Risk

description: AquaStar announced a recall involving Shrimp Skewers, Raw Peeled Tail-on after issues linked to potential foodborne illness - possible radionuclide...
keywords: Potential Foodborne Illness - possible radionuclide (Cesium-137) contamination

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
On September 23, 2025, AquaStar (USA) Corp, based in Seattle, WA, announced a voluntary recall of approximately 8,000 bags of AquaStar Raw Peeled Tail-on Shrimp Skewers due to potential contamination with the radionuclide Cesium-137 (Cs-137). These shrimp skewers, imported from Indonesia, may have been processed under unsanitary conditions, raising concerns about possible radioactive contamination.

Cesium-137 is a man-made radioactive isotope that can be present at varying levels in the environment, particularly in areas affected by contamination. Long-term, repeated exposure to Cs-137 through food consumption can increase the risk of cancer due to DNA damage in living cells.

The recalled shrimp skewers, each weighing 1.25 pounds, were sold in Food Lion stores located in Delaware, Georgia, Kentucky, Maryland, North Carolina, Pennsylvania, South Carolina, Tennessee, Virginia, and West Virginia. Sales occurred between July 7, 2025, and September 20, 2025. There have been no reported illnesses linked to this product to date.

Consumers are advised not to consume the affected shrimp skewers. Instead, they should dispose of the product or return it to the place of purchase for a full refund. For further inquiries, AquaStar can be contacted at 1-800-331-3440, Monday through Friday, from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. PST. This recall is being conducted in cooperation with the U.S. Food and Drug Administration.

headline: Goot Essa Recalls Der Mutterschaf Cheese Because of Possible Health Risk

image.caption: Goot Essa Recalls Der Mutterschaf Cheese Because of Possible Health Risk

description: Goot Essa announced a recall involving Der Mutterschaf Cheese after issues linked to potential contamination with listeria monocytogenes. FDA recall notice...
keywords: Food & Beverages Cheese/Cheese Product Foodborne Illness

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Goot Essa LLC of Howard, Pennsylvania, is recalling a specific batch of its Der Mutterschaf Cheese due to potential contamination with Listeria monocytogenes. This bacterium can cause severe and sometimes fatal infections, especially in young children, elderly individuals, pregnant women, and those with weakened immune systems. While healthy individuals may experience short-term symptoms such as high fever, severe headache, stiffness, nausea, abdominal pain, and diarrhea, Listeria infections can also lead to miscarriages and stillbirths in pregnant women.

The recall involves one lot totaling 64 pounds, identified as batch number 33, of Goot Essa brand Der Mutterschaf Cheese. This product was distributed to seven customers across New Jersey, Virginia, Connecticut, and Montana via retail stores and restaurants. The affected cheese comes in clear plastic packages of 4 oz and 8 oz sizes and carries specific UPC codes and a lot number sticker on the back.

The presence of Listeria monocytogenes was identified through routine company testing on a sample from the batch. Subsequent testing of similar batches did not show contamination. To date, no illnesses have been reported in connection with this issue. Goot Essa is actively cooperating with the FDA to complete the investigation.

Consumers who have purchased Der Mutterschaf Cheese from batch 33 are advised to return the product to the place of purchase for a full refund. For further inquiries, customers can contact Goot Essa at 1-800-490-4387, Monday through Saturday, from 8 a.m. to 4 p.m. Eastern Standard Time.

headline: Sprout Organics Expands Voluntary Recall of Sweet Potato Apple and Spinach to Include Additional Lot Codes

image.caption: Sprout Organics Expands Voluntary Recall of Sweet Potato Apple and Spinach to Include Additional Lot Codes

description: Sprout Organics announced a recall involving Sweet Potato Apple and Spinach, 3.5-ounce pouch after issues linked to potential metal contaminant - lead. FDA...
keywords: Sweet Potato Apple and Spinach, 3.5-ounce pouch

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Sprout Organics has expanded its voluntary recall of the Sweet Potato Apple and Spinach 3.5-ounce pouch to include additional lot codes due to potentially elevated lead levels. Lead exposure, even at low levels, may cause an increase in blood lead levels, with risks increasing based on the amount and duration of exposure. Children are particularly vulnerable, as prolonged exposure to lead can affect learning, development, and cause other long-term health issues.

The recalled product was sold at Walgreens, independent retailers in select states, and online. It was not distributed through other major retail chains besides Walgreens. Consumers who have the affected product should check the expiration date and lot code printed on the back bottom strip of the pouch and return it to their local store for a full refund.

No illnesses have been reported related to this recall, and no other Sprout Organics products are affected. The recall follows routine testing by the company and is being conducted with the knowledge of the U.S. Food and Drug Administration.

For more information about Sprout Organics’ product testing procedures, visit the company's FAQ page. Consumers with questions can contact Sprout Organics by phone or email.

headline: Updated Release: Southwind Foods, LLC Recalls Frozen Shrimp Because of Possible Health Risk
description: Sand Bar/Arctic Shores/Best Yet/Great American/First Street announced a recall involving Frozen Shrimp after issues linked to potential foodborne illness -...
keywords: Sand Bar/Arctic Shores/Best Yet/Great American/First Street
Potential Foodborne Illness - possible radionuclide (Cesium-137) contamination

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Southwind Foods, LLC of Carson, California, has announced a voluntary recall of a limited quantity of frozen shrimp due to possible contamination with radionuclide Cesium-137 (Cs-137). Cs-137 is a manmade radioactive isotope that can be found at varying levels in the environment, sometimes concentrated in water or food from contaminated areas. Long-term exposure to low doses of Cs-137 through consumption may increase cancer risk by damaging DNA in living cells.

The affected frozen shrimp products were distributed between June 24 and September 16, 2025, to retailers, distributors, and wholesalers across 35 states. This recall follows an FDA investigation into reports of Cs-137 contamination in shipping containers and frozen shrimp processed by PT. Bahari Makmur Sejati (BMS Foods) in Indonesia. There have been no reported illnesses linked to this issue so far.

Consumers who have purchased the recalled frozen shrimp are advised not to consume the product. Instead, they should dispose of it or return it to the purchase location for a full refund. Customers with questions can contact Southwind Foods at 1-323-262-8222, Monday through Friday from 8 a.m. to 4 p.m. Pacific Time.

More information is available on the FDA recall website at FDA Recalls, Market Withdrawals & Safety Alerts.

headline: Olympus Issues Voluntary Recall for Specific Lots of ViziShot 2 FLEX (19G) EBUS -TBNA Needles
description: Olympus announced a recall involving ViziShot 2 FLEX (19G) EBUS -TBNA needles after issues linked to device components may detach during procedures. FDA...
keywords: Device Components May Detach During Procedures

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Olympus Corporation has initiated a voluntary, global recall of specific lots of its ViziShot 2 FLEX (19G) EBUS -TBNA needles manufactured before May 12, 2025. This action follows reports of device components detaching during medical procedures, which may pose a risk to patient safety.

The ViziShot 2 FLEX needle includes a hypotube and sheath designed to protect and stabilize the device during use. However, damage to the sheath—possibly occurring during manufacturing or use—can cause the hypotube and associated plastic components to detach. These detached components are not visible on X-rays, increasing the risk of retained device fragments within the tracheobronchial tree. Potential complications include the need for bronchoscopic extraction or surgical removal of these parts.

Olympus has improved manufacturing quality controls by implementing automated inspection procedures to detect device damage prior to May 12, 2025. Customers have been advised to quarantine affected products and return them following Olympus’s instructions. Healthcare providers should verify that needles used are from unaffected lots and closely monitor patients exhibiting abnormal symptoms after procedures involving these needles.

Patients treated with impacted devices should receive standard post-procedural care, with particular attention to signs suggesting retained components. Olympus encourages reporting of any adverse events or quality issues to the FDA’s MedWatch program or directly to Olympus.

ProductModelLot NumbersManufacture Date
ViziShot 2 FLEX EBUS-TBNA Needle19GAll lots manufactured before May 12, 2025Prior to May 12, 2025

  • Contact Olympus Technical Assistance Center at 1-800-848-9024, Option 1
  • Email Olympus complaints at: complaints@olympus.com
  • Follow Olympus instructions for quarantining and returning affected devices

headline: Aquastar (USA) Corp Recalls Kroger Frozen Raw EZ Peel, Kroger Mercado Frozen Cooked Shrimp, and Aquastar Raw Shrimp Skewers Because of Possible Health Risk

image.caption: Aquastar (USA) Corp Recalls Kroger Frozen Raw EZ Peel, Kroger Mercado Frozen Cooked Shrimp, and Aquastar Raw Shrimp Skewers Because of Possible Health Risk

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
{ "content": "

On September 19, 2025, AquaStar (USA) Corp, based in Seattle, WA, announced a recall of certain shrimp products due to potential contamination with the radionuclide Cesium-137 (Cs-137). The affected products include Kroger Raw Colossal EZ Peel Shrimp, Kroger Mercado Cooked Medium Peeled Tail-Off Shrimp, and AquaStar Raw Peeled Tail-on Shrimp Skewers. These items may have been prepared, packed, or held under insanitary conditions, leading to possible contamination.

Cesium-137 is a man-made radioisotope that can be present in the environment at background or elevated levels in contaminated areas. Long-term, repeated exposure through food or water intake may increase the risk of cancer by causing DNA damage in living cells. To date, no illnesses have been reported in connection with this recall.

The recalled products were sold at numerous grocery retailers, including Baker's, City Market, Dillons, Food 4 Less, Foodsco, Fred Meyer, Fry's, Gerbes, Jay C, King Soopers, Kroger, Mariano’s, Metro Market, Pay Less Supermarkets, Pick ‘n Save, Ralphs, Smith’s, and QFC across multiple states. The items were available between June 12, 2025, and September 17, 2025.

Consumers who have purchased any of the listed shrimp products are advised not to consume them. Instead, they should dispose of the product or return it to the place of purchase for a full refund. For questions or more information, consumers can contact AquaStar (USA) Corp at 1-800-331-3440, Monday through Friday, 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. PST.

headline: Haifa Smoked Fish Recalls “Cold Smoked Salmon” and “Cold Smoked Seabass” Due to Possible Health Risk

image.caption: Haifa Smoked Fish Recalls “Cold Smoked Salmon” and “Cold Smoked Seabass” Due to Possible Health Risk

description: Haifa announced a recall involving Cold Smoked Salmon and Cold Smoked Seabass after issues linked to potential contamination with listeria monocytogenes....
keywords: Cold Smoked Salmon and Cold Smoked Seabass

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Haifa Smoked Fish of Jamaica, NY, has announced a recall of two of its products: “Cold Smoked Salmon” 8 oz. (Lot #219) and “Cold Smoked Seabass” 8 oz. (Lot #212). The recall is due to potential contamination with Listeria monocytogenes, a harmful bacterium that can cause serious infections, especially in young children, the elderly, pregnant women, and individuals with weakened immune systems.

Healthy individuals exposed to Listeria monocytogenes may experience symptoms such as high fever, severe headache, stiffness, nausea, abdominal pain, and diarrhea. However, the infections can be more severe, leading to miscarriages and stillbirths in pregnant women. The affected products were distributed nationwide through direct delivery to retail stores and distributors.

The contamination was identified following sampling and testing conducted by the New York State Department of Agriculture and Markets Food Inspectors and Food Laboratory personnel. Haifa Smoked Fish has not reported any illnesses related to this recall to date.

Consumers who have purchased these products are advised to return them to the place of purchase for a full refund. For further information, customers can contact Haifa Smoked Fish at 718-523-8899.

ProductSizeLot Number
Cold Smoked Salmon8 oz.219
Cold Smoked Seabass8 oz.212
  • Potential hazard: Listeria monocytogenes contamination
  • Risk groups: young children, elderly, pregnant women, immunocompromised individuals
  • Distribution: nationwide via direct delivery to retail stores and distributors
  • Packaging: vacuum packed on a paper board with flexible plastic
  • Consumer action: return product for full refund
  • Contact number for inquiries: 718-523-8899

headline: Sprout Organics Voluntarily Recalls One Lot of Sweet Potato Apple and Spinach Due to Possible Health Risk

image.caption: Sprout Organics Voluntarily Recalls One Lot of Sweet Potato Apple and Spinach Due to Possible Health Risk

description: Sprout Organics announced a recall involving Sweet Potato Apple and Spinach, 3.5-ounce pouch after issues linked to product may contain elevated levels of...
keywords: Sweet Potato Apple and Spinach, 3.5-ounce pouch
Product may contain elevated levels of lead

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Sprout Organics has voluntarily recalled one lot of its Sweet Potato Apple and Spinach 3.5-ounce pouch due to potential elevated levels of lead. Lead exposure, even in small amounts, can raise blood lead levels and may lead to serious health issues, especially in children. Prolonged or high-level exposure to lead can impact learning, development, and cause other long-term health complications.

The recalled product was distributed primarily through Walgreens and some independent stores in the US South region, with most sales occurring between September and December 2024. It was not sold in other major grocery chains.

Consumers who have purchased this product with the specified lot code and expiration date are advised to return it to the store of purchase for a full refund. To date, no illnesses linked to this product have been reported, and no other Sprout Organics products are affected by this recall.

For more details on Sprout Organics’ product testing and safety measures, visit their FAQ page. Questions about the recall can be directed to Sprout Organics by phone at 510-833-6089, Monday through Friday from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. Pacific Time, or by email at Info@sproutorganics.com.

headline: BD Provides Update on Voluntary Recall of Certain BD Alaris™ Pump Infusion Sets
description: BD Alaris announced a recall involving BD Alaris Pump Module model 8100 used with a subset of compatible pump infusion sets after issues linked to pump...
keywords: BD Alaris Pump Module model 8100 used with a subset of compatible pump infusion sets
Pump performance variations compared to the performance described in the user manual could impact infusion delivery

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
BD (Becton, Dickinson and Company) has expanded its Class I voluntary recall announced earlier in 2025 for the BD Alaris™ Pump Module model 8100 when used with certain compatible pump infusion sets. This recall update includes 15 additional pump infusion sets, some of which have been discontinued but may still be in inventory based on their expiration dates. The recall addresses performance variations that differ from the user manual specifications, potentially impacting infusion delivery accuracy.

These performance issues involve flow rate inaccuracies, delays in occlusion alarms, and over-infusion of post-occlusion bolus volume. Such deviations could lead to serious adverse events, including patient death, particularly affecting vulnerable groups such as neonates and critically ill patients. The factors contributing to these issues include design features of the pump infusion sets, such as filters and other in-line components.

BD has provided updated corrective actions and risk mitigation recommendations, including suggestions to use alternative BD Alaris™ Pump infusion sets if clinically appropriate. The recalled products were distributed nationwide across the United States, including Guam and Puerto Rico, as well as in Canada, Belgium, and South Africa.

Consumers and healthcare providers with concerns or complaints related to these products are encouraged to contact BD or report adverse events to the FDA MedWatch Program through the available channels.

Impacted Pump Infusion Sets (15 Additional Models)
Details of the specific 15 additional pump infusion sets were provided in BD’s customer update (not included specifically in the source text).

BD Contact Information:

  • Phone: 1-888-562-6018
  • Complaint Center: 1-844-823-5433 (Monday – Friday, 9 a.m. – 6 p.m. ET)
  • Email: productcomplaints@bd.com
  • Website: bd.com

FDA MedWatch Reporting Options for Adverse Events:

  • Online: Complete and submit reports at www.fda.gov/medwatch/report.htm
  • Phone: 1-800-FDA-1088 (1-800-332-1088)
  • Mail: Download forms from www.fda.gov/MedWatch/getforms.htm or call 1-800-332-1088 to request a form, complete it, and return by mail or fax to 1-800-FDA-0178

headline: Gooder Foods Issues Allergy Alert On - Undeclared Milk and Cashews in Goodles Vegan is Believin’ – Plant Based White Cheddar with Spirals and Goodles Here Comes Truffle – Creamy Truffle Flavored Cheddar and Shells

image.caption: Gooder Foods Issues Allergy Alert On - Undeclared Milk and Cashews in Goodles Vegan is Believin’ – Plant Based White Cheddar with Spirals and Goodles Here Comes Truffle – Creamy Truffle Flavored Cheddar and Shells

description: Goodles announced a recall involving Flavored pasta after issues linked to undeclared milk and cashews. FDA recall notice published in 2025.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Gooder Foods, Inc. has announced a recall of selected lots of two flavored pasta products from its Goodles brand due to undeclared allergens. The recall involves 5 lots of Vegan Is Believin’ – Plant Based White Cheddar with Spirals, which may contain milk not listed on the label, and 3 lots of Here Comes Truffle – Creamy Truffle Flavored Cheddar and Shells, which may contain cashew not declared on the packaging.

The affected lots were produced between April 7 and April 15, 2025, and were distributed nationally to wholesale distributors, operators, and online from April 29, 2025, through August 5, 2025. Individuals with allergies or severe sensitivities to milk or cashews may be at risk of serious or potentially life-threatening allergic reactions if they consume these products.

There have been a total of eight reported allergic reactions linked to the recalled products—six related to Here Comes Truffle and two related to Vegan Is Believin’. Consumers who have purchased these items are advised not to eat them and should return the products to the place of purchase for a refund. Anyone concerned about an allergic reaction should seek medical attention promptly.

headline: Chetak LLC Group Expands Voluntary Product Recall to Include Additional Frozen Vegetable and Fruit Products Due to Possible Health Risk
description: Deep announced a recall involving Frozen fruits and vegetables after issues linked to salmonella. FDA recall notice published in 2025.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Chetak LLC Group has expanded its voluntary recall of Deep-brand frozen vegetable and fruit products due to potential contamination with Salmonella. The recall, originally announced on July 16, 2025, now includes additional lots of frozen items produced on the same equipment between December 18, 2024, and August 18, 2025.

Salmonella infection can cause serious and sometimes fatal illnesses, particularly in young children, elderly individuals, and people with weakened immune systems. Symptoms in healthy individuals may include fever, diarrhea, nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain. In rare cases, the infection may spread to the bloodstream causing more severe conditions.

The recalled products were distributed nationwide in retail stores and were packaged in flexible plastic bags. Consumers are advised to check the lot numbers and use-by dates printed on the back panel of the packages. If you have any recalled products, stop using them immediately and discard or return them to the place of purchase. No illnesses have been reported in connection with this recall to date.

Production of the affected products has been suspended while Chetak investigates the source of contamination. For questions or more information, consumers may contact the company by email at cservice@deepfoods.com or by phone at (908) 810-7500, Monday through Friday, 9 am to 5 pm EST. This recall is being conducted in coordination with the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA).

Product Lot Number(s)

Deep-brand Sprouted Moong (Mung) IN 25058K
Deep-brand Premium Select Surti Undhiu Mix IN 25158K
Deep-brand Premium Select Singoda IN 25150K

  • For all other frozen vegetable products not included in the recall, thorough cooking is advised.
  • Production of the affected products remains suspended while the investigation continues.
  • For questions, contact Chetak LLC Group at cservice@deepfoods.com or call (908) 810-7500, Monday to Friday, 9 am to 5 pm EST.

headline: Gina Marie Bakery of Waterbury Issues Recall of Cookies Due to Undeclared Almonds, Sesame and Food Dyes

image.caption: Gina Marie Bakery of Waterbury Issues Recall of Cookies Due to Undeclared Almonds, Sesame and Food Dyes

description: No Brand announced a recall involving Cookies after issues linked to undeclared almonds, sesame, red 40, red 3, blue 1, yellow 5, red 40, yellow 6. FDA...
keywords: Undeclared almonds, sesame, Red 40, Red 3, Blue 1, Yellow 5, Red 40, Yellow 6

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Gina Marie Bakery of Waterbury, Connecticut, has issued a recall of several cookie products due to the presence of undeclared allergens, including almonds, sesame, and various food dyes. The affected cookies include Italian Mixed Vanilla Cookies, Italian Sesame Cookies, Vanilla Chocolate Dipped Cookies, Vanilla with Apricot Jam Cookies, Toasted Almond & Cherry Biscotti, and Vanilla Cookies with Raspberry Peach Jam. The undeclared allergens pose a risk of serious or life-threatening allergic reactions to individuals with allergies to nuts, sesame seeds, or certain food dyes.

The recalled products were distributed primarily in Connecticut at various grocery and specialty stores, including Big Y, Stop and Shop, Labonne’s Supermarkets, Adam’s Markets, Highland Park Markets, Price Chopper, Kensington Market, and many others. Additionally, the cookies were sold at all Nardelli’s Connecticut stores with labels added by Nardelli’s. The cookies come in 1 lb (454g) clamshell plastic containers, except for the Italian Mixed Vanilla Cookies, which are available in both 1 lb and 2 lb (908g) packages.

No illnesses have been reported in connection with these products to date. The recall was initiated after it was found that the packaging failed to disclose the presence of the mentioned allergens. Sales of the products have been suspended pending verification that the packaging labels are corrected. Consumers who have purchased any of the affected cookies are advised to return them to the place of purchase for a full refund.

For questions or more information, consumers may contact Gina Marie Bakery at 203-596-8007.

headline: Middlefield Original Cheese Co-Op Recalls Organic Gouda, Colored Cheddar, Mozzarella/Provolone, Pepper Jack, Swiss, Dilly Pickle Cheese and Monterey Jack Due to Possible Listeria Monocytogenes Contamination

image.caption: Middlefield Original Cheese Co-Op Recalls Organic Gouda, Colored Cheddar, Mozzarella/Provolone, Pepper Jack, Swiss, Dilly Pickle Cheese and Monterey Jack Due to Possible Listeria Monocytogenes Contamination

description: Middlefield Original Cheese Co-op, Sunrise Creamery announced a recall involving Cheese after issues linked to potential contamination with-listeria...
keywords: Middlefield Original Cheese Co-op, Sunrise Creamery
Food & Beverages Cheese/Cheese Product Foodborne Illness

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Middlefield Original Cheese Co-Op is conducting a recall of several cheese products produced at its Middlefield, Ohio facility due to potential contamination with Listeria monocytogenes. This bacterium can cause serious and sometimes fatal infections, particularly in young children, elderly individuals, pregnant women, and those with weakened immune systems. Healthy individuals may experience symptoms such as fever, headache, stiffness, nausea, abdominal pain, and diarrhea. Pregnant women risk miscarriages or stillbirths if infected.

The recalled products were produced on May 30, 2025, and August 13, 2025. They were distributed between July 7, 2025, and August 14, 2025, in Ohio, Minnesota, Wisconsin, and Texas via distributors, dining halls, and retail stores. The recall includes Organic Gouda, Mozzarella/Provolone, Colored Cheddar, Swiss, Pepper Jack, Monterey Jack, and Sunrise Creamery Dilly Pickle Monterey Jack cheeses in specific package sizes, lot codes, and sell-by dates.

The recall was initiated after company testing detected Listeria monocytogenes in finished products and on cutting equipment surfaces. No illnesses have been reported to date in connection with these products. Consumers who have any of the affected cheese items are advised to dispose of them or return them to the place of purchase for a refund.

For questions regarding this recall, customers and media representatives may contact Middlefield Original Cheese Co-Op General Manager Nevin Byler at 440-632-5567, Monday through Friday from 8 AM to 4 PM EST.

headline: Ice Cream Factory Issues Allergy Alert on Undeclared Almond in Vanilla G.Nutt Ice Cream

image.caption: Ice Cream Factory Issues Allergy Alert on Undeclared Almond in Vanilla G.Nutt Ice Cream

description: G.Nutt announced a recall involving Vanilla G.Nutt Ice Cream after issues linked to undeclared almond allergen. FDA recall notice published in 2025.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Ice Cream Factory, based in Mount Vernon, NY, has announced a recall of certain Vanilla G.Nutt ice cream containers due to the presence of undeclared almond allergens. This recall affects 40 pints, 8 quarts, and 3 half gallons of the product, which were distributed exclusively within New York State. Individuals with tree nut allergies or severe sensitivities are advised not to consume the affected products, as doing so could result in serious or life-threatening allergic reactions.

The impacted Vanilla G.Nutt ice cream items were available at Golden Krust retail stores located in Mount Vernon and White Plains, NY, as well as Farm Country Supermarket in Queens, NY, through direct delivery. The ice cream is sold in plastic pints and half gallons, and in white paper-coated quart containers. The expiration date on all recalled products is March 2027.

This recall was initiated after the detection of the undeclared almond allergen, traced to a temporary breakdown in the company’s production and packaging processes. Currently, no illnesses have been reported in relation to the consumption of the recalled ice cream.

Consumers who purchased the affected Vanilla G.Nutt ice cream should return it to the place of purchase for a full refund or exchange for a different flavor. For further inquiries, customers can contact Rudy Guerrino at (914) 441-9598, Monday through Friday, from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. Eastern Standard Time.

Product Size UPC Code Expiration Date
Pint 8 56781 00361 3 March 2027
Quart 8 56781 00362 0 March 2027
Half Gallon 8 56781 00363 7 March 2027

Steps for consumers:

  • Check the product size and UPC code to confirm if your Vanilla G.Nutt ice cream is part of the recall.
  • Do not consume the product if you have a tree nut allergy or sensitivity.
  • Return the recalled product to the original purchase location for a refund or exchange.
  • Contact Ice Cream Factory with any questions at (914) 441-9598 during business hours.

headline: Hans Kissle LLC Issues Allergy Alert on Undeclared Wheat (Allergen) in Hans Kissle Red Potato Bliss Salad

image.caption: Hans Kissle LLC Issues Allergy Alert on Undeclared Wheat (Allergen) in Hans Kissle Red Potato Bliss Salad

description: Hans Kissle announced a recall involving Red Bliss Potato Salad after issues linked to undeclared wheat. FDA recall notice published in 2025.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Hans Kissle LLC has issued a voluntary recall of 120 units of Hans Kissle Red Potato Bliss Salad due to the presence of an undeclared allergen, wheat. This issue poses a risk of serious or life-threatening allergic reactions for individuals with wheat allergies or severe sensitivities. The recall was prompted after it was found that containers of Tricolor Twist Pasta Salad were incorrectly labeled with the Red Bliss Potato Salad top label, which failed to declare the wheat allergen properly.

The affected product was distributed to Stop and Shop retail locations across Massachusetts, Rhode Island, Connecticut, New Jersey, and New York. Some of these products may still be in consumers’ refrigerators. The item is packaged in a 16-ounce clear plastic container with a white lid, and the incorrect top label lists it as Red Bliss Potato Salad, while the container actually holds Tricolor Twist Pasta Salad containing wheat.

Consumers who have purchased this product are advised not to consume it and return it to the place of purchase for a full refund. No illnesses or adverse reactions have been reported to date in connection with this recall. For further information or questions, customers can contact Hans Kissle by phone during business hours.

headline: Updated Release: Southwind Foods, LLC Recalls Frozen Shrimp Because of Possible Health Risk

image.caption: Updated Release: Southwind Foods, LLC Recalls Frozen Shrimp Because of Possible Health Risk

description: Sand Bar/Arctic Shores/Best Yet/Great American/First Street announced a recall involving Frozen shrimp after issues linked to due to possible radionuclide...
keywords: Sand Bar/Arctic Shores/Best Yet/Great American/First Street

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Southwind Foods, LLC of Carson, California, is voluntarily recalling a limited quantity of frozen shrimp due to possible contamination with radionuclide Cesium-137 (Cs-137). Cs-137 is a man-made radioactive isotope that can occur in the environment, especially in areas with environmental contamination. Prolonged, repeated exposure to low doses of Cs-137 through consumption may increase the risk of cancer by causing DNA damage within living cells.

The recalled frozen shrimp products were distributed between July 17 and August 8, 2025, to retailers, distributors, and wholesalers in Alabama, Arizona, California, Massachusetts, Minnesota, Pennsylvania, Utah, Virginia, and Washington. This recall follows an FDA investigation into Cs-137 contamination in shipping containers and frozen shrimp processed by PT. Bahari Makmur Sejati (BMS Foods) of Indonesia. At this time, no illnesses related to this contamination have been reported.

Consumers who have purchased the affected frozen shrimp products are advised not to consume them. Instead, they should dispose of the products or return them to the place of purchase for a full refund. For questions or concerns, consumers may contact Southwind Foods, LLC at 1-323-262-8222, Monday through Friday, from 8 am to 4 pm Pacific Time.

Additional information about this recall can be found on the FDA website at FDA Safety Alerts and Recalls.

headline: Green Lumber Holding, LLC Issues Consumer Alert on Counterfeit Products Following FDA Findings

image.caption: Green Lumber Holding, LLC Issues Consumer Alert on Counterfeit Products Following FDA Findings

description: Green Lumber announced a recall involving Dietary Supplement after issues linked to undeclared prescription drug-tadalafil. FDA recall notice published in 2025.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Green Lumber Holding, LLC has issued a consumer alert regarding counterfeit dietary supplement products labeled as Green Lumber. This alert follows testing by the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA), which identified undeclared tadalafil, a prescription drug not included in any authentic Green Lumber products, in these counterfeit items. The presence of tadalafil poses serious health risks, especially for individuals taking nitrates or medications for heart conditions and blood pressure.

The company conducted an internal investigation and found that a former employee had diverted legitimate packaging and customer information to distribute counterfeit and adulterated products. This employee has been terminated. Consumers are urged to verify the authenticity of their Green Lumber products to avoid potential health dangers.

To confirm legitimacy, check the blister pack for the correct lot number: LOTGLU13101b1EXP0926. Products without this lot number should be discontinued immediately. Consumers who suspect they have purchased counterfeit products are advised to report them directly to Green Lumber and to the FDA’s MedWatch Adverse Event Reporting Program. Anyone experiencing unexpected side effects after use should seek medical attention promptly.

Green Lumber is cooperating fully with the FDA and law enforcement to address this issue and enhance consumer protection measures. Contact details for reporting suspected counterfeit products are provided below.

Product Identifier Lot Number Expiration Date
Green Lumber Dietary Supplement (authentic blister pack) LOTGLU13101b1 09/26
  • Verify that your Green Lumber blister pack displays the lot number: LOTGLU13101b1EXP0926.
  • Immediately discontinue use of any product that does not bear this lot number.
  • Report suspected counterfeit products to Green Lumber at phone: 949.426.8622 or email: consumer-safety@greenlumber.com.
  • Report adverse events or safety concerns to the FDA’s MedWatch Adverse Event Reporting Program via the FDA website.

headline: Aquastar (USA) Corp Recalls Kroger Mercado Frozen Cooked Shrimp Because of Possible Health Risk

image.caption: Aquastar (USA) Corp Recalls Kroger Mercado Frozen Cooked Shrimp Because of Possible Health Risk

description: Kroger announced a recall involving Mercado Frozen Cooked Shrimp after issues linked to potential contaminant – cesium-137 (cs-137). FDA recall notice...

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
On August 27, 2025, AquaStar (USA) Corp of Seattle, WA announced a recall of approximately 18,000 bags of Kroger Mercado Cooked Medium Peeled Tail-Off Shrimp (net weight 2 lbs) due to potential contamination with cesium-137 (Cs-137), a man-made radioisotope. The shrimp may have been prepared, packed, or held under insanitary conditions, which could have led to contamination.

Cs-137 is known to be present in the environment at varying levels, particularly in areas affected by environmental contamination. Long-term exposure to Cs-137 through consumption of contaminated food or water can increase the risk of cancer due to DNA damage within living cells. The affected shrimp products were sold between July 24, 2025 and August 11, 2025 at various retailers including Baker’s, Gerbes, Jay C, Kroger, Mariano’s, Metro Market, Pay Less Supermarkets, and Pick ‘n Save across multiple states.

The Food and Drug Administration (FDA) is actively investigating reports of Cs-137 contamination in shipping containers and frozen shrimp products processed by PT. Bahari Makmur Sejati (BMS Foods) of Indonesia. To date, no illnesses have been reported. The FDA noted that no products testing positive for Cs-137 have entered U.S. commerce, but recommends recall of certain products from shipments tied to the supplier after detection of the contamination.

Consumers who have purchased the recalled Kroger Mercado shrimp should not consume the product. They are advised to dispose of it or return it to the place of purchase for a full refund. For further information or questions, customers can contact AquaStar at 1-800-331-3440, Monday through Friday, 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. PST. This recall is being conducted in cooperation with the FDA.

UPC Lot Code Best Before Date Product Description

011110626196 10662 5140 11/20/2027 Kroger Mercado Cooked Medium Peeled Tail-Off Shrimp, 2 lbs
  • Do not consume the recalled Kroger Mercado frozen cooked shrimp.
  • Dispose of the product safely or return it to the retailer for a full refund.
  • Contact AquaStar (USA) Corp at 1-800-331-3440 for questions or additional information (Monday-Friday, 8 a.m.–5 p.m. PST).

headline: DermaRite Industries Expands Voluntary Nationwide Recall Due to Potential Burkholderia cepacia Contamination

image.caption: DermaRite Industries Expands Voluntary Nationwide Recall Due to Potential Burkholderia cepacia Contamination

description: DermaRite announced a recall involving Hand Sanitizers, Cleansers, Skin Protectants, Deodorant after issues linked to device & drug safety - potential...
keywords: Hand Sanitizers, Cleansers, Skin Protectants, Deodorant
Device & Drug Safety - Potential Burkholderia cepacian contamination

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
DermaRite Industries, LLC has expanded its voluntary nationwide recall due to potential contamination with Burkholderia cepacia complex, a microorganism that can cause serious infections. This expansion follows an initial recall announced on July 16 and now includes additional products and lots. The affected products are used for skin protection, cleansing, deodorizing, and antifungal purposes.

The Burkholderia cepacia complex poses significant risk especially to immunosuppressed individuals, who may suffer life-threatening infections if exposed. People with minor skin lesions could experience local infections, while the infection could potentially enter the bloodstream and lead to sepsis in vulnerable populations. Healthy individuals with intact skin are at a lower risk.

The recall involves a variety of over-the-counter (OTC) products including hand sanitizers, skin protectants, antifungal creams, and antiseptic cleansers. These products were distributed nationwide across the United States and Puerto Rico. DermaRite has informed distributors and customers to immediately check their available inventory and destroy any impacted products according to their facility’s protocols.

Consumers with concerns or questions about this recall can contact Sedgwick by phone at 888-943-5190 (Monday–Friday, 8:00 am–5:00 pm EST) or via email at dermarite5186@sedgwick.com. Any adverse health effects suspected to be related to these products should be reported to a healthcare provider.

headline: Aquastar (USA) Corp Recalls Cocktail Shrimp 6oz Because of Possible Health Risk

image.caption: Aquastar (USA) Corp Recalls Cocktail Shrimp 6oz Because of Possible Health Risk

description: Aqua Star announced a recall involving Cocktail Shrimp after issues linked to potential contaminant – cesium-137 (cs-137). FDA recall notice published in 2025.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
On August 28, 2025, AquaStar (USA) Corp of Seattle, WA announced a recall of approximately 26,460 packages of their Cocktail Shrimp 6oz product imported from Indonesia. The recall concerns potential contamination with cesium-137 (Cs-137), a man-made radioisotope that may pose health risks if consumed over time through contaminated food.

The affected shrimp were sold only at Walmart stores in 29 states, including Alaska, Alabama, Arkansas, Colorado, and others, between July 31 and August 16, 2025. The product was refrigerated with a 12-day shelf life and bears various Best if Use By dates. It comes in a clear plastic tray with a red and white label, identified by UPC 19434612191 and specific lot codes.

Cs-137 contamination can elevate cancer risk due to DNA damage from prolonged low-dose exposure. No illnesses have been reported so far. Consumers are advised not to eat the recalled shrimp and to dispose of or return the product for a full refund. For questions, AquaStar can be contacted at 1-800-331-3440 during business hours.

This recall is conducted in coordination with the U.S. Food and Drug Administration for consumer safety.

headline: Country Eggs, LLC Recalls Large Brown Cage Free “Sunshine Yolks” Because of Possible Health Risk

image.caption: Country Eggs, LLC Recalls Large Brown Cage Free “Sunshine Yolks” Because of Possible Health Risk

description: Country Eggs, NIJIYA, GOLDEN YOLK announced a recall involving Large Brown Cage Free Eggs after issues linked to potential foodborne illness - salmonella....

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Country Eggs, LLC of Lucerne Valley, California has announced a recall of its Large Brown Cage Free "Sunshine Yolks" eggs due to potential contamination with Salmonella. Salmonella infection can cause serious and sometimes fatal illnesses, especially in young children, the elderly, and individuals with weakened immune systems. Symptoms of Salmonella infection include fever, diarrhea (which may be bloody), nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain. In rare cases, the infection can spread to the bloodstream and cause more severe conditions such as arterial infections, endocarditis, or arthritis.

The affected eggs were distributed in California and Nevada to grocery stores and food service distributors. The recall includes eggs packed in individual retail cartons under various brand names and sold with specific code dates and produce codes, as well as bulk foodservice packaging. Production has been suspended while the FDA and the company investigate the source of the contamination.

To date, there have been 92 reported illnesses linked to this issue, with no reported deaths. Consumers who have purchased any of the recalled products are urged to return them to the place of purchase for a full refund or to dispose of them safely. For questions, consumers can contact Country Eggs at 1 (800) 722-3447, Monday through Friday from 8am to 5pm Pacific Standard Time, or by email at INFO@countryeggsllc.com.

This recall is being conducted with the knowledge of the U.S. Food and Drug Administration. The company is committed to addressing this issue fully and implementing corrective actions to prevent future occurrences.

Product Variant Code Dates (Sell By) Produce Code
Nagatoshi Produce Brand - Large Brown Cage Free Sunshine Yolks 7/1/25 through 9/18/25 No. CA 7695
Misuho Brand - Large Brown Cage Free Sunshine Yolks 7/1/25 through 9/18/25 No. CA 7695
Nijiya Markets Brand - Large Brown Cage Free Sunshine Yolks 7/1/25 through 9/18/25 No. CA 7695
Country Eggs Bulk Pack (1/15 Dozen) - Large Brown Sunshine Yolks for Foodservice 7/1/25 through 9/18/25 Not specified

Consumers concerned about the recall should:

  • Check the sell-by dates and brand names of any Large Brown Cage Free "Sunshine Yolks" eggs purchased.
  • Return any recalled products to the place of purchase for a full refund or dispose of them safely.
  • Contact Country Eggs at 1 (800) 722-3447 during business hours or by email at INFO@countryeggsllc.com for further information.
  • Stay informed by monitoring updates from the FDA and the company regarding this recall.

headline: Unichem Pharmaceuticals (USA) Inc. Issues Voluntary Nationwide Recall of Cyclobenzaprine Hydrochloride Tablets USP 10 mg, Due to Mislabeling

image.caption: Unichem Pharmaceuticals (USA) Inc. Issues Voluntary Nationwide Recall of Cyclobenzaprine Hydrochloride Tablets USP 10 mg, Due to Mislabeling

description: Unichem Pharmaceuticals announced a recall involving Cyclobenzaprine Hydrochloride Tablets USP 10 mg after issues linked to device & drug safety – mislabel....

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Unichem Pharmaceuticals (USA) Inc. has voluntarily recalled one lot of Cyclobenzaprine Hydrochloride Tablets USP 10 mg nationwide due to mislabeling. The affected bottles labeled as Cyclobenzaprine 10 mg (90 count) actually contain Meloxicam 7.5 mg tablets. This labeling error presents a risk to patients who may unknowingly take Meloxicam instead of Cyclobenzaprine.

Meloxicam is a non-steroidal anti-inflammatory drug (NSAID) used to treat conditions such as osteoarthritis and rheumatoid arthritis. Taking Meloxicam unknowingly can lead to serious adverse events, especially for patients using other NSAIDs, blood thinners, or those with allergies or underlying health conditions. To date, no adverse events related to this recall have been reported to Unichem Pharmaceuticals.

The affected product can be identified by the lot number GMML24026A with an expiry date of September 2027, and NDC 29300-415-19 on the 90-count bottle label. This lot was distributed nationwide to distributors, retailers, and consumers. Unichem Pharmaceuticals is notifying all downstream partners, retailers, and consumers and coordinating the return of the affected product through their third-party recall provider, Inmar.

Consumers who have this product should return it to the pharmacy where it was purchased. Retail pharmacies should not dispense this lot and must contact Inmar for return instructions. Any consumers experiencing health issues potentially related to this product should contact their healthcare provider. Adverse reactions or quality issues can be reported to the FDA’s MedWatch program.

Product Lot Number Expiration Date NDC Count
Cyclobenzaprine Hydrochloride Tablets USP 10 mg (mislabeled, contains Meloxicam 7.5 mg) GMML24026A September 2027 29300-415-19 90
  • Consumers with the affected product should return it to their pharmacy.
  • Retail pharmacies and distributors holding this lot should not distribute or dispense it and contact Inmar for return instructions.
  • For questions, consumers and retailers can contact Inmar at 1-877-840-5109 or email rxrecalls@inmar.com (Monday – Friday, 9 am – 5 pm CST).
  • Adverse events or quality problems can be reported to the FDA MedWatch program online at www.fda.gov/medwatch/report.htm, by mail or fax using forms available at www.fda.gov/MedWatch/getforms.htm, or by calling 1-800-332-1088.
  • Consumers who experience any health issues related to this product should contact their healthcare provider.

headline: Viva Raw Issues Voluntary Recall of Two Lots of Dog and Cat Foods Due to Salmonella and Listeria Monocytogenes Contamination

image.caption: Viva Raw Issues Voluntary Recall of Two Lots of Dog and Cat Foods Due to Salmonella and Listeria Monocytogenes Contamination

description: Viva announced a recall involving Ground Beef for Dogs and Ground Chicken for Dogs and Cats after issues linked to salmonella and listeria monocytogenes...
keywords: Ground Beef for Dogs and Ground Chicken for Dogs and Cats
Animal & Veterinary Food & Beverages Foodborne Illness

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
On August 25, 2025, Viva Raw LLC, based in Charlotte, NC, announced a voluntary recall of two lots of dog and cat foods due to contamination with Salmonella and Listeria monocytogenes. The recall affects Lot 21495, which includes Viva Ground Beef for Dogs and Viva Beef & Turkey for Puppies, as well as Lot 21975, which includes Viva Ground Chicken for Dogs, Viva Chunked Chicken for Dogs, Viva Chicken for Cats, and Viva Pure Chicken.

Salmonella and Listeria contamination pose health risks to both pets and humans. Animals consuming the affected products may experience symptoms such as lethargy, diarrhea or bloody diarrhea, fever, vomiting, decreased appetite, and abdominal pain. Infected healthy pets can also carry and spread the bacteria to other animals or humans. People handling the contaminated products may develop symptoms including nausea, vomiting, diarrhea or bloody diarrhea, abdominal cramping, and fever. More serious complications can occur, especially in vulnerable groups such as pregnant women, young children, the elderly, and individuals with weakened immune systems.

The affected products were distributed frozen in 1 lb. vacuum-sealed bricks between July 2, 2025, and August 21, 2025. Distribution included direct consumer sales nationwide and select retailers in New York, Illinois, South Carolina, Florida, Kansas, California, and Arizona. The lot number is printed on the top of the product packaging. This recall was initiated after routine testing by the North Carolina Department of Agriculture identified the contamination. To date, no illnesses associated with these lots have been reported.

Consumers who have purchased products from Lot 21495 or Lot 21975 should stop using them immediately. Consumers can contact Viva Raw for a refund by emailing info@vivarawpets.com. The recalled products should be disposed of safely to prevent access by children, pets, or wildlife. It is important not to sell or donate the recalled food and not to feed it to pets or other animals. Additionally, pet food dishes, cups, and storage containers should be thoroughly washed and sanitized. Hand washing and sanitizing after handling the recalled products or any utensils that touched them is strongly recommended.

Lot Number Product Names Distribution Dates Packaging Distribution Locations
21495 Viva Ground Beef for Dogs; Viva Beef & Turkey for Puppies July 2, 2025 – August 21, 2025 Frozen 1 lb. bricks, clear vacuum packaging Nationwide (direct to consumers), select retailers in NY, IL, SC, FL, KS, CA, AZ
21975 Viva Ground Chicken for Dogs; Viva Chunked Chicken for Dogs; Viva Chicken for Cats; Viva Pure Chicken July 2, 2025 – August 21, 2025 Frozen 1 lb. bricks, clear vacuum packaging Nationwide (direct to consumers), select retailers in NY, IL, SC, FL, KS, CA, AZ
  • If you have recalled products, contact Viva Raw at info@vivarawpets.com for a refund.
  • Dispose of recalled products so that they are inaccessible to children, pets, and wildlife.
  • Do not sell, donate, or feed recalled products to animals.
  • Wash and sanitize pet food bowls, cups, and storage containers after disposing of recalled food.
  • Always wash and sanitize hands after handling recalled products or any utensils that contacted the products.
  • For questions, email info@vivarawpets.com or call/text (919) 371-8882, Monday to Friday, 9:00 am – 5:00 pm EST.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Southwind Foods, LLC, based in Carson, California, has voluntarily issued a recall of certain frozen shrimp products due to potential contamination with radionuclide Cesium-137 (Cs-137). This man-made radioactive isotope can pose health risks, particularly an increased cancer risk from long-term exposure to contaminated food or water. The recalled shrimp products were distributed from July 17 to August 8, 2025.

The affected products were sold under multiple brands, including Sand Bar, Arctic Shores, Best Yet, Great American, and First Street. Distribution occurred across several states: Alabama, Arizona, California, Massachusetts, Minnesota, Pennsylvania, Utah, Virginia, and Washington.

The FDA is investigating the presence of Cs-137 contamination linked to frozen shrimp processed by PT. Bahari Makmur Sejati (BMS Foods) in Indonesia. To date, no illnesses have been reported in connection with this issue. Consumers who have purchased any of the recalled shrimp products should not eat them. Instead, they should dispose of the shrimp or return it to the original place of purchase for a full refund.

For questions or additional information, consumers can contact Southwind Foods at 1-323-262-8222, Monday through Friday, from 8 a.m. to 4 p.m. Pacific Time.

headline: B. Braun Medical Issues Voluntary Nationwide Recall of Lactated Ringer’s Injection USP 1000 mL and 0.9% Sodium Chloride Injection USP 1000 mL Due to the Presence of Particulate Matter

image.caption: B. Braun Medical Issues Voluntary Nationwide Recall of Lactated Ringer’s Injection USP 1000 mL and 0.9% Sodium Chloride Injection USP 1000 mL Due to the Presence of Particulate Matter

description: B. Braun announced a recall involving Lactated Ringer’s Injection USP 1000 mL/0.9% Sodium Chloride Injection USP 1000 mL after issues linked to due to the...
keywords: Lactated Ringer’s Injection USP 1000 mL/0.9% Sodium Chloride Injection USP 1000 mL
Due to the presence of particulate matter.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
B. Braun Medical, Inc. has issued a voluntary nationwide recall of two lots of Lactated Ringer’s Injection USP 1000 mL and 0.9% Sodium Chloride Injection USP 1000 mL. The recall affects products distributed to hospitals due to the presence of particulate matter inside the containers, which poses a potential risk to patients.

Although no serious injuries, deaths, or adverse events have been reported so far, the presence of particulate matter in the solution may cause harm if infused. Possible risks include pulmonary emboli, blood vessel occlusions, phlebitis, systemic immune activation, organ dysfunction, and hemolysis. If particulate matter is detected before use, a delay may occur while obtaining a replacement. If used, medical intervention could be necessary to address potential complications.

Lactated Ringers Injection USP 1000 mL is typically used as a source of electrolytes and water for hydration in adults and pediatric patients. The 0.9% Sodium Chloride Injection USP 1000 mL is indicated for extracellular fluid replacement and treatment of metabolic alkalosis in cases of fluid loss and mild sodium depletion. Both products come packaged in boxes of 12 units and were distributed nationwide through distributors.

B. Braun has notified distributors and customers and is coordinating the return of all recalled products. Facilities holding the affected lots should immediately cease use and distribution, quarantine the products, and not destroy them until instructed. For questions related to the recall, B. Braun’s Recalls Department can be reached at 844-903-6417 between 9 AM and 5 PM EST.

Product Catalog Number NDC Number Lot Number Distribution Range Expiration Date Region Distributed
Lactated Ringers Injection USP 1000 mL E7500 0264-7750-07 J4S807 26 DEC 2024 - 10 APR 2025 31 MAY 2027 US
0.9% Sodium Chloride Injection USP 1000 mL E8000 0264-7800-09 V3K770 15 NOV 2023 - 25 SEP 2024 31 JAN 2026 US

Consumers who have experienced any adverse effects or quality problems with these products are encouraged to report them to the FDA’s MedWatch program. Reporting options include:

  • Online: www.fda.gov/medwatch/report.htm
  • Regular Mail or Fax: Download the reporting form at www.fda.gov/MedWatch/getforms.htm
  • Request a form by calling 1-800-332-1088
  • Fax completed forms to 1-800-FDA-0178

This recall is being carried out with the knowledge of the U.S. Food and Drug Administration.

headline: Middlefield Original Cheese Co-Op Recalls 100% Grass-Fed Pepper Jack Cheese and Horseradish Flavored Cheese Due to Possible Listeria monocytogenes Contamination

image.caption: Middlefield Original Cheese Co-Op Recalls 100% Grass-Fed Pepper Jack Cheese and Horseradish Flavored Cheese Due to Possible Listeria monocytogenes Contamination

description: Middlefield Original Cheese Co-op/Copia Collective/Farmers Cheese announced a recall involving Cheese after issues linked to potential contamination with...
keywords: Middlefield Original Cheese Co-op/Copia Collective/Farmers Cheese
Food & Beverages Cheese/Cheese Product Foodborne Illness

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
The Middlefield Original Cheese Co-op has issued a recall for several cheese products due to potential contamination with Listeria monocytogenes. This bacterium can cause serious and sometimes fatal infections, particularly in young children, the elderly, pregnant women, and individuals with weakened immune systems. While healthy people may experience short-term symptoms such as fever, headache, nausea, and diarrhea, pregnant women face risks of miscarriage and stillbirth from infection.

The recall includes 100% Grass-Fed Pepper Jack Cheese in various sizes, Horseradish Flavored Cheese, Monterey Jack Cheese, and Farmers Cheese. These products were produced on June 16, June 24, and July 16, 2025, and distributed within Ohio from July 14 through August 7, 2025. The recall was initiated after company testing detected the presence of Listeria monocytogenes. Production has been suspended as the source was traced to environmental contamination affecting food-contact surfaces.

There have been no reported illnesses linked to these products so far. Consumers who have purchased any of the affected products are advised to dispose of them or return them to the place of purchase for a refund. For questions about the recall, customers can contact Middlefield Original Cheese Co-op's General Manager, Nevin Byler, at 440-632-5567 Monday through Friday from 8 AM to 4 PM EST.

headline: Tandem Diabetes Care Issues Voluntary Medical Device Correction for Select t:slim X2 Insulin Pumps
description: Tandem Diabetes Care, Inc. announced a recall involving t:slim X2 insulin pumps after issues linked to potential speaker-related issue that can trigger an...
keywords: Potential speaker-related issue that can trigger an error resulting in a discontinuation of insulin delivery

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Tandem Diabetes Care, Inc. has initiated a voluntary medical device correction for certain t:slim X2 insulin pumps due to a potential speaker-related issue. This defect can trigger a Malfunction 16 alarm, resulting in the discontinuation of insulin delivery and loss of communication between the pump and continuous glucose monitoring (CGM) device. The interruption in insulin delivery and CGM data may lead to hyperglycemia, which in severe cases might require hospitalization or medical intervention.

As of the announcement, there have been 700 confirmed adverse events involving high blood sugar or the need for medical intervention, along with 59 reported injuries. No deaths have been reported. Customers in the United States affected by this issue were notified directly between July 22 and 24, 2025, with guidance on how to respond if they encounter the Malfunction 16 alarm.

Tandem Diabetes Care plans to release a software update aimed at improving early detection of speaker failure. This update will also add persistent vibration alerts to enhance safety. All pump users will be informed when the update is available and encouraged to install it promptly.

Impacted U.S. customers with questions can contact Tandem’s Technical Support Team 24/7 by email at techsupport@tandemdiabetes.com or phone at (877) 801-6901. International users should contact their local distributors; contact details are available at tandemdiabetes.com. Additional resources, including a searchable list of affected serial numbers and the customer notification letter, are available online.

ResourceLink
Customer Notification LetterPDF document
Searchable List of Impacted Serial Numbershttps://tandemdiabetes.com/mal16-2025
Tandem Diabetes Contact Informationhttps://tandemdiabetes.com

Important safety information for current users includes:

  • The Control-IQ+ system should not be used in children under 2 years of age with Type 1 diabetes or under 18 years of age with Type 2 diabetes.
  • It is not intended for patients requiring less than 5 units of insulin per day or weighing less than 20 pounds (9 kg).
  • Users must follow all instructions for use to avoid risks of insulin overdelivery or underdelivery, which could lead to hypoglycemia or hyperglycemia.
  • Additional safety information is available at tandemdiabetes.com/safetyinfo.

headline: Max Mobility / Permobil Announces Worldwide Expanded Recall and Removal of all SpeedControl Dials from the Market Due to Intermittent Cable Connection Concerns of the Dial with the SmartDrive MX2+ Motor. Users Must Use an Alternate Control Method for their SmartDrive MX2+ Power Assist Device.
description: Max Mobility/Permobil announced a recall involving Speed Control Dial Component after issues linked to due to potential safety and performance concerns. FDA...
keywords: Due to potential safety and performance concerns

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Max Mobility, LLC., under the Max Mobility/Permobil brand, has announced a worldwide expanded recall and removal of all SpeedControl Dial components used with the SmartDrive MX2+ Power Assist Device. This voluntary action addresses potential safety and performance concerns related to intermittent electrical connections between the SpeedControl Dial and the SmartDrive MX2+ motor.

The affected SpeedControl Dials were manufactured and distributed between April 25, 2022, and July 8, 2025. Users of the SpeedControl Dial have experienced incidents where the device either continued to drive when the dial was pressed or experienced unintended activation of the motor without user input. These issues may lead to involuntary movement or failure to stop, posing risks of minor to serious injuries, including cuts, bruises, bone fractures, and concussions.

This expanded recall supersedes previous recalls related to a printed circuit board assembly issue affecting units manufactured and distributed between August 17, 2023, and March 10, 2025. Since the introduction of the SpeedControl Dial, Max Mobility/Permobil has received a total of 781 complaints, including seven involving serious injuries.

The SpeedControl Dial will no longer be distributed or offered as a controller option for the SmartDrive MX2+. Alternative control options remain available, including the wired SwitchControl with an optional Buddy Button accessory and app-based controls via wearable devices. Users are encouraged to contact their SmartDrive provider or healthcare professional to determine the best alternative control solution.

Part Number Description Manufacture and Distribution Dates
MX2-3DCK SpeedControl Dial Component April 25, 2022 – July 8, 2025
MX2-3DC SpeedControl Dial Component April 25, 2022 – July 8, 2025
MX2-3DCMC SpeedControl Dial Component April 25, 2022 – July 8, 2025

If you possess or have distributed a SpeedControl Dial, it is critical to stop using the device immediately and switch to an alternate control method for the SmartDrive MX2+ Power Assist Device. Users should contact Max Mobility/Permobil for replacement options and technical support.

Max Mobility/Permobil has suspended distribution of the SpeedControl Dial and advises that the SmartDrive MX2+ device itself is unaffected when used with other control methods.

For assistance or further inquiries, please contact Max Mobility/Permobil:

  • Technical Support Team: 615-257-2930 (Monday - Friday, 8:00 am to 5:00 pm CT)
  • Order Inquiries: 1-800-736-0925 ext. 6
  • Email: sales.smartdrive@permobil.com

To report adverse events or product quality issues to the FDA’s MedWatch program, use one of the following methods:

  • Online: https://www.accessdata.fda.gov/scripts/medwatch
  • Regular mail or fax: Download the form from www.fda.gov/MedWatch/getforms.htm or call 800-332-1088 to request a form, then mail it to the address on the form or fax it to 800-332-0178.

Additional details and updates about the recall can be found on the Max Mobility/Permobil recall page: https://hub.permobil.com/max-mobility/permobil-speedcontrol-dial-recall.

headline: Dollar General Announces Voluntary Recall of Clover Valley® Instant Coffee Due to Potential Presence of Glass

image.caption: Dollar General Announces Voluntary Recall of Clover Valley® Instant Coffee Due to Potential Presence of Glass

description: Clover Valley announced a recall involving Instant coffee after issues linked to potential presence of glass. FDA recall notice published in 2025.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Dollar General Corporation has announced a voluntary recall of three lots of its Clover Valley® Instant Coffee due to the potential presence of glass fragments in the product. The recalled items are the 8-ounce jars of Clover Valley® Instant Coffee, which were sold exclusively at Dollar General stores.

The affected lots were distributed between July 9 and July 21, 2025, in multiple states across the U.S. Consumers are advised not to use the product as ingesting glass can cause serious injuries such as damage to teeth, mouth and throat lacerations, or intestinal perforation. To date, no injuries or illnesses have been reported in connection with this issue.

Customers who purchased any of the affected lots should discard the product and contact Dollar General for a full refund. Refund requests can be made by email at customercare@dollargeneral.com or by phone at 1-888-309-9030, available daily from 6 a.m. to 1 a.m. CST.

The recall is being conducted in cooperation with the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA). Dollar General is investigating the source of the contamination and apologizes for the inconvenience.

Product Description UPC Lot Number Best By Date
8-Ounce Clover Valley® Instant Coffee 876941004069 L-5163 12/13/2026
8-Ounce Clover Valley® Instant Coffee 876941004069 L-5164 12/13/2026

The product was sold in the following states: Alabama, Arkansas, Arizona, California, Colorado, Connecticut, Delaware, Florida, Georgia, Iowa, Idaho, Illinois, Indiana, Kansas, Kentucky, Louisiana, Massachusetts, Maryland, Maine, Michigan, Minnesota, Missouri, Mississippi, Montana, North Carolina, North Dakota, Nebraska, New Hampshire, New Jersey, New Mexico, Nevada, New York, Ohio, Oklahoma, Oregon, Pennsylvania, Rhode Island, South Carolina, South Dakota, Tennessee, Texas, Utah, Virginia, Vermont, Washington, Wisconsin, West Virginia, and Wyoming.

  • Do not consume the recalled Clover Valley® Instant Coffee products.
  • Discard the product immediately to avoid possible injury.
  • Contact Dollar General for a full refund via email at customercare@dollargeneral.com or by phone at 1-888-309-9030.
  • Customer service is available seven days a week from 6 a.m. to 1 a.m. CST.
