headline: DermaRite Industries Issues Voluntary Nationwide Recall of DermaKleen, Dermasarra, Kleenfoam, and Perigiene Products Due to Burkholderia cepacia Contamination

image.caption: DermaRite Industries Issues Voluntary Nationwide Recall of DermaKleen, Dermasarra, Kleenfoam, and Perigiene Products Due to Burkholderia cepacia Contamination

description: DermaKleen, Dermasarra, Kleenfoam, and Perigiene announced a recall involving OTC antiseptic lotion soaps, external analgesics, antimicrobial foam soaps,...
keywords: OTC antiseptic lotion soaps, external analgesics, antimicrobial foam soaps, and antiseptic cleansers

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
DermaRite Industries, LLC has voluntarily announced a nationwide recall of certain lots of its OTC antiseptic lotion soaps, external analgesics, antimicrobial foam soaps, and antiseptic cleansers. The recall affects products under the DermaKleen, Dermasarra, Kleenfoam, and Perigiene brands. This action was taken due to the detection of microbial contamination with Burkholderia cepacia, a bacteria known to cause serious infections.

Burkholderia cepacia complex contamination in these products poses a risk of serious and potentially life-threatening infections, especially for immunocompromised individuals or those caring for them. While healthy users with minor skin lesions may develop local infections, immunosuppressed individuals are at greater risk for infections that could spread to the bloodstream and lead to sepsis. To date, there have been no reported adverse events related to this recall.

The recalled products were distributed across the United States, including Puerto Rico. DermaRite has notified distributors and customers to inspect their inventory and promptly destroy all affected products according to their facility procedures. Consumers who have questions about the recall can contact DermaRite directly for assistance.

If you have used any of the recalled products and experienced health issues, it is advised to consult with a healthcare provider. Any adverse reactions or quality problems related to these products can be reported to the FDA’s MedWatch program.

Product Name Lot Number Expiration Date UPC
DermaKleen Antiseptic Lotion Soap with Vitamin E Specific lots as provided by DermaRite (refer to company communication) Various (refer to company communication) Listed individually per lot (refer to company communication)
KleenFoam Antimicrobial Foam Soap with Aloe Vera Specific lots as provided by DermaRite (refer to company communication) Various (refer to company communication) Listed individually per lot (refer to company communication)
PeriGiene Antiseptic Cleanser Specific lots as provided by DermaRite (refer to company communication) Various (refer to company communication) Listed individually per lot (refer to company communication)

Consumers and healthcare professionals are encouraged to report any adverse reactions or quality issues related to these products through the FDA MedWatch Adverse Event Reporting program by:

  • Submitting reports online at www.fda.gov/medwatch/report.htm
  • Downloading and mailing or faxing forms, available at www.fda.gov/MedWatch/getforms.htm
  • Requesting a reporting form by calling 1-800-332-1088 and returning it to the provided address or faxing to 1-800-FDA-0178

For further questions regarding this recall, consumers may contact Mary Goldberg at DermaRite Industries at 973-569-9000 ext. 104, Monday through Friday from 9:00 am to 5:00 pm EST or email voluntary.action@dermarite.com.

headline: Hans Kissle LLC Issues Allergy Alert on Undeclared Wheat (Allergen) in Hans Kissle Red Potato Bliss Salad

image.caption: Hans Kissle LLC Issues Allergy Alert on Undeclared Wheat (Allergen) in Hans Kissle Red Potato Bliss Salad

description: Hans Kissle announced a recall involving Red Bliss Potato Salad after issues linked to undeclared wheat. FDA recall notice published in 2025.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Hans Kissle LLC has issued a voluntary recall of 66 units of its Red Potato Bliss Salad due to the undeclared presence of wheat, an allergen. This recall is important for individuals with allergies or severe sensitivities to wheat, as consuming the product may cause serious or potentially life-threatening reactions.

The affected product was distributed to Stop and Shop stores located in Massachusetts, Rhode Island, Connecticut, New Jersey, and New York. It may still be present in some consumers’ refrigerators. Each unit is packaged in a 16-ounce clear plastic container with a white lid. The top lid label incorrectly identifies the product as Hans Kissle Red Bliss Potato Salad, though the container actually holds Tricolor Twist Pasta Salad, which contains wheat not declared on the top label.

The front label of the container correctly identifies the product as Tricolor Twist Pasta Salad. The recall was triggered after it was discovered that 66 containers were mislabeled with the incorrect top label. No illnesses or adverse reactions have been reported to date related to this issue.

Consumers who have purchased the affected product should return it to the place of purchase for a full refund. For additional questions, consumers may contact Hans Kissle customer service at 978-556-4500, Monday through Friday from 8:00 am to 5:00 pm EST.

  • Return the affected item to the point of purchase for a full refund.

headline: Doehler Dry Ingredient Solutions, LLC Recalls Member’s Mark Freeze Dried Fruit Variety Pack for Listeria monocytogenes Contamination

image.caption: Doehler Dry Ingredient Solutions, LLC Recalls Member’s Mark Freeze Dried Fruit Variety Pack for Listeria monocytogenes Contamination

description: Member’s Mark announced a recall involving Freeze dried fruit after issues linked to potential foodborne illness – listeria monocytogenes. FDA recall notice...

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Doehler Dry Ingredient Solutions, LLC has issued a recall for the Member’s Mark Freeze Dried Fruit Variety Pack (15 count) due to potential contamination with Listeria monocytogenes. This bacterium can cause serious and sometimes fatal infections, particularly in young children, elderly individuals, pregnant women, and those with weakened immune systems. While healthy individuals may experience only short-term symptoms such as fever, headache, nausea, and diarrhea, pregnant women infected with Listeria risk miscarriages or stillbirths.

The recall affects multiple lots of the Member’s Mark Freeze Dried Fruit Variety Pack, identified by specific lot numbers and expiration dates. These products were sold at Sam’s Club stores and distributed across various states between July 1 and July 25, 2025. The products come in foil pouches packaged inside corrugated boxes, with lot numbers and expiration dates printed on the bottom of the case.

No illnesses linked to this product have been reported to date. Consumers who have purchased the affected freeze dried fruit packs are advised not to consume them but to discard the products instead. Refunds are available at any Sam’s Club location. For further assistance, the company’s customer service can be contacted during business hours.

This recall is being conducted in cooperation with the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA).

headline: High Noon Announces Recall of its Vodka Seltzer Beach Pack (12 Pack) Due to Inclusion of CELSIUS® ASTRO VIBE ™ Energy Drink Cans that were Inadvertently Filled with Vodka Seltzer

image.caption: High Noon Announces Recall of its Vodka Seltzer Beach Pack (12 Pack) Due to Inclusion of CELSIUS® ASTRO VIBE ™ Energy Drink Cans that were Inadvertently Filled with Vodka Seltzer

description: High Noon, Celsius announced a recall involving Energy Drink after issues linked to cans contain alcohol. FDA recall notice published in 2025.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
High Noon has announced a recall of certain High Noon Beach Variety packs (12-pack, 12 fluid ounce cans) due to a packaging error that led to some CELSIUS® ASTRO VIBE™ Energy Drink cans being filled with vodka seltzer instead of the intended energy drink. These affected cans have silver lids, which is not usual for the CELSIUS beverage that normally has black lids.

The mislabeled CELSIUS® ASTRO VIBE™ Energy Drink, Sparkling Blue Razz Edition cans included in these packs contain alcohol and thus pose a risk of unintentional alcohol ingestion. To date, no illnesses or adverse events have been reported related to this issue. The recall affects specific production lots that were shipped primarily to distributors and retailers in Florida, Michigan, New York, Ohio, Oklahoma, South Carolina, Virginia, and Wisconsin between July 21 and July 23, 2025.

Consumers are urged to check the lot codes and lids of their CELSIUS ASTRO VIBE Energy Drink cans. If the cans have silver lids and the specified lot codes, they should not be consumed and must be disposed of immediately. High Noon Beach Variety packs and CELSIUS cans outside these lot codes are not affected and are safe to drink.

For those who have purchased the recalled products or the affected CELSIUS cans, High Noon requests contacting their Consumer Relations department via email for further guidance including refund options. This recall is conducted with oversight from the U.S. Food and Drug Administration and the Alcohol and Tobacco Tax and Trade Bureau.

headline: Hillside Orchard Farms Recalls Various Fruit Breads & Fritters Due to Undeclared Egg

image.caption: Hillside Orchard Farms Recalls Various Fruit Breads & Fritters Due to Undeclared Egg

description: Hillside Orchard Farms announced a recall involving Fruit Bread and Fritters after issues linked to undeclared allergen – egg. FDA recall notice published...

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Hillside Orchard Farms has issued a recall of several varieties of their 23 oz Fruit Breads and 7.5 oz Fritters due to the presence of an undeclared allergen, egg. This recall affects products with labels that failed to list egg as an ingredient following a label reprint. The products were distributed between November 18, 2024, and July 16, 2025.

People with an egg allergy or severe sensitivity are at risk of serious or life-threatening allergic reactions if they consume these items. To date, no illnesses have been reported in connection with this recall.

These products were sold primarily in farm markets and roadside stands in Alabama, Georgia, North Carolina, Pennsylvania, and South Carolina. Consumers who have purchased any of the affected fruit breads or fritters are advised to return them to the place of purchase for a full refund or discard the products.

For questions, consumers can contact Kiley Mitcham Houston, Vice President of Operations at Hillside Orchard Farms, at 706-782-4995, Monday through Friday from 8:30 a.m. to 5 p.m. EST. This recall is being conducted in cooperation with the U.S. Food and Drug Administration.

headline: Albertsons Companies Stores in Arkansas, Louisiana, Oklahoma and Texas Voluntarily Expands Recall to Select Items Containing Tuna Salad from Reser’s Fine Foods Due to an Ingredient Recall Linked to Possible Listeria monocytogenes Contamination
description: Randalls, Albertsons, Tom Thumb announced a recall involving Additional Tuna Salad products after issues linked to potential foodborne illness – listeria...

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Albertsons Companies has voluntarily expanded a recall of select tuna salad products containing ingredients supplied by Reser’s Fine Foods. This recall affects items sold at Albertsons, Randalls, and Tom Thumb stores in Arkansas, Louisiana, Oklahoma, and Texas. The recall follows Reser’s Fine Foods’ initial recall due to possible contamination of an ingredient—breadcrumbs used in tuna salad—with Listeria monocytogenes.

Listeria monocytogenes is a bacterium that can cause serious and sometimes fatal infections, particularly in young children, elderly individuals, pregnant women, and people with weakened immune systems. In healthy individuals, symptoms may include high fever, severe headache, stiffness, nausea, abdominal pain, and diarrhea. Pregnant women infected with Listeria are at risk of miscarriage or stillbirth.

Consumers who have purchased any of the recalled products are advised not to consume them. Instead, they should dispose of the products or return them to the store for a full refund. The U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA) also recommends thorough cleaning and sanitizing of any surfaces or containers that may have come into contact with the recalled items to prevent cross-contamination, since Listeria can survive refrigerated temperatures and spread easily.

To date, there have been no reports of illnesses or injuries related to these products. Anyone experiencing health concerns after consuming the products should consult a healthcare provider. Customers with questions can contact Albertsons Companies’ Customer Service Center at 1-877-723-3929, Monday through Friday, 5 a.m. to 9 p.m. PST.

headline: Tropicale Foods Recalls Certain Helados Mexico and La Michoacana Products Due to Undeclared Milk

image.caption: Tropicale Foods Recalls Certain Helados Mexico and La Michoacana Products Due to Undeclared Milk

description: La Michoacana, Helados Mexico announced a recall involving Ice cream products after issues linked to undeclared milk. FDA recall notice published in 2025.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Tropicale Foods, based in Ontario, California, is voluntarily recalling certain ice cream products under the Helados Mexico and La Michoacana brands. This recall affects items with specific best by dates because they contain undeclared milk. Although "cream" is listed in the ingredient information, the term "milk" is not explicitly stated, posing a risk to individuals with milk allergies or severe sensitivities.

Consumers allergic to milk who consume these products could experience serious or life-threatening allergic reactions. The affected products were distributed nationwide through retail locations. The recall follows a company audit of product labels and includes several specific items listed below. To date, one illness related to this issue has been reported.

Consumers who have purchased the affected products are advised not to consume them if they have a milk allergy or sensitivity and should dispose of them immediately. For further information or questions, consumers can contact Tropicale Foods by phone at 909-563-3090. The company’s customer service line is available from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. Pacific Time, Monday through Friday.

headline: Albertsons Companies Stores in Arkansas, Louisiana, Oklahoma and Texas Voluntarily Recalls Select Items Containing Tuna Salad from Reser’s Fine Foods Due to an Ingredient Recall Linked to Possible Listeria Monocytogenes Contamination

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Albertsons Companies stores, including Randalls and Tom Thumb locations in Arkansas, Louisiana, Oklahoma, and Texas, are voluntarily recalling select tuna salad products supplied by Reser’s Fine Foods. The recall is due to a potential contamination with Listeria monocytogenes found in the breadcrumbs used as an ingredient in the tuna salad.

Listeria monocytogenes is a harmful bacterium that can cause severe and sometimes fatal infections, especially in young children, the elderly, pregnant women, and individuals with weakened immune systems. While healthy individuals may experience symptoms such as high fever, headache, stiffness, nausea, abdominal pain, and diarrhea, pregnant women are at risk of miscarriage or stillbirth due to infection.

Consumers who have purchased the affected tuna salad items are advised not to consume them. They should dispose of the products or return them to the store for a full refund. The FDA recommends thoroughly cleaning and sanitizing any surfaces or containers that may have come in contact with the recalled products to prevent cross-contamination, as Listeria can survive at refrigerated temperatures and spread easily.

To date, there have been no reported illnesses or injuries linked to these products. Anyone experiencing symptoms or concerned about potential exposure should seek medical attention. Customers with questions can contact the Albertsons Companies Customer Service Center at 1-877-723-3929, Monday through Friday, from 5 a.m. to 9 p.m. PST.

  • Clean and sanitize any surfaces or containers that came into contact with the products to prevent cross-contamination.
  • Contact Albertsons Companies Customer Service at 1-877-723-3929 (Monday to Friday, 5 a.m. to 9 p.m. PST) with questions.
  • Seek medical advice if you experience symptoms of listeriosis or have health concerns.

More information on this recall can be found in the original company announcement and FDA postings.

headline: Jewel Osco Stores in Illinois, Indiana and Iowa Voluntarily Recalls Select Items Containing Tuna Salad from Reser’s Fine Foods Due to an Ingredient Recall Linked to Possible Listeria Monocytogenes Contamination

image.caption: Jewel Osco Stores in Illinois, Indiana and Iowa Voluntarily Recalls Select Items Containing Tuna Salad from Reser’s Fine Foods Due to an Ingredient Recall Linked to Possible Listeria Monocytogenes Contamination

description: Jewel Osco announced a recall involving Tuna Salad Products after issues linked to potential foodborne illness – listeria monocytogenes. FDA recall notice...

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Jewel Osco stores located in Illinois, Indiana, and Iowa are voluntarily recalling select tuna salad products supplied by Reser’s Fine Foods. This recall is a result of a related recall by Reser’s Fine Foods due to potential contamination with Listeria monocytogenes in the breadcrumbs used as an ingredient in the tuna salad.

Listeria monocytogenes is a bacterium that can cause severe and sometimes fatal infections, especially in young children, elderly individuals, pregnant women, and those with weakened immune systems. Symptoms in healthy individuals can include high fever, severe headache, stiffness, nausea, abdominal pain, and diarrhea. Pregnant women infected with Listeria risk miscarriages or stillbirths.

Consumers who purchased these tuna salad products are advised not to consume them. Instead, they should either dispose of the products safely or return them to their local Jewel Osco store for a full refund. The FDA also recommends thorough cleaning and sanitizing of any surfaces or containers that may have come into contact with the recalled items to prevent cross-contamination, as Listeria can survive refrigeration.

To date, no injuries or adverse reactions linked to these products have been reported. Anyone experiencing illness or concerns related to consumption should seek medical advice. For questions or more information, consumers can contact the Albertsons Companies’ Customer Service Center at 1-877-723-3929, available Monday to Friday from 5 a.m. to 9 p.m. PST.

headline: Nirwana Foods Issues Allergy Alert on Undeclared Sulfites on Golden Raisin 28Oz Pouch Label
description: Nirwana Foods announced a recall involving Golden Raisins after issues linked to undeclared sulfites. FDA recall notice published in 2025.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Nirwana Foods LLC of New Jersey has announced a recall of its Golden Raisin 28-ounce pouches due to the presence of undeclared sulfites. This allergen was not listed on the product label, which poses a risk for consumers with sulfite allergies. Exposure to sulfites can trigger serious or potentially life-threatening allergic reactions in sensitive individuals.

The affected Golden Raisin 28-ounce pouch carries the lot number 24/351-2410 and an expiration date of October 31, 2025. It was distributed at Maharaja Super Market locations in New York and Villager Farmers Market stores in New York and New Jersey. The product is packaged in clear plastic and includes the UPC code 712321993144.

This recall follows routine testing by the New York State Department of Agriculture and Markets, which identified the undeclared sulfites. As of now, there have been no reports of illness or allergic reactions related to this product. Consumers who have purchased this specific lot are advised to return the item to the store for a full refund.

For additional information or questions, consumers may contact Nirwana Foods at 1 (201) 659-2200.

headline: BD Issues Update to Voluntary Global Recall of Alaris™ and BD Alaris™ Pump Modules Serviced with Legacy Bezel Kit Assemblies
description: Alaris™ and BD Alaris™ announced a recall involving Pump Modules after issues linked to may have been serviced with previously recalled bezel kit...
keywords: May have been serviced with previously recalled bezel kit assemblies

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Becton, Dickinson and Company (BD) has issued an updated voluntary global recall concerning certain Alaris™ and BD Alaris™ Pump Modules. This recall relates to pump modules that may have been serviced using previously recalled bezel kit assemblies manufactured between April 2011 and June 2017. BD became aware through customer complaints that affected bezel kit assemblies were still being used by customers or third parties to service these pumps despite a prior recall in 2019.

The recalled bezel kit assemblies contain a specific plastic material (FR-110 bezels) that can weaken over time, potentially causing the bezel bosses to separate or break. This degradation can lead to serious infusion issues, including free flow, over-infusion, under-infusion, or interruption of infusion. The recall has been linked to incidents of serious injury and patient deaths.

Because pump modules that were not originally equipped with these bezel assemblies may have been serviced with affected parts, all Alaris™ and BD Alaris™ Pump Modules are included in this recall. BD advises customers to immediately dispose of any remaining affected bezel kit assemblies and to review service records to determine if their pump modules were serviced with these parts. Visual inspection of the bezel is recommended, and any pump found with an affected bezel should be removed from service. Customers are instructed to contact BD for further guidance.

For more information, customers can visit BD’s website or call BD at 1-888-562-6018. Complaints related to this product can be reported to the BD Complaint Center at 1-844-823-5433, Monday through Friday, 9 a.m. to 6 p.m. ET, or via email at productcomplaints@bd.com.

Adverse reactions or events should be reported to the FDA’s MedWatch Program through one of the following methods:

  • Online: Complete and submit the report at www.fda.gov/medwatch/report.htm
  • Phone: Call 1-800-FDA-1088 (1-800-332-1088)
  • Mail: Download the form at www.fda.gov/MedWatch/getforms.htm or call 1-800-332-1088 to request a reporting form, then complete and return to the address on the pre-addressed form
  • Fax: Submit completed form to 1-800-FDA-0178

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Jalux Americas, Inc. (dba J.sweets) has issued a recall for certain L’espoir Brand cookies due to undeclared allergens that pose a risk to consumers with allergies or sensitivities. The affected products include L’espoir cookies containing undeclared milk and Drycapot cookies containing undeclared tree nuts, specifically almonds and macadamia nuts.

The recall involves 32 units of L’espoir cookies and 28 units of Drycapot cookies, which were sold exclusively at J.sweets retail locations in California, Illinois, and Washington. These products were available for purchase between late May and the end of June 2025. There have been no reported illnesses associated with these items at this time.

This issue was identified during an inventory audit when it was discovered that products containing milk or tree nuts were packaged without allergen information on the label. The recall is being conducted in coordination with the U.S. Food and Drug Administration to ensure consumer safety.

Consumers who purchased the affected products, identifiable by specific lot codes and best before dates, are advised to return them to the place of purchase for a full refund. headline: Chetak LLC Group Recalls Product Because of Possible Health Risk

image.caption: Chetak LLC Group Recalls Product Because of Possible Health Risk

description: Deep announced a recall involving Sprouted Moong (sprouted mung beans) after issues linked to salmonella. FDA recall notice published in 2025.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Chetak LLC Group is voluntarily recalling frozen 16 oz. packages of “Deep Sprouted Mat (Moth)” and “Deep Sprouted Moong” due to potential contamination with Salmonella. This bacterium can cause serious illness, particularly in young children, elderly individuals, and those with weakened immune systems. Symptoms of Salmonella infection include fever, diarrhea (which may be bloody), nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain. In rare cases, the infection may become more severe, affecting the bloodstream and causing conditions such as arterial infections, endocarditis, or arthritis.

The recalled sprouted beans were distributed nationwide through retail stores under specific lot numbers. No illnesses have been reported so far in relation to this issue. The contamination was identified following routine FDA testing. Production of these products has been suspended while the FDA and the company investigate the source of the contamination.

Consumers who have purchased these 16 oz. packets of “Sprouted Mat (Moth)” or “Sprouted Moong” are advised to return the products to the place of purchase for a full refund. For further information or inquiries, customers can contact the company directly at 908-209-8878.

  • Chetak San Francisco LLC, Union City, CA
  • Chetak Chicago LLC, Streamwood, IL
  • Chetak Orlando LLC, Kissimmee, FL
  • Chetak Los Angeles LLC, Pico Rivera, CA
  • Zeenat Inc., Sugarland, TX

headline: Krasniy Oktyabr Inc. USA Issues Alert on Eviscerate Dry Salted Vobla “Aral Silver”

image.caption: Krasniy Oktyabr Inc. USA Issues Alert on Eviscerate Dry Salted Vobla “Aral Silver”

description: ARAL SILVER VOBLA announced a recall involving EVISCERATE DRY SALTED VOBLA ARAL SILVER after issues linked to potential foodborne illness – clostridium...
keywords: EVISCERATE DRY SALTED VOBLA ARAL SILVER

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
KRASNIY OKTYABR INC. USA, located in Brooklyn, NY, has issued a recall for its “ARAL SILVER VOBLA” brand product named “EVISCERATE DRY SALTED VOBLA ARAL SILVER.” The recall was initiated after routine inspections by the New York State Department of Agriculture and Markets found the fish were not properly eviscerated before processing.

The affected product consists of two whole fish, vacuum packaged in clear plastic bags with a blue label, marked as “Product of Kazakhstan.” This uneviscerated condition increases the risk of Clostridium botulinum contamination, as spores tend to concentrate in the fish’s viscera, which can lead to serious foodborne illness including botulism.

Symptoms of botulism poisoning include dizziness, blurred or double vision, difficulty speaking or swallowing, muscle weakness, and breathing difficulties. Anyone experiencing these symptoms after consuming the product should seek immediate medical attention. No illnesses have been reported so far in connection with this product.

Consumers who have purchased the DRY SALTED VOBLA ARAL SILVER product are advised to return it to the place of purchase for a full refund. For further information or questions, customers can contact KRASNIY OKTYABR INC. USA at 718-858-6720.

headline: Update – Sandoz Inc. Issues Voluntary Nationwide Recall Expansion of One Additional Lot of Cefazolin for Injection Due to Vials Being Potentially Mislabeled as Penicillin G Potassium for Injection

image.caption: Update – Sandoz Inc. Issues Voluntary Nationwide Recall Expansion of One Additional Lot of Cefazolin for Injection Due to Vials Being Potentially Mislabeled as Penicillin G Potassium for Injection

description: Sandoz announced a recall involving Cefazolin for Injection, USP, 1 gm vial after issues linked to vials incorrectly labelled as penicillin g potassium for...
keywords: Cefazolin for Injection, USP, 1 gm vial
Vials incorrectly labelled as Penicillin G Potassium for Injection contain Cefazolin for Injection

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Sandoz, Inc. has announced a voluntary nationwide recall expansion of one additional lot of Cefazolin for Injection, USP, 1 gram per vial. This action follows a customer complaint in which four vials incorrectly labeled as Penicillin G Potassium for Injection, USP, 20 million Units were found within cartons of Cefazolin for Injection, USP, 1 gram per vial. Testing confirmed that these mislabeled vials actually contained Cefazolin for Injection.

The mislabeling poses a significant health risk. Administering Cefazolin according to Penicillin G Potassium dosing instructions may result in serious and potentially life-threatening adverse effects, such as treatment failure, antibiotic resistance, allergic reactions including anaphylaxis, drug interactions, and delayed recovery. To date, no adverse events have been reported related to this recall, although there has been a complaint of the incorrect product being given to a patient.

Cefazolin for Injection is commonly used to treat bacterial infections in various parts of the body and to prevent infections around the time of surgery. Penicillin G Potassium is used for serious infections including septicemia, pneumonia, meningitis, and others. While both are beta-lactam antibiotics, they have distinct indications, dosing, and susceptible organisms, making accurate labeling essential.

Sandoz is notifying customers and arranging for return of the affected products. Healthcare providers and customers who possess the impacted lot should immediately discontinue use and contact Sedgwick, the Sandoz Reverse Distributor, to coordinate return. For questions or to report adverse reactions, contact Sandoz or the FDA MedWatch program.

Product Name Vial NDC Carton NDC Lot Number Expiration Date Manufacturer Distributor
Cefazolin for Injection, USP (25 by 1g vials) 0781-3451-70 0781-3451-96 PG4360 2027-NOV Sandoz GmbH Sandoz Inc
Penicillin G Potassium for Injection, USP 0781-6136-94 N/A PG4360 2027-NOV Sandoz GmbH Sandoz Inc
Cefazolin for Injection, USP (25 by 1g vials) 0781-3451-70 0781-3451-96 PG4362 2027-NOV Sandoz GmbH Sandoz Inc
Penicillin G Potassium for Injection, USP 0781-6136-94 N/A PG4362 2027-NOV Sandoz GmbH Sandoz Inc

Instructions for customers who possess the impacted lot:

  • Immediately stop use of the affected lot.
  • Contact Sedgwick, the Sandoz Reverse Distributor, by phone at (844) 265-7409 or email at Sandoz5615@sedgwick.com to arrange product return.
  • For questions regarding the recall process, call Sedgwick at (844) 265-7409 from 8:00 AM to 5:00 PM EST, Monday through Friday.
  • Report any adverse reactions by calling Sandoz at (800) 525-8747 (8:30 AM to 5:00 PM EST, Monday–Friday, excluding holidays) or through the FDA MedWatch program:
  • Online: www.fda.gov/medwatch/report.htm
  • By mail or fax: Download forms at www.fda.gov/MedWatch/getforms.htm or call 1-800-332-1088 to request a form. Submit completed forms to the pre-addressed mail location or fax to 1-800-FDA-0178.

This recall is being conducted with the knowledge of the U.S. Food and Drug Administration. For more details, see the original Sandoz announcement.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Danone U.S. has issued a voluntary recall of YoCrunch® yogurt products sold nationwide due to the potential presence of plastic pieces in the dome topper. The plastic fragments may pose a choking hazard if consumed. This recall affects all flavors and sizes of YoCrunch® products that include a separately packaged topper but does not impact the yogurt itself.

The recalled plastic pieces are transparent and may have sharp edges, with sizes ranging from 7 to 25 millimeters in length. The recall was initiated on July 11, 2025, following consumer complaints and is being conducted in cooperation with the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA).

Consumers who have purchased the affected YoCrunch® products are advised not to consume them. For refund information, customers can contact the YoCrunch® Consumer Care Line. The company is actively working with retail partners to remove the impacted products from store shelves and address the issue promptly.

For further assistance or questions, consumers can call the YoCrunch® Consumer Care Line at 1-877-344-4886, available Monday through Friday from 9 a.m. to 6 p.m. ET.

headline: Nostrum Laboratories, Inc. Issues Voluntary Nationwide Recall of Sucralfate Tablets USP 1 Gram Within Expiry
description: Nostrum Laboratories announced a recall involving Sucralfate Tablets USP 1 gram after issues linked to company closure and discontinuation of quality...
keywords: Company closure and discontinuation of quality activities.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Nostrum Laboratories, Inc. has announced a voluntary nationwide recall of all lots of Sucralfate Tablets USP 1 gram that are currently within their expiry date. This recall is being initiated due to the company’s closure and the discontinuation of quality control activities following its Chapter 11 bankruptcy filing on September 30, 2024. The recall specifically affects Sucralfate Tablets USP 1 gram manufactured by Nostrum Laboratories after June 2023.

The recalled product was distributed to wholesalers, retailers, manufacturers, medical facilities, and repackagers across the United States. Nostrum Laboratories advises that any further distribution or use of remaining stock should stop immediately, as the company can no longer guarantee that these products meet the required identity, strength, quality, and purity standards throughout their labeled shelf life.

Consumers and healthcare providers are encouraged to stop using Suspected lots and contact Nostrum Laboratories directly with any questions. No returns of the product will be accepted; instead, all remaining product should be destroyed. While no adverse events related to this recall have been reported, patients should consult their healthcare provider if they experience any problems potentially related to the product.

This recall is being carried out in cooperation with the U.S. Food and Drug Administration. Adverse reactions or quality issues can be reported to the FDA’s MedWatch Adverse Event Reporting program through various channels.

ProductStrengthLot NumbersExpiration Dates
Sucralfate Tablets USP1 gramAll lots manufactured after June 2023All within expiry

For reporting adverse events to the FDA MedWatch program, consumers and healthcare professionals may use the following options:

  • Submit a report online at www.fda.gov/medwatch/report.htm
  • Download and complete a reporting form from www.fda.gov/MedWatch/getforms.htm
  • Request a reporting form by calling 1-800-332-1088, then mail it to the address on the form or fax it to 1-800-FDA-0178

For additional information or questions regarding this recall, Nostrum Laboratories can be contacted via email at recallcoordinator@nostrumlabsrecall.com.

headline: World Market Recalls Emek Spread Pistachio Cacao Cream with Kadayif Due to Salmonella Contamination

image.caption: World Market Recalls Emek Spread Pistachio Cacao Cream with Kadayif Due to Salmonella Contamination

description: Emek announced a recall involving Spread Pistachio Cacao Cream with Kadayif after issues linked to salmonella. FDA recall notice published in 2025.
keywords: Spread Pistachio Cacao Cream with Kadayif

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
World Market has announced a recall of its Emek Spread Pistachio Cacao Cream with Kadayif, 9.7 oz, due to potential contamination with Salmonella. The affected product carries a best before date of April 01, 2027, and batch number 250401. Testing by the FDA confirmed the presence of Salmonella in samples of this product.

Salmonella can cause serious and sometimes fatal infections, especially in young children, the elderly, and individuals with weakened immune systems. In healthy individuals, infection may lead to symptoms such as fever, diarrhea (which may be bloody), nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain. In rare cases, Salmonella infection can cause more severe complications, including bloodstream infections and conditions like endocarditis and arthritis.

The recalled product was distributed in World Market stores across multiple states from June 11 to July 9, 2025. Consumers who have purchased this item are advised not to consume it and to return it to the place of purchase for a full refund or discard it. As of now, no illnesses have been reported in connection with this product.

For additional questions, consumers can contact World Market Customer Service at 877.967.5362, available daily from 7:00 am to 12:00 am EST.

Product Size Batch Number UPC Best Before Date
Emek Spread Pistachio Cacao Cream with Kadayif 9.7 oz 250401 8 69652 10130 1 April 01, 2027

The product is packaged in a clear glass jar with the date code etched on the top of the lid. It was distributed primarily in World Market retail stores in the following states:

headline: Updated Release: Hartford Bakery, Inc. Issues Allergy Alert on Undeclared Hazelnuts in “Lewis Bake Shop Artisan Style ½ Loaf”
description: Lewis Bake Shop announced a recall involving Artisan Style 1/2 Loaf after issues linked to undeclared hazelnuts. FDA recall notice published in 2025.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Hartford Bakery, Inc. has issued a voluntary recall for certain lots of its “Lewis Bake Shop Artisan Style ½ Loaf” due to the presence of undeclared hazelnuts. This poses a risk of serious allergic reactions for individuals with nut allergies or sensitivities. The recall involves twelve specific lot codes of the product, which were distributed across multiple states in the Midwest and Southern U.S.

The affected product is packaged in a flexible plastic bag, labeled with a net weight of 12 ounces (340 grams) and an expiration date of July 13, 2025. The packaging includes a UPC code 24126018152. While the label indicates the bread “May Contain Tree Nuts,” it does not specify that it “Contains Hazelnuts,” which led to the recall once hazelnuts were found in the product.

The issue came to light after approximately 883 loaves from six production lots were discovered to contain visible hazelnuts, attributed to a packaging error involving hazelnut-containing bread. There have been no major reports of illness, although one consumer reported digestive discomfort and others noticed the nuts before eating.

Consumers who have purchased the recalled “Lewis Bake Shop Artisan Style ½ Loaf” are advised to return the product to the place of purchase to receive a full refund. For questions or further information, customers can contact Hartford Bakery at 1-812-425-4642, Monday through Friday from 8:00 am to 3:00 pm CST, excluding holidays.

headline: LLK Trading Inc. Recalls “Needle Mushrooms” Because of Possible Health Risk

image.caption: LLK Trading Inc. Recalls “Needle Mushrooms” Because of Possible Health Risk

description: LLK TRADING INC. announced a recall involving Needle mushroom after issues linked to potential foodborne illness – listeria monocytogenes. FDA recall notice...

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
LLK TRADING INC., based in Linden, New Jersey, has issued a recall for its 200g packages of “Needle Mushrooms” due to possible contamination with Listeria monocytogenes. This bacterium can cause severe and sometimes fatal infections, particularly in young children, elderly individuals, pregnant women, and those with weakened immune systems.

Symptoms of Listeria infection in healthy individuals may include high fever, severe headache, stiffness, nausea, abdominal pain, and diarrhea. In pregnant women, the infection can lead to miscarriages and stillbirths. The recalled product was distributed to Bally Produce Corp. in Maspeth, NY, and then sold to DATANG SUPERMARKET INC.

The affected Needle Mushrooms are packaged in 200 gram containers with a clear plastic top and a blue, non-transparent bottom. So far, no illnesses have been reported related to this recall. The contamination was discovered following routine FDA testing.

Consumers who have purchased the recalled product should not consume it and are advised to return it to the place of purchase for a full refund. Sales of the product have been suspended as the FDA and LLK TRADING INC. continue to investigate the contamination source. For questions, consumers can contact the company Monday through Friday from 9:30 am to 3:00 pm (EST) at (908)-290-3061.

Product Package Size Packaging Description Distribution Locations

headline: Wiet Peeters Farm Products Limited RECALLS “Aunt Mid’s Fresh Sliced Mushrooms, Peeters Mushroom Farm Cremini Sliced and Peeters Mushroom Farm Thick Slice Mushroom " Because of Possible Listeria Monocytogenes Contamination

image.caption: Wiet Peeters Farm Products Limited RECALLS “Aunt Mid’s Fresh Sliced Mushrooms, Peeters Mushroom Farm Cremini Sliced and Peeters Mushroom Farm Thick Slice Mushroom " Because of Possible Listeria Monocytogenes Contamination

description: Peeters and Aunt Mid’s announced a recall involving Fresh sliced and Cremini sliced mushrooms after issues linked to potential foodborne illness - listeria...
keywords: Fresh sliced and Cremini sliced mushrooms

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Wiet Peeters Farm Products Limited of Charing Cross, Ontario, Canada, has issued a recall for certain sliced mushroom products due to possible contamination with Listeria monocytogenes. The affected products include Aunt Mid’s Fresh Sliced Mushrooms, Peeters Mushroom Farm Cremini Sliced Mushrooms, and Peeters Mushroom Farm Thick Slice Mushrooms. These items were distributed in Michigan and Ohio.

Listeria monocytogenes is a harmful bacterium that can cause serious infections, particularly in young children, the elderly, and individuals with weakened immune systems. In healthy people, symptoms may include high fever, severe headache, stiffness, nausea, abdominal pain, and diarrhea. Pregnant women are at increased risk as infection can lead to miscarriages and stillbirths.

The recall was prompted by testing conducted by the Canadian Food Inspection Agency, which identified Listeria monocytogenes in 227g packages of Aunt Mid’s Fresh Sliced Mushrooms. The other recalled products were produced on the same manufacturing line and are therefore included in the recall as a precaution.

No illnesses related to these products have been reported to date. Consumers who have purchased any of these recalled mushrooms are advised to return them to the place of purchase for a full refund.

Peeters Mushroom Farm Cremini Sliced 227g, flat black plastic package 0 68414 96960 3 BEST BEFORE 25JL04 (stamped on side)

headline: Sheehan Brothers Vending Issues a Voluntary Recall Due to an Undeclared Sesame Allergen

image.caption: Sheehan Brothers Vending Issues a Voluntary Recall Due to an Undeclared Sesame Allergen

description: Sheehan Brothers Vending announced a recall involving Cheeseburgers, Spicy Chicken Sandwich, Italian Mini Subs, Pepperoni Pizza Sub, Chili Cheese Coney and...
keywords: Cheeseburgers, Spicy Chicken Sandwich, Italian Mini Subs, Pepperoni Pizza Sub, Chili Cheese Coney and BBQ Riblet w/coleslaw

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Sheehan Brothers Vending has issued a voluntary recall of several of its food products due to an undeclared sesame allergen. The affected items include Cheeseburgers, Spicy Chicken Sandwich, Italian Mini Subs, Pepperoni Pizza Sub, Chili Cheese Coney, and BBQ Riblet with coleslaw. The recall was prompted by the absence of sesame allergen information on the ingredient labels.

The products were distributed in individually wrapped plastic packaging and available between July 2, 2025, and July 8, 2025. They were sold through Micro Markets and vending machine locations throughout Ohio, Northern Kentucky, and Eastern Indiana. The discovery of the undeclared allergen occurred during a routine inspection by the Ohio Department of Agriculture.

There have been no reported illnesses linked to the recall at this time. However, consumers with a sesame allergy or severe sensitivity are at risk of serious or life-threatening allergic reactions if they consume these items. Anyone experiencing allergic symptoms or foodborne illness after consuming these products should seek medical attention immediately.

Consumers who have purchased any of the affected products are advised to dispose of them and contact Sheehan Brothers Vending for a replacement. For further information or questions about the recall, individuals can reach out to Matt Ryan at 937-325-2357.
authorityLinks: FDA recall page here.

facts.product: Cheeseburgers, Spicy Chicken Sandwich, Italian Mini Subs, Pepperoni Pizza Sub, Chili Cheese Coney and BBQ Riblet w/coleslaw

headline: Hartford Bakery, Inc. Issues Allergy Alert on Undeclared Hazelnuts in “Lewis Bake Shop Artisan Style ½ Loaf”

image.caption: Hartford Bakery, Inc. Issues Allergy Alert on Undeclared Hazelnuts in “Lewis Bake Shop Artisan Style ½ Loaf”

description: Lewis Bake Shop announced a recall involving Artisan Style 1/2 Loaf after issues linked to undeclared hazelnuts. FDA recall notice published in 2025.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Hartford Bakery, Inc. has issued a voluntary recall of six lots of its “Lewis Bake Shop Artisan Style ½ Loaf” bread due to the presence of undeclared hazelnuts. This recall was initiated after it was discovered that approximately 883 loaves containing visible hazelnuts were mistakenly packaged in bags labeled as the Artisan Style ½ Loaf. The packaging does not clearly indicate the presence of hazelnuts, which poses a serious risk for individuals with nut allergies or severe sensitivities.

The recalled products were distributed to retailers across multiple states including Michigan, Wisconsin, Illinois, Indiana, Ohio, Kentucky, Tennessee, Georgia, Arkansas, Missouri, Alabama, and Mississippi, with distribution through stores such as Kroger and Walmart. The bakery identified that the issue was caused by a packaging error involving the mix-up of hazelnut-containing bread with the white bread packaging.

To date, no major injuries or allergic reactions have been reported. However, Hartford Bakery is aware of one consumer who experienced digestive discomfort and others who noticed nuts in the product before consumption. Consumers who have purchased these products are advised to return them to the store for a full refund. For further information, Hartford Bakery can be reached at 1-812-425-4642 during business hours.

headline: Natureen International Inc. Recalls Wei-Chuan Dried Black Fungus Slice (2.5oz) Because of Possible Health Risk

image.caption: Natureen International Inc. Recalls Wei-Chuan Dried Black Fungus Slice (2.5oz) Because of Possible Health Risk

description: Wei-Chuan announced a recall involving Dried Black Fungus Slice after issues linked to salmonella. FDA recall notice published in 2025.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Natureen International Inc., based in Walnut, California, has announced a recall of 244 bags of Wei-Chuan Dried Black Fungus Slice (2.5oz) due to potential contamination with Salmonella. This bacteria can cause serious health risks, particularly for young children, elderly individuals, and those with weakened immune systems. Symptoms of Salmonella infection include fever, diarrhea (which may be bloody), nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain. In rare cases, the infection can spread to the bloodstream, leading to more severe conditions such as arterial infections, endocarditis, and arthritis.

The affected product was distributed across Illinois, Indiana, Iowa, Kansas, Kentucky, Michigan, Minnesota, Missouri, Nebraska, Ohio, and Wisconsin. Distribution occurred through direct deliveries to retail stores and supermarkets from the Wei-Chuan Chicago Branch. Production has been suspended as the company and the FDA investigate the contamination source. No illnesses have been reported to date related to this recall.

Consumers who have purchased the recalled Wei-Chuan Dried Black Fungus Slice (2.5oz) are advised to return the product to the place of purchase for a full refund. For questions or further information, customers may contact Natureen International Inc. or the Wei-Chuan Chicago Office during their respective business hours.

Product Lot Number Expiration Date Package Size Distribution Areas

  • Natureen International Inc.: 626-961-9813 (Pacific Time, 9 AM – 5:30 PM)
  • Wei-Chuan Chicago Office: 708-352-8886 ext. 5201 (Central Time, 8:30 AM – 5 PM)

This recall is being conducted with the knowledge and cooperation of the U.S. Food and Drug Administration.

headline: CHS Inc. Recalls Payback Champion Lamb Feed Due to Elevated Copper Health Risk

image.caption: CHS Inc. Recalls Payback Champion Lamb Feed Due to Elevated Copper Health Risk

description: Payback® announced a recall involving Champion Lamb Text B30 with Power Booster after issues linked to elevated levels of copper. FDA recall notice...
keywords: Champion Lamb Text B30 with Power Booster
Animal & Veterinary Food & Beverages Foodborne Illness

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
CHS Inc. is voluntarily recalling approximately seven tons of Payback® Champion Lamb Text B30 with Power Booster due to potentially elevated levels of copper, which poses a health risk to sheep. Copper toxicity symptoms in sheep include lethargy, anemia, teeth grinding, increased thirst, loss of appetite, pale or yellow mucous membranes, discolored urine, and inability to stand. Death may occur within one to two days after symptoms appear. Continued consumption of the recalled product may result in serious illness or death.

This product was manufactured at the CHS facility in Great Falls, Montana, and distributed to dealers in Montana, North Dakota, and Wyoming after February 19, 2025. It was sold in 40 lb. Payback Champion feed bags directly to dealers, where end-use customers may have purchased it. The specific lot number and label date involved in the recall can be found on the feed label below the feeding directions.

Customers who have purchased this product should immediately stop using it and return it to their local dealer for a full refund. If animals have consumed this feed and display symptoms of copper toxicity, veterinary advice should be sought promptly. For further information or questions, customers can contact CHS at ANProducts@chsinc.com.

Product NameLot NumberLabel Date
Payback® Champion Lamb Text B30 with Power BoosterM#13430002/14/25

headline: Mondelēz Global LLC Conducts U.S. Voluntary Recall of Four Carton Sizes of RITZ Peanut Butter Cracker Sandwiches Due to Labeling Error

image.caption: Mondelēz Global LLC Conducts U.S. Voluntary Recall of Four Carton Sizes of RITZ Peanut Butter Cracker Sandwiches Due to Labeling Error

description: Ritz announced a recall involving Peanut butter cracker sandwiches after issues linked to undeclared allergen – peanut. FDA recall notice published in 2025.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Mondelēz Global LLC has announced a voluntary recall of four carton sizes of RITZ Peanut Butter Cracker Sandwiches sold across the United States due to a labeling error. The affected products may contain individually wrapped packs labeled as Cheese variety even though the actual contents are Peanut Butter varieties. This poses a serious risk to individuals with peanut allergies or severe sensitivities, who could experience life-threatening allergic reactions.

The recall specifically includes 8-pack, 20-pack, and 40-pack cartons of RITZ Peanut Butter Cracker Sandwiches, along with the 20-pack RITZ Filled Cracker Sandwich Variety Pack cartons. While the outer cartons are labeled correctly and include allergen advisories that the product contains peanuts, the inner individually wrapped packages may have incorrect Cheese labels. No other RITZ products or Mondelēz Global LLC products are affected.

There have been no reports of injury or illness linked to this issue so far. The recall was initiated after a supplier error caused defects in film packaging rolls, leading to the mislabeling. Consumers who are allergic to peanuts are strongly advised to not consume the recalled products and to discard any affected items.

For more information, consumers can contact Mondelēz Global LLC at 1-844-366-1171, available 24/7, with Consumer Relations specialists reachable Monday through Friday from 9 am to 6 pm ET. This recall is being carried out in coordination with the U.S. Food and Drug Administration.

image.caption: Mellace Family Brands California, Inc. Issues An Allergy Alert On Undeclared Milk Allergen In Wegmans Semi-Sweet Chocolate Nonpareils

description: Wegmans announced a recall involving Semi-sweet Chocolate after issues linked to undeclared milk allergen. FDA recall notice published in 2025.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Mellace Family Brands California, Inc. of Warren, OH, has issued a recall for Wegmans Semi-Sweet Chocolate Nonpareils due to the presence of an undeclared milk allergen. This poses a risk of serious or life-threatening allergic reactions for individuals with milk allergies or severe sensitivities. No illnesses related to this issue have been reported so far.

The affected products were distributed through Wegmans retail stores in Delaware, Maryland, Massachusetts, North Carolina, New Jersey, New York, Pennsylvania, Virginia, and Washington D.C. The product comes in an 18.5oz (1LB 2.5OZ) plastic tub labeled Wegmans Semi-Sweet Chocolate Nonpareils, UPC 0 77890 49787 6.

The recall was initiated after it was discovered that the packaging did not disclose the presence of milk, caused by a temporary manufacturing process breakdown at the supplier. Consumers who have purchased the affected product are advised to return it to the store for a full refund.

For further questions, customers can contact Wegmans Food Markets at 1-855-934-3663 daily from 8:00 AM to 8:00 PM EST.

headline: Sandoz Inc. Issues Voluntary Nationwide Recall of One Lot of Cefazolin for Injection Due to Product Mispackaging

image.caption: Sandoz Inc. Issues Voluntary Nationwide Recall of One Lot of Cefazolin for Injection Due to Product Mispackaging

description: Sandoz announced a recall involving Cefazolin for Injection, USP, 1 gm vial after issues linked to potential presence of penicillin g potassium injection...
keywords: Cefazolin for Injection, USP, 1 gm vial
Potential Presence of Penicillin G Potassium Injection Vial

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Sandoz Inc. has initiated a voluntary nationwide recall of one lot of Cefazolin for Injection, USP, 1 gram per vial due to product mispackaging. This recall affects a single lot in which four vials of Penicillin G Potassium for Injection, USP, were mistakenly included in cartons labeled as Cefazolin for Injection. Each carton contains 25 vials.

The presence of Penicillin G Potassium in place of Cefazolin poses serious health risks, including potential allergic reactions such as anaphylaxis, ineffective treatment of infections, antibiotic resistance, drug interactions, cardiac arrhythmias due to elevated potassium levels especially in patients with kidney impairment, and delayed recovery. Although Sandoz has received a complaint about the incorrect product being administered to a patient, no adverse events have been reported to date in connection with this recall.

Cefazolin for Injection is an antibacterial medication used to treat or prevent bacterial infections in various parts of the body, including pneumonia and pre/post-surgical infections. Penicillin G Potassium treats a range of serious infections such as septicemia, meningitis, anthrax, and others. Despite both being beta-lactam antibiotics, their approved uses, dosing, and target infections differ.

Healthcare providers and customers who have the affected lot are advised to stop using it immediately. Sandoz is contacting customers and arranging for the return of the recalled product. For assistance, customers should contact Sedgwick, the Sandoz reverse distributor.

Product Name Dosage Form Lot Number Quantity per Carton Issue
Cefazolin for Injection, USP 1 gm vial One (1) lot (specific lot number not provided) 25 vials per carton Four vials of Penicillin G Potassium for Injection included by mistake

Contact information for affected customers:

  • Return and recall inquiries: Sedgwick, phone (844) 491-7872 or email Sandoz6004@sedgwick.com
  • Recall process questions: Sedgwick customer service at (844) 491-7872, 8:00 AM - 5:00 PM EST, Monday to Friday
  • Report adverse reactions: Sandoz at (800) 525-8747, 8:30 AM - 5:00 PM EST, Monday to Friday (except national holidays)
  • FDA MedWatch Reporting options:
    • Online: www.fda.gov/medwatch/report.htm
    • Mail/Fax: Download form at www.fda.gov/MedWatch/getforms.htm or call 1-800-332-1088 to request a form. Submit completed form by mail or fax to 1-800-FDA-0178

This recall is conducted with the knowledge of the U.S. Food and Drug Administration.

headline: Face Rock Creamery Voluntarily Recalls Vampire Slayer Garlic Cheddar Curds Because of Possible Health Risk

image.caption: Face Rock Creamery Voluntarily Recalls Vampire Slayer Garlic Cheddar Curds Because of Possible Health Risk

description: Face Rock Creamery announced a recall involving Vampire Slayer Garlic Cheddar Curds after issues linked to potential foodborne illness – listeria...

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Face Rock Creamery of Bandon, Oregon, has initiated a voluntary recall of two specific lots of its Vampire Slayer Garlic Cheddar Curds (6oz cups) due to potential contamination with Listeria monocytogenes. This bacteria can cause serious and sometimes fatal infections, particularly in young children, elderly individuals, pregnant women, and those with weakened immune systems.

The affected product was distributed through Trader Joe’s stores in Northern California (including Monterey, Fresno, and locations north) and Northern Nevada (Carson City, Reno, Sparks). Although no illnesses have been reported to date, the company discovered the contamination through routine product testing.

Consumers who purchased the affected Vampire Slayer Garlic Cheddar Curds are advised not to consume the product and to return it to the place of purchase for a full refund. For questions, customers can reach the Face Rock Creamery Customer Care team via email at info@facerockcreamery.com. Customer support is available from 8 AM to 5 PM PST.

Face Rock Creamery is cooperating with the U.S. FDA and the Oregon Department of Agriculture while investigating the root cause of this contamination to ensure product safety and quality.

Consumers concerned about this recall are encouraged to follow these steps:

  • Do not consume the product if it matches the recall information.
  • Return the product to the place of purchase for a refund.

headline: Sabores Bakery, Dba Sabores A Tu Mesa, Issues Allergy Alert on Undeclared Milk in Mousse Desserts

image.caption: Sabores Bakery, Dba Sabores A Tu Mesa, Issues Allergy Alert on Undeclared Milk in Mousse Desserts

description: Sabores A Tu Mesa announced a recall involving Mousse Desserts after issues linked to undeclared milk allergen. FDA recall notice published in 2025.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Sabores Fit Bakery, operating under the brand Sabores A Tu Mesa, has issued a recall for its Mousse Desserts due to the presence of undeclared allergens. The products may contain milk, eggs, soy ingredients (soybean oil, soy lecithin), wheat, and tree nuts (almonds, hazelnuts) that are not listed on the packaging. This poses a serious risk of allergic reaction, including life-threatening conditions, for individuals sensitive or allergic to these ingredients.

The affected mousse desserts were distributed in retail stores within the Kissimmee and Orlando areas of Florida. These products are packaged in 8 oz plastic cups and were sold under the Sabores A Tu Mesa brand name in the following flavors: Choco Mousse, Passion Fruit Mousse, 4 Milk Mousse, 3 Milk Mousse, and 3 Milk Strawberry. The expiration dates on the recalled products range from June 26 to July 02, 2025.

No illnesses related to the consumption of these mousse desserts have been reported so far. The recall follows a routine inspection that revealed the allergens were not properly declared on the product packaging. Sabores Fit Bakery is taking corrective measures to improve labeling accuracy, product traceability, and overall food safety protocols.

Consumers who have purchased any of the affected mousse desserts should return them to the place of purchase for a full refund. For further inquiries, customers may contact Sabores Fit Bakery at 1-919-579-7694.

headline: International Foodsource, LLC. Issues Allergy Alert in Nonpareil, Semi-Sweet Chocolate (Christmas Seeds) Sold as Dark Chocolate Nonpareils

image.caption: International Foodsource, LLC. Issues Allergy Alert in Nonpareil, Semi-Sweet Chocolate (Christmas Seeds) Sold as Dark Chocolate Nonpareils

description: SE Grocers announced a recall involving Dark chocolate nonpareils after issues linked to se grocers. FDA recall notice published in 2025.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
International Foodsource, LLC of Randolph, NJ, is recalling 10 oz packages of Southeastern Grocers Dark Chocolate Nonpareils due to the presence of an undeclared milk allergen. This poses a risk of serious or potentially life-threatening allergic reactions for individuals allergic or sensitive to milk.

The affected Dark Chocolate Nonpareils were distributed by C&S Wholesale Grocers (Southeastern Grocers) and were available at retail stores located in Florida (Miami, Baldwin, Plant City) and Hammond, Louisiana. The product is packaged in clear 10 oz bags labeled as SE Grocers Dark Chocolate Nonpareils.

This recall was prompted after International Foodsource, LLC was notified on June 16, 2025, by their supplier, Weaver Nut Company of Lebanon, PA. Weaver Nut Company voluntarily recalled its Nonpareil, Semi-Sweet Chocolate (Christmas Seeds) after discovering the presence of milk was not listed in the ingredients or allergen labeling. Currently, the root cause and corrective action details from the supplier have not been received.

To date, no illnesses associated with this recall have been reported. Consumers who have purchased the product are advised to return it to the place of purchase. For additional information or questions, consumers can contact International Foodsource, LLC at 973-361-7044, Monday through Friday, 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. EST, or via email at customerservice@intlfoodsource.com.

headline: Weaver Nut Company Inc., Issues Allergy Alert on Undeclared Milk in Chocolate Nonpareils

image.caption: Weaver Nut Company Inc., Issues Allergy Alert on Undeclared Milk in Chocolate Nonpareils

description: Weaver Nut Company announced a recall involving Semi sweet chocolate pareils with white or Christmas colored seeds after issues linked to undeclared milk...
keywords: Semi sweet chocolate pareils with white or Christmas colored seeds

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Weaver Nut Company, Inc. has issued a recall for certain lots of its semi-sweet chocolate nonpareils with white or Christmas colored seeds due to the presence of undeclared milk allergens. This situation poses a risk of serious or life-threatening allergic reactions for individuals with milk allergies or severe sensitivities.

The affected products were distributed across the United States and available at various retail and grocery stores. The recall was initiated after a wholesale customer complaint and subsequent laboratory tests confirmed the presence of milk in the impacted lots, which do not declare milk on their labels. To date, no illnesses have been reported in connection with these products.

Consumers with a milk allergy or sensitivity who have purchased these products are strongly advised not to consume them. They should either discard the product immediately or return it to the place of purchase for a full refund.

For questions or additional information, consumers may contact Chris Westerhoff, Customer Service Manager at Weaver Nut Company, Inc., by phone at 717-738-3781 ext. 122 or via email at cwesterhoff@weavernut.com. Customer service is available Monday through Friday, from 9 a.m. to 4:30 p.m. EST.

headline: Medtech Products Inc. Issues Nationwide Recall of Little Remedies® Honey Cough Syrup Due to Microbial Contamination

image.caption: Medtech Products Inc. Issues Nationwide Recall of Little Remedies® Honey Cough Syrup Due to Microbial Contamination

description: Little Remedies announced a recall involving Honey Cough Syrup after issues linked to potential foodborne illness – bacillus cereus. FDA recall notice...

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Medtech Products Inc. has issued a nationwide recall of Little Remedies® Honey Cough Syrup due to potential microbial contamination with Bacillus cereus, which can cause foodborne illness. The affected product was distributed across the United States through retailers and online from December 14, 2022, through June 4, 2025.

The recall includes several lots of the 4 FL OZ (118 mL) amber bottled Honey Cough Syrup. The Lot Code appears both on the bottle label and on the bottom of the outer carton. No other Little Remedies® products are impacted by this recall. To date, no serious adverse events have been reported related to this issue.

Consumers who have this product should stop using it immediately and consult a healthcare provider if they experience any related health problems. Medtech Products Inc. is offering reimbursements for purchases of the recalled lots. For refund requests or further questions, consumers can contact the company via email, website, or phone during business hours.

Adverse reactions or quality issues may also be reported to the FDA's MedWatch program online, by mail, or by fax. This recall is being conducted in cooperation with the U.S. Food and Drug Administration.

Adverse event reports can be submitted to the FDA MedWatch program:

  • Regular Mail or Fax: Download form at www.fda.gov/MedWatch/getforms.htm or call 1-800-332-1088 to request a form. Completed forms can be mailed to the address on the pre-addressed form or faxed to 1-800-FDA-0178.

headline: Meijer Issues Recall on Frederik’s Dark Chocolate Almonds Due to Presence of Undeclared Cashews

image.caption: Meijer Issues Recall on Frederik’s Dark Chocolate Almonds Due to Presence of Undeclared Cashews

description: Frederick’s by Meijer announced a recall involving Dark Chocolate Almonds after issues linked to undeclared cashews. FDA recall notice published in 2025.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Meijer has issued a recall for certain packages of Frederik’s Dark Chocolate Almonds due to the presence of undeclared cashews. The affected products may contain dark chocolate-covered cashews, which are not listed on the label. This poses a risk of serious or life-threatening allergic reactions for individuals allergic or highly sensitive to cashews.

The recall applies to Frederik’s Dark Chocolate Almonds sold in black stand-up pouches and 8-count, 1.5-ounce multi-pack boxes available at Meijer stores in Michigan, Indiana, Illinois, Ohio, Kentucky, and Wisconsin. The products have specific sell-by dates ranging from May 5, 2026, to May 28, 2026. Meijer has not reported any illnesses linked to the recall so far.

Customers who have cashew allergies or sensitivities should stop using these products immediately and return them to any Meijer store’s customer service desk for a full refund. For further inquiries, Meijer can be contacted at 800-543-3704 daily from 7 a.m. to 1 a.m. EDT. Consumers with health concerns should contact their healthcare provider.

headline: Fuentes Farms, LLC Recalls Product Because of Possible Health Risk

image.caption: Fuentes Farms, LLC Recalls Product Because of Possible Health Risk

description: Fuentes Farms Inc announced a recall involving Cucumbers in bushel boxes after issues linked to potential foodborne illness – salmonella. FDA recall notice...

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Fuentes Farms LLC of McAllen, Texas, has issued a recall of 71 boxes of fresh cucumbers due to a potential contamination with Salmonella. This bacterium can cause serious and sometimes fatal infections, particularly in young children, elderly individuals, and those with weakened immune systems. Healthy individuals infected with Salmonella may experience symptoms such as fever, diarrhea (which may be bloody), nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain. In rare cases, the infection can become more severe, leading to bloodstream infections and other complications.

The recalled cucumbers were distributed in Texas through local vendors at flea markets in the McAllen and Alamo areas. The affected product carries a Fuentes Farms label with Lot number 357. These cucumbers are packaged in 40-pound (1-1/9 bushel) boxes marked "vegetables" on a black background. Although no illnesses have been reported in connection with this recall and it is not linked to any ongoing food outbreaks, Fuentes Farms LLC is notifying customers and urging anyone who purchased cucumbers from this lot between May 31 and June 3 to stop sale or consumption of the product immediately.

The recall follows a routine FDA sample that detected Salmonella in the finished product. Production and distribution have been halted while the company and FDA continue investigating the cause. Consumers who have purchased cucumbers labeled with Lot # 357 should dispose of them or return them to the place of purchase.

For further information or inquiries, customers can contact Fuentes Farms LLC at 1-956-340-8653 (Monday through Friday, 9:00 a.m. to 2:00 p.m. CDT) or via email at sales@fuentesfarmsllc.com.

Product Lot Number Package Size Distribution Area Recall Dates
Fresh Cucumbers with Fuentes Farms Label 357 40 lbs (1-1/9 bushel boxes) Texas (McAllen and Alamo flea markets) May 31 to June 3
  • Do not consume or sell cucumbers from Lot #357 purchased during the recall period.
  • Dispose of the cucumbers safely or return them to the place of purchase.
  • Contact Fuentes Farms LLC for questions at 1-956-340-8653 (M-F, 9:00 a.m.–2:00 p.m. CDT) or email sales@fuentesfarmsllc.com.

headline: Vita-Warehouse Corp. Issues Allergy Alert on Undeclared Peanut Allergen in ALDI Welby®, Berkley Jensen®, and VitaGlobe™ Vitamin B12 Gummy Products

image.caption: Vita-Warehouse Corp. Issues Allergy Alert on Undeclared Peanut Allergen in ALDI Welby®, Berkley Jensen®, and VitaGlobe™ Vitamin B12 Gummy Products

description: Welby, Berkely Jensen and VitaGlobe announced a recall involving Gummy products after issues linked to undeclared allergen - peanut. FDA recall notice...

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Vita Warehouse Corp. has issued a voluntary recall for one lot of specific Vitamin B12 gummy products under the Welby®, Berkley Jensen®, and VitaGlobe™ brands. The recall is due to the possible presence of undeclared peanut allergen in these products, which poses a serious risk to individuals allergic or sensitive to peanuts. The affected products were distributed nationwide through ALDI and BJ’s retail stores, as well as online.

The recall concerns products with lot number 248046601 and expiration date October 2026. No allergic reactions or illnesses related to these products have been reported so far. The issue was identified after routine testing by ALDI revealed a potential presence of peanut allergen despite the products being labeled as peanut-free. Vita Warehouse Corp. conducted additional internal testing that did not detect peanuts; however, the recall is being conducted out of caution to protect consumer safety.

Consumers allergic to peanuts who have purchased the affected lot are urged to stop using the product immediately and return it to the place of purchase for a full refund or discard it. Anyone experiencing an allergic reaction should seek medical attention promptly. For questions, consumers can contact Vita Warehouse Corp. at 1-855-214-0100 during business hours.

This recall is being done with the knowledge of the U.S. Food and Drug Administration to ensure product safety and consumer trust. No other lots or products from Welby®, Berkley Jensen®, or VitaGlobe™ are impacted.

headline: Turkana Food Inc. Recall Flora Dried Apricots with Undeclared Sulfites on Product Labeling Because of Possible Health Risk

image.caption: Turkana Food Inc. Recall Flora Dried Apricots with Undeclared Sulfites on Product Labeling Because of Possible Health Risk

description: Floria announced a recall involving Dried Apricots after issues linked to potential or undeclared allergen - sulfites. FDA recall notice published in 2025.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Turkana Food Inc., based in Kenilworth, NJ, is recalling 352 cases of Floria Dried Apricots due to undeclared sulfites on the product label. The presence of sulfites, a potential allergen, was not disclosed on the packaging, presenting a health risk to sulfite-sensitive consumers.

The affected Floria Dried Apricots were distributed across multiple states including Florida, Kentucky, Virginia, New York, New Jersey, Tennessee, Massachusetts, Texas, Illinois, Indiana, Michigan, Rhode Island, Pennsylvania, North Carolina, Maryland, Ohio, Alabama, Missouri, and California. The recall is limited to the 200-gram paper packaging with specific lot and expiration details as noted below.

This action follows a routine sampling by the New York State Department of Agriculture and Markets that detected sulfites in the finished product which were not listed on the label. As of June 12, 2025, no illnesses have been reported related to this issue. Turkana Food Inc. has halted production and distribution while working with the FDA to resolve the labeling problem.

Consumers who have purchased Floria Dried Apricots with the specified lot number are advised not to eat the product and should return it to the place of purchase for a full refund. Questions can be directed to Turkana Food Inc. at the provided contact number during business hours.

Product Lot Number UPC Expiration Date
Floria Dried Apricots, 200 Gram Paper Packaging 440090478-15-333 2539560010 11/2026

  • Return the product to the place of purchase for a full refund.
  • Contact Turkana Food Inc. at 908-810-8800, Monday through Friday, 8am to 6pm EST, for further questions.

headline: Medtronic Announces Voluntary Recall of Select Newport™ HT70 and Newport™ HT70 Plus Ventilators and Certain Related Newport™ Service Parts

image.caption: Medtronic Announces Voluntary Recall of Select Newport™ HT70 and Newport™ HT70 Plus Ventilators and Certain Related Newport™ Service Parts

description: Newport™ announced a recall involving Newport™ HT70 and HT70 Plus ventilators and certain related service parts after issues linked to device & drug safety...
keywords: Newport™ HT70 and HT70 Plus ventilators and certain related service parts

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Medtronic has issued a voluntary recall of certain Newport™ HT70 and Newport™ HT70 Plus ventilators, as well as related Newport™ service parts, due to a potential safety defect. The U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA) has classified this recall as a Class I recall, indicating a serious risk to health.

Investigations identified potential failure in two capacitors on the ventilator’s controller Printed Circuit Board Assembly (PCBA), which could cause the device to shut down unexpectedly or cause the shutdown alert alarm to fail. Such failures may result in inadequate ventilation, posing serious risks including reduced oxygen levels, increased carbon dioxide levels, brain injury, or death.

Since May 2025, Medtronic has advised customers to discontinue clinical use of affected devices and replace them with alternative ventilation methods. The company will no longer service these ventilators and related parts. A total of 63 medical device reports have been received, including two serious injuries and one death associated with this issue.

Medtronic serves patients using Newport™ HT70 ventilators in various settings, including home care and hospitals, particularly for infant and pediatric patients. Customers with affected products are urged to contact Medtronic customer service or report any adverse events to the FDA and Medtronic.

Product Name Manufacture Date Use By Date
Newport™ HT70 Ventilator Specific dates not provided in source Not specified
Newport™ HT70 Plus Ventilator Specific dates not provided in source Not specified
Certain Newport™ Service Parts Post-May 2025 manufacture dates assumed Not specified
  • Remove affected ventilators and service parts from clinical use immediately.
  • Replace with alternate ventilation methods.
  • Contact Medtronic Technical Support at 800-255-6774 (option 1, then option 1) with questions.
  • Medtronic Customer Service: 800-962-9888 for additional information.
  • Report adverse events or product quality problems to the FDA MedWatch program online at FDA MedWatch, by fax, or mail.
  • Call FDA MedWatch at (800) FDA-1088 to report safety concerns.
