headline: P. East Trading Corp Distributors Issues Alert on Uneviscerated 'Salted Smoked Split Herring’ Due to Potential Clostridium Botulinum Contamination
description: No Brand announced a recall involving Uneviscerated Salted Smoked Split Herring after issues linked to potential foodborne illness – clostridium botulinum....

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
P. East Trading Corp. of Bronx, New York, has issued a recall for its Salted Smoked Split Herring due to potential contamination with Clostridium botulinum, a bacterium that can cause severe and potentially fatal foodborne illness. The recalled product was found to be over 5 inches in length and uneviscerated, conditions that increase the risk of botulinum spores concentration in the fish’s viscera. Consumers are strongly advised not to consume the product even if it appears normal in look or smell.

Clostridium botulinum contamination can lead to botulism, a serious illness characterized by symptoms such as general weakness, dizziness, double vision, difficulty speaking or swallowing, breathing difficulties, muscle weakness, abdominal distension, and constipation. Immediate medical attention is recommended for anyone experiencing these symptoms after consuming the product.

The affected Salted Smoked Split Herring was distributed to retail locations across New York, New Jersey, and Connecticut. It was packaged in 18-pound wooden boxes labeled with container code Lot 1 PRC5073 and produced in Canada by Sea Star Seafood Ltd. Retailers may have repackaged the product into deli-style or other retail packaging, with varying codes depending on the place of purchase.

No illnesses have been reported in connection with this recall. Consumers who have purchased the product from the listed retail locations are urged to return it to the place of purchase for a full refund. For additional information or questions, consumers can contact P. East Trading Corp. at (718) 991-6070 or via email at peastl@gmail.com, attention Jay Hong, Office Manager.

headline: Bornstein Seafoods Inc Recalls Cooked & Peeled Ready-To-Eat Coldwater Shrimp Meat Because of Possible Health Risk

image.caption: Bornstein Seafoods Inc Recalls Cooked & Peeled Ready-To-Eat Coldwater Shrimp Meat Because of Possible Health Risk

description: Bornstein Seafoods announced a recall involving Coldwater Shrimp Meat after issues linked to potential foodborne illness – listeria monocytogenes. FDA...

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Bornstein Seafoods Inc., located in Bellingham, Washington, is voluntarily recalling approximately 44,550 pounds of its Cooked & Peeled Ready-To-Eat Coldwater Shrimp Meat. The recall is due to potential contamination with Listeria monocytogenes, a harmful bacterium that can cause serious and sometimes fatal infections, particularly in young children, elderly individuals, pregnant women, and those with weakened immune systems.

The recalled products were distributed to distributors and retailers in California, Oregon, Washington, and British Columbia, Canada. There is also a possibility that these products were further distributed to retailers nationwide. The shrimp meat is sold under the Bornstein Seafoods brand in 1-pound or 5-pound plastic bags.

Although no illnesses have been reported so far, consuming products contaminated with Listeria monocytogenes can lead to symptoms such as high fever, severe headache, stiffness, nausea, abdominal pain, and diarrhea. Pregnant women are at particular risk, as the infection may cause miscarriages and stillbirths.

Consumers who have purchased the affected shrimp products should return them to the place of purchase for a full refund. For any inquiries, consumers may contact Bornstein Seafoods via email at Andrew@bornstein.com.

headline: August Egg Company Recalls Shell Eggs Because of Possible Health Risk

image.caption: August Egg Company Recalls Shell Eggs Because of Possible Health Risk

description: Multiple brand names announced a recall involving Brown cage free and brown certified organic eggs after issues linked to salmonella. FDA recall notice...
keywords: Brown cage free and brown certified organic eggs

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
August Egg Company of Hilmar, California, has announced a voluntary recall of approximately 1,700,000 dozen brown cage-free and brown certified organic eggs due to potential contamination with Salmonella enteritidis. Salmonella infections can cause serious illness, especially in young children, elderly individuals, and those with weakened immune systems. Symptoms of infection often include fever, diarrhea (which may be bloody), nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain. In rare cases, it can lead to more severe conditions such as bloodstream infections, arterial infections, endocarditis, or arthritis.

The affected eggs were distributed between February 3, 2025, and May 15, 2025, with sell-by dates ranging from March 4, 2025, to June 4, 2025, in California and Nevada. Additionally, shipments were made to Walmart stores in multiple states—including California, Washington, Nevada, Arizona, Wyoming, New Mexico, Nebraska, Indiana, and Illinois—between February 3, 2025, and May 6, 2025. Those eggs have sell-by dates from March 4, 2025, through June 19, 2025.

The recalled egg cartons will have a plant code number of P-6562 or CA5330 and include Julian Dates between 32 and 126 printed on one side of the carton. These cartons may be made of fiber or plastic. Consumers are advised to check their egg cartons for these identifying codes and dates and to return any affected products to the place of purchase for a full refund.

August Egg Company has stopped selling fresh shell eggs and has been diverting eggs to an egg-breaking plant for pasteurization, which eliminates potential pathogens. Consumers with questions can contact the company directly at 1-800-710-2554, Monday through Friday, 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. Pacific Time.

Distribution Dates Sell-By Dates Locations Plant Codes Julian Dates
Feb 3, 2025 – May 15, 2025 Mar 4, 2025 – Jun 4, 2025 California, Nevada (Save Mart, FoodMaxx, Lucky, Smart & Final, Safeway, Raleys, Food 4 Less, Ralphs) P-6562, CA5330 32 to 126
Feb 3, 2025 – May 6, 2025 Mar 4, 2025 – Jun 19, 2025 CA, WA, NV, AZ, WY, NM, NE, IN, IL (Walmart locations) P-6562, CA5330 32 to 126
  • Check egg cartons for plant codes P-6562 or CA5330 and Julian Dates between 32 and 126.
  • If you have purchased recalled eggs, return them to the original place of purchase for a refund.
  • Contact August Egg Company at 1-800-710-2554 with questions (9 a.m.–5 p.m. PT, Monday to Friday).
  • Seek medical attention if you experience symptoms of Salmonella infection after consuming eggs.

headline: Church & Dwight Co., Inc. Issues Voluntary Nationwide Recall of Zicam® Cold Remedy Nasal Swabs, Zicam® Nasal AllClear Swabs, and Orajel™ Baby Teething Swabs Due to Microbial Contamination

image.caption: Church & Dwight Co., Inc. Issues Voluntary Nationwide Recall of Zicam® Cold Remedy Nasal Swabs, Zicam® Nasal AllClear Swabs, and Orajel™ Baby Teething Swabs Due to Microbial Contamination

description: Zicam® and Orajel™ announced a recall involving Cold Remedy Nasal Swabs, Nasal AllClear Swabs, Baby Teething Swabs after issues linked to microbial...
keywords: Cold Remedy Nasal Swabs, Nasal AllClear Swabs, Baby Teething Swabs

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Church & Dwight Co., Inc. is voluntarily recalling certain lots of Zicam® Cold Remedy Nasal Swabs, Zicam® Nasal AllClear Swabs, and Orajel™ Baby Teething Swabs due to potential microbial contamination. The contamination, identified as fungi found in the cotton swab components, poses a health risk to consumers, particularly those with compromised immune systems, underlying medical conditions, or damaged nasal mucosa. While no serious adverse events have been reported to date, the contamination could lead to serious and life-threatening infections.

The affected products were distributed nationwide in the United States and Puerto Rico. Consumers are advised to stop using these swab products immediately. Affected products include all lots within expiry for the recalled items. Church & Dwight is offering a full refund upon return of the affected product.

Consumers with questions or seeking a refund can contact Church & Dwight’s Consumer Relations team at (800) 981-4710 Monday through Friday from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET or visit www.churchdwightrecall.com. Adverse reactions or quality problems can be reported to the FDA’s MedWatch program online, by mail, or by fax.

This recall is conducted in coordination with the U.S. Food and Drug Administration and applies exclusively to specified Zicam and Orajel swab products. Other Zicam and Orajel products, including Zicam RapidMelts, are not impacted by this recall.

Product Name UPC Lot Numbers Additional Details
Zicam® Cold Remedy Nasal Swabs 732216301205 All lots within expiry Zinc-free, homeopathic cold remedy swab designed to shorten the duration of the common cold
Zicam® Nasal AllClear Swabs 732216301656 All lots within expiry Nasal cleansing swab product (discontinued December 2024)
Orajel™ Baby Teething Swabs 310310400002 All lots within expiry Pre-moistened swabs designed to soothe teething discomfort in infants and toddlers

Consumers who have used these products and experienced adverse effects can report to the FDA MedWatch program using one of the following methods:

  • Online: FDA MedWatch Online
  • Regular Mail or Fax: Download the reporting form or request by calling 1-800-332-1088, then complete and return it to the address on the form or fax to 1-800-FDA-0178

description: Multiple brands announced a recall involving Fresh cucumbers and salsa and salads containing fresh cucumbers after issues linked to potential to be...

Potential to be contaminated with Salmonella

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
TGD Cuts, LLC, located in Jessup, Maryland, has voluntarily recalled certain tub and tray items containing cucumbers supplied by Bedner Growers Inc. due to potential contamination with Salmonella. Salmonella infection can cause serious and sometimes fatal illnesses, particularly in young children, elderly individuals, and those with weakened immune systems.

Symptoms of Salmonella infection include fever, diarrhea (which may be bloody), nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain. In rare cases, the infection can spread to the bloodstream and cause more severe conditions such as arterial infections, endocarditis, or arthritis.

The recall specifically affects products with use-by dates ranging from May 20, 2025, to May 28, 2025. These items were distributed to retail and foodservice locations across Maryland, Virginia, Pennsylvania, New Jersey, and North Carolina. There have been no reported illnesses linked to these products to date. As the affected items are beyond their shelf life, they are no longer expected to be found in commerce.

Consumers who possess any of the recalled products should discard them immediately or return them to the place of purchase for a full refund. For further information or questions, customers can contact TGD Cuts, LLC at (410) 799-5700 during business hours, Monday through Friday, 6:30 a.m. to 3:00 p.m. Eastern Standard Time.

Specific tubs and trays containing cucumber from Bedner Growers Inc. May 20, 2025 – May 28, 2025 Maryland, Virginia, Pennsylvania, New Jersey, North Carolina

headline: Firehook of Virginia Issues Allergy Alert on Undeclared Sesame in Classic Sea Salt Crackers

image.caption: Firehook of Virginia Issues Allergy Alert on Undeclared Sesame in Classic Sea Salt Crackers

description: Firehook announced a recall involving Crackers after issues linked to undeclared sesame. FDA recall notice published in 2025.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Firehook of Virginia has issued a recall for one lot of Firehook brand Classic Sea Salt Organic Crackers due to undeclared sesame. This poses a risk of a serious or life-threatening allergic reaction for individuals with sesame allergies or severe sensitivities.

The recalled product is an 8-ounce package of Firehook artisan baked Classic Sea Salt Crackers, sold in a clear package. The specific lot affected has a Best By Date of 09/29/25 and a UPC code of 8 99055 00063 5. The crackers were distributed at retail stores across several states including Connecticut, Massachusetts, Maryland, Maine, North Carolina, New Hampshire, New Jersey, New York, Pennsylvania, Rhode Island, and Virginia.

The recall was initiated on May 30, 2025, after discovering that sesame-containing crackers were labeled incorrectly due to a temporary breakdown in the company's production and packaging processes. At this time, no illnesses have been reported related to this issue.

Consumers who have purchased the 8-ounce Classic Sea Salt Crackers with the specified Best By Date are advised to return the product to the place of purchase for a full refund. headline: Amneal Pharmaceutical LLC Issues a Nationwide Recall of Sulfamethoxazole / Trimethoprim Tablets, USP, 400 mg/80 mg Only, Due to Microbial Contamination

image.caption: Amneal Pharmaceutical LLC Issues a Nationwide Recall of Sulfamethoxazole / Trimethoprim Tablets, USP, 400 mg/80 mg Only, Due to Microbial Contamination

description: Amneal announced a recall involving Sulfamethoxazole/Trimethoprim Tablets, USP, 400 mg/80 mg after issues linked to microbial contamination. FDA recall...

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Amneal Pharmaceutical LLC has initiated a nationwide recall of three lots of Sulfamethoxazole/Trimethoprim Tablets, USP, 400 mg/80 mg due to microbial contamination. The affected tablets may show black spots on the surface, which were identified following a product quality complaint. This contamination poses a health risk as oral products contaminated with Aspergillus can cause serious and potentially life-threatening infections, especially in patients with weakened immune systems.

The recalled lots were distributed to wholesalers and distributors across the United States between December 4, 2024, and May 15, 2025. This recall only affects the 400 mg/80 mg strength of Sulfamethoxazole/Trimethoprim Tablets and does not impact other lot numbers or strengths. To date, no adverse events, illnesses, or injuries related to this recall have been reported.

Sulfamethoxazole/Trimethoprim Tablets, USP, 400 mg/80 mg are used to treat urinary tract infections, acute otitis media in pediatric patients, acute exacerbations of chronic bronchitis, enteritis caused by Shigella flexneri, and traveler's diarrhea in adults. The product is available in 100 tablet and 500 tablet bottles. Consumers should check the lot number on the bottle label or consult their pharmacy if the tablets came from a pharmacy vial.

Amneal is contacting customers directly to arrange the return of recalled products. Wholesalers, distributors, and retailers are instructed to notify their customers and provide guidance on returning the affected tablets. Consumers with questions or those needing assistance with returns and reimbursement should contact Amneal Pharmaceutical LLC.

headline: Camerican International, Inc. Issues Allergy Alert on Undeclared Milk in Aldi Brand Casa Mamita Churro Bites Filled with Chocolate Hazelnut Cream

image.caption: Camerican International, Inc. Issues Allergy Alert on Undeclared Milk in Aldi Brand Casa Mamita Churro Bites Filled with Chocolate Hazelnut Cream

description: Aldi brand Casa Mamita announced a recall involving Churro Bites after issues linked to undeclared milk. FDA recall notice published in 2025.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Camerican International, Inc. of Paramus, NJ is recalling 7.05 oz boxes of Aldi brand Casa Mamita Churro Bites Filled with Chocolate Hazelnut Cream due to the presence of undeclared milk. This poses a serious risk for individuals with milk allergies or severe sensitivities, who could experience life-threatening allergic reactions if they consume the product.

The affected product was distributed in select Aldi stores across Alabama, Arkansas, Florida, Georgia, Iowa, Illinois, Kentucky, Louisiana, Missouri, Mississippi, North Carolina, South Carolina, and Tennessee. The recall was triggered after a consumer reported milk-containing Churro Bites being sold in packaging that did not list milk as an ingredient. An investigation identified a temporary breakdown in production and packaging processes as the cause, which has since been resolved.

No illnesses have been reported related to this issue. Consumers who have purchased the affected product are advised to return it to the place of purchase for a full refund. For further information or questions, the company can be contacted by phone or email.

description: Shore Lunch announced a recall involving Breading and Batter Mix after issues linked to presence of undeclared milk. FDA recall notice published in 2025.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Homegrown Family Foods has issued a recall for its Shore Lunch Oven Style Breader & Batter Mix 6-ounce boxes due to the presence of undeclared milk. This undeclared allergen poses a serious risk to individuals with milk allergies or severe sensitivities, as consumption may result in life-threatening allergic reactions.

The recalled product was primarily distributed in retail stores across Illinois, Indiana, Iowa, Minnesota, Nebraska, New York, North Dakota, Ohio, South Dakota, and Wisconsin. Distribution occurred between April 29, 2024, and May 1, 2025. The affected items have specific Best By dates, lot numbers, and UPC codes for easy identification.

To date, one allergic reaction related to this recall has been reported, with the affected individual having recovered. The recall was initiated after a consumer reported an allergic reaction on April 23, 2025, which led to the discovery that milk-containing product was packaged without proper milk allergen labeling.

Consumers who have purchased the affected Shore Lunch Oven Style Breader & Batter Mix and have a dairy allergy or sensitivity are advised not to consume the product. Instead, they should return it to the place of purchase for a full refund. For further questions, consumers may contact Homegrown Family Foods at 706-403-5768, Monday through Friday from 8:00 am to 4:00 pm ET, or email QAinquiries@homegrownfamilyfood.com.

headline: Isabelle’s Kitchen Inc. Recalls Refrigerated Deli Salads Containing Fresh Cucumbers Because of Possible Health Risk

image.caption: Isabelle’s Kitchen Inc. Recalls Refrigerated Deli Salads Containing Fresh Cucumbers Because of Possible Health Risk

description: Kings, Isabelle’s Kitchen Inc., Maple Avenue Foods announced a recall involving Salads containing fresh cucumbers. after issues linked to potential to be...
keywords: Kings, Isabelle’s Kitchen Inc., Maple Avenue Foods
Potential to be contaminated with Salmonella

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Isabelle’s Kitchen, Inc., based in Harleysville, PA, is recalling 946 cases of refrigerated deli salads containing fresh cucumbers supplied by Bedner Grower’s, Inc. due to potential contamination with Salmonella. Salmonella infection can cause serious health issues, including fever, diarrhea (sometimes bloody), nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain. Vulnerable populations such as young children, elderly, and individuals with weakened immune systems are at increased risk of severe or potentially fatal infections.

The recalled refrigerated deli salads were distributed to foodservice distributors and local supermarkets in New Hampshire, Florida, Maryland, South Carolina, New Jersey, North Carolina, Pennsylvania, and Virginia. These products are packaged in white plastic containers inside white or brown corrugated boxes under the brand names "Isabelle’s Kitchen," "Maple Avenue Foods," or "Kings." It is possible that repackaged items sold at the supermarket level may have different sell-by dates, but none will exceed the original manufacture sell-by date.

Consumers are advised not to consume these products. The recall only affects specific deli salad items produced using cucumbers processed on May 14 and May 15, 2025, bearing designated Julian code use-by dates. There have been no reported illnesses linked to these products to date. Retailers have been notified to remove and destroy the recalled products and inform consumers accordingly.

For more information or questions, consumers can contact Isabelle’s Kitchen, Inc. at 800-355-7252 between 8 AM and 5 PM EST. Individuals experiencing health issues after consuming the products should seek medical advice. This recall is being conducted in coordination with the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA).

headline: Supreme Service Solutions LLC Voluntarily Recalls Supreme Vegetable Products Because of Possible Health Risk
description: Supreme Produce announced a recall involving Fresh cucumbers and salad and vegetable trays containing fresh cucumbers. after issues linked to due to...
keywords: Fresh cucumbers and salad and vegetable trays containing fresh cucumbers.
Due to possible contamination with Salmonella

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Supreme Service Solutions LLC, operating as Supreme Produce, has voluntarily recalled fresh cucumbers and salad and vegetable trays containing fresh cucumbers due to possible contamination with Salmonella. This recall involves products purchased from Bedner Growers Inc. and distributed through Kroger and its affiliated retail stores.

Salmonella is a bacteria that can cause serious and sometimes fatal infections, especially in young children, elderly individuals, and those with weakened immune systems. Symptoms of infection include fever, diarrhea (which may be bloody), nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain. Rarely, infection can lead to more severe complications such as bloodstream infections, endocarditis, or arthritis.

The recall was initiated after the FDA notified Bedner Growers Inc. of a Salmonella outbreak linked to these cucumbers. This outbreak has affected 26 individuals across multiple states, including Alabama, California, Colorado, Florida, Illinois, Kansas, Kentucky, Michigan, North Carolina, New York, Ohio, Pennsylvania, South Carolina, Tennessee, and Virginia. The recalled products were distributed to Kroger stores and affiliates in Indiana, Illinois, Ohio, Kentucky, Tennessee, Mississippi, Missouri, Arkansas, and Michigan.

Consumers are advised not to consume these products and to discard any remaining items. While the products are likely past their shelf life and may no longer be available for sale, anyone in possession of the recalled items should take precautionary measures. There have been no reported illnesses or consumer complaints directly linked to products purchased from Supreme Produce to date.

For health-related concerns, consumers should consult their physician. Additional information about the recall can be obtained by contacting Bedner Growers, Inc. at 866-222-9180, Monday through Friday, 8:00 a.m. to 5:00 p.m. EDT.

headline: The Coastal Companies Issues Voluntary Recall on Items with Fresh Start Cucumbers Due to the Potential for Salmonella Contamination

image.caption: The Coastal Companies Issues Voluntary Recall on Items with Fresh Start Cucumbers Due to the Potential for Salmonella Contamination

description: East Coast Fresh, TOPS, Wellsley Farms, WEIS, AHOLD, Jack and Olive, Created Fresh, Spring and Sprout announced a recall involving Salads, salsas, and other...
keywords: East Coast Fresh, TOPS, Wellsley Farms, WEIS, AHOLD, Jack and Olive, Created Fresh, Spring and Sprout
Salads, salsas, and other products containing salsa containing fresh cucumbers

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
The Coastal Companies has announced a voluntary recall of 17 products containing fresh cucumbers supplied by Bedner Growers, Inc. and distributed by Fresh Start Produce Sales, Inc. due to potential Salmonella contamination. These products were distributed between May 6, 2025, and May 21, 2025, across several states including Washington D.C., Ohio, Virginia, Michigan, West Virginia, Delaware, Maryland, Massachusetts, Pennsylvania, New Jersey, Vermont, and North Carolina.

The recalled products include various salads, salsas, and other items under brands such as East Coast Fresh, TOPS, Wellsley Farms, WEIS, AHOLD, Jack and Olive, Created Fresh, Spring and Sprout. Consumers, restaurants, retailers, and wholesalers are advised not to consume, sell, or serve these products.

If you have any of the affected products in your possession, you should record the number of cases and destroy the product immediately. Do not return them to the store. If product ingredients are unclear and may contain cucumbers, it is recommended to discard them to avoid any risk.

Salmonella infection can cause serious health issues, particularly in young children, elderly individuals, and those with weakened immune systems. Symptoms include fever, diarrhea, nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain. Those who believe they may have contracted Salmonella should seek medical attention promptly. To date, there have been no reported illnesses linked directly to The Coastal Companies’ recalled products.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
On May 24, 2025, Santa Monica Seafood, located in Rancho Dominguez, California, announced a voluntary recall of its Atlantic Salmon Portions with Seafood Stuffing. The recall is due to the presence of undeclared soy in the product, which poses a risk to individuals with soy allergies or severe sensitivities. Consuming the product could cause a serious or life-threatening allergic reaction.

The affected product was distributed in Aldi Stores across California, Nevada, and Arizona. To date, no illnesses related to this issue have been reported. The recall was initiated after the supplier identified a labeling error during a routine inspection, where soy was not listed as an ingredient despite being present in the product.

Consumers who have purchased the Atlantic Salmon Portions with Seafood Stuffing are advised to return the product to the store for a full refund. This recall is being carried out in cooperation with the U.S. Food and Drug Administration.

For further inquiries, Santa Monica Seafood can be contacted at 1-800-969-8862, Monday through Friday, from 8:00 a.m. to 5:00 p.m. PST.

headline: Spectrum Brands Pet LLC Recalls a Single Lot of Tetra Reptomin Reptile Food for Aquatic Turtles Because of Possible Salmonella Health Risk

image.caption: Spectrum Brands Pet LLC Recalls a Single Lot of Tetra Reptomin Reptile Food for Aquatic Turtles Because of Possible Salmonella Health Risk

description: Tetra announced a recall involving ReptoMin 3-In-1 SELECT-A-FOOD reptile food after issues linked to salmonella contamination. FDA recall notice published...
keywords: Animal & Veterinary Food & Beverages Foodborne Illness

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Spectrum Brands Pet LLC (SPG) is voluntarily recalling a single lot of its Tetra ReptoMin 3-In-1 SELECT-A-FOOD reptile food due to possible contamination with Salmonella. This product is designed for aquatic turtles and is packaged in a 1.56 oz plastic container with a green label. Only the lot with code 951790 is affected by this recall. No other lots or product lines are included, and they may continue to be used.

Salmonella can pose health risks to both animals consuming the product and humans handling it. Symptoms in humans can include nausea, vomiting, diarrhea (including bloody diarrhea), abdominal cramping, and fever. Serious complications, though rare, can involve infections of the arteries, endocarditis, arthritis, muscle pain, eye irritation, and urinary tract symptoms. In pets, particularly aquatic turtles, symptoms may include lethargy, diarrhea, vomiting, fever, decreased appetite, and abdominal pain. Infected pets that appear healthy can still spread bacteria to other animals and people.

Consumers who have handled or fed this product are advised to thoroughly wash and sanitize hands, pet food bowls, and any containers or surfaces exposed to the recalled food. If pets show symptoms after consuming the recalled product, owners are urged to contact their veterinarian. The affected product was distributed nationwide between May 6, 2025, and May 16, 2025, through major retailers including Petco, Walmart, Chewy, Amazon, and Meijer.

Spectrum Brands has not received reports of illness linked to this recall but is cooperating with the U.S. Food and Drug Administration to ensure safety. The recall follows internal testing that suggested a component ingredient of the reptile food may contain Salmonella, despite the final product testing negative. Consumers who purchased the implicated lot should stop using it immediately and contact the point of purchase for a full refund. For questions, contact SPG at 1-800-526-0650 or aquaticssupport@spectrumbrands.com, Monday through Friday from 9 am to 5 pm EST.

Product Name Lot Code Package Size UPC Distribution Dates Retailers
Tetra ReptoMin 3-In-1 SELECT-A-FOOD reptile food 951790 1.56 oz plastic container 0 46798 78626 9 May 6, 2025 – May 16, 2025 Petco, Walmart, Chewy, Amazon, Meijer
  • Stop using the recalled product immediately.
  • Contact the retailer for a full refund.
  • Wash and sanitize hands, pet food bowls, utensils, and surfaces after handling the product.
  • If pets show symptoms such as lethargy, diarrhea, vomiting, or fever after eating the product, contact a veterinarian.
  • If humans develop symptoms of Salmonella infection after contact with the product, seek medical advice.
  • For additional information or questions, contact Spectrum Brands Pet LLC at 1-800-526-0650 or aquaticssupport@spectrumbrands.com, Monday to Friday, 9 am to 5 pm EST.

headline: Albertsons Companies Voluntarily Recalls Three Store-Made Deli Items Containing Recalled Cucumber Supplied by Fresh Creative Foods Due to Possible Salmonella Contamination

image.caption: Albertsons Companies Voluntarily Recalls Three Store-Made Deli Items Containing Recalled Cucumber Supplied by Fresh Creative Foods Due to Possible Salmonella Contamination

description: Ready Meals & Star Market announced a recall involving Greek Salad after issues linked to salmonella. FDA recall notice published in 2025.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Albertsons Companies has voluntarily recalled three store-made deli items containing cucumbers supplied by Fresh Creative Foods due to possible Salmonella contamination. This action follows a recall initiated by Fresh Creative Foods, a division of Reser’s Fine Foods, Inc., linked to an ongoing Salmonella illness outbreak. The cucumbers involved in the recall were grown by Bedner Growers, Inc. in Boynton Beach, Florida.

Salmonella infection can cause serious and potentially fatal illnesses, especially in young children, the elderly, and individuals with weakened immune systems. Symptoms often include fever, diarrhea, nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain. The affected deli items were sold in several store banners across multiple states in the Northeast and Mid-Atlantic regions.

Consumers who have purchased any of the recalled Greek salad items are advised not to consume them. Instead, they should dispose of the products or return them to the store for a full refund. Anyone experiencing illness potentially related to these products should seek medical care. For further questions, Albertsons Companies Customer Service is available at 1-877-723-3929, Monday through Friday from 5 a.m. to 9 p.m. PST.

headline: Walmart Inc. Recalls Marketside Fresh Cut Cucumber Slices in Select Texas Stores Because of Possible Health Risk

image.caption: Walmart Inc. Recalls Marketside Fresh Cut Cucumber Slices in Select Texas Stores Because of Possible Health Risk

description: Marketside announced a recall involving Fresh cut cucumber slices after issues linked to salmonella. FDA recall notice published in 2025.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Walmart Inc. announced a voluntary recall of Marketside Fresh Cut Cucumber Slices sold in select Texas stores due to potential Salmonella contamination. The recall follows an FDA notification regarding whole cucumbers supplied by Bedner Growers, Inc., which are linked to reported salmonellosis illnesses.

Consumers are advised not to consume, serve, sell, or distribute the recalled cucumber slices. It is recommended to discard these products or return them to the point of purchase for a full refund. To prevent cross-contamination, surfaces that may have come into contact with the cucumbers should be thoroughly cleaned and sanitized.

Salmonella infection can cause serious health issues, especially in young children, the elderly, and those with weakened immune systems. Symptoms typically begin 6 hours to 6 days after exposure and include fever, diarrhea, nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain. Although no illnesses have been reported from this specific recalled product, anyone experiencing symptoms after consuming it should consult a healthcare provider.

Consumers unsure if they have purchased the affected cucumbers can visit Walmart's help page or contact Walmart's Customer Care Team at 1-800-925-6278.

Product Description UPC/PLU Average Unit Weight Date Codes
Marketside Fresh Cut Cucumber Slices 62969 1.5 lbs All date codes up to 5/24/2025

Consumers affected by this recall should take the following actions:

  • Do not consume, serve, use, sell, or distribute the recalled products.
  • Dispose of or return the product for a full refund.
  • Clean and sanitize any surfaces that may have come into contact with the product to avoid cross-contamination.
  • Contact a healthcare provider if you have consumed the product and experience symptoms of salmonellosis.
  • For questions, visit https://www.walmart.com/help or call Walmart's Customer Care Team at 1-800-925-6278.
  • Refer to the FDA outbreak advisory for additional details.

headline: JFE Franchising, Inc. Recalls A Limited Number of Cucumber Products Because Of Possible Health Risk
description: Snowfruit & Snowfox announced a recall involving Fresh cut cucumber items and sushi products after issues linked to salmonella. FDA recall notice published...
keywords: Fresh cut cucumber items and sushi products

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
JFE Franchising, Inc. of Houston, TX, is voluntarily recalling a limited number of fresh-cut cucumber products and sushi items containing cucumber due to potential contamination with Salmonella. Salmonella bacteria can cause serious and sometimes fatal infections, especially in young children, elderly individuals, and people with weakened immune systems. Symptoms in healthy individuals often include fever, diarrhea (which may be bloody), nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain, typically starting 6 hours to 6 days after exposure and lasting 4 to 7 days.

To date, no illnesses or consumer complaints have been reported related to the recalled products. The recall is in response to a prior recall of cucumbers by Bedner Growers, Inc., whose cucumbers were supplied to retailers and used as ingredients in JFE Franchising’s products. These items were sold through Kroger, Roundy’s (including Pick n Save and Metro Market), and Weis Market locations across several states including Indiana, Kentucky, Illinois, Michigan, Tennessee, Mississippi, Arkansas, Alabama, Wisconsin, New Jersey, Pennsylvania, West Virginia, Delaware, Maryland, Virginia, and New York.

Consumers are advised not to consume the recalled Snowfruit and Snowfox branded products listed below. Instead, they should return the items to the place of purchase for a full refund or dispose of them. For questions, customers can contact JFE Franchising, Inc. via email at hello@snowfruit.com or hello@snowfox.com. The company monitors these email addresses Monday through Friday from 9 a.m. to 4 p.m. CST.

JFE Franchising, Inc. is actively taking measures to address this recall and ensure consumer safety. More information is available through the FDA’s outbreak advisory.

headline: PennRose Farms Issues Recall of Whole Cucumbers Because Of Possible Health Risk

image.caption: PennRose Farms Issues Recall of Whole Cucumbers Because Of Possible Health Risk

description: PennRose Farms announced a recall involving Whole cucumbers after issues linked to salmonella. FDA recall notice published in 2025.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
PennRose Farms, LLC has issued a recall for 5-pound mesh bags of whole cucumbers due to possible contamination with Salmonella. Salmonella can cause severe and sometimes fatal infections, particularly in young children, elderly individuals, and those with weakened immune systems. Symptoms of Salmonella infection include fever, diarrhea (which may be bloody), nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain. In rare cases, the infection can spread to the bloodstream and cause more serious conditions such as arterial infections, endocarditis, and arthritis.

The cucumbers in question were repacked by PennRose Farms but originally recalled by Bedner Growers, Inc., as notified by their supplier Fresh Start Produce Sales, Inc. The affected products are 5-pound mesh bags bearing the Restaurant Depot and PennRose Farms logos. These were packaged between May 2 and May 5, 2025, and shipped to Restaurant Depot distribution centers across New Jersey, Georgia, Florida, Illinois, and Ohio.

Consumers, retailers, distributors, and restaurants are urged not to consume, sell, serve, or distribute the recalled products. Restaurants and retailers that have already sold or used the cucumbers should notify consumers of the potential health risks. Consumers who have purchased the affected cucumbers should discard them immediately and can contact PennRose Farms for more information.

If you have any health concerns related to this recall, please consult your healthcare provider. PennRose Farms is working in coordination with the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA) regarding this recall.

headline: UMARY- USA.COM Issues Voluntary Nationwide Recall of UNAVY ÁCIDO HIALURÓNICO Caplets and UMOVY ÁCIDO HIALURÓNICO Caplets Due to the Presence of Undeclared Drug Ingredients Dexamethasone, Diclofenac and Omeprazole

image.caption: UMARY- USA.COM Issues Voluntary Nationwide Recall of UNAVY ÁCIDO HIALURÓNICO Caplets and UMOVY ÁCIDO HIALURÓNICO Caplets Due to the Presence of Undeclared Drug Ingredients Dexamethasone, Diclofenac and Omeprazole

description: UNAVY & UMOVY announced a recall involving Unavy Acidio Hialuronico (30 caplets/850 mg) and Umovy Acidio Hialuronico (30 caplets/850 mg) after issues linked...
keywords: Unavy Acidio Hialuronico (30 caplets/850 mg) and Umovy Acidio Hialuronico (30 caplets/850 mg)
The product contains undeclared Dexamethasone, Diclofenac and Omeprazole

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
UMARY USA has issued a voluntary nationwide recall of all lots of Unavy Ácido Hialurónico (30 caplets/850 mg) and Umovy Ácido Hialurónico (30 caplets/850 mg) due to the presence of undeclared drug ingredients: dexamethasone, diclofenac, and omeprazole. These products were marketed as dietary supplements but have been identified as unapproved new drugs by the FDA because they contain active pharmaceutical ingredients not listed on the labels.

Dexamethasone is a corticosteroid that may impair immune function and cause serious side effects such as high blood sugar, muscle injuries, psychiatric conditions, cardiovascular events, and adrenal gland suppression, with potential withdrawal symptoms upon abrupt discontinuation. Diclofenac is a non-steroidal anti-inflammatory drug (NSAID) that can cause cardiovascular, gastrointestinal, renal, and allergic reactions, especially in individuals taking other NSAIDs or anticoagulants. Omeprazole, a proton pump inhibitor, may mask serious stomach conditions including ulcers and cancer.

Because of these risks, consumers are advised to stop using these products immediately and consult their healthcare provider for guidance on safely discontinuing use. UMARY USA is notifying distributors and customers through press releases and emails and is organizing return and refund procedures for affected products.

Consumers with questions can contact UMARY USA at umaryusa2025@gmail.com, available 24/7. Any adverse events or quality issues related to these products should be reported to the FDA's MedWatch program.

Brand Product Name Quantity Dosage per Caplet Container Description Lot Numbers Expiration Dates
UNAVY Ácido Hialurónico 30 caplets 850 mg White plastic bottle with black background label, white and yellow writing All lots All expiration dates
UMOVY Ácido Hialurónico 30 caplets 850 mg Black plastic bottle with black label, white and blue lettering All lots All expiration dates

Distribution of these products occurred nationwide via online sales exclusively through umary-usa.com.

To report adverse events or quality problems related to these products, consumers and healthcare professionals can:

  • Submit reports online at www.fda.gov/medwatch/report.htm1
  • Download and mail or fax the MedWatch form available at www.fda.gov/MedWatch/getforms.htm2
  • Call 1-800-332-1088 to request a reporting form
  • Fax completed forms to 1-800-FDA-0178

content.subtitle: Recall Summary

Publix Super Markets, Inc. has announced a voluntary recall of GreenWise Pear, Kiwi, Spinach & Pea Baby Food pouches due to potential contamination with elevated levels of lead. The affected product is produced by Bowman Andros LLC and distributed across Publix stores in its eight-state operating area.

The product subject to this recall is sold in 4-ounce plastic pouches and was identified through routine sampling. The recall is being conducted in coordination with the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA) to ensure consumer safety.

Publix has removed all potentially impacted products from store shelves. There have been no reported illnesses associated with the consumption of this product to date. Consumers who have purchased this baby food may return it to their local Publix store for a full refund.

For additional information or questions, customers can contact Publix’s customer care department toll-free at 1-800-242-1227 or visit the company’s website at publix.com.

headline: Ukrop’s Homestyle Foods Announces Recall Due to Possible Health Risk

image.caption: Ukrop’s Homestyle Foods Announces Recall Due to Possible Health Risk

description: Ukrop’s announced a recall involving Marinated cucumber Salad after issues linked to salmonella. FDA recall notice published in 2025.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Ukrop’s Homestyle Foods, LLC, based in Richmond, VA, has issued a recall of three marinated cucumber salad products due to potential contamination with Salmonella. This action follows a related recall by Bedner Growers of cucumbers, which are an ingredient in these products. Salmonella infection can cause serious illness, especially in young children, the elderly, and individuals with weakened immune systems.

Salmonella symptoms often include fever, diarrhea (which may be bloody), nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain. In rare cases, the infection can become severe by spreading to the bloodstream, leading to complications such as infected aneurysms, endocarditis, or arthritis.

The recall affects specific sell-by dates of Ukrop’s marinated cucumber salad sold in various sizes, and products have been distributed to multiple retailers across several states. Consumers who purchased the recalled products are advised to return them to the place of purchase for a full refund.

For further questions, consumers can contact Ukrop’s Homestyle Foods' customer service at 804-340-3050, Monday through Friday, from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. EST.

Product Net Weight Sell-By Date Range
Marinated Cucumber Salad 16 ounces 5/11/25 through 5/26/25
Marinated Cucumber Salad 36 ounces 5/11/25 through 5/26/25
Marinated Cucumber Salad Bulk 5 lbs. 5/11/25 through 5/26/25

The affected products were available at the following retailers:

  • Food Lion Stores in Virginia and North Carolina
  • Harris Teeter, Williamsburg, VA
  • Kroger Stores in Virginia, West Virginia, Ohio, and Kentucky
  • Libbie Market, Richmond, VA
  • Ukrop’s Market Hall, Richmond, VA

All retail locations have been notified and are in the process of removing the recalled products from their shelves.

Additional company and recall information is available from Ukrop’s Homestyle Foods at ukropshomestylefoods.com or by phone at 804-340-3050.

For official updates and more details regarding this recall and related advisories, visit the FDA website at FDA.

headline: Big Y Foods Recalls Made-To-Order Subs, Wraps and Paninis Sold in Massachusetts and Connecticut Because of Possible Health Risk
description: Big Y Foods announced a recall involving Made-to-order subs, wraps and paninis after issues linked to salmonella. FDA recall notice published in 2025.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Big Y Foods of Springfield, MA, is voluntarily recalling certain Made-To-Order Subs, Wraps, and Paninis sold between May 20 and May 21, 2025. This recall affects products sold at select Big Y retail locations in Massachusetts and Connecticut due to potential contamination with Salmonella. Salmonella is a bacteria that can cause serious and sometimes fatal infections, particularly in young children, elderly individuals, and people with weakened immune systems.

Individuals infected with Salmonella may experience symptoms such as fever, diarrhea (which may be bloody), nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain. In rare cases, the infection can spread to the bloodstream, causing severe complications such as arterial infections, endocarditis, or arthritis.

The recall is linked to sliced cucumbers supplied by Smartcuts, an ingredient option for subs, wraps, and paninis at the sandwich service line in Big Y’s Kitchen Department. The cucumbers are part of a wider Bedner Growers Inc. Cucumber Outbreak investigation. Following notification, Big Y Foods ceased operations at the affected service lines in all listed stores, discarded remaining product, and conducted thorough cleaning and sanitization.

Consumers who purchased Made-To-Order subs, wraps, or paninis on the specified dates at the affected stores are advised to return the product or present their receipt for a full refund. No illnesses have been reported in connection with this recall to date. For questions, customers can contact Big Y Foods or Smartcuts as detailed below.

headline: New Grains Gluten Free Bakery Issues Allergy Alert on Undeclared Eggs, Tree Nuts, Soy, and Milk in Bakery Products
description: New Grain Gluten Free Bakery announced a recall involving Breads, bagels, caramel bars, cookies and croutons after issues linked to undeclared allergen –...
keywords: Breads, bagels, caramel bars, cookies and croutons
Undeclared Allergen – Egg, tree nuts Soy and Milk

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
New Grains Gluten Free Bakery has issued a recall for several of its bakery products due to undeclared allergens, including eggs, tree nuts, soy, and milk. These allergens are not listed on the product labels, posing a serious risk of allergic reactions for individuals with sensitivities to these ingredients. The recalled products include various breads, bagels, cookies, and croutons distributed in Utah.

The affected items were distributed between April 7 and April 21, 2025, under lot numbers 90–107. The products were sold in retail stores within Utah, with breads and croutons packaged in clear vacuum-sealed plastic bags and cookies in clear regular plastic bags. The products are labeled as "gluten-free" and bear the New Grains brand name with label colors in red, purple, orange, blue, green, or pink.

The recall was initiated after it was discovered that some products contained eggs, tree nuts, soy, and milk but lacked proper allergen labeling. To date, no illnesses have been reported. Consumers who have purchased these products are advised not to consume them and to return them to the place of purchase for a full refund.

For additional information or questions, customers can contact New Grains Gluten Free Bakery at 801-980-5751 between 10 AM and 3 PM MST, Monday through Friday. This recall is conducted with the knowledge of the U.S. Food and Drug Administration.

headline: Bedner Growers, Inc. Recalls Cucumbers Because of Possible Health Risk
description: No Brand Name announced a recall involving Cucumbers after issues linked to salmonella. FDA recall notice published in 2025.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Bedner Growers, Inc., located in Boynton Beach, Florida, is voluntarily recalling cucumbers sold at its Bedner’s Farm Fresh Market stores between April 29, 2025, and May 14, 2025. The recall is due to potential contamination with Salmonella, a bacteria that can cause serious and sometimes fatal infections, particularly in young children, elderly individuals, and those with weakened immune systems.

Salmonella infection symptoms include fever, diarrhea (which may be bloody), nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain. In rare cases, the infection can spread to the bloodstream and cause severe conditions such as infected aneurysms, endocarditis, and arthritis. The recalled cucumbers were sold directly to consumers at three Bedner’s Farm Fresh Market locations in Florida: Boynton Beach, Delray Beach, and West Palm Beach.

The cucumbers involved in this recall do not bear any identifying stickers or labels. Customers who purchased cucumbers at these locations between April 29, 2025, and May 14, 2025, are advised to discard them and avoid consumption. The cucumbers were also sold to a wholesale distributor that has been instructed to notify its customers about the recall and provide further instructions.

This recall is linked by the FDA to a Salmonella outbreak resulting in 26 reported illnesses across multiple states: Alabama, California, Colorado, Florida, Illinois, Kansas, Kentucky, Michigan, North Carolina, New York, Ohio, Pennsylvania, South Carolina, Tennessee, and Virginia. The recall does not affect cucumbers sold after May 14, 2025, nor does it include any other agricultural products at Bedner’s Farm Fresh Markets. Bedner Growers, Inc. has ceased production, packing, and sales of cucumbers as the growing season has ended.

Product Retail Locations Recall Dates Reason for Recall
Cucumbers (No brand name, no labels) Bedner’s Farm Fresh Markets in Boynton Beach, Delray Beach, and West Palm Beach, FL April 29, 2025 – May 14, 2025 Potential Salmonella contamination
  • If you have purchased cucumbers during this period at the specified locations, do not consume them and discard immediately.
  • The cucumbers were also distributed to a wholesale distributor, which is notifying its customers with recall instructions.
  • For additional information, consumers can contact Bedner Growers, Inc. at 866-222-9180, Monday through Friday, 8:00 a.m. to 5:00 p.m. EDT.
  • This recall is conducted in coordination with the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA).
  • For more details, see the FDA Outbreak Advisory.

Company website here.

content.text: Salmonella

If you have purchased affected cucumbers or have questions, contact Bedner Growers, Inc. at 866-222-9180 for more information and guidance on what to do next.

headline: Element 112, LLC dba Madeline’s Pâtisserie Issues Allergy Alert on Undeclared Wheat in Croissants and Croissant Buns
description: Madeline’s Pâtisserie announced a recall involving Croissants and Croissant Buns after issues linked to undeclared allergen - wheat.. FDA recall notice...

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Element 112, LLC dba Madeline’s Pâtisserie has announced a voluntary recall of certain ready-to-eat croissants and croissant buns due to an undeclared allergen. The products were incorrectly labeled with “flour” on the ingredient statement instead of specifying “wheat,” which poses a risk for individuals with wheat allergies or sensitivities.

The affected items, sold fully baked and frozen in food service cases of 24 units, were produced between March 10, 2025, and May 7, 2025. These products were distributed to several food distributors in Michigan and Ohio.

Consumers and distributors who have these products are advised to immediately remove them from sale or distribution. The company is offering replacements with properly labeled packaging and has taken corrective measures to resolve the labeling error.

For further assistance or to arrange product replacement, contact Madeline’s Pâtisserie at info@madelinespatisserie.com.

headline: South Asian Food Inc. Issues Allergy Alert on Undeclared Peanuts in "Bengal King Family Pack Vegetable Singara"

image.caption: South Asian Food Inc. Issues Allergy Alert on Undeclared Peanuts in "Bengal King Family Pack Vegetable Singara"

description: Bengal King announced a recall involving Family Pack Vegetable Singara after issues linked to undeclared allergen – peanut. FDA recall notice published in 2025.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
South Asian Food Inc., located in Maspeth, NY, has issued a recall for its 1875g packages of Bengal King Family Pack Vegetable Singara. This recall is due to the possible presence of undeclared peanuts, which pose a serious risk for individuals with peanut allergies or severe peanut sensitivities.

The affected product was distributed nationwide at retail stores and through mail orders. The issue was identified when a peanut-containing ingredient was found in the product but not listed on the label, caused by unintentional human error during packaging. Production of this product has been suspended as the company works with the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA) to correct the problem.

To date, no allergic reactions or illnesses related to this recall have been reported. Consumers who have purchased the recalled Bengal King Family Pack Vegetable Singara are urged not to consume it and should return the package to the place of purchase for a full refund.

For further questions, consumers may contact South Asian Food Inc. at 718-894-2507 between 10:00 AM and 5:00 PM Eastern Time, Monday through Friday.

headline: Ariana Sweets Inc. Issues Allergy Alert on Undeclared Sesame and Wheat in AFGHANI CORN BREAD (“Doda”)

image.caption: Ariana Sweets Inc. Issues Allergy Alert on Undeclared Sesame and Wheat in AFGHANI CORN BREAD (“Doda”)

description: Ariana announced a recall involving Afghani Corn Bread after issues linked to undeclared allergen – sesame, wheat. FDA recall notice published in 2025.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
On May 13, 2025, Ariana Sweets Inc., based in Fremont, California, announced a recall of its AFGHANI CORN BREAD “Doda” product manufactured before April 24, 2025. The recall is due to the presence of undeclared allergens, specifically sesame and wheat. These allergens were not listed on the product packaging, which poses a serious risk to individuals with allergies or sensitivities to sesame or wheat.

The affected product is a shelf-stable, 24 oz round sweet bread packaged in a plastic bag and distributed to retail locations within California. This product has a UPC number of 753182432670. No allergic reactions or illnesses have been reported at this time.

The recall was initiated following a routine inspection by the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA), which found that the product contained sesame and wheat but was labeled without these allergens. Consumers who have allergy or sensitivity to sesame or wheat are advised not to consume this product. They should either dispose of it or return it to the place of purchase to receive a full refund.

Customers with questions or concerns can contact Ariana Sweets Inc. by phone at (510) 795-9600 or via email at info@arianasweets.com. Customer service is available from 8:00 a.m. to 4:30 p.m. PST, Monday through Friday.

headline: NatureMills US Inc. Issues Allergy Alert on Undeclared Wheat, Milk, and Sesame in Rice Mixes, Soups, Spice Mixes, Porridge Mix, Papads and Vadam Products

image.caption: NatureMills US Inc. Issues Allergy Alert on Undeclared Wheat, Milk, and Sesame in Rice Mixes, Soups, Spice Mixes, Porridge Mix, Papads and Vadam Products

description: Nature Mills announced a recall involving Rice Mixes, Soups, Spice Mixes, Porridge Mix, Papads and Vadam Products after issues linked to undeclared allergen...
keywords: Rice Mixes, Soups, Spice Mixes, Porridge Mix, Papads and Vadam Products

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
NatureMills US Inc., located in Prosper, Texas, has issued a recall of select rice mixes, soups, spice mixes, porridge mix, papads, and vadam products due to undeclared allergens including wheat, milk, and sesame. These allergens were not listed on the product labels, posing a risk of serious or life-threatening allergic reactions for individuals with allergies or severe sensitivities to these ingredients.

The affected products were sold nationwide in the United States through the NatureMills website between December 1, 2023, and May 10, 2025. The recall was initiated after a routine internal audit identified missing allergen information on the labels, traced back to an oversight in the packaging process. No illnesses have been reported in relation to this recall so far.

Consumers who have purchased any of these products and have allergies to wheat, milk, or sesame are advised not to consume them. Instead, they should dispose of the items and contact NatureMills US Inc. for a full refund or replacement. The company can be reached via email at info@naturemills.com or by phone at 1-833-628-8736, available from 9 AM to 5 PM CST. Additional information is also available on the company’s recall webpage at www.naturemills.com/pages/recall.

headline: Knockro Issues Allergy Alert on Undeclared Almonds in Bonya Yogurt Parfaits

image.caption: Knockro Issues Allergy Alert on Undeclared Almonds in Bonya Yogurt Parfaits

description: Bonya announced a recall involving Low Fat Yogurt Parfaits after issues linked to undeclared allergen – tree nuts (almonds). FDA recall notice published in...

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Knockro Inc. of Pennsylvania has issued a recall for its Bonya-branded low fat yogurt parfait products due to the presence of undeclared almonds in the granola component. This undeclared tree nut allergen poses a risk of serious or potentially life-threatening allergic reactions to consumers with almond allergies or severe sensitivities.

The affected yogurt parfaits were distributed nationwide through retail stores. These products are packaged in 12-ounce clear plastic cups and have an expiration date of May 5, 2025. To date, no illnesses related to this recall have been reported.

The recall was initiated after the company discovered that the almond-containing granola was packaged without listing almonds on the label. Investigations revealed that this issue stemmed from a temporary failure in the production and packaging processes at Knockro Inc.

Consumers who have purchased the Bonya low fat yogurt parfaits with the affected packaging are advised to return them to the place of purchase for a full refund. For further questions, customers may contact Knockro Inc. at 914-313-6905.

headline: Fresh & Ready Foods Voluntarily Recalls Ready-to-Eat Sandwiches and Snack Items Sold in Arizona, California, Nevada and Washington Due to Possible Listeria monocytogenes Contamination

image.caption: Fresh & Ready Foods Voluntarily Recalls Ready-to-Eat Sandwiches and Snack Items Sold in Arizona, California, Nevada and Washington Due to Possible Listeria monocytogenes Contamination

description: Fresh & Ready Foods, Fresh Take Crave Away, City Point Market Fresh Food to Go announced a recall involving Ready to eat sandwiches and snack items after...
keywords: Fresh & Ready Foods, Fresh Take Crave Away, City Point Market Fresh Food to Go
Ready to eat sandwiches and snack items

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Fresh & Ready Foods LLC has announced a voluntary recall of certain ready-to-eat sandwiches and snack items sold in Arizona, California, Nevada, and Washington due to potential contamination with Listeria monocytogenes. The recalled products were distributed between April 18, 2025, and April 28, 2025, primarily in vending and breakroom areas within corporate offices, medical buildings, and healthcare facilities in the affected states. The recall specifically targets products with “Use By” dates ranging from April 22, 2025, to May 19, 2025.

The issue was identified during an FDA site inspection through environmental monitoring, which detected Listeria monocytogenes on a piece of equipment used in the production process. Fresh & Ready Foods has responded by removing the impacted equipment and implementing corrective actions to maintain food safety and comply with FDA guidance.

Listeria monocytogenes can cause severe and potentially fatal infections, especially in young children, elderly individuals, people with weakened immune systems, and pregnant women. Symptoms may include high fever, severe headache, stiffness, nausea, abdominal pain, and diarrhea. Pregnant women infected with Listeria are at risk of miscarriage or stillbirth. No illnesses have been reported in connection with the recalled products to date, but the recall is a precautionary measure to ensure consumer safety.

Consumers who purchased these products should check the “Use By” date printed on the front of the package under the “Net Weight.” Any products with the affected dates should be discarded immediately. For questions or to request a refund, consumers can contact Fresh & Ready Foods at RecallConcern@HotLineGlobal.com or call 1-855-424-8390 Monday through Friday, from 5:00 am to 3:00 pm PST.

Brand Product Description Distributed States Distribution Dates Use By Dates
Fresh & Ready Foods, Fresh Take Crave Away, City Point Market Fresh Food to Go Ready-to-eat sandwiches and snack items Arizona, California, Nevada, Washington 04/18/2025 – 04/28/2025 04/22/2025 – 05/19/2025

headline: New Grains Gluten Free Bakery Issues Allergy Alert on Undeclared Eggs, Soy, and Milk in Bakery Products
description: New Grain Gluten Free Bakery announced a recall involving Breads, bagels, cookies and croutons after issues linked to products may contain undeclared eggs,...
keywords: Products may contain undeclared eggs, soy, and milk

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
New Grains Gluten Free Bakery has issued a recall for several of its bakery products, including breads, bagels, cookies, and croutons, due to the presence of undeclared allergens: eggs, soy, and milk. These allergens were not disclosed on the product labels, which poses a risk of serious or life-threatening allergic reactions for individuals with allergies or severe sensitivities to these ingredients.

The affected items were distributed in Utah through retail stores between April 7 and April 21 under lot numbers 90–107. The breads and croutons were packaged in clear vacuum-sealed plastic bags, while the cookies came in regular clear plastic bags. All recalled products bear the New Grains Gluten Free Bakery brand and are labeled as "gluten-free." The label colors vary and include red, purple, orange, blue, green, and pink.

No illnesses related to this recall have been reported at this time. Consumers who have purchased any of the listed products are advised not to eat them and to return the products to the place of purchase for a full refund. For further assistance, New Grains Gluten Free Bakery can be contacted at 801-980-5751, Monday through Friday, from 10 AM to 3 PM MST.

This recall is conducted in cooperation with the U.S. Food and Drug Administration.

headline: Advantage Health Matters Inc Recalls "Organic Jumbo Pumpkin Seeds" Because of Possible Health Risk

image.caption: Advantage Health Matters Inc Recalls "Organic Jumbo Pumpkin Seeds" Because of Possible Health Risk

description: Organic traditions announced a recall involving Organic Jumbo Pumpkin Seeds after issues linked to potential foodborne illness - salmonella. FDA recall...

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Advantage Health Matters Inc., located in North York, Ontario, Canada, has issued a recall of its 8-ounce packages of "Organic Jumbo Pumpkin Seeds" due to potential contamination with Salmonella. Salmonella infection can cause serious and sometimes fatal illnesses, particularly in young children, elderly individuals, and those with weakened immune systems. Symptoms in healthy people typically include fever, diarrhea (which may be bloody), nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain.

The recalled product was distributed in the states of New York, New Jersey, and Virginia through retail stores and mail orders. To date, no illnesses have been reported in connection with this recall. The recall was initiated following a supplier recall from another country. Advantage Health Matters has suspended production of this product while investigating the source of the contamination.

Consumers who have purchased the recalled "Organic Jumbo Pumpkin Seeds" are advised to return the product to the place of purchase for a full refund. For further questions, consumers may contact the company via email at info@organictraditions.com.

headline: EnShiShiXiangNiShangMaoYouXianGongSi Issues Voluntary Nationwide Recall of ENDURANCE BOOST WITH HORNY GOAT WEED Capsules Due To Presence of Undeclared Propoxyphenylsildenafil and Sildenafil

image.caption: EnShiShiXiangNiShangMaoYouXianGongSi Issues Voluntary Nationwide Recall of ENDURANCE BOOST WITH HORNY GOAT WEED Capsules Due To Presence of Undeclared Propoxyphenylsildenafil and Sildenafil

description: Endurance Boost announced a recall involving Dietary supplement for male performance and energy after issues linked to undeclared propoxyphenylsildenafil,...
keywords: Dietary supplement for male performance and energy

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
EnShiShiXiangNiShangMaoYouXianGongSi has issued a voluntary nationwide recall of one lot of its dietary supplement capsules, ENDURANCE BOOST WITH HORNY GOAT WEED (20 Count). This product, marketed for male performance and energy, was found to contain undeclared Propoxyphenylsildenafil and Sildenafil. Sildenafil is a pharmaceutical ingredient used to treat erectile dysfunction and cannot be legally included in dietary supplements.

The presence of these unlisted drug substances poses health risks, especially for individuals who take nitrates for conditions such as heart disease, diabetes, high blood pressure, or high cholesterol. These interactions can cause dangerously low blood pressure, which may be life threatening. The recalled product lot is identified as LOT: 250214PRO with an expiration date of 02/14/2027.

To date, no adverse events related to this recall have been reported. The company is notifying customers through this announcement and arranging for the return of all recalled products. Consumers who have this lot should immediately stop using the capsules and consult their healthcare provider if they have experienced any related health issues.

For questions or to report problems, consumers may contact EnShiShiXiangNiShangMaoYouXianGongSi by email or submit adverse event reports to the FDA. The recall is conducted in cooperation with the U.S. Food and Drug Administration.

headline: New England Village Foods Issues Allergy Alert on Undeclared Almonds and Sesame in “19th Hole Snack Mix”

image.caption: New England Village Foods Issues Allergy Alert on Undeclared Almonds and Sesame in “19th Hole Snack Mix”

description: New England Village Snacks announced a recall involving 19th Hole Snack Mix after issues linked to undeclared almonds and sesame. FDA recall notice...

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
New England Village Foods of Milford, New Hampshire, has announced a recall of all lots of its “19th Hole Snack Mix” sold under the New England Village Snacks brand. This recall affects 5-ounce and 10-ounce containers due to the presence of undeclared almonds and sesame. The omission of these allergens on the labeling poses a risk of serious or life-threatening allergic reactions for individuals allergic to almonds or sesame seeds.

The affected products were distributed to small independent grocery markets and convenience stores in New England, New York, and Pennsylvania. These products are packaged in clear, reclosable cups and tubs with lids marked by the following UPCs: 609465693477 for the 5-ounce size and 642147152459 for the 10-ounce size. The allergen labeling did not disclose the presence of almonds, and the “Contains” statement failed to include sesame despite its presence in the ingredient list.

The recall was initiated after a customer reported discovering almonds in the product where the packaging did not disclose them. An FDA review confirmed the labeling issue, identifying that a temporary breakdown in the company’s packaging process caused this error. Production of the “19th Hole Snack Mix” has been temporarily suspended as a precautionary measure.

No illnesses have been reported in connection with this recall. Consumers who have purchased these products are advised to return them to the place of purchase for a full refund. For questions or further information, consumers can contact New England Village Foods at 1.603.554.1873, available Monday through Friday from 7 AM to 4 PM Eastern Time.

Product Name Package Size UPC
19th Hole Snack Mix 5-ounce 609465693477
19th Hole Snack Mix 10-ounce 642147152459
  • Return affected products to the place of purchase for a full refund.
  • Contact New England Village Foods with questions at 1.603.554.1873 (Monday–Friday, 7 AM–4 PM Eastern).
  • Avoid consuming the product if allergic to almonds or sesame to prevent allergic reactions.

headline: East Trading Inc., Issues Alert on Undeclared Sulfites in “Licorice Plum”

image.caption: East Trading Inc., Issues Alert on Undeclared Sulfites in “Licorice Plum”

description: President Brand announced a recall involving Licorice plum after issues linked to potential or undeclared allergen – sulfites unapproved color – amaranth...
keywords: Potential or Undeclared Allergen – sulfites Unapproved color – Amaranth (E123)

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
East CK Trading, Inc., located in Long Island City, NY, has issued a recall for its 8-ounce packages of “Licorice Plum” food treats. This recall is due to the presence of undeclared sulfites and the use of an unapproved color additive, Amaranth (E123), in the product.

Individuals with severe sensitivity to sulfites may experience serious or potentially life-threatening allergic reactions if they consume this product. Testing revealed that the “Licorice Plum” contains 29.1 milligrams of sulfites per serving, a level significantly above the 10 milligrams per serving reported to cause severe reactions in some asthmatics, including anaphylactic shock in sulfite-sensitive individuals.

The recalled product was distributed nationwide through retail stores and mail orders. To date, no illnesses or allergic reactions related to the consumption of this product have been reported. The recall was triggered following routine inspection and laboratory analysis by the New York State Department of Agriculture and Markets Food Inspectors and Food Laboratory personnel, which identified sulfites not declared on the product label.

Consumers who have purchased the 8-ounce “Licorice Plum” are advised to return the product to the place of purchase for a full refund. For further information or questions, customers can contact East CK Trading, Inc. at 1-718-857-0008.

Product Package Size UPC Code Reason for Recall
Licorice Plum 8 ounces (clear plastic bottle) 0077-20729 Undeclared sulfites; Unapproved color Amaranth (E123)
  • Return recalled product to place of purchase for a full refund.
  • Contact East CK Trading, Inc. at 1-718-857-0008 for questions.
  • Be aware that consumption of 10 mg or more of sulfites can cause severe reactions in sensitive individuals, and the recalled product contains 29.1 mg per serving.

headline: Vietti Food Group Issues Allergy Alert on Undeclared Soy in 15-oz Yellowstone Brown Sugar Molasses Baked Beans

image.caption: Vietti Food Group Issues Allergy Alert on Undeclared Soy in 15-oz Yellowstone Brown Sugar Molasses Baked Beans

description: Yellowstone announced a recall involving Baked Beans after issues linked to potential or undeclared allergen - soy. FDA recall notice published in 2025.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Vietti Food Group of Nashville, TN has announced a recall of 4,515 cases of Yellowstone Brown Sugar Molasses Baked Beans (15 oz.) due to the presence of undeclared soy. This recall is relevant for individuals who have an allergy or severe sensitivity to soy, as consuming the product could result in serious or potentially life-threatening allergic reactions.

The affected product was distributed to retailers across multiple states, including Arizona, Colorado, Delaware, Florida, Georgia, Illinois, Iowa, Kansas, Kentucky, Louisiana, Michigan, Mississippi, Missouri, New Hampshire, New York, North Carolina, Ohio, Oregon, Pennsylvania, Tennessee, Texas, Utah, and Virginia. The specific item can be identified by the Lot Code labeled "Best if Used By Feb 17, 2028," which is printed on the bottom of each can.

At present, there have been no reported illnesses or adverse reactions linked to the consumption of this product. Consumers who have purchased this item are advised to return it to the original place of purchase to receive a full refund.

headline: Ray & Mascari Inc. Recalls 4 Count Vine Ripe Tomatoes Because of Possible Health Risk

image.caption: Ray & Mascari Inc. Recalls 4 Count Vine Ripe Tomatoes Because of Possible Health Risk

description: Ray & Mascari Inc. announced a recall involving Tomatoes after issues linked to potential foodborne illness - salmonella. FDA recall notice published in 2025.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Ray & Mascari Inc. of Indianapolis, Indiana, is voluntarily recalling their 4 Count Vine Ripe Tomatoes packaged in 20 oz. (1 lb. 4 oz) clam shell containers due to a potential risk of Salmonella contamination. The affected products carry UPC# 7 96553 20062 1 and are identified by master case Lot# RM250424 15250B or Lot# RM250427 15250B. This recall follows notification from Hanshaw & Capling Farms, the original supplier, who initiated a recall after detecting possible Salmonella presence in their facility.

Salmonella infections can cause serious health issues, especially for young children, elderly individuals, and those with weakened immune systems. Symptoms commonly include fever, diarrhea (sometimes bloody), nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain. In rare cases, Salmonella can lead to severe complications such as bloodstream infections, arterial infections, endocarditis, and arthritis.

The recalled 4 Count Vine Ripe Tomatoes were sold in plastic clamshell containers labeled “Packed by Ray & Mascari Inc., Indianapolis, IN 46204” and distributed primarily through Gordon Food Service Stores across Illinois, Indiana, Kentucky, Michigan, Missouri, Mississippi, New York, Ohio, Pennsylvania, Tennessee, and Wisconsin. Consumers who have purchased this product are advised not to eat the tomatoes and to discard them immediately.

No illnesses related to this recall have been reported to date. Consumers with questions or who wish to report any illness related to the product can contact Ray & Mascari Inc. at 1-317-637-0234, Monday through Saturday, 6 am to 5 pm EST.

  • Do not consume the recalled tomatoes.
  • Discard any 4 Count Vine Ripe Tomatoes matching the above description and lot numbers.
  • Contact Ray & Mascari Inc. at 1-317-637-0234 with questions or to report illness.
  • Contact hours: Monday-Saturday, 6am-5pm EST.

headline: Williams Farms Repack LLC Recalls Tomatoes Due to Possible Salmonella Contamination

image.caption: Williams Farms Repack LLC Recalls Tomatoes Due to Possible Salmonella Contamination

description: H&C Farms Label announced a recall involving Tomatoes after issues linked to potential foodborne illness - salmonella. FDA recall notice published in 2025.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Williams Farms Repack LLC has announced a recall of several tomato products due to potential contamination with Salmonella. This contamination poses a risk of serious illness, particularly for young children, the elderly, and individuals with weakened immune systems. Salmonella infection can cause symptoms such as fever, diarrhea (which may be bloody), nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain. In rare cases, the infection can spread to the bloodstream and lead to more severe conditions like arterial infections, endocarditis, and arthritis.

The recall follows notification from Southeast Tomato Distributors on April 29, 2025, regarding possible Salmonella contamination in tomatoes supplied from H&C Farms. To date, no illnesses have been reported in connection with these products.

The recalled products were distributed to wholesalers and distributors in Georgia, North Carolina, and South Carolina between April 23, 2025, and April 28, 2025. Consumers who have purchased these products are advised not to eat them and to either return the tomatoes to the place of purchase for a full refund or discard them.

For questions about the recall, consumers may contact Jason Breland at 843-866-7707 or 843-599-5154 Monday through Friday, from 8:00 am to 5:00 pm EST. The recall is being conducted with the knowledge of the U.S. Food and Drug Administration.

The recall was initiated after it was found that the packaging did not properly label the presence of milk protein. Consumers who have purchased Ankimo Monkfish Liver are advised not to eat the product and to return it to the place of purchase for a full refund.

For questions or further information, consumers can contact JJWV Marketing Corporation at 1-562-906-9988, available Monday through Friday from 8:00 am to 5:00 pm Pacific Standard Time. This recall is being conducted in cooperation with the U.S. Food and Drug Administration.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Trader Joe’s and Taylor Fresh Foods have initiated a voluntary recall of a limited quantity of Trader Joe’s Sesame Miso Salad with Salmon due to the potential presence of an undeclared milk allergen. Specifically, some packages of the salad were mistakenly included with a sealed packet of parmesan cheese crumbles instead of the intended crispy onion topping.

This issue affects products with Use By dates of April 28, 2025, and April 29, 2025. Approximately 500 units were distributed to Trader Joe’s stores across multiple states. There have been no reported adverse reactions to date. The recall is limited solely to the mentioned product and dates, with no other Trader Joe’s products or brands involved. Consumers who purchased the affected salad are advised to discard it to prevent possible allergic reactions.
