headline: Health Fixer Issues Voluntary Nationwide Recall of Male Ultra, Malextra, Electro Buzz, Ultra Armor and Male Ultra Pro Due to Undeclared Chloropretadalafil, Propoxyphenylsildenafil and Sildenafil

image.caption: Health Fixer Issues Voluntary Nationwide Recall of Male Ultra, Malextra, Electro Buzz, Ultra Armor and Male Ultra Pro Due to Undeclared Chloropretadalafil, Propoxyphenylsildenafil and Sildenafil

description: Health Fixer announced a recall involving Male Enhancement Dietary Supplements after issues linked to undeclared chloropretadalafil,...

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Health Fixer has issued a voluntary nationwide recall of several male enhancement dietary supplements due to the presence of undeclared substances. Laboratory testing revealed that the products, marketed under the names Male Ultra, Malextra, Electro Buzz, Ultra Armor, and Male Ultra Pro, contain chloropretadalafil, propoxyphenylsildenafil, and sildenafil. These ingredients are phosphodiesterase (PDE-5) inhibitors or analogues, which are not approved for use in dietary supplements and pose potential health risks.

Consumption of these products may lead to serious adverse effects, especially for individuals taking nitrates for heart conditions, as the combination can cause a dangerous drop in blood pressure. People with diabetes, high blood pressure, high cholesterol, or heart disease who use nitrates are particularly at risk. Health Fixer has not received any reports of adverse events related to this recall to date.

The affected dietary supplements are packaged in blister packs containing 10 capsules each and were distributed nationwide through Amazon Online Shopping. Consumers who have purchased these products should discontinue use immediately and consult their healthcare provider if they have experienced any health concerns. For questions, Health Fixer can be contacted by phone or email.

Consumers with questions about the recall can contact Health Fixer Monday through Friday, 9 a.m. to 6 p.m. Mountain Time, at 414-888-8818 or via email at ultrahelpbyfixer@gmail.com. Anyone experiencing adverse reactions or quality problems with these products should consult their healthcare provider and report the issues to the FDA's MedWatch program.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Mauna Loa Macadamia Nut Company LLC of Kea’au, HI, has voluntarily issued a recall for certain Mauna Loa Dark Chocolate Covered Macadamias products due to the potential presence of undeclared almonds and cashews. This allergy alert affects consumers who have allergies or severe sensitivities to these nuts, as consuming the product could lead to serious or life-threatening allergic reactions.

The recall involves Mauna Loa Dark Chocolate Covered Macadamias packaged in 0.6oz and 4oz bags. These products were distributed across various states including Arizona, California, Colorado, Florida, Hawaii, Illinois, Maine, Michigan, New Jersey, Oregon, Pennsylvania, Texas, Utah, Virginia, Washington, Wisconsin, and also Guam.

The issue was identified through Mauna Loa’s internal quality control process, which found that the affected batches, manufactured by a third-party co-manufacturer, contained undeclared almonds and cashews. No illnesses or adverse reactions have been reported in connection with this recall to date.

Consumers who have purchased the affected products and have an allergy to almonds or cashews should not consume the product. Instead, they are advised to return the items to the place of purchase for a full refund. For further inquiries, customers can contact Mauna Loa’s Customer Service at 1-888-255-5998, available Monday through Friday.

headline: Cronus Pharma LLC initiating Voluntary Recall for (Dexased™ -Aspen and Dexmedvet™ -Cronus Pharma LLC) of Dexmedetomidine Hydrochloride Injection 0.5 mg/mL For Veterinary Use Due to Presence of Particulate Matter

image.caption: Cronus Pharma LLC initiating Voluntary Recall for (Dexased™ -Aspen and Dexmedvet™ -Cronus Pharma LLC) of Dexmedetomidine Hydrochloride Injection 0.5 mg/mL For Veterinary Use Due to Presence of Particulate Matter

description: Dexased™ and Dexmedvet™ announced a recall involving Dexmedetomidine Hydrochloride Injection 0.5 mg/mL after issues linked to presence of particulate...

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Cronus Pharma LLC has announced a voluntary recall of two veterinary products containing Dexmedetomidine Hydrochloride Injection 0.5 mg/mL due to the presence of visible particulate matter. The affected products are marketed under the brand names Dexased™ (Aspen) and Dexmedvet™ (Cronus Pharma LLC). These injectable sedative and analgesic medications are used in dogs and cats to facilitate clinical examinations, minor surgical and dental procedures, and as a preanesthetic prior to general anesthesia.

The recall involves one batch consisting of two distinct lot numbers distributed between December 2024 and April 2025. The particulate matter observed includes crystal-like particles, white floating material, or precipitates found during visual inspection of control samples. The presence of particulate matter in an injectable product poses potential risks, such as inflammation, granuloma, fibrosis, or blood vessel blockage that could lead to stroke, life-threatening clots, or death.

Pet owners and veterinary professionals who have received these lots are advised to stop using the affected products immediately and follow return instructions provided in their recall notification. If any animal shows signs of adverse reactions—such as pain, weakness, swelling, paralysis, fever, respiratory difficulty, vomiting, decreased activity, vocalization, or loss of consciousness—prompt veterinary consultation is strongly recommended.

Cronus Pharma LLC is working closely with distributors to remove the recalled products from distribution and arrange for their return. Consumers may contact the company for technical questions or product return details via phone, fax, or email. Adverse events can also be reported to the FDA through their dedicated contact channels.

Brand NDC Number Lot Number Manufacturing Date Expiration Date Distribution Dates

Dexmedvet™ (Cronus Pharma LLC) 69043-038-10 C24041AV2 Nov 2024 Oct 2026 March 2025 to April 4, 2025

For technical questions or to report issues, contact Cronus Pharma LLC:

  • Phone: 1-844-227-6687 (Monday–Friday, 9 a.m. – 5 p.m. CDT)
  • Fax: 732-647-1272
  • Email: pv@cronuspharmausa.com

To report adverse reactions or quality problems to the FDA:

  • Online: http://www.FDA.gov/reportanimalae

headline: New England Village Foods Issues Allergy Alert on Undeclared Almonds in “19th Hole Snack Mix”

image.caption: New England Village Foods Issues Allergy Alert on Undeclared Almonds in “19th Hole Snack Mix”

description: New England Village Snacks announced a recall involving 19th Hole Snack Mix after issues linked to undeclared almonds. FDA recall notice published in 2025.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
New England Village Foods, based in Milford, NH, is recalling all lots of their “19th Hole Snack Mix” sold under the New England Village Snacks brand. The recall affects both 5-ounce and 10-ounce containers due to undeclared almonds in the product. This poses a serious risk to individuals with almond allergies, as consumption could trigger a severe or potentially life-threatening allergic reaction.

The affected products were distributed to small independent grocery markets and convenience stores across New England, New York, and Pennsylvania. The almond-containing snack mix was packaged in clear cups and tubs with re-closable lids, but the packaging failed to disclose the presence of almonds due to a temporary issue in the company’s packaging process. Production of the product has been temporarily suspended as a precaution.

No illnesses have been reported so far. Consumers who purchased the 5-ounce or 10-ounce “19th Hole Snack Mix” are advised to return the product to the place of purchase for a full refund. For questions or additional information, consumers can contact New England Village Foods at 1.603.554.1873, Monday through Friday, from 7 AM to 4 PM Eastern Time.

headline: Amneal Pharmaceutical LLC Issues a Nationwide Recall of Ropivacaine Hydrochloride Injection, USP 500mg/100mL, Due to the Potential Presence of Particulate Matter

image.caption: Amneal Pharmaceutical LLC Issues a Nationwide Recall of Ropivacaine Hydrochloride Injection, USP 500mg/100mL, Due to the Potential Presence of Particulate Matter

description: Amneal Pharmaceuticals LLC. announced a recall involving Ropivacaine Hydrochloride Injection, USP, 500mg/100mL IV bag after issues linked to product may...
keywords: Ropivacaine Hydrochloride Injection, USP, 500mg/100mL IV bag
Product may contain an inert fiber identified as polypropylene fibers from the IV bag

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Amneal Pharmaceuticals LLC has announced a nationwide recall of two lots of Ropivacaine Hydrochloride Injection, USP, 500mg/100mL infusion bags. The recall is due to the potential presence of inert polypropylene fiber particulates originating from the IV bag. These fibers could pose serious health risks if introduced into the epidural or intrathecal spaces during administration.

The product is commonly used for local or regional anesthesia in surgery and acute pain management. Exposure to polypropylene particulate matter in the epidural space may trigger inflammatory processes such as meningitis or cause spinal cord damage. If administered intrathecally, these particulates could lead to inflammation, hydrocephalus, embolization, and organ damage. Currently, there have been no reported adverse events or injuries linked to this recall.

The affected lots, AL240003 and AL240004, both expiring in January 2026, were distributed nationwide to wholesalers and distributors between April 23, 2024, and November 8, 2024. Amneal Pharmaceuticals is contacting customers directly to facilitate the return of recalled products and requests that wholesalers and distributors inform hospital and user facilities about the recall.

Healthcare providers and users with questions or requiring further assistance regarding this recall can contact Amneal Pharmaceuticals through the provided phone, fax, or email channels. The recall is conducted with the knowledge of the U.S. Food and Drug Administration.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Gerber Products Company has previously recalled and discontinued all batches of Gerber® Soothe N Chew® Teething Sticks due to a potential choking hazard for babies and young children. This recall was initiated on January 31, 2025, following consumer complaints regarding choking incidents. The affected products were distributed nationwide and include specific varieties of the teething sticks.

Consumers who have purchased Gerber® Soothe N Chew® Teething Sticks are advised not to feed these products to their children. The company recommends returning the recalled items to the retailer for a full refund. Additionally, consumers should avoid purchasing these teething sticks if found for sale. Anyone with health concerns related to the product should consult a healthcare provider.

For further assistance, Gerber offers support 24/7 through their customer service line at 1-800-4-GERBER (1-800-443-7237). Gerber is working closely with the U.S. Food & Drug Administration (FDA) in connection with this recall.

headline: Harvest NYC Inc Recalls Enoki Mushroom Due to Possible Health Risk

image.caption: Harvest NYC Inc Recalls Enoki Mushroom Due to Possible Health Risk

description: Hofood99 Inc announced a recall involving Enoki Mushrooms after issues linked to due to possibility of contamination with listeria monocytogenes.. FDA...
keywords: Due to possibility of contamination with Listeria monocytogenes.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Harvest NYC Inc, located in Brooklyn, NY, has issued a recall of its 200g packages of Enoki Mushrooms due to possible contamination with Listeria monocytogenes. This bacterium can cause severe and sometimes fatal infections, particularly in young children, elderly individuals, pregnant women, and those with weakened immune systems. Healthy individuals may experience symptoms such as high fever, severe headache, stiffness, nausea, abdominal pain, and diarrhea.

The affected products were distributed nationwide through retail stores. The recall was initiated after the New York State Department of Agriculture and Markets Food Laboratory detected Listeria monocytogenes in samples taken from a store in Buffalo, NY. At this time, no illnesses have been reported in connection with these products.

Consumers who purchased the recalled Enoki Mushrooms—specifically 200g packages sold between January 11 and January 31, 2025—are advised to either dispose of the product immediately or return it to the place of purchase for a full refund. For further inquiries, customers may contact Harvest NYC Inc at (718) 596-0777.

headline: FDA Classifies Q’Apel Medical Inc.’s Worldwide Medical Device Recall and Discontinuation of its 072 Aspiration System (Hippo) as Class I
description: Q’Apel Medical announced a recall involving 072 Aspiration System after issues linked to distal tip design outside scope of 510k clearance. FDA recall...
keywords: Distal Tip Design outside scope of 510k clearance

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
The U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA) has classified Q’Apel Medical Inc.’s voluntary recall and discontinuation of its 072 Aspiration System (also known as Hippo with Cheetah Delivery Tool) as a Class I recall. The recall involves a total of 1,617 units and was initiated by Q’Apel on February 26, 2025, following a Warning Letter from the FDA concerning whether the distal tip design of the Hippo aspiration catheter fell within the scope of its 510(k) clearance.

As a result, Q’Apel has chosen to voluntarily remove all affected product lots from distribution and discontinue the 072 Aspiration System line. This decision supports the company’s strategic shift toward newer technologies. The product was distributed in the United States, Qatar, United Arab Emirates, and the Republic of Kazakhstan.

Q’Apel Medical has reported three adverse events related to the Hippo product, including tip detachment (retrieved without patient injury), vessel rupture, and vasospasm. The company’s investigation suggests that factors other than the device’s distal tip likely contributed to these events.

Customers and consignees who have these products should immediately cease use and return any remaining inventory to Q’Apel. The company has contacted all customers and distributors to facilitate the return process and will continue monitoring the situation.

headline: May Flower International Inc., Issue Allergy Alert on Undeclared Wheat in “Beijing Soybean Paste”

image.caption: May Flower International Inc., Issue Allergy Alert on Undeclared Wheat in “Beijing Soybean Paste”

description: May Flower International announced a recall involving Soybean Paste after issues linked to undeclared wheat. FDA recall notice published in 2025.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
May Flower International Inc. of Maspeth, NY, has issued a recall for its 8.82-ounce (250g) packages of “Beijing Soybean Paste” due to the presence of undeclared wheat. This poses a risk of serious or life-threatening allergic reactions to individuals with wheat allergies.

The recalled product was distributed nationwide in retail stores and is packaged in plastic containers labeled with UPC 6917799000385. The packaging shows that the product was packed for Fu Xiang Yuan Trading Inc., New York City, NY 11378. The distribution period ranges from March 25, 2023, to March 5, 2024.

The recall was prompted by a routine FDA inspection that found the product listing flour as an ingredient, but failing to declare wheat, which is a major allergen. No illnesses or allergic reactions have been reported so far in connection with this product.

Consumers who have purchased the “Beijing Soybean Paste” in the described packaging should return it to the place of purchase for a full refund. Questions can be directed to the company at 1-718-366-8668, Monday through Friday, from 9:00 AM to 6:00 PM Eastern Time.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary

Supplement Manufacturing Partners, Inc. has announced a voluntary recall of Dorado Nutrition brand Spermidine Maximum Strength 10 MG per serving due to the presence of undeclared wheat. This undeclared allergen poses a risk of serious or potentially life-threatening allergic reactions for individuals with wheat allergies or severe sensitivities.

The recalled product was sold online through Amazon between May 22, 2024, and April 4, 2025. It was also distributed in Germany under the name Spermidin 60 Kapseln 38g by Deep Green GmbH, also known as Space Garden. The affected Dorado Nutrition Spermidine is packaged in white capsule bottles containing 120 capsules, featuring a blue outlined label.

The recall was initiated following product testing that revealed the presence of wheat not listed on the label. To date, no allergic or adverse reactions have been reported. The mislabeled products have been removed from sale to prevent further risk to consumers.

Consumers who have purchased the affected products are advised to return them to their place of purchase for a full refund. For further inquiries, the company can be contacted via email at SMPQuality@smpnutra.com or by phone at 833-810-9896, Monday through Friday, 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. EST.

headline: Blue Ridge Beef Issues Voluntary Recall of Blue Ridge Beef Puppy Mix and Kitten Mix Due to Salmonella & Listeria Contamination

image.caption: Blue Ridge Beef Issues Voluntary Recall of Blue Ridge Beef Puppy Mix and Kitten Mix Due to Salmonella & Listeria Contamination

description: Blue Ridge Beef announced a recall involving Puppy Mix and Kitten Mix after issues linked to salmonella & listeria contamination.. FDA recall notice...
keywords: Animal & Veterinary Food & Beverages Pet Food Foodborne Illness

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Blue Ridge Beef has issued a voluntary recall of certain lots of its Puppy Mix and Kitten Mix due to potential contamination with Salmonella and Listeria bacteria. The recalled products include 2 lb logs from Lot #N25 1230 of the Puppy Mix and Lot #N26 0114 of the Kitten Mix. These products were distributed between January 3 and January 24, 2025, primarily to retail stores in Virginia, Maryland, Pennsylvania, Connecticut, Massachusetts, New York State, Tennessee, Rhode Island, and Wisconsin.

Salmonella and Listeria can pose health risks to both pets and humans. Pets consuming the contaminated food may experience symptoms such as lethargy, diarrhea (possibly bloody), fever, vomiting, decreased appetite, or abdominal pain. Infected animals can also carry and spread the infections without showing symptoms. Humans handling the products may experience nausea, vomiting, diarrhea or bloody diarrhea, abdominal cramps, and fever. More severe complications can occur, including arterial infections and other serious conditions. Anyone experiencing symptoms after contact with the recalled products should seek medical advice, and pet owners should contact their veterinarians if their animals have consumed the affected food and show illness.

The recall was initiated following a customer complaint and subsequent testing by the New York State Department of Agriculture and Markets, which confirmed contamination with Salmonella and Listeria. Blue Ridge Beef advises consumers to return the recalled products to the place of purchase or destroy them safely to prevent access by children, pets, or wildlife. Consumers should not feed these products to animals, sell, or donate them. Proper hygiene, including washing and sanitizing hands, pet food bowls, cups, and storage containers after handling the recalled products, is strongly recommended.

For additional information, consumers can contact Blue Ridge Beef via email at blueridgebeefnc@yahoo.com or by phone at 704-873-2072, Monday through Friday from 8:00 am to 5:00 pm EST. This recall is being conducted with the knowledge of the Food and Drug Administration.

Product Size Lot Number
Blue Ridge Beef Puppy Mix 2 lb N25 1230
Blue Ridge Beef Kitten Mix 2 lb N26 0114
  • Do not feed recalled products to pets or other animals.
  • Return recalled products to the place of purchase or dispose of them safely.
  • Do not sell or donate recalled products.
  • Wash and sanitize pet food bowls, cups, and storage containers thoroughly.
  • Always wash and sanitize hands after handling recalled products or related utensils.
  • Contact your healthcare provider if you experience symptoms of Salmonella or Listeria infection after contact with the recalled products.
  • Contact your veterinarian if your pet has consumed the recalled products and shows symptoms of illness.
  • For more information, email blueridgebeefnc@yahoo.com or call 704-873-2072 (Monday-Friday, 8:00 am – 5:00 pm EST).

image.caption: Duda Farm Fresh Foods, Inc. Emite Aviso Para 1.587 Cajas de Marketside 4in/1.6 oz Bundle Pack por Posible Riesgo Para la Salud Celery Sticks

description: Marketside announced a recall involving Celery Sticks after issues linked to due to possibility of contamination with listeria monocytogenes.. FDA recall...
keywords: Due to possibility of contamination with Listeria monocytogenes.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Duda Farm Fresh Foods, Inc. has issued a voluntary recall warning concerning a specific batch of Marketside Celery Sticks (4 inches / 1.6 oz), washed and ready to eat, with an expiration date of March 23, 2025. Although this product is past its expiration date and no longer available in stores, consumers may have frozen it for later use. The recall notice was prompted by the possible presence of Listeria monocytogenes, identified through random sampling by the Georgia Department of Agriculture at a store in Georgia, where one of several samples tested positive.

Listeria monocytogenes is a bacterium that can cause severe and sometimes fatal infections, especially in young children, elderly individuals, people with weakened immune systems, and pregnant women. While healthy individuals may experience only brief symptoms such as high fever, severe headache, stiffness, nausea, abdominal pain, and diarrhea, infection during pregnancy can lead to miscarriage or stillbirth. To date, no illnesses linked to this product have been reported.

The recalled product was sold exclusively at Walmart stores in selected states, and is identified by the UPC code 6 81131 16151 0 and lot code P047650, both printed on the packaging. This recall applies only to the specified celery sticks and not to any other Marketside or Duda Farm Fresh Foods products. All affected products are expired and have been removed from store shelves.

Consumers with this product in their possession are advised not to consume it and to dispose of it properly. For further inquiries, consumers can contact Duda Farm Fresh Foods via email at foodsafety@duda.com.

headline: Duda Farm Fresh Foods, Inc. Issues Advisory for 1,587 Cases of 4 in/1.6 oz Bundle Marketside Celery Sticks Because of Possible Health Risk

image.caption: Duda Farm Fresh Foods, Inc. Issues Advisory for 1,587 Cases of 4 in/1.6 oz Bundle Marketside Celery Sticks Because of Possible Health Risk

description: Marketside announced a recall involving Celery Sticks after issues linked to due to possibility of contamination with listeria monocytogenes.. FDA recall...
keywords: Due to possibility of contamination with Listeria monocytogenes.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Duda Farm Fresh Foods, Inc. has issued a voluntary advisory concerning 1,587 cases of Marketside Celery Sticks due to a potential health risk from contamination with Listeria monocytogenes. The affected product is a 4 count bundle of 4 in/1.6 oz washed and ready-to-eat celery sticks with a best if used by date of March 23, 2025. Although these products are past their expiration date and no longer available for sale in stores, consumers may still have them frozen at home.

The advisory was prompted after random sampling by the Georgia Department of Agriculture detected Listeria monocytogenes in a sample from a store in Georgia. Listeria monocytogenes can cause serious infections, especially in young children, elderly individuals, pregnant women, and people with weakened immune systems. Symptoms in healthy individuals can include high fever, severe headache, stiffness, nausea, abdominal pain, and diarrhea. Pregnant women exposed to the bacteria are at risk of miscarriage or stillbirth.

To date, no illnesses have been reported in connection with this product. Consumers who have this specific Marketside Celery Sticks product in their possession, including any frozen packages, are advised not to consume it and to discard it immediately. This advisory only applies to the product identified below and does not affect any other Marketside or Duda Farm Fresh Foods products.

headline: Trophy Nut Co. Issues Allergy Alert Due to Undeclared Cashews in Heinen’s Honey Roasted Peanuts

image.caption: Trophy Nut Co. Issues Allergy Alert Due to Undeclared Cashews in Heinen’s Honey Roasted Peanuts

description: Heinen’s announced a recall involving Honey Roasted Peanuts after issues linked to undeclared cashew allergen. FDA recall notice published in 2025.
keywords: Food & Beverages Nuts & Nut Products Allergens

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Trophy Nut Company of Tipp City, Ohio, is recalling 143 cases of Heinen’s 8.25 oz cans of Honey Roasted Peanuts due to the potential presence of undeclared cashews. These products may pose a serious health risk to individuals with cashew allergies or severe sensitivities, as consuming them could lead to life-threatening allergic reactions.

The affected Honey Roasted Peanuts were distributed exclusively in Ohio and Illinois through Heinen’s Grocery stores between October 24, 2024, and April 4, 2025. The product is packaged in an 8.25 oz composite can with a lid, featuring “best if used by” and “best by” dates stamped on the bottom of the can.

This recall was prompted by a consumer complaint reporting that the Honey Roasted Peanuts contained cashews. An investigation revealed the issue resulted from a temporary breakdown in the company’s production and packaging processes. To date, one allergic reaction related to this issue has been reported.

Consumers who have purchased the affected product should return it to any local Heinen’s Grocery store for a full refund. For further inquiries, Trophy Nut’s Customer Service can be reached at 1-800-729-6887, Monday through Friday, 8:00 am to 4:00 pm EST, or via email at custserv@trophynut.com.

headline: Caraluzzi’s Markets Issues Allergy Alert on Undeclared Egg in Caraluzzi’s Italian Style Seafood Burger, 8 oz

image.caption: Caraluzzi’s Markets Issues Allergy Alert on Undeclared Egg in Caraluzzi’s Italian Style Seafood Burger, 8 oz

description: Caraluzzi’s announced a recall involving Italian Style Seafood Burgers after issues linked to undeclared egg allergen. FDA recall notice published in 2025.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Caraluzzi’s Markets of Bethel, Connecticut, is recalling the 8 oz Caraluzzi’s Italian Style Seafood Burgers due to the presence of undeclared egg. This undeclared allergen poses a serious risk for individuals with an egg allergy or severe sensitivity, potentially causing life-threatening allergic reactions if consumed.

The affected product was sold in-store at Caraluzzi’s Markets locations in Bethel, Wilton, Newtown, and Danbury, Connecticut. The seafood burger comes packaged in 8 oz clear packaging with a blue overwrap label. The expiration date of these products is January 16, 2026, and the package UPC is 0-95864-80008-8. Sales occurred between February 18 and April 2, 2025.

The recall was prompted after a consumer complaint led to an investigation that identified the presence of egg in products with packaging that did not disclose this allergen. To date, one allergic reaction has been reported. The mislabeled seafood burgers have been removed from stores.

Consumers who have purchased this product are advised to return it to the place of purchase for a full refund. For further information or questions, customers may contact Caraluzzi’s Markets at customerservice@caraluzzis.com, call 203-748-3547, or visit the customer service desk at any Caraluzzi’s Markets location.

headline: Max Mobility / Permobil Expands Nationwide Recall of SmartDrive Speed Control Dial Due to the Motor Being Unresponsive to the User
description: Max Mobility/Permobil announced a recall involving Speed Control Dial Component after issues linked to expanded recall due to potential safety and...
keywords: Expanded recall due to potential safety and performance concerns

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Max Mobility, LLC., trading under the Max Mobility/Permobil brand, has announced an expansion of the nationwide recall of their Speed Control Dial component used with the SmartDrive MX2+ Power Assist Device. The recall, initially issued on December 20, 2024, now includes all Speed Control Dials manufactured and distributed between August 17, 2023, and March 10, 2025. This expansion addresses products that may have been inadvertently mixed with redesigned units and shipped after the original recall date.

The Speed Control Dial controls the SmartDrive motor, enabling users to power or deactivate the device. However, a material change identified in the printed circuit board assembly (PCBA) has led to performance issues, including unintended motor activation while the dial appears off, failure to start or stop motor movement properly, and unexpected shutdowns.

As of March 27, 2025, Max Mobility/Permobil received 781 complaints concerning the Speed Control Dial and five serious injury reports, which include fractures (hip, tibia, malleolus bone, ribs) and a concussion. Minor injuries such as skin irritation, cuts, and bruises have also been reported. Involuntary motor activation or inability to turn off the device raises risks not only for the user but also for bystanders in close proximity.

Consumers who have these Speed Control Dial units are advised to discontinue use immediately and switch to alternate wired controllers or wearable controller apps. A replacement Speed Control Dial can be requested through the company’s field action portal. Technical support and adverse event reporting options are available as detailed below.

Brand Model Number Manufacturing Dates
Max Mobility / Permobil MX2-3DCK / MX2-3DC August 17, 2023 – March 10, 2025

Reported Performance Issues Include:

  • Speed Control Dial does not fully stop the drive unit when set to zero position.
  • Unintended activation of SmartDrive motor while the dial is at zero and flashing in standby mode.
  • Motor unexpectedly shuts down after dial is rotated forward from zero position.
  • No motor activation when dial is rotated forward from zero position.

Reported Injuries:

  • Minor: skin irritation, minor cuts, bruises.
  • Serious: muscle/ligament strains or tears, bone fractures (hip, tibia, malleolus bone, ribs), concussion.
  • Risk to bystanders near the user due to involuntary or persistent motor activation.

Recommended Consumer Actions:

  • Immediately stop using the Speed Control Dial.
  • Use alternative wired controllers such as Switch Control buttons, Switch Control with mono jack and mechanical switch, or wearable controllers via compatible Apple Watch or Samsung Galaxy Watch Wear OS app.
  • Request replacement Speed Control Dial units by visiting the field action portal: https://hub.permobil.com/smartdrive-scd-voluntary-field-action.

Contact Information for Support and Reporting:

  • Max Mobility / Permobil Technical Support: (615) 257-2930, available Monday through Friday, 8:00 am to 5:00 pm CT.
  • To report adverse reactions or quality problems to the FDA:
    • Online: MedWatch Online Reporting
    • Regular mail or fax: Download forms at FDA MedWatch Forms or call 800-332-1088 to request a form; mail to the address on the form or fax to 800-332-0178.

For further assistance or information, contact:

  • Max Mobility/Permobil, 300 Duke Drive, Lebanon, TN 37090
  • Phone: 615-257-2930
  • Website: www.permobil.com
  • Audra Watt, VP Marketing, Permobil Americas - Phone: +1 615 992 4221, Email: audra.watt@permobil.com

headline: Bausch + Lomb Announces Voluntary Recall of enVista Aspire™, enVista Envy™ and Certain enVista® Monofocal Intraocular Lenses in the U.S.
description: enVista Aspire announced a recall involving enVista Aspire™, enVista Envy™ and Certain enVista® Monofocal Intraocular Lenses after issues linked to...
keywords: enVista Aspire™, enVista Envy™ and Certain enVista® Monofocal Intraocular Lenses
Increased frequency of reports of toxic anterior segment syndrome

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Bausch + Lomb Corporation has announced a voluntary recall of certain intraocular lenses (IOLs) within its enVista platform in the United States. This recall is prompted by an increased frequency of reports of toxic anterior segment syndrome (TASS), a rare inflammatory complication that can develop 12 to 48 hours following cataract surgery. The exact cause of these complications is currently under investigation.

The recall includes all lots of the enVista Aspire™, enVista Aspire Toric, enVista Envy™, enVista Envy Toric, as well as enVista monofocal and enVista monofocal Toric intraocular lens models sold in the U.S. According to Bausch + Lomb, reported cases of TASS associated with these lenses have been treated successfully without the need to remove the lenses. These cases represent approximately 1-2% of all implanted enVista lenses, and patients have shown a positive prognosis following treatment.

Patients who have recently undergone cataract surgery and are experiencing eye discomfort are advised to contact their eye care professional immediately. Eye care professionals are urged to monitor patients carefully for at least 48 hours post-surgery and to instruct patients to report any new or worsening symptoms promptly.

Further communication and instructions regarding the recall and product return are being issued to eye care professionals. Consumers and clinicians seeking more information or wishing to report adverse events can contact Bausch + Lomb Customer Service or the U.S. FDA’s MedWatch program.

Recalled Products
enVista Aspire™
enVista Aspire Toric
enVista Envy™
enVista Envy Toric
enVista monofocal
enVista monofocal Toric
  • For patients experiencing eye discomfort after cataract surgery: contact your eye care professional immediately.
  • Eye care professionals: monitor patients for at least 48 hours post-surgery and instruct patients to report new or worsening symptoms promptly.
  • To report an adverse event, contact Bausch + Lomb Customer Service at 1-800-338-2020, option 1.
  • Alternatively, report to the U.S. FDA MedWatch program at 1-800-332-1088 to request a reporting form.
  • For additional recall information, contact Bausch + Lomb Customer Service at 1-800-338-2020, option 9 (8:00 am to 4:30 pm Eastern, Monday through Friday).
  • Access related resources: Message from CEO Brent Saunders, U.S. Recall Notice, and TASS Fact Sheet.

headline: T.W. Garner Food Company Issues Recall on Texas Pete® Habanero Buffalo Sauce Due To Potential Presence of Undeclared Sulfites and Sweet CHAbanero Sweet Sriracha Habanero Sauce Due To Mislabeling

image.caption: T.W. Garner Food Company Issues Recall on Texas Pete® Habanero Buffalo Sauce Due To Potential Presence of Undeclared Sulfites and Sweet CHAbanero Sweet Sriracha Habanero Sauce Due To Mislabeling

description: Texas Pete announced a recall involving Condiments after issues linked to potential or undeclared allergen-sulfites. FDA recall notice published in 2025.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
T.W. Garner Food Company has announced a recall of select lots and sizes of Texas Pete® Habanero Buffalo Sauce and Texas Pete® Sweet CHAbanero Sweet Sriracha Habanero Sauce. The recall is due to potential undeclared sulfites in the Habanero Buffalo Sauce and mislabeling issues with the Sweet CHAbanero Sauce. Specifically, some bottles of the Habanero Buffalo Sauce were found to contain Sriracha Sauce, which includes sodium bisulfite (a sulfite preservative) not declared on the label. This poses a risk for consumers with sulfite allergies or sensitivities, who may experience serious or life-threatening reactions if they consume the product.

Additionally, the Sweet CHAbanero Sauce is being recalled because certain lots do not contain the labeled ingredient "Aged Peppers" (Red Habanero Peppers, Salt, Vinegar), meaning the product's contents do not match the label. No illnesses or adverse health events have been reported in connection with these products to date.

The affected products were distributed between February 5, 2025, and March 26, 2025, and were shipped to retail stores and distribution centers in California, Colorado, Connecticut, Illinois, Massachusetts, North Carolina, New Jersey, Ohio, Virginia, and Vermont. Consumers who have purchased these products are advised to dispose of them or return them to the point of purchase for a full refund.

For additional information or questions, consumers may contact Mrs. Katerine Cardoso, Director of Quality Assurance, at (336) 231-6417 Monday through Friday from 8:00 am to 6:00 pm EST, or via email at ContactUs@garnerfoods.com.

headline: Supercan Bulk Recalls a Single Lot of Pig Ear Slivers Pet Treats Due to Salmonella Contamination

image.caption: Supercan Bulk Recalls a Single Lot of Pig Ear Slivers Pet Treats Due to Salmonella Contamination

description: Supercan announced a recall involving Pig ear slivers after issues linked to potential foodborne illness - salmonella. FDA recall notice published in 2025.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Supercan Bulk, based in Miami, Florida, is recalling one lot of Supercan Pig Ear Slivers - Thick Cut Piggy Ear Slices pet treats due to potential Salmonella contamination. The affected product comes in 500-piece bags, weighing 15.9 oz, with an expiration date of November 9, 2026. The recall was initiated after the Washington State Department of Agriculture collected and tested samples on March 4, 2025, confirming the presence of Salmonella.

Salmonella can cause illness in both pets and humans. Pets exposed to contaminated treats may show signs such as lethargy, diarrhea (including bloody diarrhea), fever, vomiting, decreased appetite, and abdominal pain. Healthy pets can also be carriers of Salmonella and may infect other animals or humans. Humans infected with Salmonella may experience nausea, vomiting, diarrhea (sometimes bloody), abdominal cramps, and fever. More serious complications can occur in rare cases.

The recalled products were distributed on April 11, 2024, exclusively at the North 40 store in Washington State. To date, no illnesses have been reported in connection with this recall. Supercan Bulk is working with the FDA to ensure the affected products are safely disposed of and urges consumers who have purchased the recalled treats to either return them to the place of purchase or safely dispose of them to prevent access by children, pets, and wildlife.

Consumers are advised not to sell, donate, or feed the recalled products to any animals. It is important to thoroughly wash and sanitize pet food bowls, cups, and storage containers after exposure. Handwashing with soap is highly recommended after handling the recalled products or any contaminated surfaces. For further information or to request a refund, customers can contact Supercan Bulk at (689) 808-5419, Monday through Friday, from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET.

Product Package Size Lot Number Expiration Date Distribution Location Distribution Date
Supercan Pig Ear Slivers - Thick Cut Piggy Ear Slices 500 pieces / 15.9 oz bag Single recalled lot (not specified) 11/09/2026 North 40 store, Washington State 04/11/2024
  • Do not feed pets or other animals the recalled product.
  • Return the product to the place of purchase or dispose of it safely.
  • Wash and sanitize pet food bowls, cups, and storage containers.
  • Wash and sanitize hands, utensils, and surfaces after contact with the recalled product.
  • Contact healthcare providers if humans develop symptoms after handling the product.
  • Contact a veterinarian if pets show symptoms after consuming the product.
  • For more information or to request a refund, call Supercan Bulk at (689) 808-5419, Monday to Friday, 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET.

headline: Panaderia Salvadorena, Inc. Issues Allergy Alert On Undeclared Milk In Quesadilla De Queso

image.caption: Panaderia Salvadorena, Inc. Issues Allergy Alert On Undeclared Milk In Quesadilla De Queso

description: Panaderia/Bakery announced a recall involving Quesadilla de Queso bread after issues linked to may contain undeclared milk. FDA recall notice published in 2025.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Panaderia Salvadorena Inc., located in Providence, Rhode Island, has issued a recall for its 14-ounce packages of Quesadilla de Queso bread due to the presence of undeclared milk. This poses a serious risk to individuals with milk allergies, as consuming the product may trigger life-threatening allergic reactions.

The affected Quesadilla de Queso bread was distributed through direct delivery in Rhode Island and Massachusetts. The product comes in a clear plastic package weighing 14 ounces, labeled with "Quesadilla de Queso" on the top and featuring an expiration date of June 27, 2025, or earlier.

The recall follows an inspection by the Rhode Island Department of Health, which found that the packaging did not disclose the presence of milk, listing only sour cream and butter as ingredients. The issue was traced back to a temporary failure in the company’s packaging process. Panaderia Salvadorena has recalled the affected product and confirmed that the problem has been resolved.

Consumers who have purchased the Quesadilla de Queso bread in the specified packaging and expiration dates are advised to return it to the place of purchase for a full refund. No illnesses have been reported in connection with this recall to date.

Product Package Size Expiration Date Distribution Area
Quesadilla de Queso bread 14 ounces 06/27/2025 or prior Rhode Island, Massachusetts

For any questions regarding this recall, consumers may contact Panaderia Salvadorena Inc. directly from Monday through Saturday, 8:00 a.m. to 6:00 p.m., at 401-421-8410.
authorityLinks: FDA recall page here.

content.subtitle: Reason for Recall
content.text: May contain undeclared milk

content.subtitle: What Consumers Should Do
content.text: Panaderia Salvadorena Inc. is recalling Quesadilla de Queso bread due to the potential presence of undeclared milk. Consumers should check their product packaging for any lot information and avoid consumption if concerned. For questions or more information, customers can contact the company directly by calling 401-421-8410.

headline: Tony's Chocolonely Recalls Two Chocolate Products Because They May Contain Small Stones

image.caption: Tony's Chocolonely Recalls Two Chocolate Products Because They May Contain Small Stones

description: Tony’s Chocolonely Inc. announced a recall involving Dark Chocolate Almond Sea Salt Bar and Everything Bar after issues linked to potential presence of...
keywords: Dark Chocolate Almond Sea Salt Bar and Everything Bar

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Tony’s Chocolonely Inc., based in New York, New York, is voluntarily recalling two chocolate products due to the potential presence of small stones. The affected items include the Dark Chocolate Almond Sea Salt Bar and the Everything Bar. These products were distributed nationally across the United States through retail stores and online via the official Tony’s Chocolonely website.

The recall concerns seven specific lots of the 6.35 oz (180g) bars, which were distributed between February 7, 2025, and March 24, 2025. The issue was identified after 12 reports from consumers outside the United States and Canada found small stones in the product. These foreign objects were not removed during the third-party almond harvesting and processing stages. No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.

Consumers who have purchased any of the affected lots are advised not to consume the product. Instead, they should return it to the original place of purchase for a refund or a replacement or dispose of it safely. Additional information on how to recognize affected lots and claim a refund or replacement can be found on the company’s recall webpage.
